The system shall return an appropriate failure response whenever Mobile Number Verification cannot be completed successfully.

| Scenario | System Response |
|----------|-----------------|
| Invalid Mobile OTP | Display a generic verification failure message and allow the customer to retry within the permitted verification attempt limit. |
| Expired Mobile OTP | Inform the customer that the Mobile OTP has expired and provide an option to request a new OTP. |
| Maximum OTP Verification Attempts Exceeded | Inform the customer that the verification attempt limit has been exceeded and require a new Mobile OTP to continue verification. |
| OTP Resend Limit Exceeded | Inform the customer that the maximum resend limit has been reached and request them to retry after the configured waiting period. |
| Mobile Number Already Verified | Inform the customer that the Mobile Number has already been verified. |
| SMS Delivery Failure | Inform the customer that the OTP could not be delivered and allow the customer to request another OTP in accordance with the configured resend policy. |
| SMS Gateway Service Unavailable | Inform the customer that the verification service is temporarily unavailable and request them to try again later. |
| Customer Account Not Found | Display a generic verification failure message without revealing whether the Mobile Number exists in the system. |
| Account Not Eligible for Verification | Inform the customer that the account is not eligible for Mobile Number Verification in its current state. |
| Unexpected System Error | Display a generic system error message and advise the customer to try again later. |

---

**Sample Failure Messages**

> **Invalid Mobile OTP. Please try again.**

> **The Mobile OTP has expired. Please request a new OTP.**

> **Maximum verification attempts exceeded. Please request a new Mobile OTP.**

> **You have reached the maximum OTP resend limit. Please try again later.**

> **This Mobile Number has already been verified.**

> **We couldn't deliver your OTP at the moment. Please try again shortly.**

> **The verification service is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later.**

> **Verification could not be completed. Please try again.**

#### 3.1.4.13 Non-Functional Considerations

| Category | Requirement |
|----------|-------------|
| Performance | The system shall complete Mobile OTP verification within **2 seconds** under normal operating conditions, excluding SMS delivery latency. |
| Availability | The Mobile Number Verification service shall maintain a minimum availability of **99.9%**, excluding scheduled maintenance periods. |
| Scalability | The system shall support concurrent Mobile Number Verification requests without degradation in response time or reliability. |
| Security | Mobile OTPs shall be generated using a cryptographically secure random number generator and shall never be stored or transmitted in plain text. |
| Data Privacy | Mobile Numbers, OTPs, and verification-related information shall be processed in accordance with applicable data protection and privacy regulations. |
| Encryption | All communication between the client, application services, and SMS Gateway shall be encrypted using HTTPS with TLS 1.2 or higher. |
| Auditability | All Mobile OTP generation, resend requests, verification attempts, verification status changes, SMS delivery events, and security-related activities shall be recorded in the audit logs with timestamps. |
| Reliability | The system shall ensure that duplicate or delayed SMS messages do not result in multiple successful verifications for the same customer account. |
| Fault Tolerance | Temporary SMS Gateway failures shall not affect customer account integrity. The system shall allow OTP regeneration according to the configured resend policy after service recovery. |
| Maintainability | OTP validity duration, resend limits, verification attempt limits, and SMS provider configurations shall be externally configurable without requiring application code changes. |
| Monitoring | The system shall continuously monitor OTP generation success rate, SMS delivery success rate, verification success rate, verification failures, resend requests, and SMS Gateway availability. |
| Logging | Application logs shall capture operational events, integration failures, and unexpected exceptions without exposing Mobile OTP values or other sensitive customer information. |
| Usability | The Mobile Number Verification interface shall clearly guide customers through the verification process and provide meaningful, user-friendly messages for both successful and unsuccessful verification attempts. |
| Compliance | Mobile Number Verification shall comply with the organization's security policies and all applicable regulatory and legal requirements related to customer authentication and personal data protection. |

#### 3.1.4.14 Acceptance Criteria

| ID | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|---------------------|
| AC-MOBILE-001 | The system shall generate a unique six (6)-digit Mobile OTP when Mobile Number Verification is initiated. |
| AC-MOBILE-002 | The generated Mobile OTP shall expire after **10 minutes** and shall not be accepted thereafter. |
| AC-MOBILE-003 | The system shall successfully verify the customer's Mobile Number only when the submitted OTP matches the latest active OTP within the validity period. |
| AC-MOBILE-004 | The system shall invalidate the Mobile OTP immediately after successful verification or after exceeding the maximum verification attempt limit. |
| AC-MOBILE-005 | The system shall update the Mobile Verification Status to **VERIFIED** and record the Mobile Verification Timestamp upon successful verification. |
| AC-MOBILE-006 | The system shall activate the customer account only after both the Email Address and Mobile Number have been successfully verified. |
| AC-MOBILE-007 | The system shall enforce the configured OTP resend limit and verification attempt limit for every customer account. |
| AC-MOBILE-008 | The system shall record all Mobile OTP generation, resend requests, verification attempts, verification status changes, SMS delivery events, and security-related activities in the audit logs. |
| AC-MOBILE-009 | The system shall display user-friendly success and failure messages without exposing internal validation logic or sensitive system information. |
| AC-MOBILE-010 | The Mobile Number Verification feature shall satisfy all defined functional, security, performance, and compliance requirements specified in this Software Requirements Specification (SRS). |

### 3.1.5 Forgot Password

#### 3.1.5.1 Description

The **Forgot Password** functionality enables a registered customer to securely initiate the password recovery process when they are unable to remember their account password.

The functionality allows the customer to request a password reset using their registered **Email Address** or **Mobile Number**. Upon receiving a valid password reset request, the system shall verify that the customer account exists and is eligible for password recovery. The system shall then generate a secure One-Time Password (OTP) and deliver it to the customer's registered Email Address or Mobile Number through the appropriate communication channel.

The customer shall be required to successfully verify the generated OTP before being permitted to reset the account password. The system shall ensure that password reset requests are performed only for eligible customer accounts and shall prevent unauthorized password recovery attempts through secure validation, rate limiting, OTP expiration, retry restrictions, and security monitoring.

Upon successful OTP verification, the customer shall be allowed to proceed to the **Reset Password** functionality, where a new password can be configured in accordance with the platform's password policy.

The Forgot Password functionality shall maintain customer account security by ensuring that existing passwords are never disclosed, OTPs are securely generated and validated, password recovery activities are fully auditable, and sensitive account information is protected throughout the recovery process.

The system shall record every password recovery request, OTP generation event, OTP verification attempt, OTP resend request, successful password recovery initiation, failed recovery attempt, and security-related activity in the audit log for security monitoring, compliance, forensic analysis, and operational traceability.

#### 3.1.5.2 Actors

##### Primary Actor

- Customer

##### Supporting Actors

- Authentication Service
- OTP Generation Service
- OTP Verification Service
- Email Service
- SMS Gateway Service
- Audit Logging Service

#### 3.1.5.3 Preconditions

The following conditions shall be satisfied before a customer can initiate the Forgot Password process:

1. The customer account shall exist in the system.
2. The customer account shall not be permanently deleted.
3. The customer shall provide a registered Email Address or Mobile Number associated with the customer account.
4. The Authentication Service shall be operational.
5. The OTP Generation Service shall be available.
6. The OTP Verification Service shall be available.
7. The Email Service and SMS Gateway Service shall be operational for OTP delivery through the selected communication channel.
8. The platform shall be accessible over a secure HTTPS connection.
9. The customer shall have access to the registered Email Address or Mobile Number to receive the One-Time Password (OTP).
10. The customer shall have an active internet connection.
11. The platform shall allow password recovery only for customer accounts that are eligible for password reset according to the platform's security policies.

#### 3.1.5.4 Trigger

The Forgot Password process is initiated when a customer selects the **Forgot Password** option on the Login page, provides a registered Email Address or Mobile Number, and submits the password recovery request to reset the account password.

#### 3.1.5.5 Input Fields

| Field Name | Data Type | Mandatory | Description | Validation |
|------------|-----------|-----------|-------------|------------|
| Email Address / Mobile Number | String | Yes | Registered Email Address or Mobile Number associated with the customer account used to identify the account for password recovery. | Shall be a valid registered Email Address or Mobile Number. |
| Recovery Method | Enumeration | Yes | Indicates the preferred password recovery channel. | Supported values: **EMAIL**, **SMS**. |
| OTP | Numeric | No* | One-Time Password (OTP) generated by the system for customer verification. Required during the OTP verification step. | Shall contain exactly six (6) numeric digits and match the latest active OTP issued by the system. |
| Resend OTP | Action | No | Allows the customer to request a new OTP when the previous OTP has expired or has not been received. | Subject to OTP resend policy and rate limiting. |

**Notes:**

- The customer shall provide either the registered **Email Address** or **Mobile Number** to identify the account.
- The customer shall select one of the supported recovery methods available for the registered account.
- The OTP field becomes mandatory only after the system successfully generates and delivers an OTP.
- The Email Address or Mobile Number used during password recovery shall not be modifiable after OTP generation unless the customer restarts the Forgot Password process.
- Only one active OTP shall exist for a customer account at any given time.
- Requesting a new OTP shall invalidate any previously issued unexpired OTP.

#### 3.1.5.6 Business Rules

1. The system shall allow password recovery only for registered customer accounts.

2. The customer shall initiate the Forgot Password process using either the registered **Email Address** or **Mobile Number** associated with the customer account.

3. The system shall verify that the submitted Email Address or Mobile Number belongs to an existing customer account before initiating the password recovery process.

4. The system shall generate a unique six (6)-digit numeric One-Time Password (OTP) for every valid password recovery request.

5. The generated OTP shall be valid for **10 minutes** from the time of generation.

6. The system shall maintain only one active password recovery OTP for a customer account at any given time.

7. Whenever a new OTP is generated, all previously generated unexpired OTPs for the same password recovery request shall be invalidated automatically.

8. The system shall deliver the OTP only through the customer-selected recovery channel (Email or SMS), provided that the selected channel is registered with the customer account.

9. The customer shall be allowed to request OTP resend using the **Resend OTP** functionality.

10. The system shall permit a maximum of **three (3) OTP resend requests within one hour**. Additional resend requests within the same period shall be rejected.

11. The system shall permit a maximum of **five (5) OTP verification attempts** for a single OTP. Upon exceeding the maximum limit, the OTP shall be invalidated automatically.

12. The system shall verify only the latest active OTP issued for the customer account.

13. The system shall reject expired, invalid, revoked, or previously used OTPs.

14. Successful OTP verification shall authorize the customer to proceed to the **Reset Password** functionality. Password modification shall not occur during the Forgot Password process.

15. The Forgot Password process shall not reveal the customer's existing password under any circumstance.

16. The system shall not disclose whether the submitted Email Address or Mobile Number exists in the platform through user-facing error messages.

17. Every password recovery request, OTP generation, OTP resend request, successful verification, failed verification attempt, and security-related event shall be recorded in the audit log.

18. All Forgot Password requests, OTP transmissions, and verification operations shall be processed only over secure HTTPS connections.

19. The system shall enforce rate limiting to protect the Forgot Password functionality from brute-force attacks, OTP abuse, enumeration attacks, and other malicious activities.

20. The Forgot Password functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, security, privacy, and data protection policies.

#### 3.1.5.7 Validations

| Validation Category | Validation Rule |
|---------------------|-----------------|
| Recovery Identifier | The customer shall provide either a registered **Email Address** or **Mobile Number** to initiate the password recovery process. |
| Email Address Format | When an Email Address is used, it shall comply with the platform's supported email format. |
| Mobile Number Format | When a Mobile Number is used, it shall contain only numeric characters and comply with the supported country format. |
| Recovery Method | The selected recovery method shall be one of the supported values (**EMAIL** or **SMS**) and shall correspond to a registered communication channel for the customer account. |
| Account Existence | The submitted Email Address or Mobile Number shall be associated with an existing customer account. |
| Account Eligibility | Password recovery shall be permitted only for customer accounts eligible for password reset according to the platform's security policies. |
| OTP Required | The OTP field shall be mandatory during the OTP verification step. |
| OTP Format | The OTP shall contain exactly six (6) numeric digits. |
| OTP Expiry | The OTP shall be accepted only within the configured validity period of **10 minutes**. |
| OTP Match | The submitted OTP shall exactly match the latest active OTP issued for the customer account. |
| OTP Reuse | Previously used, revoked, or invalidated OTPs shall not be accepted. |
| OTP Attempts | A maximum of **five (5)** OTP verification attempts shall be permitted for a single OTP. |
| OTP Resend | The customer shall not exceed **three (3)** OTP resend requests within one hour. |
| Rate Limiting | Password recovery requests exceeding the configured threshold shall be temporarily restricted to prevent abuse and brute-force attacks. |
| Input Sanitization | All user inputs shall be sanitized to prevent SQL Injection, Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), HTML Injection, Command Injection, and other malicious input attacks. |
| Secure Communication | All Forgot Password requests and OTP verification requests shall be processed only over HTTPS. |
| Error Messages | Failure responses shall not disclose whether the submitted Email Address or Mobile Number exists in the platform. |
| Audit Validation | Every password recovery request, OTP generation, OTP resend request, successful verification, failed verification attempt, and security-related event shall be recorded in the audit log. |

#### 3.1.5.8 Main Flow

1. The customer navigates to the Login page.

2. The customer selects the **Forgot Password** option.

3. The system displays the Forgot Password page.

4. The customer enters the registered Email Address or Mobile Number.

5. The customer selects the preferred password recovery method (**EMAIL** or **SMS**), where applicable.

6. The customer submits the password recovery request.

7. The system validates all mandatory input fields.

8. The system validates the format of the submitted Email Address or Mobile Number.

9. The system sanitizes all input values to prevent malicious input attacks.

10. The system verifies that the customer account exists and is eligible for password recovery.

11. The system generates a unique six (6)-digit One-Time Password (OTP).

12. The system securely stores the generated OTP along with its expiration time.

13. The system delivers the OTP to the customer's registered Email Address or Mobile Number through the selected recovery channel.

14. The system displays the OTP Verification page.

15. The customer enters the received OTP.

16. The customer submits the OTP for verification.

17. The system validates the submitted OTP.

18. The system verifies that the OTP has not expired.

19. The system verifies that the submitted OTP matches the latest active OTP.

20. The system verifies that the maximum OTP verification attempt limit has not been exceeded.

21. Upon successful OTP verification, the system invalidates the verified OTP to prevent reuse.

22. The system records the successful password recovery verification event in the audit log.

23. The system authorizes the customer to proceed to the **Reset Password** functionality.

24. The system redirects the customer to the **Reset Password** page.

#### 3.1.5.9 Alternate Flows

##### AF-1: Customer Account Not Found

1. The customer submits an unregistered Email Address or Mobile Number.
2. The system processes the request without revealing whether the account exists.
3. The system displays a generic response indicating that, if the account exists, password recovery instructions will be sent.
4. The system records the request for audit and security monitoring purposes.

---

##### AF-2: Invalid OTP

1. The customer enters an incorrect OTP.
2. The system rejects the verification request.
3. The system increments the failed OTP verification attempt counter.
4. The system records the failed verification attempt in the audit log.
5. The system displays a generic verification failure message.
6. The customer may retry verification if the maximum attempt limit has not been exceeded.

---

##### AF-3: Expired OTP

1. The customer submits an OTP after its validity period has expired.
2. The system rejects the verification request.
3. The system invalidates the expired OTP.
4. The system informs the customer that the OTP has expired.
5. The customer is prompted to request a new OTP.

---

##### AF-4: Maximum OTP Verification Attempts Exceeded

1. The customer exceeds the maximum permitted OTP verification attempts.
2. The system invalidates the active OTP.
3. The system temporarily blocks further verification attempts for the current OTP.
4. The system instructs the customer to request a new OTP.
5. The system records the security event in the audit log.

---

##### AF-5: OTP Resend Limit Exceeded

1. The customer exceeds the maximum permitted OTP resend requests within the configured time window.
2. The system rejects the resend request.
3. The system informs the customer that the resend limit has been exceeded.
4. The customer is requested to retry after the configured waiting period.
5. The system records the resend limit violation for security monitoring.

---

##### AF-6: Email Service or SMS Gateway Service Unavailable

1. The system is unable to deliver the OTP due to an Email Service or SMS Gateway Service failure.
2. The system records the delivery failure.
3. The system informs the customer that the OTP could not be delivered.
4. The customer is requested to retry later or use the **Resend OTP** option once the service becomes available.

---

##### AF-7: Rate Limit Exceeded

1. The customer exceeds the permitted password recovery request threshold within the configured time window.
2. The system temporarily blocks additional password recovery requests.
3. The system informs the customer to retry after the specified waiting period.
4. The system records the rate-limiting event for security monitoring.

---

##### AF-8: Unexpected System Error

1. An unexpected application or infrastructure error occurs during the Forgot Password process.
2. The system safely terminates the password recovery process.
3. The system records detailed diagnostic information in the application logs.
4. The customer receives a generic error message without exposing internal implementation details.

#### 3.1.5.10 Postconditions

##### Successful Password Recovery Verification

Upon successful OTP verification, the system shall:

1. Verify the customer's identity successfully.
2. Invalidate the verified OTP to prevent reuse.
3. Record the successful password recovery verification event in the audit log.
4. Authorize the customer to access the **Reset Password** functionality.
5. Create a secure password reset session or reset authorization token with a limited validity period.
6. Redirect the customer to the **Reset Password** page.

##### Failed Password Recovery Verification

Upon unsuccessful OTP verification, the system shall:

1. Reject the password recovery verification request.
2. Increment the failed OTP verification attempt counter, where applicable.
3. Record the failed verification event in the audit log.
4. Invalidate the OTP if it has expired or if the maximum verification attempt limit has been exceeded.
5. Prevent access to the **Reset Password** functionality.
6. Return an appropriate verification failure response without exposing sensitive system information.

#### 3.1.5.11 Success Response

Upon successful completion of the Forgot Password verification process, the system shall:

- Verify the customer's identity successfully using the submitted OTP.
- Invalidate the verified OTP to prevent reuse.
- Create a secure password reset session or password reset authorization token.
- Authorize the customer to access the **Reset Password** functionality.
- Redirect the customer to the **Reset Password** page.
- Display a confirmation message indicating that identity verification has been completed successfully.
- Record the successful password recovery verification event in the audit log.
- Allow the customer to set a new password in accordance with the platform's password policy.

#### 3.1.5.12 Failure Response

The system shall reject or terminate the Forgot Password process under the following conditions:

- Required input fields are missing.
- The submitted Email Address or Mobile Number is invalid.
- The selected recovery method is not supported or is unavailable for the customer account.
- The submitted OTP is missing.
- The submitted OTP format is invalid.
- The submitted OTP is incorrect.
- The submitted OTP has expired.
- The submitted OTP has already been used.
- The submitted OTP has been revoked or invalidated.
- The maximum OTP verification attempt limit has been exceeded.
- The maximum OTP resend request limit has been exceeded.
- The password recovery request exceeds the configured rate-limiting threshold.
- The Email Service or SMS Gateway Service is unavailable.
- The OTP Generation Service or OTP Verification Service is unavailable.
- The Authentication Service is unavailable.
- An unexpected internal server error occurs during the password recovery process.

In all failure scenarios, the system shall return a generic password recovery failure response without revealing whether the submitted Email Address or Mobile Number exists in the platform, thereby preventing user enumeration and protecting sensitive account information.

#### 3.1.5.13 Non-Functional Considerations

- All Forgot Password requests, OTP generation requests, OTP verification requests, and password recovery communications shall be transmitted only over secure HTTPS connections.
- OTPs shall be generated using a cryptographically secure random number generation mechanism.
- OTP values shall never be stored, logged, or transmitted in plain text.
- The system shall complete at least **95% of successful Forgot Password requests within 2 seconds**, excluding external Email or SMS delivery time.
- The system shall complete at least **95% of successful OTP verification requests within 2 seconds** under normal operating conditions.
- The Forgot Password service shall support concurrent password recovery requests from multiple customers without significant performance degradation.
- The system shall implement rate limiting, brute-force protection, and request throttling to prevent abuse of the password recovery functionality.
- Every password recovery request, OTP generation event, OTP resend request, successful verification, failed verification attempt, and security-related event shall be recorded in the audit log for compliance and forensic analysis.
- Failure responses shall not expose sensitive security information, internal implementation details, or account existence.
- The Forgot Password functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, security, privacy, and applicable data protection policies.
- Password recovery sessions or reset authorization tokens shall have a configurable expiration time and shall become invalid immediately after successful password reset or expiration.
- The password recovery process shall be highly available and resilient to temporary failures of dependent services, with appropriate retry and error-handling mechanisms where applicable.

#### 3.1.5.14 Acceptance Criteria

| ID | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|---------------------|
| **AC-FP-001** | Given a registered customer account, when a valid Email Address or Mobile Number is submitted, then the system shall initiate the Forgot Password process successfully. |
| **AC-FP-002** | Given a valid password recovery request, when the system generates an OTP, then the OTP shall be delivered to the customer's registered Email Address or Mobile Number through the selected recovery channel. |
| **AC-FP-003** | Given a valid and unexpired OTP, when the customer submits the OTP, then the system shall successfully verify the customer's identity and allow access to the **Reset Password** functionality. |
| **AC-FP-004** | Given an incorrect, expired, revoked, or previously used OTP, when verification is attempted, then the system shall reject the verification request. |
| **AC-FP-005** | Given five (5) unsuccessful OTP verification attempts, when another verification attempt is made, then the system shall invalidate the active OTP and require the customer to request a new OTP. |
| **AC-FP-006** | Given three (3) OTP resend requests within one hour, when another resend request is made within the same period, then the system shall reject the resend request in accordance with the configured resend policy. |
| **AC-FP-007** | Given successful OTP verification, when the verification process is completed, then the system shall create a secure password reset session or authorization token and redirect the customer to the **Reset Password** page. |
| **AC-FP-008** | The Forgot Password process shall not disclose whether the submitted Email Address or Mobile Number exists in the platform through user-facing responses. |
| **AC-FP-009** | Every password recovery request, OTP generation event, OTP resend request, successful verification, failed verification attempt, and security-related event shall be recorded in the audit log. |
| **AC-FP-010** | The Forgot Password functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, security, privacy, and data protection requirements while protecting customer account information throughout the recovery process. |

### 3.1.6 Reset Password

#### 3.1.6.1 Description

The **Reset Password** functionality enables a customer who has successfully completed the **Forgot Password** verification process to securely create a new password for their account.

The functionality shall be accessible only after successful identity verification through the Forgot Password process. The system shall validate the customer's password reset authorization session or token before allowing the customer to configure a new password.

The customer shall provide a new password and confirm the new password. The system shall validate the submitted password against the platform's password policy, verify that the confirmation password matches, and ensure that the new password is not identical to the customer's current password.

Upon successful validation, the system shall securely hash the new password using the platform's approved password hashing algorithm and update the customer's account credentials. The password reset authorization session or token shall be invalidated immediately after successful password reset to prevent reuse.

As an additional security measure, the system shall invalidate all active authenticated sessions associated with the customer account and require the customer to authenticate again using the newly configured password.

The Reset Password functionality shall ensure secure password management by protecting password confidentiality, preventing unauthorized password changes, maintaining complete audit records, and complying with the platform's authentication, security, and privacy requirements.

The system shall record every successful password reset, failed password reset attempt, password policy validation failure, session invalidation event, and other security-related activities in the audit log for compliance, forensic analysis, and operational traceability.

#### 3.1.6.2 Actors

##### Primary Actor

- Customer

##### Supporting Actors

- Authentication Service
- Password Management Service
- Session Management Service
- JWT Token Service
- Audit Logging Service

#### 3.1.6.2 Actors

##### Primary Actor

- Customer

##### Supporting Actors

- Authentication Service
- Password Management Service
- Session Management Service
- JWT Token Service
- Audit Logging Service

#### 3.1.6.3 Preconditions

The following conditions shall be satisfied before a customer can reset the account password:

1. The customer shall have successfully completed the **Forgot Password** verification process.
2. The customer shall possess a valid password reset authorization session or reset token issued by the system.
3. The password reset authorization session or token shall not be expired, revoked, or previously used.
4. The customer account shall exist in the system.
5. The customer account shall be eligible for password reset according to the platform's security policies.
6. The Authentication Service shall be operational.
7. The Password Management Service shall be available.
8. The Session Management Service shall be operational.
9. The platform shall be accessible over a secure HTTPS connection.
10. The customer shall provide a new password that complies with the platform's password policy.
11. The customer shall provide a matching confirmation password.

#### 3.1.6.4 Trigger

The Reset Password process is initiated when a customer, after successfully completing the **Forgot Password** verification process, enters a new password and its confirmation on the **Reset Password** page and submits the password reset request.

#### 3.1.6.5 Input Fields

| Field Name | Data Type | Mandatory | Description | Validation |
|------------|-----------|-----------|-------------|------------|
| New Password | Password | Yes | The new password that the customer wants to configure for the account. | Shall comply with the platform's password policy. |
| Confirm New Password | Password | Yes | Re-entered password used to confirm the new password. | Shall exactly match the **New Password** field. |
| Password Reset Token / Reset Session | String | Yes | Secure authorization token or session created after successful Forgot Password verification. | Shall be valid, unexpired, unused, and associated with the customer account. |

**Notes:**

- The customer shall enter the new password only after successful identity verification through the **Forgot Password** process.
- Password fields shall always be masked while being entered.
- The platform shall not pre-fill, display, or expose any existing password.
- The new password shall not be identical to the customer's current password.
- The password reset token or authorization session shall be generated by the system and shall not be modifiable by the customer.
- The password reset token or authorization session shall become invalid immediately after a successful password reset or upon expiration.

#### 3.1.6.6 Business Rules

1. Password reset shall be permitted only after successful completion of the **Forgot Password** identity verification process.
2. A valid password reset authorization session or reset token shall be required before allowing a password reset.
3. The password reset authorization session or token shall expire after **15 minutes** from the time of issuance.
4. A password reset authorization session or token shall be usable only once.
5. Upon successful password reset, the authorization session or token shall be immediately invalidated.
6. The new password shall comply with the platform's password policy.
7. The new password shall contain a minimum of **8 characters** and a maximum of **64 characters**.
8. The new password shall include at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one numeric digit, and one special character.
9. The new password shall not be identical to the customer's current password.
10. The **New Password** and **Confirm New Password** fields shall contain identical values.
11. Passwords shall never be stored, transmitted, or logged in plain text.
12. The system shall securely hash the new password using the platform-approved password hashing algorithm before storing it.
13. Upon successful password reset, all active authenticated sessions associated with the customer account shall be invalidated.
14. The customer shall be required to authenticate again using the newly configured password.
15. The system shall record every successful password reset, failed password reset attempt, password validation failure, session invalidation event, and security-related activity in the audit log.
16. Password reset requests shall be processed only over secure HTTPS connections.
17. The system shall protect against replay attacks by rejecting expired, revoked, or previously used password reset tokens.
18. Failure responses shall not disclose sensitive security information or internal implementation details.
19. The system shall enforce rate limiting on password reset requests to mitigate abuse and automated attacks.
20. The password reset functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, security, privacy, and applicable data protection policies.

#### 3.1.6.7 Validations

| Validation | Rule | Failure Response |
|------------|------|------------------|
| Password Reset Authorization | A valid password reset session or reset token shall exist before allowing password reset. | Password reset request shall be rejected. |
| Reset Token Validity | The password reset token or authorization session shall not be expired, revoked, or previously used. | Password reset request shall be rejected. |
| New Password Required | The **New Password** field shall not be empty. | "New Password is required." |
| Confirm Password Required | The **Confirm New Password** field shall not be empty. | "Confirm New Password is required." |
| Password Length | Password length shall be between **8 and 64 characters**. | "Password does not meet the minimum security requirements." |
| Uppercase Validation | Password shall contain at least one uppercase letter (A–Z). | "Password does not meet the minimum security requirements." |
| Lowercase Validation | Password shall contain at least one lowercase letter (a–z). | "Password does not meet the minimum security requirements." |
| Numeric Validation | Password shall contain at least one numeric digit (0–9). | "Password does not meet the minimum security requirements." |
| Special Character Validation | Password shall contain at least one approved special character. | "Password does not meet the minimum security requirements." |
| Password Match Validation | **New Password** and **Confirm New Password** shall contain identical values. | "Passwords do not match." |
| Password Reuse Validation | The new password shall not be identical to the customer's current password. | "New password cannot be the same as the current password." |
| Password Hashing Validation | Password shall be securely hashed before storage. Plain-text password storage shall not be permitted. | Password update shall be rejected and logged. |
| HTTPS Validation | Password reset requests shall be accepted only over secure HTTPS connections. | Request shall be rejected. |
| Input Sanitization | All submitted inputs shall be validated and sanitized before processing. | Invalid request shall be rejected. |
| Rate Limiting Validation | Password reset requests exceeding configured limits shall be rejected. | "Too many requests. Please try again later." |
| Session Invalidation Validation | Upon successful password reset, all active authenticated sessions shall be invalidated. | Session invalidation failure shall be logged and handled according to security policy. |
| Audit Validation | Every password reset attempt, successful or unsuccessful, shall be recorded in the audit log. | Audit logging failure shall be reported according to operational policy. |

#### 3.1.6.8 Main Flow

1. The customer successfully completes the **Forgot Password** process.
2. The system generates a secure password reset authorization session or reset token.
3. The system redirects the customer to the **Reset Password** page.
4. The customer enters a **New Password**.
5. The customer enters the **Confirm New Password**.
6. The customer submits the password reset request.
7. The system validates the password reset authorization session or reset token.
8. The system verifies that the reset session or token has not expired.
9. The system verifies that the reset session or token has not been previously used or revoked.
10. The system validates that all mandatory fields are provided.
11. The system validates the new password against the platform's password policy.
12. The system verifies that the **New Password** and **Confirm New Password** values match.
13. The system verifies that the new password is different from the customer's current password.
14. The system securely hashes the new password using the approved password hashing algorithm.
15. The system updates the customer's password in the authentication repository.
16. The system invalidates the password reset authorization session or reset token.
17. The system invalidates all active authenticated sessions associated with the customer account.
18. The system records the password reset event in the audit log.
19. The system displays a password reset success confirmation message.
20. The system redirects the customer to the **Login** page.
21. The customer logs in using the newly configured password.
22. The system authenticates the customer using the updated credentials.
23. The system establishes a new authenticated session upon successful login.
24. The customer gains access to the platform.

#### 3.1.6.9 Alternate Flows

##### AF-1: Invalid or Expired Password Reset Token

1. The customer submits the password reset request.
2. The system determines that the password reset authorization session or reset token is invalid, expired, revoked, or previously used.
3. The system rejects the password reset request.
4. The system records the security event in the audit log.
5. The system redirects the customer to the **Forgot Password** page to initiate a new password recovery process.

---

##### AF-2: Password Policy Validation Failure

1. The customer submits a new password.
2. The system determines that the password does not satisfy the platform's password policy.
3. The system rejects the password reset request.
4. The system displays an appropriate password policy validation message.
5. The customer enters a compliant password and resubmits the request.

---

##### AF-3: Password Confirmation Mismatch

1. The customer submits the password reset request.
2. The system detects that the **New Password** and **Confirm New Password** values do not match.
3. The system rejects the request.
4. The system informs the customer that the passwords do not match.
5. The customer corrects the values and submits the request again.

---

##### AF-4: New Password Matches Current Password

1. The customer submits a new password.
2. The system determines that the new password is identical to the current password.
3. The system rejects the request.
4. The system informs the customer that a different password must be selected.
5. The customer provides a different password and retries.

---

##### AF-5: Rate Limit Exceeded

1. The customer submits excessive password reset requests within the configured time window.
2. The system detects that the rate limit has been exceeded.
3. The system temporarily rejects the request.
4. The security event is recorded in the audit log.
5. The customer may retry after the configured waiting period.

---

##### AF-6: Authentication or Password Management Service Unavailable

1. The customer submits the password reset request.
2. The Authentication Service or Password Management Service is unavailable.
3. The system cannot complete the password reset process.
4. The failure is logged for operational monitoring.
5. The system informs the customer that the request cannot be processed at the moment and to try again later.

---

##### AF-7: Unexpected System Error

1. The customer submits the password reset request.
2. An unexpected system error occurs during processing.
3. The system safely terminates the transaction without modifying the customer's password.
4. The error is recorded in the audit log.
5. The customer receives a generic error message without exposing internal system details.

#### 3.1.6.10 Postconditions

##### Successful Password Reset

After a successful password reset:

1. The customer's password shall be securely updated in the authentication repository.
2. The new password shall be stored only in its securely hashed form.
3. The password reset authorization session or reset token shall be permanently invalidated.
4. All active authenticated sessions associated with the customer account shall be invalidated.
5. The customer shall be required to authenticate again using the newly configured password.
6. The password reset event shall be recorded in the audit log.
7. The customer shall be redirected to the **Login** page.
8. The previous password shall no longer be accepted for authentication.

##### Failed Password Reset

If the password reset process fails:

1. The customer's existing password shall remain unchanged.
2. No password update shall be committed to the authentication repository.
3. Invalid, expired, revoked, or previously used password reset tokens shall be rejected.
4. Failed password reset attempts shall be recorded in the audit log.
5. The customer shall not be granted access to the platform until successful authentication.
6. Appropriate validation or security error messages shall be displayed without exposing sensitive system information.
7. The system shall maintain data integrity by rolling back any partially completed password update operation.

#### 3.1.6.11 Success Response

Upon successful password reset, the system shall:

- Successfully update the customer's password in the authentication repository using the platform-approved password hashing algorithm.
- Permanently invalidate the password reset authorization session or reset token.
- Invalidate all active authenticated sessions associated with the customer account.
- Require the customer to authenticate again using the newly configured password.
- Display a confirmation message indicating that the password has been reset successfully.
- Redirect the customer to the **Login** page.
- Allow authentication only with the newly configured password.
- Reject authentication attempts using the previous password.
- Record the successful password reset, session invalidation, and related security events in the audit log.
- Ensure that no sensitive password information, reset token, or internal security details are included in the response.

#### 3.1.6.12 Failure Response

The system shall reject the password reset request and return an appropriate response under the following conditions:

- The password reset authorization session or reset token is missing.
- The password reset authorization session or reset token is invalid.
- The password reset authorization session or reset token has expired.
- The password reset authorization session or reset token has been revoked or previously used.
- The **New Password** field is missing.
- The **Confirm New Password** field is missing.
- The new password does not satisfy the platform's password policy.
- The **New Password** and **Confirm New Password** values do not match.
- The new password is identical to the customer's current password.
- The password reset request exceeds the configured rate limit.
- The Authentication Service is unavailable.
- The Password Management Service is unavailable.
- The Session Management Service is unavailable.
- The request is received over an insecure communication channel.
- Invalid, malformed, or potentially malicious input is detected.
- An unexpected internal system error occurs while processing the password reset request.

For every failed password reset attempt, the system shall:

- Preserve the customer's existing password without modification.
- Prevent the password reset operation from being completed.
- Record the failure and associated security event in the audit log.
- Avoid exposing sensitive information such as password values, reset tokens, account status, or internal implementation details.
- Return a generic and secure error response consistent with the platform's authentication and security policies.

#### 3.1.6.13 Non-Functional Considerations

- All Reset Password requests shall be transmitted only over secure HTTPS connections.
- Passwords shall never be stored, logged, cached, or transmitted in plain text.
- The system shall hash passwords using the platform-approved password hashing algorithm before storing them in the authentication repository.
- The password reset authorization session or reset token shall be cryptographically secure, single-use, and expire after the configured validity period.
- The system shall complete at least **95% of successful password reset requests within 2 seconds** under normal operating conditions, excluding network latency.
- The password reset functionality shall support concurrent password reset requests from multiple customers without significant performance degradation.
- Upon successful password reset, all active authenticated sessions associated with the customer account shall be invalidated immediately to prevent unauthorized access.
- The system shall require the customer to authenticate again using the newly configured password before accessing any protected resources.
- Password reset requests shall be protected against replay attacks, brute-force attacks, session hijacking, and other common authentication threats through appropriate security controls.
- Every successful password reset, failed password reset attempt, password validation failure, token validation failure, session invalidation event, and other security-related activities shall be recorded in the audit log for compliance and forensic analysis.
- Failure responses shall not expose sensitive information such as password values, reset tokens, account status, authentication logic, or internal implementation details.
- The Reset Password functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, security, privacy, password management, and applicable data protection policies.
- The password reset process shall be highly available and resilient to temporary failures of dependent services, with appropriate retry and error-handling mechanisms where applicable.

#### 3.1.6.14 Acceptance Criteria

| ID | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|---------------------|
| **AC-RP-001** | Given a customer has successfully completed the **Forgot Password** process, when the customer accesses the **Reset Password** page using a valid password reset session or token, then the system shall allow the customer to submit a new password. |
| **AC-RP-002** | Given a valid password reset request, when the customer submits a new password that complies with the platform's password policy and matches the confirmation password, then the system shall successfully update the customer's password. |
| **AC-RP-003** | Given a successful password reset, when the password update is completed, then the system shall securely hash the password before storing it in the authentication repository. |
| **AC-RP-004** | Given a successful password reset, when the password is updated, then the system shall immediately invalidate the password reset session or token and all active authenticated sessions associated with the customer account. |
| **AC-RP-005** | Given an invalid, expired, revoked, or previously used password reset session or token, when the customer attempts to reset the password, then the system shall reject the request and require the customer to initiate a new **Forgot Password** process. |
| **AC-RP-006** | Given the submitted password does not satisfy the platform's password policy or does not match the confirmation password, when the customer submits the password reset request, then the system shall reject the request without updating the password. |
| **AC-RP-007** | Given a successful password reset, when the customer attempts to authenticate, then the previous password shall no longer be accepted and authentication shall be permitted only with the newly configured password. |
| **AC-RP-008** | Every successful password reset, failed password reset attempt, password validation failure, token validation failure, session invalidation event, and security-related activity shall be recorded in the audit log. |
| **AC-RP-009** | The Reset Password functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, security, privacy, and applicable data protection requirements while ensuring password confidentiality and integrity. |
| **AC-RP-010** | The Reset Password functionality shall prevent unauthorized password changes by accepting password reset requests only through valid, secure, and single-use password reset authorization sessions or tokens. |

### 3.1.7 Customer Logout

#### 3.1.7.1 Description

The **Customer Logout** functionality enables an authenticated customer to securely terminate the current authenticated session and prevent further access to protected platform resources until the customer successfully authenticates again.

A customer may initiate logout from any authenticated page of the platform by selecting the **Logout** option. Upon receiving the logout request, the system shall validate the active authenticated session and invalidate the associated session identifier, access token, refresh token, or other authentication credentials according to the platform's authentication mechanism.

Following successful logout, the system shall remove or invalidate all authentication artifacts associated with the current session to prevent unauthorized reuse. The customer shall be redirected to the **Login** page or the platform's public landing page.

If the customer attempts to access any protected resource after logout without authenticating again, the system shall deny access and require successful authentication before granting access.

The Logout functionality shall maintain complete audit records for security monitoring, ensure secure session termination, protect against session hijacking and token reuse, and comply with the platform's authentication, security, and privacy requirements.

#### 3.1.7.2 Actors

##### Primary Actor

- Customer

##### Supporting Actors

- Authentication Service
- Session Management Service
- JWT Token Service
- Audit Logging Service

#### 3.1.7.3 Preconditions

The following conditions shall be satisfied before a customer can log out of the platform:

1. The customer shall be successfully authenticated.
2. The customer shall have an active authenticated session.
3. The authentication token or session identifier shall be valid.
4. The Authentication Service shall be operational.
5. The Session Management Service shall be available.
6. The platform shall be accessible over a secure HTTPS connection.
7. The customer shall have permission to access the Logout functionality.
8. The Audit Logging Service shall be operational to record logout events.

---

#### 3.1.7.4 Trigger

The Logout process is initiated when an authenticated customer selects the **Logout** option from the application interface or when the platform initiates a forced logout due to security or administrative policies.

---

#### 3.1.7.5 Input Fields

| Field Name | Data Type | Mandatory | Description | Validation |
|------------|-----------|-----------|-------------|------------|
| Access Token / Session ID | String | Yes | Active authentication token or session identifier associated with the authenticated customer. | Shall be valid and active. |
| Refresh Token | String | No* | Refresh token associated with the authenticated session, if applicable. | Shall belong to the authenticated customer. |

**Notes:**

- Customers are not required to manually enter any input while performing logout.
- Authentication credentials shall be obtained from the active authenticated session.
- The Logout functionality shall terminate only the current authenticated session unless the platform explicitly supports logout from all devices.
- Logout requests shall be processed only for authenticated customers.

---

#### 3.1.7.6 Business Rules

1. Only authenticated customers shall be permitted to perform logout.
2. Logout shall invalidate the current authenticated session.
3. The associated access token shall be immediately invalidated after successful logout.
4. The associated refresh token shall be revoked after successful logout.
5. Logged-out authentication credentials shall not be reusable.
6. Access to protected resources shall be denied after logout.
7. The customer shall be redirected to the Login page or public landing page after successful logout.
8. Logout requests shall be processed only over secure HTTPS connections.
9. Session termination shall be immediate after successful logout.
10. Every logout event shall be recorded in the audit log.
11. Forced logout initiated by security policies or administrators shall follow the same session invalidation process.
12. Logout shall not modify customer profile or business data.
13. Multiple logout requests for an already terminated session shall be handled gracefully.
14. Expired or invalid sessions shall not be reactivated during logout processing.
15. Failure responses shall not expose internal authentication details.
16. Logout operations shall comply with authentication, security, privacy, and applicable data protection policies.

---

#### 3.1.7.7 Validations

| Validation | Rule | Failure Response |
|------------|------|------------------|
| Authentication Validation | The customer shall be authenticated before initiating the logout process. | "Authentication required." |
| Active Session Validation | An active authenticated session shall exist for the customer. | "No active session found." |
| Session Ownership Validation | The authenticated session shall belong to the requesting customer. | "Unauthorized logout request." |
| Access Token Validation | The access token shall be valid, active, and associated with the authenticated session. | "Invalid or expired authentication session." |
| Refresh Token Validation | If refresh tokens are used, the refresh token shall belong to the authenticated customer. | "Invalid refresh token." |
| Session Expiry Validation | The authenticated session shall not have expired before processing the logout request. | "Session has expired. Please log in again." |
| Session Already Logged Out Validation | The session shall not have already been terminated. | "Session is no longer active." |
| Token Revocation Validation | All authentication tokens associated with the current session shall be revoked after successful logout. | Logout operation shall fail securely and be logged. |
| JWT Signature Validation | For JWT-based authentication, the JWT signature shall be successfully verified before logout processing. | "Invalid authentication token." |
| CSRF Validation | For session-based authentication, the CSRF token shall be validated before processing the logout request. | "Invalid security token." |
| HTTPS Validation | Logout requests shall be accepted only over secure HTTPS connections. | Request shall be rejected. |
| Request Integrity Validation | The logout request shall be validated to ensure it has not been tampered with or replayed. | Invalid request shall be rejected. |
| Input Sanitization Validation | All request parameters associated with the logout operation shall be validated and sanitized before processing. | Invalid request shall be rejected. |
| Customer Account Validation | The customer account associated with the authenticated session shall exist and remain valid. | Logout request shall be rejected. |
| Account Status Validation | The customer account shall not be permanently deleted or disabled during logout processing. | Logout request shall be rejected. |
| Concurrent Logout Validation | Multiple simultaneous logout requests for the same session shall be handled safely, and only one request shall successfully terminate the session. | Duplicate logout request shall be ignored gracefully. |
| Session Invalidation Validation | The current authenticated session shall be completely invalidated before the logout process is considered successful. | Logout shall not be marked successful until session invalidation completes. |
| Refresh Token Revocation Validation | All refresh tokens associated with the current session shall be revoked immediately after successful logout. | Token revocation failure shall be logged and handled according to security policy. |
| Protected Resource Validation | After successful logout, the customer shall no longer be able to access protected resources without authenticating again. | Access shall be denied and authentication required. |
| Browser Cache Validation | Sensitive authenticated pages shall not be accessible through browser back navigation after logout where technically feasible using appropriate cache-control headers. | Protected content shall not be displayed. |
| Audit Validation | Every successful logout, failed logout attempt, forced logout, token revocation, session termination, and security-related activity shall be recorded in the audit log. | Audit logging failure shall be reported according to operational policy. |

#### 3.1.7.8 Main Flow

1. The customer successfully authenticates and accesses the platform.
2. The system establishes an authenticated session for the customer.
3. The customer navigates to an authenticated page of the platform.
4. The customer selects the **Logout** option.
5. The system receives the logout request.
6. The system validates that the customer is currently authenticated.
7. The system verifies the active authenticated session.
8. The system validates the access token or session identifier.
9. The system validates the refresh token, if applicable.
10. The system verifies that the authenticated session belongs to the requesting customer.
11. The system validates the integrity of the logout request.
12. The system invalidates the current authenticated session.
13. The system revokes the associated access token.
14. The system revokes the associated refresh token.
15. The system clears all server-side authentication data associated with the current session.
16. The system removes or invalidates client-side authentication credentials as applicable.
17. The system updates the customer's authentication state to **Logged Out**.
18. The system records the successful logout event in the audit log.
19. The system generates a successful logout response.
20. The system redirects the customer to the **Login** page or public landing page.
21. The customer attempts to access a protected resource.
22. The system detects that no valid authenticated session exists.
23. The system denies access to the protected resource.
24. The system prompts the customer to authenticate again before granting access.

#### 3.1.7.9 Alternate Flows

##### AF-1: Customer Not Authenticated

1. The customer initiates the logout request.
2. The system determines that no authenticated session exists.
3. The logout request is rejected.
4. The system redirects the customer to the **Login** page.
5. The security event is recorded in the audit log, if applicable.

---

##### AF-2: Invalid or Expired Authentication Token

1. The customer initiates the logout request.
2. The system detects that the access token or session identifier is invalid or has expired.
3. The logout request cannot be processed using the invalid session.
4. Any residual authentication artifacts associated with the request are safely discarded.
5. The customer is redirected to the **Login** page.

---

##### AF-3: Session Already Terminated

1. The customer submits a logout request.
2. The system determines that the session has already been terminated.
3. The system safely ignores the duplicate logout request.
4. The customer is redirected to the **Login** page.
5. No duplicate session termination occurs.

---

##### AF-4: Concurrent Logout Requests

1. Multiple logout requests are received simultaneously for the same authenticated session.
2. The system processes only the first valid logout request.
3. The authenticated session is invalidated.
4. Remaining duplicate requests are handled gracefully.
5. The customer is redirected to the **Login** page.

---

##### AF-5: Token Revocation Failure

1. The customer initiates logout.
2. The system successfully invalidates the session but encounters an error while revoking one or more authentication tokens.
3. The system records the failure in the audit log.
4. Appropriate recovery or retry procedures are initiated according to the platform's security policy.
5. The customer is informed that logout has been completed.

---

##### AF-6: Session Management Service Unavailable

1. The customer initiates logout.
2. The Session Management Service is unavailable.
3. The system cannot complete session termination.
4. The failure is recorded for operational monitoring.
5. The customer receives a generic error message requesting another attempt later.

---

##### AF-7: Authentication Service Unavailable

1. The customer initiates logout.
2. The Authentication Service becomes unavailable during processing.
3. The logout transaction cannot be completed.
4. The failure is recorded in the audit log.
5. The customer receives a generic system error message.

---

##### AF-8: Unexpected System Error

1. The customer initiates logout.
2. An unexpected system error occurs during processing.
3. The system safely terminates the transaction without exposing sensitive authentication information.
4. The error is recorded in the audit log.
5. The customer receives a generic error response without revealing internal implementation details.

#### 3.1.7.10 Postconditions

##### Successful Logout

After a successful logout:

1. The current authenticated session shall be permanently invalidated.
2. The associated access token shall be revoked and rendered unusable.
3. The associated refresh token, if applicable, shall be revoked.
4. All authentication credentials associated with the current session shall become invalid.
5. The customer's authentication state shall be updated to **Logged Out**.
6. The customer shall no longer be able to access protected resources without authenticating again.
7. The customer shall be redirected to the **Login** page or the platform's public landing page.
8. The logout event shall be recorded in the audit log.
9. Browser and server-side authentication artifacts shall be cleared or invalidated according to the platform's security policy.
10. Any subsequent request to protected resources shall require successful authentication.

##### Failed Logout

If the logout process fails:

1. The authenticated session shall remain in its current state unless explicitly invalidated.
2. Authentication credentials shall not be partially revoked.
3. The failure shall be recorded in the audit log.
4. The customer shall receive an appropriate generic error response.
5. Sensitive authentication information shall not be disclosed.
6. The system shall maintain authentication consistency and data integrity.
7. Appropriate recovery procedures shall be initiated according to the platform's security policy.

---

#### 3.1.7.11 Success Response

Upon successful logout, the system shall:

- Successfully terminate the customer's authenticated session.
- Revoke the associated access token.
- Revoke the associated refresh token, where applicable.
- Remove or invalidate all authentication artifacts associated with the current session.
- Update the customer's authentication status to **Logged Out**.
- Deny access to all protected platform resources until the customer authenticates again.
- Redirect the customer to the **Login** page or public landing page.
- Display a confirmation message indicating that logout has been completed successfully.
- Record the logout event, token revocation, and session termination in the audit log.
- Ensure that no authentication tokens, session identifiers, or sensitive security information are exposed in the response.

---

#### 3.1.7.12 Failure Response

The system shall reject or safely terminate the logout request and return an appropriate response under the following conditions:

- No authenticated session exists.
- The authentication token or session identifier is invalid.
- The authentication token has expired.
- The session has already been terminated.
- The authentication credentials belong to another customer.
- The Session Management Service is unavailable.
- The Authentication Service is unavailable.
- Token revocation fails during logout processing.
- The logout request is received over an insecure communication channel.
- Invalid, malformed, or potentially malicious requests are detected.
- An unexpected internal system error occurs while processing the logout request.

For every failed logout request, the system shall:

- Preserve authentication consistency.
- Prevent unauthorized session termination.
- Record the failure and associated security event in the audit log.
- Avoid exposing authentication details, session identifiers, tokens, or internal implementation details.
- Return a generic and secure error response consistent with the platform's authentication and security policies.

---

#### 3.1.7.13 Non-Functional Considerations

- All logout requests shall be processed only over secure HTTPS connections.
- The system shall complete at least **95% of successful logout requests within 2 seconds** under normal operating conditions.
- Session termination shall be immediate after successful logout.
- Access tokens and refresh tokens shall be securely revoked upon logout.
- The system shall prevent reuse of revoked authentication credentials.
- The Logout functionality shall support concurrent logout requests without significant performance degradation.
- Authentication state changes shall be consistent across distributed services.
- Every successful logout, failed logout attempt, forced logout, token revocation event, and session termination event shall be recorded in the audit log.
- Failure responses shall not expose authentication credentials, session identifiers, internal security mechanisms, or implementation details.
- The Logout functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, security, privacy, and applicable data protection policies.
- The system shall remain resilient to temporary failures of authentication or session management services using appropriate recovery mechanisms.
- Browser cache and client-side storage shall be handled according to the platform's security policy to reduce the risk of accessing protected content after logout.

---

#### 3.1.7.14 Acceptance Criteria

| ID | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|---------------------|
| **AC-LO-001** | Given an authenticated customer, when the customer selects **Logout**, then the system shall successfully terminate the current authenticated session. |
| **AC-LO-002** | Given a successful logout request, when logout processing completes, then the associated access token and refresh token shall be revoked. |
| **AC-LO-003** | Given a successful logout, when the customer attempts to access a protected resource, then the system shall deny access and require authentication. |
| **AC-LO-004** | Given a successful logout, when the customer attempts to reuse the previous authentication token, then the system shall reject the request. |
| **AC-LO-005** | Given an invalid, expired, or already terminated session, when a logout request is submitted, then the system shall safely reject or gracefully handle the request. |
| **AC-LO-006** | Given multiple simultaneous logout requests for the same session, when processing occurs, then only one request shall terminate the session while duplicate requests are handled safely. |
| **AC-LO-007** | Every successful logout, failed logout attempt, session termination, token revocation, and security-related activity shall be recorded in the audit log. |
| **AC-LO-008** | The Logout functionality shall prevent access to protected resources after logout until the customer successfully authenticates again. |
| **AC-LO-009** | The Logout functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, security, privacy, and applicable data protection requirements. |
| **AC-LO-010** | The Logout functionality shall securely invalidate authentication credentials without exposing sensitive security information to the customer. |

### 3.1.8 Refresh Access Token

#### 3.1.8.1 Description

The **Refresh Access Token** functionality enables an authenticated customer to obtain a new Access Token using a valid Refresh Token without requiring the customer to log in again.

The functionality shall improve both security and user experience by allowing Access Tokens to remain short-lived while maintaining authenticated user sessions through securely managed Refresh Tokens.

When an Access Token expires, the customer application shall submit the associated Refresh Token to the Authentication Service. The system shall validate the Refresh Token, verify its integrity, authenticity, expiration, revocation status, and ownership before issuing a new Access Token.

Upon successful validation, the system shall generate a new Access Token and, where configured by the platform's security policy, rotate the Refresh Token by issuing a new Refresh Token and revoking the previous one.

The Refresh Access Token functionality shall reject expired, revoked, invalid, tampered, reused, or compromised Refresh Tokens. If token validation fails, the customer shall be required to authenticate again through the Login process.

The system shall securely manage token lifecycle operations, prevent replay attacks, support token rotation, maintain complete audit records, and comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, security, privacy, and applicable data protection requirements.

#### 3.1.8.2 Actors

##### Primary Actor

- Customer

##### Supporting Actors

- Authentication Service
- Authorization Service
- JWT Token Service
- Session Management Service
- Audit Logging Service

#### 3.1.8.3 Preconditions

The following conditions shall be satisfied before a customer can request a new Access Token:

1. The customer shall have successfully authenticated using the Login functionality.
2. The customer shall possess a valid Refresh Token issued by the platform.
3. The Refresh Token shall not be expired.
4. The Refresh Token shall not be revoked.
5. The Refresh Token shall not have been previously invalidated or reused.
6. The Refresh Token shall belong to the authenticated customer.
7. The associated customer account shall exist in the system.
8. The customer account shall be active and eligible for authentication.
9. The Authentication Service shall be operational.
10. The Authorization Service shall be available.
11. The JWT Token Service shall be operational.
12. The Session Management Service shall be available.
13. The platform shall be accessible over a secure HTTPS connection.
14. The token refresh request shall comply with the platform's authentication and security policies.
15. The refresh request shall originate from a trusted and authenticated client application.

#### 3.1.8.4 Trigger

The **Refresh Access Token** process is initiated when a customer's **Access Token** expires or is about to expire, and the client application submits a valid **Refresh Token** to the Authentication Service to obtain a new Access Token without requiring the customer to log in again.

---

#### 3.1.8.5 Input Fields

| Field Name | Data Type | Mandatory | Description | Validation |
|------------|-----------|-----------|-------------|------------|
| Refresh Token | String | Yes | Secure token issued during successful authentication, used to obtain a new Access Token. | Shall be valid, unexpired, unrevoked, untampered, and associated with the authenticated customer. |
| Device Identifier | String | No | Unique identifier of the customer device initiating the token refresh request. | If provided, shall match the registered authenticated device. |
| Client Identifier | String | No | Identifier of the client application requesting token refresh. | Shall be a registered and trusted client application. |

**Notes:**

- The customer is not required to manually provide these values; they are typically transmitted automatically by the authenticated client application.
- The Refresh Token shall never be exposed in the user interface.
- The Refresh Token shall be securely stored by the client application.
- The Refresh Token shall be transmitted only over secure HTTPS connections.
- The Refresh Token shall remain confidential throughout its lifecycle.
- Depending on the platform's security policy, the Refresh Token may be rotated after every successful refresh operation.
- A revoked, expired, reused, or compromised Refresh Token shall never be accepted.

#### 3.1.8.6 Business Rules

1. Only authenticated customers possessing a valid Refresh Token shall be permitted to request a new Access Token.
2. The Refresh Token shall be cryptographically signed and verifiable by the Authentication Service.
3. The Refresh Token shall belong to the customer requesting the token refresh.
4. The Refresh Token shall have a configurable expiration period as defined by the platform's security policy.
5. Expired Refresh Tokens shall be rejected.
6. Revoked Refresh Tokens shall be rejected.
7. Previously invalidated or reused Refresh Tokens shall be rejected.
8. Tampered or malformed Refresh Tokens shall be rejected.
9. The system shall validate the integrity and authenticity of every Refresh Token before issuing a new Access Token.
10. The system shall verify that the associated customer account exists and is active.
11. Suspended, blocked, locked, or permanently deleted customer accounts shall not be permitted to refresh authentication tokens.
12. Upon successful validation, the system shall generate a new Access Token.
13. The newly generated Access Token shall contain updated claims and a new expiration time.
14. The Access Token shall be digitally signed before being returned to the client application.
15. Depending on the platform's security policy, the system shall rotate the Refresh Token by issuing a new Refresh Token and revoking the previously issued Refresh Token.
16. If Refresh Token rotation is enabled, only the latest issued Refresh Token shall remain valid.
17. The system shall invalidate compromised Refresh Tokens immediately upon detection.
18. Multiple simultaneous refresh requests using the same Refresh Token shall be handled according to the platform's token rotation policy to prevent replay attacks.
19. Token refresh requests shall be processed only over secure HTTPS connections.
20. Refresh Tokens shall never be logged, exposed, or transmitted in plain text.
21. Access Tokens and Refresh Tokens shall be generated using cryptographically secure random values.
22. Token refresh operations shall be protected against replay attacks, token theft, token substitution, brute-force attacks, and other authentication-related threats.
23. The system shall enforce configurable rate limiting on token refresh requests to mitigate abuse.
24. Every successful token refresh, failed validation, token rotation, token revocation, replay attack detection, and security-related event shall be recorded in the audit log.
25. Failure responses shall not disclose sensitive authentication information, internal validation logic, cryptographic details, or account status.
26. The Refresh Access Token functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, security, privacy, and applicable data protection policies.

#### 3.1.8.7 Validations

| Validation | Rule | Failure Response |
|------------|------|------------------|
| Refresh Token Required | A Refresh Token shall be provided with every token refresh request. | "Refresh Token is required." |
| Refresh Token Format Validation | The Refresh Token shall conform to the platform's supported token format. | "Invalid Refresh Token." |
| Refresh Token Signature Validation | The digital signature of the Refresh Token shall be successfully verified before processing. | "Invalid Refresh Token." |
| Refresh Token Integrity Validation | The Refresh Token shall not be modified, corrupted, or tampered with. | "Invalid Refresh Token." |
| Refresh Token Expiry Validation | The Refresh Token shall not have expired. | "Refresh Token has expired. Please log in again." |
| Refresh Token Revocation Validation | The Refresh Token shall not have been revoked by the system. | "Refresh Token is no longer valid." |
