23. Rollback mechanisms shall restore the previous state if the password update cannot be completed successfully.
24. Password integrity shall be maintained throughout the entire operation.

##### Maintainability

25. Password policy rules shall be configurable without requiring application code changes wherever feasible.
26. Security policies shall support centralized configuration and administration.
27. Authentication, authorization, session management, and password management components should remain modular for easier maintenance.
28. Audit logs shall support operational troubleshooting and security investigations.

##### Auditability

29. Every successful password change shall generate an audit record.
30. Failed password change attempts shall be logged according to the platform's security policy.
31. Audit records shall include the customer identifier, request identifier, timestamp, event type, and outcome where applicable.
32. Audit logs shall be protected from unauthorized modification and deletion.
33. Audit records shall comply with the organization's retention and compliance policies.

##### Compliance

34. The Change Password functionality shall comply with applicable data protection, privacy, cybersecurity, and regulatory requirements.
35. Password handling shall comply with the organization's information security standards.
36. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be processed in accordance with applicable privacy regulations.
37. Password management practices shall align with recognized secure authentication guidelines adopted by the organization.

---

#### 3.1.12.14 Acceptance Criteria

| AC ID | Acceptance Criteria |
|-------|---------------------|
| AC-CP-001 | Only authenticated customers shall be able to change their own password. |
| AC-CP-002 | The current password shall be verified before allowing a password change. |
| AC-CP-003 | The new password shall comply with the configured password policy. |
| AC-CP-004 | The new password and confirmation password shall exactly match. |
| AC-CP-005 | The new password shall be different from the current password. |
| AC-CP-006 | Password reuse shall be restricted according to the configured password history policy. |
| AC-CP-007 | Passwords shall never be stored, logged, or transmitted in plain text. |
| AC-CP-008 | The new password shall be securely hashed before being stored. |
| AC-CP-009 | The password update shall complete successfully as a single atomic transaction. |
| AC-CP-010 | Transaction rollback shall occur automatically if any validation or update fails. |
| AC-CP-011 | Existing sessions and authentication tokens shall be handled according to the platform's security policy after a successful password change. |
| AC-CP-012 | Authentication using the previous password shall fail after a successful password update. |
| AC-CP-013 | Authentication using the newly changed password shall succeed after the password change is completed. |
| AC-CP-014 | Every successful and failed password change event shall be logged according to the platform's audit policy. |
| AC-CP-015 | Failure responses shall not expose passwords, password hashes, authentication secrets, or internal security implementation details. |
| AC-CP-016 | All password change requests shall be processed only over secure HTTPS connections. |
| AC-CP-017 | Unauthorized password change attempts shall be rejected. |
| AC-CP-018 | The Change Password functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, security, privacy, audit, and applicable regulatory requirements. |

### 3.1.13 Account Deactivation

#### 3.1.13.1 Description

The **Account Deactivation** functionality allows an authenticated customer to temporarily deactivate their Food Delivery Platform account without permanently deleting customer data. Once deactivated, the customer shall no longer be able to access platform services until the account is reactivated through the supported account recovery or reactivation process.

Account deactivation is intended for customers who wish to temporarily stop using the platform while preserving their personal information, order history, saved addresses, wallet balance, loyalty points, preferences, payment methods, and other account-related information for future use.

The system shall securely verify the customer's identity before processing the deactivation request. During the deactivation process, the system shall terminate or invalidate active authenticated sessions, revoke authentication tokens according to the platform's security policy, record the deactivation event in the audit log, and update the customer's account status to **Deactivated**.

A deactivated account shall not be permitted to authenticate, place orders, manage profile information, access customer resources, perform wallet operations, redeem loyalty points, or use any authenticated platform functionality until the account has been successfully reactivated.

The deactivation process shall be performed as a secure, auditable, and atomic transaction while ensuring compliance with the platform's authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, and applicable regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.13.2 Actors

- Customer
- Authentication Service
- Authorization Service
- Customer Profile Service
- Account Management Service
- Session Management Service
- Token Management Service
- Customer Wallet Service
- Loyalty Management Service
- Notification Service
- Audit Logging Service
- Security Monitoring Service

#### 3.1.13.3 Preconditions

1. The customer shall be successfully authenticated before initiating the account deactivation process.
2. The customer shall have an active authenticated session.
3. The customer account shall exist in the platform.
4. The customer account shall be in **Active** status.
5. The authenticated customer shall be authorized to deactivate only their own account.
6. The Authentication Service shall be available.
7. The Authorization Service shall be available.
8. The Account Management Service shall be available.
9. The Customer Profile Service shall be available.
10. The Session Management Service shall be available.
11. The Token Management Service shall be available.
12. The Notification Service shall be available for sending deactivation confirmation notifications where applicable.
13. The Audit Logging Service shall be available for recording security and account lifecycle events.
14. The Security Monitoring Service shall be available for monitoring account-related security activities where applicable.
15. The customer's identity shall be verified according to the platform's authentication policy before processing the deactivation request.
16. The deactivation request shall be initiated over a secure HTTPS connection.
17. The customer shall explicitly confirm the account deactivation request before the operation is executed.
18. The system shall verify that the account is not already in the **Deactivated** state.
19. The system shall validate that no authorization or authentication policy prevents account deactivation.
20. The platform shall be capable of securely invalidating active sessions and authentication tokens according to the configured security policy.
21. All required backend services participating in the account deactivation workflow shall be operational.
22. The system shall be capable of updating the customer's account status as a single atomic transaction.
23. The platform shall comply with applicable security, privacy, audit, and regulatory requirements before processing the account deactivation request.

#### 3.1.13.4 Trigger

The **Account Deactivation** process is initiated when an authenticated customer navigates to the **Account Settings**, **Privacy & Security**, or **Account Management** section of the application, selects the **Deactivate Account** option, reviews the deactivation information, confirms the action, completes any required identity verification, and submits the account deactivation request.

---

#### 3.1.13.5 Input Fields

| Field Name | Data Type | Mandatory | Description | Validation |
|------------|-----------|-----------|-------------|------------|
| Customer ID | UUID | System Generated | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. | Shall belong to the authenticated customer. |
| Access Token | String | Yes | Authentication token used to authorize the account deactivation request. | Shall be valid, active, and associated with the authenticated customer. |
| Session ID | String | Yes | Active authenticated session identifier. | Shall be valid and not expired. |
| Current Password | String | Conditional | Password entered to verify the customer's identity before account deactivation, if required by the platform's security policy. | Shall match the currently stored password. |
| Verification Code (OTP) | String | Conditional | One-Time Password sent to the registered email address or mobile number when additional identity verification is required. | Shall be valid, unexpired, and match the generated verification code. |
| Deactivation Reason | Enum / String | Optional | Customer-selected or customer-entered reason for temporarily deactivating the account. | Shall be one of the supported predefined reasons or within the permitted character limit for custom input. |
| Additional Comments | String | Optional | Additional feedback provided by the customer regarding the deactivation decision. | Shall not exceed the configured maximum character limit. |
| Deactivation Confirmation | Boolean | Yes | Explicit confirmation indicating that the customer intends to deactivate the account. | Shall be **True** before processing the request. |

**Notes:**

- The **Customer ID** shall be derived from the authenticated session and shall not be editable.
- The **Access Token** and **Session ID** shall be validated before processing the deactivation request.
- The **Current Password** may be mandatory depending on the platform's authentication and security policy.
- The **Verification Code (OTP)** shall be required only when additional identity verification is configured or risk-based authentication is triggered.
- The **Deactivation Reason** shall be stored for reporting and analytics purposes where applicable and shall not affect the customer's eligibility for future account reactivation.
- The **Additional Comments** field is optional and shall not contain executable scripts or malicious content.
- The customer shall explicitly acknowledge the consequences of account deactivation before the request is processed.
- Upon successful account deactivation, all active sessions, refresh tokens, remembered devices, and authentication credentials shall be invalidated according to the platform's security policy.
- Personally Identifiable Information (PII), authentication credentials, and security-related information shall never be exposed in request or response payloads beyond what is required for secure processing.

#### 3.1.13.6 Business Rules

1. Only authenticated customers shall be permitted to deactivate their own customer account.
2. The system shall allow account deactivation only for the authenticated customer associated with the current authenticated session.
3. Customers shall not be permitted to deactivate another customer's account.
4. The system shall validate customer authentication and authorization before processing the account deactivation request.
5. The customer account shall exist before the deactivation process begins.
6. Only accounts in the **Active** state shall be eligible for deactivation.
7. Accounts that are already in the **Deactivated** state shall not be processed again for deactivation.
8. The customer shall explicitly confirm the account deactivation request before the operation is executed.
9. Identity verification using the current password, OTP, or other approved authentication mechanisms shall be performed where required by the platform's security policy.
10. The deactivation request shall be accepted only through secure HTTPS connections.
11. All submitted input shall be validated and sanitized before processing.
12. The account status shall be updated from **Active** to **Deactivated** as a single atomic transaction.
13. If any validation or processing step fails, the entire deactivation transaction shall be rolled back.
14. Upon successful deactivation, all active authenticated sessions shall be invalidated according to the platform's security policy.
15. All active access tokens, refresh tokens, remembered devices, and other authentication credentials shall be revoked or invalidated according to the configured security policy.
16. A deactivated customer account shall not be permitted to authenticate until the account has been successfully reactivated.
17. A deactivated customer shall not be permitted to place food orders.
18. A deactivated customer shall not be permitted to modify profile information.
19. A deactivated customer shall not be permitted to manage addresses or saved places.
20. A deactivated customer shall not be permitted to manage saved payment methods.
21. A deactivated customer shall not be permitted to access customer wallet operations until the account is reactivated.
22. Wallet balances, loyalty points, referral benefits, saved payment methods, order history, invoices, and customer profile information shall be preserved unless modified by an independent business process.
23. Pending, completed, cancelled, refunded, and historical orders shall remain available for business record retention in accordance with organizational policies.
24. The customer may continue to receive mandatory legal, financial, security, or regulatory communications after account deactivation where required by applicable laws or organizational policies.
25. Optional marketing communications shall follow the customer's communication preferences and applicable privacy regulations.
26. The deactivation reason and optional customer feedback shall be stored where applicable for reporting, analytics, and service improvement purposes.
27. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall remain protected throughout the deactivation process.
28. Sensitive authentication credentials shall never be stored, logged, or exposed in plain text.
29. Every successful account deactivation, failed authorization attempt, failed authentication attempt, validation failure, and security-related event shall be recorded in the audit log where required.
30. Failure responses shall not expose sensitive authentication information, internal implementation details, security configurations, database information, or infrastructure details.
31. Concurrent account deactivation requests shall be handled according to the platform's concurrency control policy.
32. The Account Deactivation functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, security, privacy, audit, data retention, and applicable regulatory requirements.
33. Successful account deactivation shall update the account status, deactivation timestamp, and other lifecycle metadata where maintained by the platform.
34. Account reactivation shall be handled exclusively through the supported Account Reactivation process and shall not occur automatically.

#### 3.1.13.7 Validations

| Validation | Rule | Failure Response |
|------------|------|------------------|
| Authentication Validation | The customer shall be successfully authenticated before initiating account deactivation. | "Authentication required." |
| Access Token Validation | The Access Token shall be valid, active, and digitally verifiable. | "Invalid or expired authentication token." |
| Session Validation | An active authenticated session shall exist. | "Session has expired. Please log in again." |
| Session Expiry Validation | The authenticated session shall not have expired before processing the request. | "Session has expired." |
| Session Revocation Validation | The authenticated session shall not have been revoked or terminated. | "Session is no longer valid." |
| Customer Identification Validation | The authenticated customer shall be uniquely identified from the active session or Access Token. | "Unable to identify customer." |
| Customer Account Validation | The associated customer account shall exist. | "Customer account not found." |
| Customer Account Status Validation | The customer account shall be in the **Active** state. | "Account cannot be deactivated in its current status." |
| Already Deactivated Validation | The account shall not already be in the **Deactivated** state. | "Account is already deactivated." |
| Authorization Validation | The authenticated customer shall be authorized to deactivate the account. | "Access denied." |
| Ownership Validation | Customers shall be permitted to deactivate only their own account. | "Unauthorized account deactivation attempt." |
| Current Password Validation | When required by the platform's security policy, the submitted Current Password shall match the securely stored password. | "Current password is incorrect." |
| OTP Validation | When OTP verification is required, the submitted verification code shall match the generated OTP. | "Invalid verification code." |
| OTP Expiry Validation | The submitted OTP shall not be expired. | "Verification code has expired." |
| Deactivation Confirmation Validation | The customer shall explicitly confirm the account deactivation request. | "Account deactivation confirmation is required." |
| Deactivation Reason Validation | If provided, the deactivation reason shall conform to the supported values or configured character limits. | "Invalid deactivation reason." |
| Additional Comments Validation | If provided, additional comments shall not exceed the configured maximum length and shall not contain prohibited content. | "Invalid comments provided." |
| Input Sanitization Validation | All submitted values shall be sanitized before processing. | "Invalid request." |
| HTTPS Validation | Account deactivation requests shall be accepted only over secure HTTPS connections. | Request shall be rejected. |
| Request Integrity Validation | The deactivation request shall not be modified or tampered with during transmission. | "Request validation failed." |
| Transaction Validation | The account deactivation process shall complete successfully as a single atomic transaction. | "Unable to deactivate account." |
| Session Invalidation Validation | Active authenticated sessions shall be successfully invalidated according to the platform's security policy after successful deactivation. | "Unable to complete account deactivation." |
| Token Revocation Validation | Access tokens, refresh tokens, and remembered device tokens shall be revoked or invalidated according to the configured security policy. | "Unable to complete account deactivation." |
| Concurrent Request Validation | Concurrent account deactivation requests shall be processed according to the configured concurrency policy. | "Another account operation is currently being processed." |
| Audit Validation | Every successful account deactivation, failed authentication, failed authorization, validation failure, and security-related event shall be recorded in the audit log where applicable. | Audit logging failure shall be handled according to operational policy. |

#### 3.1.13.8 Main Flow

1. The customer navigates to the **Account Settings**, **Privacy & Security**, or **Account Management** section of the application.
2. The customer selects the **Deactivate Account** option.
3. The system displays information explaining the consequences of account deactivation.
4. The customer reviews the deactivation information.
5. The customer optionally selects a deactivation reason.
6. The customer optionally provides additional comments.
7. The customer explicitly confirms the account deactivation request.
8. If required by the platform's security policy, the customer enters the current password.
9. If additional identity verification is required, the customer completes OTP or other approved verification.
10. The customer submits the account deactivation request.
11. The client application sends the request along with the authenticated Access Token and active Session ID.
12. The system receives the account deactivation request.
13. The Authentication Service validates the Access Token.
14. The system verifies the digital integrity of the authentication token.
15. The Session Management Service validates the authenticated session.
16. The system verifies that the session has not expired.
17. The system verifies that the session has not been revoked or terminated.
18. The Authorization Service verifies that the authenticated customer is authorized to deactivate the account.
19. The system identifies the authenticated customer from the validated session.
20. The system retrieves the authenticated Customer ID.
21. The system verifies that the customer account exists.
22. The system verifies that the customer account is currently in the **Active** state.
23. The system verifies that the account is not already deactivated.
24. The system validates all mandatory input fields.
25. The system validates the customer's explicit deactivation confirmation.
26. If required, the system securely verifies the submitted current password.
27. If required, the system validates the submitted OTP or other identity verification mechanism.
28. The system validates and sanitizes all submitted input.
29. The system begins a database transaction.
30. The system stores the deactivation reason and optional customer feedback where applicable.
31. The system updates the customer account status from **Active** to **Deactivated**.
32. The system records the account deactivation timestamp.
33. The system updates account lifecycle metadata where maintained by the platform.
34. The Token Management Service revokes or invalidates active access tokens, refresh tokens, remembered devices, and other authentication credentials according to the platform's security policy.
35. The Session Management Service invalidates all active authenticated sessions according to the platform's security policy.
36. The system commits the transaction after successful completion of all deactivation operations.
37. The Notification Service sends an account deactivation confirmation notification through the configured communication channels where applicable.
38. The Audit Logging Service records the successful account deactivation event.
39. The Security Monitoring Service records the security event where applicable.
40. The system returns a successful account deactivation response.
41. The customer is signed out of the application if the current session remains active until response completion.
42. Any subsequent authentication attempt using the deactivated account is denied until the account is successfully reactivated.
43. The account deactivation process completes successfully.

#### 3.1.13.9 Alternate Flows

**AF-1: Invalid Access Token**

1. The customer submits the account deactivation request.
2. The Authentication Service validates the Access Token.
3. The Access Token is determined to be invalid, expired, malformed, or tampered with.
4. The request is rejected immediately.
5. The system returns an authentication failure response.
6. The account remains in the **Active** state.

---

**AF-2: Session Expired or Revoked**

1. The system validates the authenticated session.
2. The session is found to be expired, revoked, or terminated.
3. The account deactivation request is rejected.
4. The customer is required to authenticate again.
5. No account information is modified.

---

**AF-3: Customer Account Not Found**

1. Authentication succeeds successfully.
2. The system attempts to retrieve the customer account.
3. The associated customer account cannot be found.
4. The deactivation process is terminated.
5. The system returns an appropriate error response.
6. No account status is modified.

---

**AF-4: Account Already Deactivated**

1. The system retrieves the customer account.
2. The account status is identified as **Deactivated**.
3. The request is rejected.
4. The system informs the customer that the account is already deactivated.
5. No additional processing is performed.

---

**AF-5: Current Password Verification Failed**

1. The platform requires password verification.
2. The customer submits the Current Password.
3. The submitted password does not match the securely stored password.
4. The account deactivation request is rejected.
5. The failed authentication attempt is recorded according to the platform's security policy.
6. The account remains active.

---

**AF-6: OTP Verification Failed**

1. The platform requires OTP verification.
2. The customer submits the verification code.
3. The submitted OTP is invalid or expired.
4. The account deactivation request is rejected.
5. The customer receives an appropriate verification failure response.
6. No account status changes are performed.

---

**AF-7: Deactivation Confirmation Not Provided**

1. The customer initiates account deactivation.
2. The system verifies the confirmation flag.
3. The required confirmation is missing or not accepted.
4. The request is rejected.
5. The customer is prompted to explicitly confirm the deactivation request.
6. The account remains active.

---

**AF-8: Authorization Failure**

1. The Authentication Service successfully authenticates the customer.
2. The Authorization Service determines that the customer is not authorized to perform the requested operation.
3. The request is rejected.
4. The security event is recorded where applicable.
5. No account information is modified.

---

**AF-9: Database Transaction Failure**

1. The system begins the account deactivation transaction.
2. An unexpected database or infrastructure failure occurs.
3. The transaction is rolled back completely.
4. The account status remains unchanged.
5. The customer receives an internal error response.
6. The operational failure is recorded for investigation.

---

**AF-10: Session or Token Invalidation Failure**

1. The account status is successfully updated.
2. The system encounters an error while invalidating active sessions or authentication tokens.
3. The platform handles the failure according to the configured security policy.
4. Additional recovery or retry mechanisms may be executed.
5. The security event is recorded for administrative review.

---

**AF-11: Concurrent Account Deactivation Request**

1. Multiple account deactivation requests are received simultaneously.
2. The system applies the configured concurrency control policy.
3. Conflicting requests are detected.
4. Only one request is processed successfully.
5. Remaining conflicting requests are rejected or retried according to the platform's concurrency policy.
6. Account consistency is maintained throughout the operation.

---

**AF-12: Notification Delivery Failure**

1. The account is successfully deactivated.
2. The Notification Service attempts to deliver the deactivation confirmation.
3. Notification delivery fails due to a temporary service or communication error.
4. The account remains successfully deactivated.
5. The notification failure is logged for retry or operational follow-up.
6. The customer may receive the notification once the notification service becomes available.

#### 3.1.13.10 Postconditions

1. The customer account status shall be updated from **Active** to **Deactivated** upon successful completion of the account deactivation process.
2. The account deactivation timestamp shall be recorded where maintained by the platform.
3. Account lifecycle metadata shall be updated to reflect the successful deactivation event.
4. The customer shall no longer be permitted to authenticate using the deactivated account.
5. All active authenticated sessions shall be invalidated according to the platform's security policy.
6. Active access tokens, refresh tokens, remembered devices, and other authentication credentials shall be revoked or invalidated according to the configured security policy.
7. The customer shall be signed out from all authenticated devices where applicable.
8. The customer shall not be permitted to place new food orders while the account remains deactivated.
9. The customer shall not be permitted to modify profile information while the account remains deactivated.
10. The customer shall not be permitted to manage addresses, saved places, payment methods, notification preferences, or other authenticated account settings while the account remains deactivated.
11. Wallet balances shall remain preserved unless modified through an independent financial process.
12. Loyalty points, referral benefits, and reward information shall remain preserved according to the platform's business policies.
13. Order history, invoices, transaction history, ratings, reviews, and other historical customer records shall remain retained according to organizational retention policies.
14. Saved payment methods and customer preferences shall remain stored unless removed through an independent business process.
15. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall remain securely protected after account deactivation.
16. The customer shall continue to receive mandatory legal, financial, security, or regulatory communications where required.
17. Optional communications shall follow the customer's configured communication preferences and applicable privacy regulations.
18. The successful account deactivation event shall be recorded in the audit log.
19. Security monitoring systems shall receive applicable account lifecycle events where integrated.
20. The deactivation reason and optional customer feedback shall be retained where applicable for reporting and analytics purposes.
21. No partial updates shall remain if the deactivation transaction fails; the account shall remain in its previous consistent state.
22. Sensitive authentication information, access tokens, password hashes, cryptographic material, and internal security information shall never be exposed through responses, logs, or audit records.
23. The customer shall receive an appropriate success or failure response based on the outcome of the request.
24. The account shall remain in the **Deactivated** state until successfully restored through the supported **Account Reactivation** process.
25. The Account Deactivation process shall complete in compliance with the platform's authentication, authorization, security, privacy, audit, data retention, and applicable regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.13.11 Success Response

Upon successful completion of the Account Deactivation process, the system shall return a success response confirming that the customer's account has been temporarily deactivated without exposing sensitive authentication, security, or internal system information.

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Status | Indicates that the account deactivation request was completed successfully. |
| Status Code | Standard application/API success status code (for example, HTTP 200 OK). |
| Message | Confirmation that the account has been successfully deactivated. |
| Customer ID | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. |
| Account Status | Current account status after processing (Deactivated). |
| Deactivated At | Timestamp indicating when the account was successfully deactivated. |
| Session Status | Indicates that authenticated sessions have been invalidated according to the platform's security policy. |
| Token Status | Indicates that authentication tokens have been revoked or invalidated according to the configured security policy. |
| Reactivation Available | Indicates that the account may be restored through the supported Account Reactivation process. |
| Request ID | Unique identifier for tracking the account deactivation request. |
| Response Timestamp | Date and time when the response was generated. |

**Example Success Message**

```text
Your account has been successfully deactivated.
```

**Success Response Characteristics**

1. The response shall confirm that the account has been successfully deactivated.
2. The response shall indicate that the account status is **Deactivated**.
3. The response shall not include the customer's password.
4. The response shall not include password hashes, access tokens, refresh tokens, OTP values, or other authentication credentials.
5. The response shall not disclose internal authentication, authorization, or security implementation details.
6. The response shall not expose Personally Identifiable Information (PII) beyond the information required by the platform's response standards.
7. The response shall indicate that authenticated sessions and authentication credentials have been handled according to the platform's security policy.
8. The response shall indicate that the account may be restored through the supported Account Reactivation process where applicable.
9. The response shall include the request identifier for audit and operational troubleshooting purposes.
10. The response shall be transmitted only through secure HTTPS connections.
11. The response shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, security, privacy, audit, and API response standards.

#### 3.1.13.12 Failure Response

When the Account Deactivation request cannot be completed, the system shall return an appropriate failure response without exposing sensitive authentication information, internal security mechanisms, customer credentials, or infrastructure details.

| Failure Scenario | Response Message |
|------------------|------------------|
| Authentication Required | Authentication required. |
| Invalid Access Token | Invalid or expired authentication token. |
| Session Expired | Session has expired. Please log in again. |
| Session Revoked | Session is no longer valid. |
| Customer Account Not Found | Customer account not found. |
| Customer Account Already Deactivated | Account is already deactivated. |
| Customer Account Inactive | Account cannot be deactivated in its current status. |
| Unauthorized Account Deactivation Attempt | Access denied. |
| Current Password Incorrect | Current password is incorrect. |
| Invalid Verification Code | Invalid verification code. |
| Verification Code Expired | Verification code has expired. |
| Account Deactivation Confirmation Missing | Account deactivation confirmation is required. |
| Invalid Deactivation Reason | Invalid deactivation reason. |
| Invalid Additional Comments | Invalid comments provided. |
| Invalid Request Data | Invalid request. |
| Request Integrity Failure | Request validation failed. |
| Concurrent Account Operation | Another account operation is currently being processed. |
| Session Invalidation Failure | Unable to complete account deactivation. |
| Token Revocation Failure | Unable to complete account deactivation. |
| Database Transaction Failure | Unable to deactivate account. Please try again later. |
| Internal Server Error | An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later. |

**Failure Response Characteristics**

1. Every failure response shall contain an appropriate application or HTTP error status code.
2. Failure responses shall never disclose passwords, password hashes, OTP values, access tokens, refresh tokens, session identifiers, cryptographic material, or authentication secrets.
3. Failure responses shall never expose internal authentication, authorization, session management, or token management implementation details.
4. Failure responses shall never reveal database schema, infrastructure configuration, stack traces, server details, or other internal system information.
5. Validation failures shall clearly identify the input error without exposing sensitive security information.
6. Authentication and authorization failures shall return generic security messages to minimize information disclosure.
7. Failed deactivation operations shall ensure that no partial account status updates are committed.
8. The customer's account shall remain in its previous consistent state whenever the deactivation process fails before successful transaction completion.
9. All applicable failure events shall be recorded in the audit log according to the platform's security policy.
10. Failure responses shall be transmitted only through secure HTTPS connections.
11. Failure responses shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, security, privacy, audit, logging, and API response standards.
12. Operational failures shall be logged for monitoring, troubleshooting, and security analysis without exposing sensitive customer information in client-facing responses.

#### 3.1.13.13 Non-Functional Considerations

##### Performance

1. The Account Deactivation operation should complete within the platform's defined response time under normal operating conditions.
2. Authentication, authorization, identity verification, and account status update operations shall be optimized to provide a consistent user experience.
3. The system shall support concurrent account deactivation requests from multiple authenticated customers without performance degradation.
4. Database transactions shall minimize lock duration and contention while maintaining data consistency.
5. Session invalidation and token revocation shall be completed within acceptable operational time limits.

##### Availability

6. The Account Deactivation functionality shall be available according to the platform's defined service availability objectives.
7. Temporary service failures shall not result in inconsistent account status.
8. High availability mechanisms shall ensure continuity of account lifecycle management services.
9. Planned maintenance activities shall not compromise the integrity of account deactivation operations.

##### Scalability

10. The platform shall support growth in the number of customer accounts without degrading account deactivation performance.
11. Authentication, authorization, session management, notification, and account management services shall support horizontal scalability where applicable.
12. The solution shall efficiently process increasing volumes of account lifecycle operations.

##### Security

13. All account deactivation requests shall be transmitted exclusively over secure HTTPS connections.
14. Authentication and authorization shall be enforced before processing every account deactivation request.
15. Customer identity verification shall be performed according to the platform's security policy.
16. Active sessions, authentication tokens, refresh tokens, and remembered devices shall be invalidated according to the configured security policy following successful account deactivation.
17. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall remain protected throughout the account deactivation process.
18. The system shall protect against unauthorized account deactivation attempts, session hijacking, replay attacks, brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, and other applicable security threats.
19. Sensitive authentication credentials shall never be stored, logged, cached, or exposed in plain text.
20. Security events shall be monitored and logged in accordance with organizational security policies.

##### Reliability

21. The account deactivation process shall execute as a single atomic transaction.
22. System failures shall not result in partially deactivated customer accounts.
23. Rollback mechanisms shall restore the previous account state if the deactivation process cannot be completed successfully.
24. Data consistency shall be maintained across account management, authentication, session management, notification, and audit services.

##### Maintainability

25. Account lifecycle rules shall be configurable without requiring application code changes wherever feasible.
26. Authentication, authorization, notification, and session management components should remain modular for easier maintenance.
27. Security policies shall support centralized administration and configuration.
28. Audit records shall support operational troubleshooting and security investigations.

##### Auditability

29. Every successful account deactivation shall generate an audit record.
30. Failed account deactivation attempts shall be logged according to the platform's security policy.
31. Audit records shall include the customer identifier, request identifier, timestamp, event type, and operation outcome where applicable.
32. Audit logs shall be protected from unauthorized modification, deletion, or disclosure.
33. Audit records shall comply with the organization's retention, governance, and regulatory requirements.

##### Compliance

34. The Account Deactivation functionality shall comply with applicable data protection, privacy, cybersecurity, and regulatory requirements.
35. Customer information shall be processed in accordance with applicable privacy regulations.
36. Account lifecycle management shall comply with the organization's information security standards.
37. Data retention, audit logging, and customer communication practices shall comply with applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

---

#### 3.1.13.14 Acceptance Criteria

| AC ID | Acceptance Criteria |
|-------|---------------------|
| AC-AD-001 | Only authenticated customers shall be able to deactivate their own account. |
| AC-AD-002 | Only customer accounts in the **Active** state shall be eligible for account deactivation. |
| AC-AD-003 | The customer shall explicitly confirm the account deactivation request before processing. |
| AC-AD-004 | Identity verification shall be performed according to the platform's security policy before completing account deactivation. |
| AC-AD-005 | The account status shall be updated from **Active** to **Deactivated** upon successful completion. |
| AC-AD-006 | The account deactivation operation shall complete as a single atomic transaction. |
| AC-AD-007 | All active authenticated sessions and authentication credentials shall be invalidated according to the platform's security policy after successful account deactivation. |
| AC-AD-008 | The customer shall not be permitted to authenticate or access authenticated platform functionality while the account remains deactivated. |
| AC-AD-009 | Wallet balance, loyalty points, order history, customer profile information, saved addresses, and payment methods shall remain preserved unless modified by independent business processes. |
| AC-AD-010 | Transaction rollback shall occur automatically if any validation or processing step fails. |
| AC-AD-011 | Every successful and failed account deactivation event shall be recorded according to the platform's audit policy. |
| AC-AD-012 | Failure responses shall not expose authentication credentials, internal security implementation details, or sensitive customer information. |
| AC-AD-013 | All account deactivation requests shall be processed only through secure HTTPS connections. |
| AC-AD-014 | Unauthorized account deactivation attempts shall be rejected. |
| AC-AD-015 | The Account Deactivation functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, security, privacy, audit, data retention, and applicable regulatory requirements. |

### 3.1.14 Account Reactivation

#### 3.1.14.1 Description

The **Account Reactivation** functionality allows a customer whose account is currently in the **Deactivated** state to restore access to the Food Delivery Platform by completing the required authentication and identity verification process. Upon successful reactivation, the customer's account status shall be updated from **Deactivated** to **Active**, allowing the customer to resume using authenticated platform services.

The system shall securely verify the customer's identity before processing the reactivation request. Depending on the platform's security policy, identity verification may include password authentication, One-Time Password (OTP) verification, email verification, mobile verification, or other approved authentication mechanisms.

Successful account reactivation shall restore access to customer profile information, saved addresses, wallet balance, loyalty points, saved payment methods, order history, notification preferences, and other customer resources that were preserved during account deactivation.

The system shall record the account reactivation event in the audit log, update account lifecycle metadata, and generate appropriate security and operational notifications where applicable. If required by the platform's security policy, new authenticated sessions and authentication tokens shall be issued after successful account reactivation.

The account reactivation process shall be executed as a secure, auditable, and atomic transaction while ensuring compliance with the platform's authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, and applicable regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.14.2 Actors

- Customer
- Authentication Service
- Authorization Service
- Account Management Service
- Customer Profile Service
- Session Management Service
- Token Management Service
- Identity Verification Service
- OTP Service
- Email Service
- SMS Notification Service
- Customer Wallet Service
- Loyalty Management Service
- Notification Service
- Audit Logging Service
- Security Monitoring Service

#### 3.1.14.3 Preconditions

1. The customer account shall exist in the platform.
2. The customer account shall be in the **Deactivated** state.
3. The customer shall initiate the account reactivation request using a supported platform interface.
4. The Authentication Service shall be available.
5. The Authorization Service shall be available.
6. The Account Management Service shall be available.
7. The Customer Profile Service shall be available.
8. The Session Management Service shall be available.
9. The Token Management Service shall be available.
10. The Identity Verification Service shall be available.
11. The OTP Service shall be available when OTP verification is required.
12. The Email Service shall be available when email verification is required.
13. The SMS Notification Service shall be available when mobile verification is required.
14. The Notification Service shall be available for sending account reactivation notifications where applicable.
15. The Audit Logging Service shall be available for recording account lifecycle and security events.
16. The Security Monitoring Service shall be available for monitoring security-related account activities where applicable.
17. The customer's identity shall be successfully verified according to the platform's authentication and security policy before account reactivation is permitted.
18. The customer shall successfully complete all mandatory verification steps, including password authentication, OTP verification, email verification, mobile verification, or other approved identity verification mechanisms, as applicable.
19. The reactivation request shall be initiated over a secure HTTPS connection.
20. The system shall verify that the account is not already in the **Active** state.
21. The system shall validate that no security restriction, administrative suspension, fraud investigation, regulatory hold, legal restriction, or platform policy prevents account reactivation.
22. The platform shall be capable of restoring the customer's account status as a single atomic transaction.
23. All backend services participating in the account reactivation workflow shall be operational.
24. The platform shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, data protection, and regulatory requirements before processing the account reactivation request.

#### 3.1.14.4 Trigger

The **Account Reactivation** process is initiated when a customer with a **Deactivated** account accesses the login page, selects the **Reactivate Account** option, or navigates to the **Account Recovery** or **Account Reactivation** section of the application, completes the required identity verification, and submits the account reactivation request.

---

#### 3.1.14.5 Input Fields

| Field Name | Data Type | Mandatory | Description | Validation |
|------------|-----------|-----------|-------------|------------|
| Customer ID | UUID | Conditional | Unique identifier of the customer account being reactivated. | Shall belong to an existing deactivated customer account. |
| Registered Email Address | String | Conditional | Email address associated with the customer account. | Shall match the registered email address. |
| Registered Mobile Number | String | Conditional | Mobile number associated with the customer account. | Shall match the registered mobile number. |
| Username / Login ID | String | Conditional | Customer's registered login identifier. | Shall correspond to an existing deactivated customer account. |
| Password | String | Conditional | Customer password used for identity verification where required. | Shall match the securely stored password. |
| Verification Code (OTP) | String | Conditional | One-Time Password sent to the registered email address or mobile number. | Shall be valid, unexpired, and match the generated verification code. |
| Reactivation Confirmation | Boolean | Yes | Explicit confirmation indicating the customer's intention to reactivate the account. | Shall be **True** before processing the request. |
| Device Information | Object | Optional | Information about the device initiating the reactivation request. | Shall comply with platform security validation rules. |
| IP Address | String | System Generated | IP address from which the reactivation request originates. | Shall be captured for audit and security monitoring purposes. |
| User Agent | String | System Generated | Browser or application information of the requesting device. | Shall be captured for audit and security monitoring purposes. |

**Notes:**

- At least one unique customer identifier (Customer ID, Registered Email Address, Registered Mobile Number, or Username/Login ID) shall be provided to identify the account.
- The account identified by the submitted information shall exist and be in the **Deactivated** state.
- The **Password** field shall be required when password verification is mandated by the platform's security policy.
- The **Verification Code (OTP)** shall be required when OTP-based identity verification is enabled or triggered through risk-based authentication.
- The **Reactivation Confirmation** field shall explicitly indicate the customer's intent to restore the account.
- The **Device Information**, **IP Address**, and **User Agent** may be captured for fraud detection, security monitoring, and audit purposes.
- Passwords and verification codes shall never be stored, logged, cached, or transmitted in plain text beyond secure request processing.
- All submitted data shall be transmitted only through secure HTTPS connections.
- Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and authentication credentials shall be handled in accordance with the platform's privacy, security, and regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.14.6 Business Rules

1. Only customer accounts in the **Deactivated** state shall be eligible for account reactivation.
2. The system shall verify that the customer account exists before processing the reactivation request.
3. The system shall not permit reactivation of accounts that are already in the **Active** state.
4. The customer shall successfully complete identity verification before account reactivation is permitted.
5. Identity verification shall be performed using one or more approved authentication mechanisms as defined by the platform's security policy.
6. Password verification shall be required when mandated by the platform's authentication policy.
7. OTP verification shall be required when enabled by the platform or triggered through risk-based authentication.
8. Email verification or mobile verification may be required based on the configured security policy.
9. The customer shall explicitly confirm the account reactivation request before the operation is executed.
10. The reactivation request shall be accepted only through secure HTTPS connections.
11. All submitted input shall be validated and sanitized before processing.
12. The system shall verify that no administrative restriction prevents account reactivation.
13. Accounts under fraud investigation, legal hold, regulatory restriction, or administrative suspension shall not be eligible for reactivation until the restriction is removed.
14. The account status shall be updated from **Deactivated** to **Active** as a single atomic transaction.
15. If any validation or processing step fails, the entire reactivation transaction shall be rolled back.
16. Upon successful reactivation, the customer shall regain access to authenticated platform functionality.
17. Customer profile information shall remain unchanged during the account reactivation process.
18. Saved addresses, saved places, and customer preferences shall remain preserved.
19. Wallet balance shall remain unchanged unless modified through an independent financial process.
20. Loyalty points, referral rewards, and customer benefits shall remain preserved according to business policies.
21. Saved payment methods shall remain available unless previously removed through an independent business process.
22. Order history, invoices, transaction history, ratings, reviews, and other historical records shall remain available according to organizational retention policies.
23. If required by the platform's security policy, new authenticated sessions and authentication tokens shall be generated after successful account reactivation.
24. Previously invalidated sessions, access tokens, refresh tokens, and remembered devices shall not be automatically restored.
25. The customer shall authenticate using the normal login process after successful account reactivation unless automatically signed in by the platform.
26. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall remain protected throughout the account reactivation process.
27. Authentication credentials, verification codes, access tokens, refresh tokens, and other sensitive security information shall never be stored, logged, cached, or exposed in plain text.
28. Every successful account reactivation, failed authentication attempt, failed authorization attempt, failed identity verification, validation failure, and security-related event shall be recorded in the audit log where required.
29. Security monitoring systems shall receive applicable account lifecycle events according to the organization's monitoring policy.
30. Failure responses shall not expose authentication credentials, verification logic, internal security mechanisms, infrastructure details, or implementation information.
31. Concurrent account reactivation requests shall be handled according to the platform's concurrency control policy.
32. Successful account reactivation shall update the account status, reactivation timestamp, and other lifecycle metadata where maintained by the platform.
33. Notification of successful account reactivation shall be sent through the configured communication channels where applicable.
34. The Account Reactivation functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, security, privacy, audit, data protection, and applicable regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.14.7 Validations

| Validation | Rule | Failure Response |
|------------|------|------------------|
| Customer Identification Validation | The customer account shall be uniquely identified using the provided Customer ID, Registered Email Address, Registered Mobile Number, or Username/Login ID. | "Unable to identify customer account." |
| Customer Account Validation | The customer account shall exist in the platform. | "Customer account not found." |
| Account Status Validation | The customer account shall be in the **Deactivated** state. | "Account cannot be reactivated in its current status." |
| Already Active Validation | The account shall not already be in the **Active** state. | "Account is already active." |
| Identity Verification Validation | The customer shall successfully complete all required identity verification steps. | "Identity verification failed." |
| Password Validation | When password verification is required, the submitted password shall match the securely stored password. | "Incorrect password." |
| OTP Validation | When OTP verification is required, the submitted verification code shall match the generated OTP. | "Invalid verification code." |
| OTP Expiry Validation | The submitted OTP shall not be expired. | "Verification code has expired." |
| Email Verification Validation | When email verification is required, the registered email address shall be successfully verified. | "Email verification failed." |
| Mobile Verification Validation | When mobile verification is required, the registered mobile number shall be successfully verified. | "Mobile verification failed." |
| Reactivation Confirmation Validation | The customer shall explicitly confirm the account reactivation request. | "Account reactivation confirmation is required." |
| Administrative Restriction Validation | The account shall not be under administrative suspension, legal restriction, fraud investigation, or regulatory hold that prevents reactivation. | "Account cannot be reactivated at this time." |
| Authorization Policy Validation | The account shall satisfy all platform security and authorization policies required for reactivation. | "Account reactivation is not permitted." |
| Input Validation | All mandatory input fields shall be provided. | "Required information is missing." |
| Input Sanitization Validation | All submitted values shall be validated and sanitized before processing. | "Invalid request." |
| HTTPS Validation | Account reactivation requests shall be accepted only over secure HTTPS connections. | Request shall be rejected. |
| Request Integrity Validation | The reactivation request shall not be modified or tampered with during transmission. | "Request validation failed." |
| Transaction Validation | The account reactivation process shall complete successfully as a single atomic transaction. | "Unable to reactivate account." |
| Account Status Update Validation | The account status shall be successfully updated from **Deactivated** to **Active**. | "Unable to update account status." |
| Session Creation Validation | When applicable, new authenticated sessions shall be created successfully according to the platform's security policy. | "Unable to establish a new session." |
| Authentication Token Generation Validation | When applicable, new authentication tokens shall be generated successfully. | "Unable to generate authentication credentials." |
| Notification Validation | Account reactivation notifications shall be generated successfully where applicable. | Notification delivery failure shall be handled according to operational policy. |
| Concurrent Request Validation | Concurrent account reactivation requests shall be processed according to the configured concurrency policy. | "Another account operation is currently being processed." |
| Audit Validation | Every successful account reactivation, failed identity verification, failed authorization, validation failure, and security-related event shall be recorded in the audit log where applicable. | Audit logging failure shall be handled according to operational policy. |

#### 3.1.14.8 Main Flow

1. The customer accesses the **Login**, **Account Recovery**, or **Account Reactivation** page of the application.
2. The customer selects the **Reactivate Account** option.
3. The system displays the account reactivation interface.
4. The customer provides one or more supported account identifiers such as Customer ID, Registered Email Address, Registered Mobile Number, or Username/Login ID.
5. The customer explicitly confirms the intention to reactivate the account.
6. If required by the platform's security policy, the customer enters the account password.
7. If required, the system generates a One-Time Password (OTP).
8. The OTP Service delivers the verification code to the registered email address or mobile number.
9. The customer enters the received verification code.
10. The customer submits the account reactivation request.
11. The system receives the account reactivation request.
12. The system validates all mandatory input fields.
13. The system identifies the customer account using the submitted account identifier.
14. The system verifies that the customer account exists.
15. The system verifies that the account status is **Deactivated**.
16. The system verifies that the account is not already in the **Active** state.
17. The system validates that no administrative restriction, legal restriction, fraud investigation, or regulatory hold prevents account reactivation.
18. The Identity Verification Service initiates customer identity verification.
19. If password verification is required, the system securely validates the submitted password.
20. If OTP verification is required, the OTP Service validates the submitted verification code.
21. If email verification is required, the Email Service validates the registered email address.
22. If mobile verification is required, the SMS Notification Service validates the registered mobile number.
23. The system confirms that all required identity verification steps have been successfully completed.
24. The system validates the customer's explicit reactivation confirmation.
25. The system sanitizes and validates all submitted input.
26. The system begins a database transaction.
27. The Account Management Service updates the customer account status from **Deactivated** to **Active**.
28. The system records the account reactivation timestamp.
29. The system updates account lifecycle metadata where maintained by the platform.
30. The system preserves existing customer profile information, saved addresses, wallet balance, loyalty points, payment methods, preferences, and historical records.
31. If required by the platform's security policy, the Session Management Service creates new authenticated sessions.
32. If required, the Token Management Service generates new authentication tokens.
33. The system commits the transaction after successful completion of all account reactivation operations.
34. The Notification Service sends an account reactivation confirmation through the configured communication channels where applicable.
35. The Audit Logging Service records the successful account reactivation event.
36. The Security Monitoring Service records the account lifecycle event where applicable.
37. The system returns a successful account reactivation response.
38. If automatic sign-in is enabled by the platform's security policy, the customer is authenticated using the newly created session.
39. Otherwise, the customer is redirected to the login page to authenticate using normal login credentials.
40. The customer regains access to authenticated platform functionality.
41. The account reactivation process completes successfully.

#### 3.1.14.9 Alternate Flows

**AF-1: Customer Account Not Found**

1. The customer submits the account reactivation request.
2. The system searches for the customer account using the provided identifier.
3. No matching customer account is found.
4. The reactivation request is rejected.
5. The system returns an appropriate error response.
6. No account information is modified.

---

**AF-2: Account Already Active**

1. The system retrieves the customer account.
2. The account status is identified as **Active**.
3. The account reactivation request is rejected.
4. The customer is informed that the account is already active.
5. No account lifecycle information is modified.

---

**AF-3: Identity Verification Failed**

1. The Identity Verification Service initiates customer verification.
2. The customer fails one or more required identity verification steps.
3. The account reactivation request is rejected.
4. The verification failure is recorded according to the platform's security policy.
5. The customer account remains in the **Deactivated** state.

---

**AF-4: Incorrect Password**

1. Password verification is required by the platform.
2. The customer submits the account password.
3. The submitted password does not match the securely stored password.
4. The request is rejected.
5. The failed authentication attempt is recorded.
6. The customer account remains **Deactivated**.

---

**AF-5: Invalid or Expired OTP**

1. OTP verification is required.
2. The customer submits the verification code.
3. The submitted OTP is invalid or has expired.
4. The request is rejected.
5. The customer is prompted to request or enter a valid verification code.
6. The account status remains unchanged.

---

**AF-6: Administrative or Regulatory Restriction**

1. The system validates the account eligibility for reactivation.
2. The account is found to be under administrative suspension, fraud investigation, legal restriction, or regulatory hold.
3. The account reactivation request is rejected.
4. The customer is informed that the account cannot be reactivated at this time.
5. The restriction event is recorded for audit purposes.

---

**AF-7: Reactivation Confirmation Not Provided**

1. The customer initiates the account reactivation process.
2. The system validates the reactivation confirmation.
3. The required confirmation is not provided.
4. The request is rejected.
5. The customer is prompted to explicitly confirm the reactivation request.
6. The account remains **Deactivated**.

---

**AF-8: Concurrent Account Reactivation Request**

1. Multiple account reactivation requests are received simultaneously.
2. The system applies the configured concurrency control policy.
3. Conflicting requests are detected.
4. One request is processed successfully while the remaining requests are rejected or retried according to the platform's concurrency policy.
5. Account consistency is maintained throughout the operation.

---

**AF-9: Database Transaction Failure**

1. The system begins the account reactivation transaction.
2. An unexpected database or infrastructure failure occurs.
3. The transaction is rolled back completely.
4. The account status remains **Deactivated**.
5. The customer receives an internal error response.
6. The operational failure is recorded for investigation.

---

**AF-10: Session or Token Generation Failure**

1. The account status is successfully updated to **Active**.
2. The system encounters an error while creating authenticated sessions or generating authentication tokens.
3. The platform handles the failure according to the configured security policy.
4. Recovery or retry mechanisms may be initiated where applicable.
5. The security event is recorded for administrative review.

---

**AF-11: Notification Delivery Failure**

1. The account is successfully reactivated.
2. The Notification Service attempts to deliver the account reactivation confirmation.
3. Notification delivery fails due to a temporary communication or service failure.
4. The account remains successfully reactivated.
5. The notification failure is recorded for retry or operational follow-up.
6. The customer may receive the notification once the notification service becomes available.

---

**AF-12: Security Policy Violation**

1. The system detects suspicious activity, abnormal behavior, or a security policy violation during the reactivation process.
2. The reactivation request is suspended or rejected according to the platform's security policy.
3. Additional verification may be required before further processing.
4. The security event is recorded in the audit and monitoring systems.
5. The customer account remains in the **Deactivated** state until the security requirements are satisfied.

#### 3.1.14.10 Postconditions

1. The customer account status shall be successfully updated from **Deactivated** to **Active** upon completion of the account reactivation process.
2. The account reactivation timestamp shall be recorded where maintained by the platform.
3. Account lifecycle metadata shall be updated to reflect the successful reactivation event.
4. The customer shall regain access to authenticated platform functionality.
5. The customer shall be permitted to authenticate using valid credentials after successful account reactivation.
6. If required by the platform's security policy, new authenticated sessions shall be established.
7. If required, new access tokens, refresh tokens, and authentication credentials shall be generated according to the platform's security policy.
8. Previously invalidated sessions, access tokens, refresh tokens, and remembered devices shall remain invalid unless explicitly restored through an independent security process.
9. Customer profile information shall remain unchanged.
10. Saved addresses and saved places shall remain available.
11. Customer preferences, notification preferences, language preferences, and privacy settings shall remain preserved.
12. Wallet balance shall remain unchanged unless modified through an independent financial process.
13. Loyalty points, referral rewards, promotional benefits, and customer rewards shall remain preserved according to business policies.
14. Saved payment methods shall remain available unless previously removed through an independent business process.
15. Order history, invoices, transaction history, ratings, reviews, and other historical records shall remain accessible according to organizational retention policies.
16. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall remain securely protected throughout the account reactivation process.
17. The successful account reactivation event shall be recorded in the audit log.
18. Security monitoring systems shall receive applicable account lifecycle events where integrated.
19. Account reactivation notifications shall be generated through the configured communication channels where applicable.
20. No partial updates shall remain if the reactivation transaction fails; the account shall remain in its previous consistent **Deactivated** state.
21. Authentication credentials, verification codes, access tokens, refresh tokens, cryptographic material, and other sensitive security information shall never be exposed through responses, logs, or audit records.
22. The customer shall receive an appropriate success or failure response based on the outcome of the request.
23. The account shall remain in the **Active** state until modified through another supported account lifecycle process.
24. The Account Reactivation process shall complete in compliance with the platform's authentication, authorization, security, privacy, audit, data protection, and applicable regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.14.11 Success Response

Upon successful completion of the Account Reactivation process, the system shall return a success response confirming that the customer's account has been successfully restored without exposing sensitive authentication, security, or internal system information.

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Status | Indicates that the account reactivation request was completed successfully. |
| Status Code | Standard application/API success status code (for example, HTTP 200 OK). |
| Message | Confirmation that the account has been successfully reactivated. |
| Customer ID | Unique identifier of the customer account. |
| Account Status | Current account status after processing (Active). |
| Reactivated At | Timestamp indicating when the account was successfully reactivated. |
| Session Status | Indicates whether a new authenticated session has been created according to the platform's security policy. |
| Authentication Token Status | Indicates whether new authentication tokens have been generated where applicable. |
| Request ID | Unique identifier for tracking the account reactivation request. |
| Response Timestamp | Date and time when the response was generated. |

**Example Success Message**

```text
Your account has been successfully reactivated.
```

**Success Response Characteristics**

1. The response shall confirm that the account has been successfully reactivated.
2. The response shall indicate that the account status is **Active**.
3. The response shall not include passwords, password hashes, OTP values, access tokens, refresh tokens, session identifiers, or other authentication credentials unless explicitly required by the platform's authentication policy.
4. The response shall not expose internal authentication, authorization, identity verification, or security implementation details.
5. The response shall not disclose Personally Identifiable Information (PII) beyond the information permitted by the platform's response standards.
6. The response shall indicate whether a new authenticated session has been established where applicable.
7. The response shall indicate whether new authentication tokens have been generated according to the platform's security policy.
8. The response shall include the request identifier for audit, monitoring, and operational troubleshooting purposes.
9. The response shall be transmitted only through secure HTTPS connections.
10. The response shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, security, privacy, audit, and API response standards.
11. The response shall accurately reflect the final outcome of the account reactivation transaction.

#### 3.1.14.12 Failure Response

When the Account Reactivation request cannot be completed, the system shall return an appropriate failure response without exposing sensitive authentication information, customer credentials, internal security mechanisms, or infrastructure details.

| Failure Scenario | Response Message |
|------------------|------------------|
| Customer Account Not Found | Customer account not found. |
| Account Already Active | Account is already active. |
| Account Not Eligible for Reactivation | Account cannot be reactivated in its current status. |
| Identity Verification Failed | Identity verification failed. |
| Incorrect Password | Incorrect password. |
| Invalid Verification Code | Invalid verification code. |
| Verification Code Expired | Verification code has expired. |
| Email Verification Failed | Email verification failed. |
| Mobile Verification Failed | Mobile verification failed. |
| Reactivation Confirmation Missing | Account reactivation confirmation is required. |
| Administrative Restriction | Account cannot be reactivated at this time. |
| Fraud Investigation | Account cannot be reactivated while under security review. |
| Legal or Regulatory Restriction | Account reactivation is currently restricted. |
| Authorization Policy Failure | Account reactivation is not permitted. |
| Required Information Missing | Required information is missing. |
| Invalid Request Data | Invalid request. |
| Request Integrity Failure | Request validation failed. |
| Session Creation Failure | Unable to establish a new session. |
| Authentication Token Generation Failure | Unable to generate authentication credentials. |
| Notification Delivery Failure | Notification could not be delivered. |
| Concurrent Account Operation | Another account operation is currently being processed. |
| Database Transaction Failure | Unable to reactivate account. Please try again later. |
| Internal Server Error | An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later. |

**Failure Response Characteristics**

1. Every failure response shall contain an appropriate application or HTTP error status code.
2. Failure responses shall never disclose passwords, password hashes, OTP values, access tokens, refresh tokens, session identifiers, cryptographic material, authentication secrets, or security credentials.
3. Failure responses shall never expose internal authentication, authorization, identity verification, session management, or token management implementation details.
4. Failure responses shall never reveal database schema, infrastructure configuration, server information, stack traces, or internal system architecture.
5. Validation failures shall clearly identify the input error without exposing sensitive security information.
6. Authentication and identity verification failures shall return generic security messages to minimize information disclosure.
7. Failed account reactivation operations shall ensure that no partial account updates are committed.
8. The customer account shall remain in its previous consistent **Deactivated** state whenever the reactivation transaction is not successfully completed.
9. All applicable failure events shall be recorded in the audit log according to the platform's security policy.
10. Failure responses shall be transmitted only through secure HTTPS connections.
11. Failure responses shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, security, privacy, audit, logging, and API response standards.
12. Operational failures shall be logged for monitoring, troubleshooting, security analysis, and incident investigation without exposing sensitive customer information in client-facing responses.

#### 3.1.14.13 Non-Functional Considerations

##### Performance

1. The Account Reactivation operation should complete within the platform's defined response time under normal operating conditions.
2. Identity verification, account validation, and account status update operations shall be optimized to provide a responsive user experience.
3. The system shall support concurrent account reactivation requests from multiple customers without significant performance degradation.
4. Database transactions shall minimize lock duration while maintaining data consistency.
5. Session creation and authentication token generation shall be completed within acceptable operational time limits.

##### Availability

6. The Account Reactivation functionality shall be available according to the platform's defined service availability objectives.
7. Temporary service failures shall not result in inconsistent account lifecycle states.
8. High availability mechanisms shall ensure continuity of authentication and account management services.
9. Planned maintenance activities shall not compromise the integrity of account reactivation operations.

##### Scalability

10. The platform shall support growth in the number of customer accounts without degrading account reactivation performance.
11. Authentication, identity verification, session management, notification, and account management services shall support horizontal scalability where applicable.
12. The solution shall efficiently process increasing volumes of account lifecycle operations.

##### Security

13. All account reactivation requests shall be transmitted exclusively over secure HTTPS connections.
14. Identity verification shall be completed before permitting account reactivation.
15. Authentication and authorization controls shall be enforced according to the platform's security policy.
16. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall remain protected throughout the account reactivation process.
17. Passwords, verification codes, access tokens, refresh tokens, session identifiers, and authentication credentials shall never be stored, logged, cached, or exposed in plain text.
18. The system shall protect against unauthorized account reactivation attempts, brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, replay attacks, session hijacking, account enumeration, and other applicable security threats.
19. Security events shall be monitored and logged in accordance with organizational security policies.
