20. Sensitive security information shall never be exposed through application responses, logs, monitoring systems, or audit records.

##### Reliability

21. The account reactivation process shall execute as a single atomic transaction.
22. System failures shall not result in partially reactivated customer accounts.
23. Rollback mechanisms shall restore the previous account state if account reactivation cannot be completed successfully.
24. Data consistency shall be maintained across authentication, account management, notification, session management, and audit services.

##### Maintainability

25. Identity verification policies and account lifecycle rules shall be configurable without requiring application code changes wherever feasible.
26. Authentication, identity verification, notification, session management, and account management components should remain modular for easier maintenance.
27. Security policies shall support centralized administration and configuration.
28. Audit records shall support operational troubleshooting and security investigations.

##### Auditability

29. Every successful account reactivation shall generate an audit record.
30. Failed account reactivation attempts shall be logged according to the platform's security policy.
31. Audit records shall include the customer identifier, request identifier, timestamp, event type, verification method, and operation outcome where applicable.
32. Audit logs shall be protected from unauthorized modification, deletion, or disclosure.
33. Audit records shall comply with the organization's retention, governance, and regulatory requirements.

##### Compliance

34. The Account Reactivation functionality shall comply with applicable data protection, privacy, cybersecurity, and regulatory requirements.
35. Customer information shall be processed in accordance with applicable privacy regulations.
36. Identity verification and account lifecycle management shall comply with the organization's information security standards.
37. Data retention, audit logging, customer communication, and authentication practices shall comply with applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

---

#### 3.1.14.14 Acceptance Criteria

| AC ID | Acceptance Criteria |
|-------|---------------------|
| AC-AR-001 | Only customer accounts in the **Deactivated** state shall be eligible for account reactivation. |
| AC-AR-002 | The customer account shall exist before account reactivation is processed. |
| AC-AR-003 | The customer shall successfully complete all required identity verification steps according to the platform's security policy. |
| AC-AR-004 | The customer shall explicitly confirm the account reactivation request before processing. |
| AC-AR-005 | The account status shall be updated from **Deactivated** to **Active** upon successful completion of the account reactivation process. |
| AC-AR-006 | The account reactivation operation shall complete as a single atomic transaction. |
| AC-AR-007 | Customer profile information, wallet balance, loyalty points, saved addresses, payment methods, and historical records shall remain preserved after successful account reactivation. |
| AC-AR-008 | New authenticated sessions and authentication tokens shall be created according to the platform's security policy where applicable. |
| AC-AR-009 | Previously invalidated sessions and authentication credentials shall not be automatically restored unless permitted by the platform's security policy. |
| AC-AR-010 | Transaction rollback shall occur automatically if any validation or processing step fails. |
| AC-AR-011 | Every successful and failed account reactivation event shall be recorded according to the platform's audit policy. |
| AC-AR-012 | Failure responses shall not expose authentication credentials, internal security implementation details, or sensitive customer information. |
| AC-AR-013 | All account reactivation requests shall be processed only through secure HTTPS connections. |
| AC-AR-014 | Accounts under administrative suspension, fraud investigation, legal restriction, or regulatory hold shall not be reactivated until the applicable restriction is removed. |
| AC-AR-015 | The Account Reactivation functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, security, privacy, audit, data protection, and applicable regulatory requirements. |

### 3.1.15 Account Deletion

#### 3.1.15.1 Description

The **Account Deletion** functionality allows a customer to permanently delete their Food Delivery Platform account after successfully completing all required authentication, identity verification, and confirmation steps. Unlike **Account Deactivation**, account deletion is an irreversible account lifecycle operation that permanently removes or anonymizes customer information in accordance with the platform's data retention policy, applicable legal requirements, and regulatory obligations.

Before processing the account deletion request, the system shall securely verify the customer's identity using one or more approved authentication mechanisms, such as password verification, One-Time Password (OTP), email verification, mobile verification, or other security controls defined by the platform's authentication policy.

The system shall evaluate whether the customer account is eligible for deletion by validating applicable business rules, including pending orders, ongoing financial transactions, unresolved disputes, regulatory holds, fraud investigations, legal obligations, or any other restrictions that prevent permanent account deletion.

Upon successful completion of all validations, the system shall permanently delete or irreversibly anonymize customer data according to the organization's data retention and privacy policies. Data that must be retained for legal, financial, taxation, fraud prevention, security, audit, regulatory compliance, or dispute resolution purposes shall be preserved only for the required retention period and protected in accordance with applicable laws.

Following successful account deletion, the system shall invalidate all authenticated sessions, revoke authentication credentials, disable future authentication for the deleted account, remove customer access to all authenticated platform resources, record the account deletion event in the audit log, and generate applicable operational and security notifications.

The Account Deletion process shall be executed as a secure, auditable, compliant, and atomic transaction while ensuring compliance with the platform's authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, data retention, cybersecurity, and applicable regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.15.2 Actors

- Customer
- Authentication Service
- Authorization Service
- Account Management Service
- Customer Profile Service
- Identity Verification Service
- Session Management Service
- Token Management Service
- OTP Service
- Email Service
- SMS Notification Service
- Order Management Service
- Payment Service
- Customer Wallet Service
- Loyalty Management Service
- Refund Management Service
- Notification Service
- Audit Logging Service
- Data Retention Service
- Data Privacy & Compliance Service
- Security Monitoring Service
- Fraud Detection Service
- Legal & Compliance Service

#### 3.1.15.3 Preconditions

1. The customer account shall exist in the platform.
2. The customer shall be successfully authenticated before initiating the account deletion process.
3. The customer shall have an active authenticated session unless an alternate secure verification workflow is supported by the platform.
4. The customer shall be authorized to permanently delete only their own customer account.
5. The Authentication Service shall be available.
6. The Authorization Service shall be available.
7. The Account Management Service shall be available.
8. The Customer Profile Service shall be available.
9. The Identity Verification Service shall be available.
10. The Session Management Service shall be available.
11. The Token Management Service shall be available.
12. The OTP Service shall be available when OTP verification is required.
13. The Email Service shall be available when email verification is required.
14. The SMS Notification Service shall be available when mobile verification is required.
15. The Order Management Service shall be available for validating pending and active orders.
16. The Payment Service shall be available for validating pending payment transactions.
17. The Customer Wallet Service shall be available for validating wallet balance and pending wallet transactions.
18. The Loyalty Management Service shall be available for validating loyalty points and rewards where applicable.
19. The Refund Management Service shall be available for validating pending refund requests.
20. The Data Retention Service shall be available for applying organizational data retention policies.
21. The Data Privacy & Compliance Service shall be available for enforcing privacy and regulatory requirements.
22. The Notification Service shall be available for sending account deletion notifications where applicable.
23. The Audit Logging Service shall be available for recording account lifecycle and security events.
24. The Security Monitoring Service shall be available for monitoring account deletion activities.
25. The Fraud Detection Service shall be available for validating fraud-related restrictions where applicable.
26. The Legal & Compliance Service shall be available for validating legal, regulatory, and compliance restrictions.
27. The customer's identity shall be successfully verified according to the platform's authentication and security policy before account deletion is permitted.
28. The customer shall successfully complete all mandatory verification steps, including password verification, OTP verification, email verification, mobile verification, or other approved identity verification mechanisms, as applicable.
29. The customer shall explicitly acknowledge that account deletion is permanent and may be irreversible.
30. The account shall not have pending orders, active deliveries, unresolved disputes, pending payment transactions, pending refunds, or other business restrictions that prevent deletion.
31. The account shall not be under fraud investigation, administrative suspension, legal hold, regulatory restriction, or any other condition that prohibits permanent deletion.
32. The account deletion request shall be initiated over a secure HTTPS connection.
33. The platform shall be capable of executing the account deletion process as a single atomic transaction.
34. All backend services participating in the account deletion workflow shall be operational.
35. The platform shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, data retention, cybersecurity, and regulatory requirements before processing the account deletion request.

#### 3.1.15.4 Trigger

The **Account Deletion** process is initiated when an authenticated customer navigates to the **Account Settings**, **Privacy & Security**, or **Account Management** section of the application, selects the **Delete Account** option, reviews the permanent account deletion information, completes all required identity verification steps, explicitly acknowledges the irreversible nature of the operation, and submits the account deletion request.

---

#### 3.1.15.5 Input Fields

| Field Name | Data Type | Mandatory | Description | Validation |
|------------|-----------|-----------|-------------|------------|
| Customer ID | UUID | System Generated | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. | Shall belong to the authenticated customer account. |
| Access Token | String | Yes | Authentication token used to authorize the account deletion request. | Shall be valid, active, and associated with the authenticated customer. |
| Session ID | String | Yes | Active authenticated session identifier. | Shall be valid and not expired. |
| Current Password | String | Conditional | Password entered to verify the customer's identity when required by the platform's security policy. | Shall match the securely stored password. |
| Verification Code (OTP) | String | Conditional | One-Time Password sent to the customer's registered email address or mobile number. | Shall be valid, unexpired, and match the generated verification code. |
| Deletion Reason | Enum / String | Optional | Customer-selected or customer-entered reason for permanently deleting the account. | Shall conform to supported values or configured character limits. |
| Additional Comments | String | Optional | Additional feedback provided by the customer regarding account deletion. | Shall not exceed the configured maximum character limit. |
| Permanent Deletion Confirmation | Boolean | Yes | Explicit confirmation indicating the customer's intention to permanently delete the account. | Shall be **True** before processing the request. |
| Terms Acknowledgement | Boolean | Yes | Confirmation that the customer understands the permanent and potentially irreversible nature of account deletion. | Shall be **True** before processing the request. |
| Device Information | Object | Optional | Information about the device initiating the account deletion request. | Shall comply with platform security validation rules. |
| IP Address | String | System Generated | IP address from which the account deletion request originates. | Shall be captured for audit and security monitoring purposes. |
| User Agent | String | System Generated | Browser or application information of the requesting device. | Shall be captured for audit and security monitoring purposes. |

**Notes:**

- The **Customer ID** shall be derived from the authenticated session and shall not be editable.
- The **Access Token** and **Session ID** shall be validated before processing the account deletion request.
- The **Current Password** shall be required when mandated by the platform's authentication and security policy.
- The **Verification Code (OTP)** shall be required when OTP-based identity verification is enabled or triggered through risk-based authentication.
- The **Permanent Deletion Confirmation** shall explicitly indicate the customer's intention to permanently delete the account.
- The **Terms Acknowledgement** shall confirm that the customer understands the consequences of permanent account deletion before the request is processed.
- The **Deletion Reason** and **Additional Comments** may be stored for reporting, analytics, and service improvement purposes in accordance with the platform's privacy and data retention policies.
- The **Device Information**, **IP Address**, and **User Agent** may be captured for fraud detection, security monitoring, audit, and compliance purposes.
- Passwords, verification codes, authentication tokens, and other sensitive credentials shall never be stored, logged, cached, or transmitted in plain text beyond secure request processing.
- All submitted information shall be transmitted only through secure HTTPS connections.
- Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and authentication credentials shall be processed in accordance with the platform's privacy, security, audit, data retention, and applicable regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.15.6 Business Rules

1. Only authenticated customers shall be permitted to permanently delete their own customer account.
2. The system shall allow account deletion only for the authenticated customer associated with the current authenticated session.
3. Customers shall not be permitted to delete another customer's account.
4. The system shall validate customer authentication and authorization before processing the account deletion request.
5. The customer account shall exist before the account deletion process begins.
6. The customer shall successfully complete all required identity verification steps before account deletion is permitted.
7. Identity verification shall be performed using one or more approved authentication mechanisms as defined by the platform's security policy.
8. Password verification shall be required when mandated by the platform's authentication policy.
9. OTP verification shall be required when enabled by the platform or triggered through risk-based authentication.
10. Email verification or mobile verification may be required based on the configured security policy.
11. The customer shall explicitly confirm the permanent account deletion request before the operation is executed.
12. The customer shall explicitly acknowledge that account deletion is permanent and may be irreversible.
13. The account deletion request shall be accepted only through secure HTTPS connections.
14. All submitted input shall be validated and sanitized before processing.
15. The system shall verify that no pending orders, active deliveries, scheduled deliveries, or order fulfillment activities exist before permitting account deletion.
16. The system shall verify that no pending payment transactions exist before account deletion.
17. The system shall verify that no pending wallet transactions exist before account deletion.
18. The system shall verify that no pending refund requests or unresolved financial settlements exist before account deletion.
19. The system shall verify that no unresolved customer disputes, complaints, or support cases prevent account deletion according to business policy.
20. Accounts under fraud investigation, administrative suspension, legal hold, regulatory restriction, compliance review, or security investigation shall not be eligible for permanent deletion until the applicable restriction is removed.
21. The system shall evaluate applicable legal, taxation, financial, audit, cybersecurity, and regulatory retention requirements before permanently deleting customer information.
22. Data required for legal, financial, taxation, fraud prevention, audit, dispute resolution, or regulatory compliance shall be retained only for the required retention period in accordance with applicable laws and organizational policies.
23. Customer information that is no longer required to be retained shall be permanently deleted or irreversibly anonymized according to the platform's data retention policy.
24. The account deletion operation shall execute as a single atomic transaction.
25. If any validation or processing step fails, the entire account deletion transaction shall be rolled back.
26. Upon successful account deletion, all authenticated sessions shall be invalidated according to the platform's security policy.
27. All active access tokens, refresh tokens, remembered devices, API tokens, and other authentication credentials shall be revoked or invalidated.
28. Authentication using the deleted customer account shall no longer be permitted.
29. Deleted customer accounts shall not be eligible for automatic account reactivation.
30. Any future use of the platform by the deleted customer shall require registration of a new customer account unless prohibited by applicable policies or regulations.
31. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be processed in accordance with applicable privacy regulations throughout the account deletion process.
32. Sensitive authentication credentials shall never be stored, logged, cached, or exposed in plain text.
33. The deletion reason and optional customer feedback may be retained only where permitted by applicable privacy regulations and organizational retention policies.
34. Every successful account deletion, failed authentication attempt, failed authorization attempt, failed identity verification, validation failure, restriction validation, and security-related event shall be recorded in the audit log where required.
35. Security monitoring systems shall receive applicable account lifecycle events according to the organization's monitoring policy.
36. Failure responses shall not expose authentication credentials, verification logic, internal security mechanisms, infrastructure details, retained customer data, or implementation information.
37. Concurrent account deletion requests shall be handled according to the platform's concurrency control policy.
38. Successful account deletion shall update account lifecycle metadata where retention of such metadata is legally permitted.
39. Notification of successful account deletion shall be sent through the configured communication channels where applicable and legally permissible.
40. The Account Deletion functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, security, privacy, audit, cybersecurity, data retention, and applicable regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.15.7 Validations

| Validation | Rule | Failure Response |
|------------|------|------------------|
| Authentication Validation | The customer shall be successfully authenticated before initiating account deletion. | "Authentication required." |
| Access Token Validation | The Access Token shall be valid, active, and digitally verifiable. | "Invalid or expired authentication token." |
| Session Validation | An active authenticated session shall exist. | "Session has expired. Please log in again." |
| Session Expiry Validation | The authenticated session shall not have expired before processing the request. | "Session has expired." |
| Session Revocation Validation | The authenticated session shall not have been revoked or terminated. | "Session is no longer valid." |
| Customer Identification Validation | The authenticated customer shall be uniquely identified from the active session or Access Token. | "Unable to identify customer." |
| Customer Account Validation | The customer account shall exist. | "Customer account not found." |
| Authorization Validation | The authenticated customer shall be authorized to permanently delete the account. | "Access denied." |
| Ownership Validation | Customers shall be permitted to permanently delete only their own account. | "Unauthorized account deletion attempt." |
| Password Validation | When password verification is required, the submitted Current Password shall match the securely stored password. | "Current password is incorrect." |
| OTP Validation | When OTP verification is required, the submitted verification code shall match the generated OTP. | "Invalid verification code." |
| OTP Expiry Validation | The submitted OTP shall not be expired. | "Verification code has expired." |
| Email Verification Validation | When email verification is required, the registered email address shall be successfully verified. | "Email verification failed." |
| Mobile Verification Validation | When mobile verification is required, the registered mobile number shall be successfully verified. | "Mobile verification failed." |
| Permanent Deletion Confirmation Validation | The customer shall explicitly confirm the permanent account deletion request. | "Account deletion confirmation is required." |
| Terms Acknowledgement Validation | The customer shall acknowledge that account deletion is permanent and may be irreversible. | "Acknowledgement is required before deleting the account." |
| Pending Orders Validation | The customer account shall not have pending orders, active deliveries, or scheduled deliveries. | "Account cannot be deleted while active orders exist." |
| Pending Payment Validation | The customer account shall not have pending payment transactions. | "Pending payment transactions must be completed before account deletion." |
| Wallet Balance Validation | The account shall satisfy the platform's wallet policy before deletion. | "Wallet balance must be resolved before account deletion." |
| Pending Refund Validation | The account shall not have pending refund requests or unsettled financial transactions. | "Pending refunds must be completed before account deletion." |
| Customer Dispute Validation | The account shall not have unresolved customer disputes or support cases that prevent deletion. | "Account cannot be deleted while unresolved cases exist." |
| Fraud Investigation Validation | The account shall not be under fraud investigation or security review. | "Account cannot be deleted at this time." |
| Legal & Regulatory Validation | No legal, taxation, compliance, or regulatory restriction shall prevent account deletion. | "Account deletion is currently restricted." |
| Data Retention Validation | Applicable data retention requirements shall be successfully evaluated before account deletion. | "Unable to process account deletion due to data retention requirements." |
| Input Validation | All mandatory input fields shall be provided. | "Required information is missing." |
| Input Sanitization Validation | All submitted values shall be validated and sanitized before processing. | "Invalid request." |
| HTTPS Validation | Account deletion requests shall be accepted only over secure HTTPS connections. | Request shall be rejected. |
| Request Integrity Validation | The account deletion request shall not be modified or tampered with during transmission. | "Request validation failed." |
| Transaction Validation | The account deletion process shall complete successfully as a single atomic transaction. | "Unable to delete account." |
| Session Invalidation Validation | Active authenticated sessions shall be successfully invalidated after account deletion. | "Unable to complete account deletion." |
| Authentication Credential Revocation Validation | Authentication tokens, refresh tokens, remembered devices, and other authentication credentials shall be successfully revoked or invalidated. | "Unable to complete account deletion." |
| Concurrent Request Validation | Concurrent account deletion requests shall be processed according to the configured concurrency policy. | "Another account operation is currently being processed." |
| Audit Validation | Every successful account deletion, failed authentication, failed authorization, failed identity verification, validation failure, restriction validation, and security-related event shall be recorded in the audit log where applicable. | Audit logging failure shall be handled according to operational policy. |

#### 3.1.15.8 Main Flow

1. The customer navigates to the **Account Settings**, **Privacy & Security**, or **Account Management** section of the application.
2. The customer selects the **Delete Account** option.
3. The system displays detailed information explaining the consequences of permanent account deletion.
4. The system informs the customer that account deletion may be irreversible.
5. The system informs the customer about applicable data retention, legal, financial, and regulatory obligations.
6. The customer reviews the account deletion information.
7. The customer optionally selects an account deletion reason.
8. The customer optionally provides additional comments.
9. The customer explicitly confirms the permanent account deletion request.
10. The customer acknowledges that the deletion process is permanent and may be irreversible.
11. If required by the platform's security policy, the customer enters the current password.
12. If additional identity verification is required, the system generates a One-Time Password (OTP).
13. The OTP Service sends the verification code to the customer's registered email address or mobile number.
14. The customer enters the received verification code.
15. The customer submits the account deletion request.
16. The client application sends the request together with the authenticated Access Token and active Session ID.
17. The system receives the account deletion request.
18. The Authentication Service validates the Access Token.
19. The system verifies the integrity of the authentication token.
20. The Session Management Service validates the authenticated session.
21. The system verifies that the authenticated session has not expired.
22. The system verifies that the authenticated session has not been revoked or terminated.
23. The Authorization Service verifies that the authenticated customer is authorized to delete the account.
24. The system identifies the authenticated customer from the validated session.
25. The system retrieves the authenticated Customer ID.
26. The system verifies that the customer account exists.
27. The Identity Verification Service performs the required identity verification.
28. If required, the system securely validates the submitted current password.
29. If required, the OTP Service validates the submitted verification code.
30. The system validates the customer's explicit deletion confirmation.
31. The system validates the customer's acknowledgement of the permanent deletion consequences.
32. The system validates that no pending orders, active deliveries, or scheduled deliveries exist.
33. The system validates that no pending payment transactions exist.
34. The system validates that no pending wallet transactions exist according to the platform's financial policy.
35. The system validates that no pending refund requests or unsettled financial transactions exist.
36. The system validates that no unresolved customer disputes or support cases prevent account deletion.
37. The Fraud Detection Service verifies that the account is not under fraud investigation.
38. The Legal & Compliance Service validates that no legal, regulatory, taxation, or compliance restriction prevents account deletion.
39. The Data Retention Service evaluates applicable data retention obligations.
40. The system validates and sanitizes all submitted input.
41. The system begins a database transaction.
42. The system records the deletion reason and optional customer feedback where permitted.
43. The Session Management Service invalidates all authenticated sessions.
44. The Token Management Service revokes or invalidates all authentication credentials, access tokens, refresh tokens, remembered devices, and other security credentials.
45. The system permanently deletes or irreversibly anonymizes customer information according to the applicable data retention policy.
46. The system preserves only the information that must legally or regulatorily be retained.
47. The system updates account lifecycle metadata where legally permissible.
48. The system commits the transaction after successful completion of all account deletion operations.
49. The Notification Service sends an account deletion confirmation through the configured communication channels where applicable and legally permitted.
50. The Audit Logging Service records the successful account deletion event.
51. The Security Monitoring Service records the account lifecycle and security events where applicable.
52. The system returns a successful account deletion response.
53. Any future authentication attempt using the deleted account is rejected.
54. The customer permanently loses access to authenticated platform services associated with the deleted account.
55. The Account Deletion process completes successfully.

#### 3.1.15.9 Alternate Flows

**AF-1: Password Verification Not Required**

1. The customer initiates the account deletion request.
2. The platform determines that password verification is not required based on the configured authentication policy.
3. The system skips the password verification step.
4. The remaining account deletion workflow continues normally.

---

**AF-2: OTP Verification Not Required**

1. The customer initiates the account deletion request.
2. The platform determines that OTP verification is not required.
3. No verification code is generated or sent.
4. The remaining account deletion workflow continues normally.

---

**AF-3: Email Verification Required**

1. The platform determines that email verification is required.
2. A verification code or verification link is sent to the customer's registered email address.
3. The customer successfully completes email verification.
4. The account deletion workflow resumes.

---

**AF-4: Mobile Verification Required**

1. The platform determines that mobile verification is required.
2. An OTP is sent to the customer's registered mobile number.
3. The customer successfully verifies the OTP.
4. The account deletion workflow resumes.

---

**AF-5: Multi-Factor Authentication Required**

1. The platform determines that additional identity verification is required.
2. The customer completes all configured authentication factors.
3. Identity verification succeeds.
4. The account deletion workflow continues.

---

**AF-6: Pending Orders Exist**

1. The system detects one or more pending orders, active deliveries, or scheduled deliveries.
2. The account deletion request is rejected.
3. The customer is informed that account deletion cannot proceed until all active order-related activities are completed.
4. No customer information is deleted.

---

**AF-7: Pending Payment Transaction Exists**

1. The system detects one or more pending payment transactions.
2. The deletion request is rejected.
3. The customer is instructed to complete or cancel the pending transaction.
4. No customer information is deleted.

---

**AF-8: Pending Wallet Transaction or Wallet Restriction Exists**

1. The Customer Wallet Service identifies wallet-related restrictions according to the platform's financial policy.
2. The account deletion request is rejected.
3. The customer is informed about the wallet-related restriction.
4. The account remains active.

---

**AF-9: Pending Refund Exists**

1. The Refund Management Service identifies pending refund requests or unsettled financial transactions.
2. The deletion request is rejected.
3. The customer is informed that refunds must be completed before account deletion.
4. The account remains active.

---

**AF-10: Unresolved Customer Dispute Exists**

1. The system detects unresolved disputes, complaints, or customer support cases.
2. The account deletion request is rejected.
3. The customer is instructed to resolve the outstanding issues.
4. The account remains active.

---

**AF-11: Fraud Investigation in Progress**

1. The Fraud Detection Service determines that the customer account is under fraud investigation.
2. Account deletion is blocked.
3. The request is rejected according to the platform's fraud prevention policy.
4. The account remains active.

---

**AF-12: Legal or Regulatory Restriction Exists**

1. The Legal & Compliance Service identifies a legal hold, regulatory restriction, taxation requirement, court order, or compliance obligation.
2. The deletion request is rejected.
3. The customer is informed that the account cannot currently be deleted.
4. The account remains active.

---

**AF-13: Data Retention Requirement Prevents Immediate Deletion**

1. The Data Retention Service determines that certain customer information must be retained.
2. The system permanently deletes or anonymizes only the information eligible for deletion.
3. Legally required records are retained according to applicable regulations.
4. The account deletion process completes in compliance with the applicable retention policy.

---

**AF-14: Session Expires During Processing**

1. The authenticated session expires before the deletion request is completed.
2. The request is terminated.
3. The customer is required to authenticate again.
4. No account information is deleted.

---

**AF-15: Identity Verification Fails**

1. Password verification, OTP verification, email verification, mobile verification, or another required authentication factor fails.
2. The account deletion request is rejected.
3. No deletion operation is performed.
4. The customer may retry according to the configured security policy.

---

**AF-16: Concurrent Account Operation Detected**

1. Another account management operation is already being processed.
2. The account deletion request is temporarily rejected.
3. The customer is informed to retry after the current operation completes.
4. No deletion operation is performed.

---

**AF-17: Database Transaction Failure**

1. A database or persistence error occurs during the deletion transaction.
2. The system rolls back the entire transaction.
3. No partial deletion is committed.
4. The account remains unchanged.

---

**AF-18: Internal Service Failure**

1. One or more dependent backend services become unavailable during processing.
2. The account deletion transaction is rolled back.
3. No customer information is permanently deleted.
4. An appropriate error response is returned.

---

**AF-19: Audit Logging Failure**

1. The Audit Logging Service encounters an operational failure.
2. The platform handles the failure according to the configured operational policy.
3. The deletion request may be retried, queued, or rejected based on organizational security requirements.
4. No inconsistent account state shall be created.

---

**AF-20: Successful Account Deletion**

1. All validations complete successfully.
2. All required customer information is permanently deleted or irreversibly anonymized according to the applicable retention policy.
3. Authentication sessions and security credentials are revoked.
4. Audit logs and security events are recorded.
5. A confirmation notification is sent where applicable.
6. The Account Deletion process completes successfully.

#### 3.1.15.10 Postconditions

1. The customer's account shall be permanently deleted or irreversibly anonymized in accordance with the platform's data retention policy and applicable legal, financial, taxation, privacy, and regulatory requirements.
2. The account shall no longer be available for customer authentication.
3. The deleted customer account shall no longer appear as an active account within the platform.
4. All authenticated sessions associated with the deleted account shall be invalidated.
5. All active access tokens, refresh tokens, remembered devices, API tokens, and other authentication credentials shall be revoked or invalidated.
6. Any future authentication attempt using the deleted account credentials shall be rejected.
7. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) eligible for deletion shall be permanently removed or irreversibly anonymized according to applicable privacy regulations.
8. Customer information that must legally be retained shall remain securely stored for the required retention period.
9. Order history, payment records, invoices, taxation records, financial records, audit records, fraud investigation records, and other legally required information shall be retained only where mandated by applicable laws or organizational policies.
10. Customer information retained for legal or regulatory purposes shall no longer be available for normal customer-facing operations.
11. Customer profile information shall no longer be editable.
12. Customer preferences, saved addresses, saved payment preferences, notification preferences, and other customer-specific profile data shall be permanently deleted where permitted.
13. Customer wallet information shall be handled according to the platform's financial and regulatory policies.
14. Loyalty information shall be processed according to the organization's loyalty program retention policy.
15. Pending authentication challenges associated with the account shall be invalidated.
16. Any active password reset requests shall be cancelled.
17. Any active email verification requests shall be cancelled.
18. Any active mobile verification requests shall be cancelled.
19. Any active account reactivation workflows shall be cancelled.
20. Security monitoring systems shall receive the appropriate account lifecycle events where applicable.
21. The Audit Logging Service shall record the successful completion of the account deletion process.
22. The audit log shall include the customer identifier (where legally permissible), deletion timestamp, verification outcome, request source, initiating device information, IP address, processing status, and other required audit metadata.
23. The system shall record the deletion reason and optional customer feedback where permitted by applicable privacy regulations.
24. A confirmation notification shall be sent through the configured communication channels where applicable and legally permissible.
25. No confidential authentication credentials shall remain active after account deletion.
26. No partially completed deletion state shall exist after successful transaction completion.
27. If any failure occurred before transaction commitment, the system shall preserve the original customer account without partial deletion.
28. The account deletion transaction shall either complete successfully or be fully rolled back.
29. All operations performed during the account deletion process shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, security, privacy, cybersecurity, audit, data retention, and applicable regulatory requirements.
30. The Account Deletion use case shall be considered successfully completed only after all mandatory post-deletion activities have finished successfully.

#### 3.1.15.11 Success Response

| Field | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Success | Indicates whether the account deletion operation completed successfully. |
| Status Code | HTTP status code returned by the system (e.g., 200 OK). |
| Response Code | Internal application response code indicating successful account deletion. |
| Message | Confirmation message indicating that the account has been permanently deleted or processed according to the applicable data retention policy. |
| Customer ID | Unique identifier of the customer whose account was deleted (included only where legally permissible). |
| Deletion Status | Indicates the final status of the account deletion process. |
| Deletion Timestamp | Date and time when the account deletion process was successfully completed. |
| Data Processing Status | Indicates whether customer data was permanently deleted, anonymized, or retained according to applicable legal and regulatory requirements. |
| Session Status | Indicates that all authenticated sessions have been invalidated. |
| Token Revocation Status | Indicates that authentication credentials have been revoked or invalidated. |
| Notification Status | Indicates whether account deletion confirmation notifications were successfully initiated. |
| Audit Status | Indicates whether the account deletion event was successfully recorded in the audit log. |
| Request ID | Unique identifier used for request tracing and operational support. |
| Timestamp | Date and time when the response was generated. |

**Example Success Response**

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "statusCode": 200,
  "responseCode": "ACCOUNT_DELETED_SUCCESSFULLY",
  "message": "Your account has been permanently deleted in accordance with the platform's data retention and regulatory policies.",
  "customerId": "CUS-102845",
  "deletionStatus": "COMPLETED",
  "deletionTimestamp": "2026-07-18T16:35:42Z",
  "dataProcessingStatus": "DELETED_OR_ANONYMIZED_AS_PER_POLICY",
  "sessionStatus": "ALL_SESSIONS_INVALIDATED",
  "tokenRevocationStatus": "ALL_TOKENS_REVOKED",
  "notificationStatus": "CONFIRMATION_NOTIFICATION_SENT",
  "auditStatus": "AUDIT_LOGGED",
  "requestId": "REQ-AD-987654321",
  "timestamp": "2026-07-18T16:35:43Z"
}
```

---

#### 3.1.15.12 Failure Response

| Failure Scenario | Response Message |
|------------------|------------------|
| Authentication failed | Authentication required. |
| Invalid or expired access token | Invalid or expired authentication token. |
| Session expired | Session has expired. Please log in again. |
| Session revoked | Session is no longer valid. |
| Customer account not found | Customer account not found. |
| Unauthorized account deletion attempt | Access denied. |
| Invalid current password | Current password is incorrect. |
| Invalid OTP | Invalid verification code. |
| OTP expired | Verification code has expired. |
| Email verification failed | Email verification failed. |
| Mobile verification failed | Mobile verification failed. |
| Permanent deletion confirmation missing | Account deletion confirmation is required. |
| Acknowledgement missing | Acknowledgement is required before deleting the account. |
| Pending orders exist | Account cannot be deleted while active orders exist. |
| Pending payment exists | Pending payment transactions must be completed before account deletion. |
| Wallet restriction exists | Wallet balance or wallet-related restrictions must be resolved before account deletion. |
| Pending refund exists | Pending refunds must be completed before account deletion. |
| Unresolved customer dispute exists | Account cannot be deleted while unresolved cases exist. |
| Fraud investigation in progress | Account cannot be deleted at this time. |
| Legal or regulatory restriction | Account deletion is currently restricted. |
| Data retention restriction | Unable to process account deletion due to data retention requirements. |
| Mandatory information missing | Required information is missing. |
| Invalid request data | Invalid request. |
| Request integrity validation failed | Request validation failed. |
| Concurrent account operation detected | Another account operation is currently being processed. |
| Database transaction failure | Unable to delete account. Please try again later. |
| Dependent service unavailable | Service temporarily unavailable. Please try again later. |
| Internal server error | An unexpected error occurred while processing your request. |

**Example Failure Response**

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "statusCode": 409,
  "responseCode": "ACCOUNT_DELETION_NOT_ALLOWED",
  "message": "Account cannot be deleted while active orders exist.",
  "errorCode": "PENDING_ORDER_EXISTS",
  "requestId": "REQ-AD-987654321",
  "timestamp": "2026-07-18T16:40:15Z"
}
```

#### 3.1.15.13 Non-Functional Considerations

##### Performance

1. The account deletion request shall be accepted and acknowledged within the platform's configured response time under normal operating conditions.
2. Identity verification shall complete within the organization's acceptable authentication response time.
3. Validation of orders, payments, wallet, refunds, compliance, and other dependent services shall be optimized to minimize overall processing time.
4. The account deletion transaction shall execute efficiently without causing unnecessary database locking.
5. Large-scale account deletion requests shall not significantly impact overall platform performance.
6. The system shall support concurrent account deletion requests while maintaining data consistency.
7. Resource utilization during account deletion shall remain within acceptable operational thresholds.

---

##### Availability

1. The Account Deletion functionality shall be available according to the platform's service availability objectives.
2. Temporary unavailability of dependent services shall be handled gracefully according to the platform's resiliency policy.
3. Critical failures shall not leave customer accounts in an inconsistent state.
4. Planned maintenance activities shall follow the organization's operational procedures.

---

##### Scalability

1. The system shall support increasing numbers of account deletion requests without significant degradation in performance.
2. Backend services participating in the deletion workflow shall support horizontal and vertical scaling where applicable.
3. The architecture shall support future expansion of validation rules without major redesign.
4. The solution shall support enterprise-scale customer volumes.

---

##### Reliability

1. The account deletion process shall execute as a single atomic transaction.
2. Partial account deletion shall never occur.
3. All transaction failures shall result in a complete rollback.
4. System recovery procedures shall preserve data consistency after failures.
5. Retry mechanisms shall prevent duplicate account deletion operations.

---

##### Security

1. All account deletion requests shall be transmitted using secure HTTPS connections.
2. Authentication and authorization shall be enforced before processing any deletion request.
3. Identity verification shall comply with the organization's security policies.
4. Authentication credentials shall never be stored or logged in plain text.
5. Sensitive customer information shall remain encrypted during transmission and storage where applicable.
6. Authentication tokens shall be securely revoked after successful account deletion.
7. Security events shall be monitored for suspicious deletion activities.
8. The platform shall protect against unauthorized, automated, replay, brute-force, session hijacking, and request tampering attacks.

---

##### Privacy

1. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be processed according to applicable privacy regulations.
2. Customer information eligible for deletion shall be permanently deleted or irreversibly anonymized.
3. Only legally required information shall be retained after account deletion.
4. Customer data shall not be disclosed to unauthorized users during the deletion process.
5. Privacy controls shall comply with applicable organizational and regulatory requirements.

---

##### Audit & Compliance

1. Every account deletion request shall generate appropriate audit records.
2. Authentication failures, authorization failures, validation failures, successful deletions, and security events shall be logged where applicable.
3. Audit logs shall remain tamper-resistant.
4. Audit records shall be retained according to organizational retention policies.
5. The account deletion process shall comply with applicable legal, financial, taxation, cybersecurity, audit, privacy, and regulatory requirements.

---

##### Maintainability

1. Business rules shall be configurable where applicable.
2. Validation rules shall be maintainable without significant architectural changes.
3. Logging and monitoring configurations shall support operational troubleshooting.
4. The implementation shall follow the platform's coding standards and architectural guidelines.

---

##### Observability

1. Operational metrics shall be collected for account deletion requests.
2. Success rates and failure rates shall be monitored.
3. Authentication failures and authorization failures shall be monitored.
4. Processing latency shall be measured.
5. Security-related events shall be observable through centralized monitoring systems.

---

##### Data Integrity

1. Customer information shall remain consistent throughout the deletion transaction.
2. Referential integrity shall be maintained according to applicable retention policies.
3. Duplicate account deletion requests shall not create inconsistent system states.
4. Data integrity validations shall execute before transaction commitment.

---

##### Usability

1. The account deletion workflow shall clearly communicate the consequences of permanent deletion.
2. User-facing error messages shall be meaningful without exposing sensitive implementation details.
3. Customers shall receive clear confirmation before permanent deletion.
4. Confirmation notifications shall be understandable and actionable where applicable.

---

##### Disaster Recovery

1. Recovery procedures shall maintain transactional consistency.
2. Failed deletion operations shall not result in partial data removal.
3. Disaster recovery procedures shall comply with the organization's Recovery Time Objective (RTO) and Recovery Point Objective (RPO).
4. Business continuity procedures shall support secure recovery after major failures.

#### 3.1.15.14 Acceptance Criteria

| AC ID | Acceptance Criteria |
|-------|----------------------|
| AC-AD-001 | The system shall allow only authenticated customers to initiate the Account Deletion process. |
| AC-AD-002 | The system shall allow customers to permanently delete only their own account. |
| AC-AD-003 | The system shall verify customer authorization before processing the deletion request. |
| AC-AD-004 | The system shall verify that the customer account exists before initiating account deletion. |
| AC-AD-005 | The system shall require successful identity verification before permitting permanent account deletion. |
| AC-AD-006 | When required by the platform's security policy, the system shall validate the customer's current password. |
| AC-AD-007 | When OTP verification is required, the system shall validate the submitted verification code before continuing. |
| AC-AD-008 | The customer shall explicitly confirm the permanent account deletion request before processing begins. |
| AC-AD-009 | The customer shall acknowledge that account deletion is permanent and may be irreversible. |
| AC-AD-010 | The system shall reject account deletion requests submitted without mandatory confirmations. |
| AC-AD-011 | The system shall validate that no pending orders, active deliveries, or scheduled deliveries exist before permitting account deletion. |
| AC-AD-012 | The system shall validate that no pending payment transactions exist before permitting account deletion. |
| AC-AD-013 | The system shall validate that no pending wallet restrictions prevent account deletion according to the platform's financial policy. |
| AC-AD-014 | The system shall validate that no pending refund requests or unsettled financial transactions exist before permitting account deletion. |
| AC-AD-015 | The system shall validate that no unresolved customer disputes or support cases prevent account deletion. |
| AC-AD-016 | The system shall prevent account deletion when the account is under fraud investigation, administrative restriction, legal hold, regulatory restriction, or compliance review. |
| AC-AD-017 | The system shall evaluate applicable data retention requirements before permanently deleting customer information. |
| AC-AD-018 | The system shall permanently delete or irreversibly anonymize customer information that is eligible for deletion. |
| AC-AD-019 | The system shall retain only information that must legally or regulatorily be preserved. |
| AC-AD-020 | The account deletion operation shall execute as a single atomic transaction. |
| AC-AD-021 | If any processing step fails, the entire account deletion transaction shall be rolled back. |
| AC-AD-022 | After successful account deletion, all authenticated sessions shall be invalidated. |
| AC-AD-023 | After successful account deletion, all authentication credentials, access tokens, refresh tokens, remembered devices, and other security credentials shall be revoked or invalidated. |
| AC-AD-024 | The deleted customer account shall no longer be usable for authentication. |
| AC-AD-025 | Future login attempts using the deleted account shall be rejected. |
| AC-AD-026 | The system shall generate a successful confirmation response after account deletion completes. |
| AC-AD-027 | The system shall send account deletion confirmation notifications where applicable and legally permissible. |
| AC-AD-028 | The Audit Logging Service shall record successful account deletion events. |
| AC-AD-029 | Authentication failures, authorization failures, validation failures, and security-related events shall be logged where applicable. |
| AC-AD-030 | The Security Monitoring Service shall receive applicable account lifecycle and security events. |
| AC-AD-031 | Failure responses shall not expose authentication credentials, internal implementation details, infrastructure information, or sensitive customer data. |
| AC-AD-032 | All account deletion requests shall be processed only through secure HTTPS connections. |
| AC-AD-033 | Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be processed in accordance with applicable privacy regulations. |
| AC-AD-034 | The Account Deletion functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, cybersecurity, data retention, financial, legal, and regulatory requirements. |
| AC-AD-035 | The Account Deletion use case shall be considered complete only after all validations, deletion operations, session invalidation, credential revocation, audit logging, notification processing, and post-processing activities have completed successfully. |

### 3.1.16 Address Management

#### 3.1.16.1 Description

The **Address Management** functionality enables authenticated customers to securely manage their delivery addresses within the Food Delivery Platform. The module allows customers to create, view, update, organize, and delete delivery addresses associated with their customer account, ensuring accurate and efficient order delivery.

The Address Management module shall support maintaining multiple delivery addresses such as **Home**, **Work**, **Friends & Family**, **Hotel**, **Temporary**, or other custom address labels supported by the platform. Each address shall store comprehensive delivery information including recipient name, mobile number, address lines, locality, city, state, postal code, country, geographic coordinates, landmarks, delivery instructions, and other delivery-related information.

The system shall support multiple address capture methods including manual address entry, GPS-assisted location detection, interactive map selection, reverse geocoding, and address auto-completion where supported. The platform shall validate address information to improve delivery accuracy and minimize failed deliveries due to incomplete or invalid address details.

The Address Management functionality shall maintain additional address metadata including address labels, latitude, longitude, building name, apartment or unit number, floor number, entrance information, landmark details, accessibility instructions, and delivery notes to assist delivery partners during order fulfillment.

The system shall verify whether an address falls within the platform's supported delivery service area. Addresses outside the delivery zone may be stored for future use based on business policy but shall be clearly identified as currently unavailable for delivery.

Customers shall be allowed to maintain multiple saved addresses subject to configurable platform limits. The system shall prevent unnecessary duplicate address records and ensure address information remains accurate, complete, and consistent.

The Address Management module shall integrate with Order Management, Restaurant Discovery, Delivery Management, Delivery Partner Assignment, Distance Calculation, Delivery Charge Calculation, ETA Calculation, Promotions, Taxation, and other platform services that require customer delivery location information.

The Address Management functionality shall protect customer location data and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) by enforcing authentication, authorization, encryption, privacy controls, audit logging, and applicable security policies throughout the address lifecycle.

Every address creation, modification, deletion, validation failure, serviceability check, location update, and other address lifecycle event shall be recorded in the audit log where required by organizational policies and applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

The Address Management functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, privacy, security, geolocation, audit, data protection, cybersecurity, and applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.16.2 Actors

- Customer
- Authentication Service
- Authorization Service
- Customer Profile Service
- Address Management Service
- Address Validation Service
- Geolocation Service
- Maps Service
- Reverse Geocoding Service
- GPS Location Service
- Delivery Serviceability Service
- Restaurant Discovery Service
- Order Management Service
- Delivery Management Service
- Delivery Partner Assignment Service
- Distance Calculation Service
- Delivery Charge Calculation Service
- ETA Calculation Service
- Tax Calculation Service
- Promotion & Coupon Service
- Notification Service
- Audit Logging Service
- Security Monitoring Service
- Data Privacy & Compliance Service

#### 3.1.16.3 Preconditions

1. The customer account shall exist in the platform.
2. The customer shall be successfully authenticated before accessing the Address Management functionality.
3. The customer shall have an active authenticated session.
4. The customer shall be authorized to manage only the delivery addresses associated with their own customer account.
5. The Authentication Service shall be available.
6. The Authorization Service shall be available.
7. The Customer Profile Service shall be available.
8. The Address Management Service shall be available.
9. The Address Validation Service shall be available.
10. The Geolocation Service shall be available when location-based features are used.
11. The Maps Service shall be available when map-based address selection is supported.
12. The Reverse Geocoding Service shall be available when converting geographic coordinates into address information.
13. The GPS Location Service shall be available when automatic location detection is requested by the customer.
14. The Delivery Serviceability Service shall be available for validating delivery coverage where applicable.
15. The Restaurant Discovery Service shall be available when nearby restaurant availability depends on the selected address.
16. The Order Management Service shall be available for validating address usage during active orders where applicable.
17. The Delivery Management Service shall be available for delivery-related address validations where applicable.
18. The Distance Calculation Service shall be available where delivery distance calculations are required.
19. The Delivery Charge Calculation Service shall be available where delivery charges depend on the selected address.
20. The ETA Calculation Service shall be available where estimated delivery time depends on the delivery location.
21. The Tax Calculation Service shall be available where location-based taxation rules are applicable.
22. The Promotion & Coupon Service shall be available where promotions depend on the delivery location.
23. The Notification Service shall be available for sending address-related notifications where applicable.
24. The Audit Logging Service shall be available for recording address lifecycle events.
25. The Security Monitoring Service shall be available for monitoring address-related security activities.
26. The Data Privacy & Compliance Service shall be available for enforcing privacy and regulatory requirements.
27. The customer shall have the necessary permissions to create, view, update, or delete delivery addresses.
28. The address management request shall be initiated through a secure HTTPS connection.
29. The system shall be capable of validating mandatory address information before processing the request.
30. The platform shall be capable of validating delivery serviceability for the specified location where applicable.
31. All dependent backend services participating in the Address Management workflow shall be operational.
32. The platform shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, security, geolocation, audit, data protection, cybersecurity, and regulatory requirements before processing any address management request.

#### 3.1.16.4 Trigger

The **Address Management** process is initiated when an authenticated customer navigates to the **My Addresses**, **Manage Addresses**, **Profile**, **Checkout**, or any other address management section of the application and performs one of the supported address management operations, including viewing saved addresses, adding a new address, updating an existing address, deleting an address, validating a delivery location, selecting an address during checkout, or managing delivery address information.

---

#### 3.1.16.5 Input Fields

| Field Name | Data Type | Mandatory | Description | Validation |
|------------|-----------|-----------|-------------|------------|
| Customer ID | UUID | System Generated | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. | Shall belong to the authenticated customer account. |
| Address ID | UUID | Conditional | Unique identifier of the delivery address. Required for update, delete, or view operations. | Shall exist and belong to the authenticated customer. |
| Recipient Name | String | Yes | Name of the person receiving the order. | Shall not be empty and shall be within the configured character limit. |
| Mobile Number | String | Yes | Contact number of the recipient. | Shall be a valid mobile number as per platform rules. |
| Address Label | Enum / String | Yes | User-friendly address label (e.g., Home, Work, Hotel, Friends & Family, Other). | Shall match supported values or configured length limits. |
| Address Line 1 | String | Yes | Primary street or building address. | Shall not be empty. |
| Address Line 2 | String | Optional | Additional address information. | Shall comply with configured length limits. |
| Landmark | String | Optional | Nearby landmark for easier delivery. | Shall comply with configured length limits. |
| Building Name | String | Optional | Building or apartment name. | Shall comply with configured length limits. |
| Floor Number | String | Optional | Floor number of the building. | Shall comply with configured length limits. |
| Unit / Apartment Number | String | Optional | Apartment, suite, or unit number. | Shall comply with configured length limits. |
| Area / Locality | String | Yes | Area or locality of the delivery address. | Shall not be empty. |
| City | String | Yes | City name. | Shall not be empty. |
| State / Province | String | Yes | State or province name. | Shall not be empty. |
| Postal Code | String | Yes | ZIP or Postal Code. | Shall conform to supported postal code format. |
| Country | String | Yes | Country name. | Shall be supported by the platform. |
| Latitude | Decimal | Conditional | Latitude coordinate of the delivery location. | Shall be within valid geographic range. |
| Longitude | Decimal | Conditional | Longitude coordinate of the delivery location. | Shall be within valid geographic range. |
| Delivery Instructions | String | Optional | Special instructions for delivery partners. | Shall comply with configured length limits. |
| Access Token | String | Yes | Authentication token. | Shall be valid and active. |
| Session ID | String | Yes | Active authenticated session identifier. | Shall be valid and not expired. |
| Device Information | Object | Optional | Information about the requesting device. | Shall comply with platform security policies. |
| IP Address | String | System Generated | IP address of the requesting client. | Shall be captured for audit purposes. |
| User Agent | String | System Generated | Browser or application information. | Shall be captured for audit purposes. |

**Notes:**

- **Customer ID** shall be derived from the authenticated session and shall not be editable.
- **Address ID** shall be mandatory only for update, delete, or retrieval of a specific address.
- **Recipient Name** should accurately identify the person receiving the order.
- **Mobile Number** should be reachable by the delivery partner if required.
- **Latitude** and **Longitude** shall be mandatory when map-based location selection or GPS-based address capture is used.
- The **Address Label** shall help customers quickly identify frequently used delivery locations.
- **Delivery Instructions** may include gate numbers, security instructions, apartment directions, or other delivery-related information.
- **Device Information**, **IP Address**, and **User Agent** may be recorded for security monitoring, fraud detection, operational analytics, and audit purposes.
- Sensitive authentication credentials shall never be stored, logged, or transmitted in plain text.
- All address information shall be transmitted only through secure HTTPS connections.
- Personally Identifiable Information (PII), location data, and customer address information shall be processed in accordance with the platform's privacy, security, audit, data protection, geolocation, and applicable regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.16.6 Business Rules

1. Only authenticated customers shall be permitted to access the Address Management functionality.
2. Customers shall be permitted to create, view, update, and delete only the delivery addresses associated with their own customer account.
3. The system shall validate customer authentication and authorization before processing any address management request.
4. Every delivery address shall be uniquely associated with a single customer account.
5. A customer may maintain multiple delivery addresses subject to the platform's configurable maximum address limit.
6. Each delivery address shall contain all mandatory address information before it can be saved.
7. Mandatory address information shall include recipient name, mobile number, address line, locality, city, state, postal code, country, and other mandatory fields configured by the platform.
8. Optional address information such as landmark, building name, floor number, apartment number, delivery instructions, and accessibility details may be stored when provided by the customer.
9. The system shall support predefined address labels such as **Home**, **Work**, **Hotel**, **Friends & Family**, **Temporary**, **Other**, and additional labels configured by the platform.
10. Customers shall be permitted to create custom address labels where supported by business policy.
11. Address labels shall not affect delivery eligibility or pricing.
12. The system shall validate all submitted address information before storing it.
13. The system shall sanitize all user-provided input to prevent invalid or malicious data.
14. Address information shall comply with the platform's supported address format and validation rules.
15. Postal codes shall be validated according to the supported country or region.
16. Mobile numbers shall comply with the platform's supported mobile number format.
17. Geographic coordinates shall be validated whenever latitude and longitude are provided.
18. Latitude and longitude shall correspond to a valid geographic location.
19. When map-based address selection is used, the system shall associate the selected coordinates with the stored address.
20. When GPS-based location detection is used, customer permission shall be obtained before accessing device location information.
21. Reverse geocoding may be used to convert geographic coordinates into a human-readable address.
22. The system shall verify delivery serviceability for the specified address where applicable.
23. Addresses outside the supported delivery area may be stored according to business policy but shall be clearly identified as unavailable for delivery.
24. Restaurant availability, delivery charges, taxes, promotions, and estimated delivery time may vary depending on the selected delivery address.
25. The system shall prevent duplicate address records according to the configured duplicate detection policy.
26. Customers shall not be permitted to modify or delete delivery addresses currently restricted by active business operations where applicable.
27. Address deletion shall not affect completed orders that reference the deleted address.
28. Historical orders shall retain the delivery address information associated with the order at the time it was placed.
29. Updating an address shall not modify address information stored in previously completed orders.
30. Address modifications shall apply only to future orders.
31. The system shall protect customer location information and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) throughout the address lifecycle.
32. Customer address information shall be processed in accordance with applicable privacy, security, geolocation, audit, data protection, and regulatory requirements.
33. Sensitive customer information shall never be exposed to unauthorized users.
34. Every address creation, update, deletion, validation failure, serviceability check, and significant address lifecycle event shall be recorded in the audit log where applicable.
35. Security monitoring systems shall receive address-related security events according to organizational policy.
36. Failure responses shall not expose authentication credentials, internal implementation details, service architecture, or sensitive customer information.
37. Concurrent address modification requests shall be handled according to the platform's concurrency control policy.
38. All address management operations shall be performed over secure HTTPS connections.
39. The Address Management functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, privacy, security, geolocation, audit, cybersecurity, data protection, and applicable legal and regulatory requirements.
40. The platform shall ensure data consistency across all services consuming customer delivery address information.

#### 3.1.16.7 Validations

| Validation | Rule | Failure Response |
|------------|------|------------------|
| Authentication Validation | The customer shall be successfully authenticated before accessing the Address Management functionality. | "Authentication required." |
| Access Token Validation | The Access Token shall be valid, active, and digitally verifiable. | "Invalid or expired authentication token." |
| Session Validation | An active authenticated session shall exist. | "Session has expired. Please log in again." |
| Session Expiry Validation | The authenticated session shall not have expired. | "Session has expired." |
| Session Revocation Validation | The authenticated session shall not have been revoked or terminated. | "Session is no longer valid." |
| Customer Identification Validation | The authenticated customer shall be uniquely identified from the active session. | "Unable to identify customer." |
| Customer Account Validation | The customer account shall exist. | "Customer account not found." |
| Authorization Validation | The authenticated customer shall be authorized to manage delivery addresses. | "Access denied." |
| Address Ownership Validation | The specified Address ID shall belong to the authenticated customer. | "Address not found or access denied." |
| Address Limit Validation | The customer shall not exceed the maximum number of saved addresses configured by the platform. | "Maximum address limit reached." |
| Recipient Name Validation | Recipient Name shall be provided and comply with configured length restrictions. | "Invalid recipient name." |
| Mobile Number Validation | Mobile Number shall be present and conform to the supported mobile number format. | "Invalid mobile number." |
| Address Label Validation | Address Label shall match supported labels or configured custom label rules. | "Invalid address label." |
| Address Line 1 Validation | Address Line 1 shall be provided. | "Address Line 1 is required." |
| Area / Locality Validation | Area or Locality shall be provided. | "Area or locality is required." |
| City Validation | City shall be provided. | "City is required." |
| State Validation | State or Province shall be provided. | "State is required." |
| Postal Code Validation | Postal Code shall conform to the supported postal code format. | "Invalid postal code." |
| Country Validation | Country shall be supported by the platform. | "Unsupported country." |
| Latitude Validation | When latitude is provided, it shall be within the valid geographic range (-90 to +90). | "Invalid latitude." |
| Longitude Validation | When longitude is provided, it shall be within the valid geographic range (-180 to +180). | "Invalid longitude." |
| Geographic Coordinate Validation | Latitude and Longitude shall represent a valid geographic location. | "Invalid location coordinates." |
| GPS Permission Validation | Device location permission shall be granted before GPS-based location detection is performed. | "Location permission denied." |
| Map Location Validation | Selected map location shall represent a valid delivery location. | "Invalid map location selected." |
| Reverse Geocoding Validation | Reverse geocoding shall successfully resolve the provided coordinates when applicable. | "Unable to identify address for the selected location." |
| Address Validation | Submitted address information shall satisfy the platform's address validation rules. | "Invalid address information." |
| Duplicate Address Validation | Duplicate delivery addresses shall not be stored according to the configured duplicate detection policy. | "This address already exists." |
| Delivery Serviceability Validation | Delivery serviceability shall be validated for the specified address where applicable. | "Delivery is currently unavailable for this location." |
| Input Validation | All mandatory fields shall be provided. | "Required information is missing." |
| Input Sanitization Validation | All submitted input shall be validated and sanitized before processing. | "Invalid request." |
| HTTPS Validation | Address management requests shall be accepted only through secure HTTPS connections. | Request shall be rejected. |
| Request Integrity Validation | The request shall not be modified or tampered with during transmission. | "Request validation failed." |
| Concurrent Modification Validation | Concurrent address modification requests shall be processed according to the configured concurrency policy. | "Another address operation is currently being processed." |
| Audit Validation | Address creation, update, deletion, validation failures, and security events shall be recorded in the audit log where applicable. | Audit logging failure shall be handled according to operational policy. |

#### 3.1.16.8 Main Flow

1. The customer navigates to the **My Addresses**, **Manage Addresses**, **Profile**, **Checkout**, or another address management section of the application.
2. The customer selects one of the supported address management operations.
3. The system verifies that the customer is authenticated.
4. The Authentication Service validates the Access Token.
5. The Session Management component validates the active authenticated session.
6. The Authorization Service verifies that the customer is authorized to manage delivery addresses.
7. The system identifies the authenticated customer.
8. The system retrieves the customer profile.
9. The Address Management Service retrieves all delivery addresses associated with the authenticated customer.
10. The system displays the customer's saved delivery addresses.
11. The customer selects **Add New Address**.
12. The system displays the address creation form.
13. The customer enters the recipient name.
14. The customer enters the recipient mobile number.
15. The customer selects or enters an address label.
16. The customer enters Address Line 1.
17. The customer optionally enters Address Line 2.
18. The customer optionally enters the building name.
19. The customer optionally enters the apartment or unit number.
20. The customer optionally enters the floor number.
21. The customer enters the area or locality.
22. The customer enters the city.
23. The customer enters the state or province.
24. The customer enters the postal code.
25. The customer selects the country.
26. The customer optionally enters landmark information.
27. The customer optionally enters delivery instructions.
28. The customer may choose to use the device's current GPS location.
29. If GPS location is selected, the system requests location permission from the customer.
30. Upon approval, the GPS Location Service retrieves the customer's geographic coordinates.
31. The customer may alternatively select the delivery location using the interactive map.
32. The Maps Service captures the selected latitude and longitude.
33. The Reverse Geocoding Service converts the geographic coordinates into a readable address where applicable.
34. The customer reviews the populated address information.
35. The customer submits the address details.
36. The system validates all mandatory fields.
