6. The system shall support searching by configurable address attributes including, but not limited to, address label, recipient name, mobile number, locality, landmark, street, area, city, state, postal code, and country.
7. The system shall support partial keyword matching according to the configured search strategy.
8. The system shall support case-insensitive searches.
9. The system shall normalize leading, trailing, and consecutive whitespace before processing search requests.
10. The system may support fuzzy matching according to platform configuration.
11. The system shall validate all search input before processing the request.
12. The system shall sanitize all user-provided input to prevent malicious or invalid search queries.
13. Empty search keywords may return all saved delivery addresses according to platform configuration.
14. Search results shall include only active and accessible customer addresses unless otherwise permitted by business rules.
15. Search results may prioritize the customer's default delivery address according to the configured ranking policy.
16. Search results may be ordered using configurable sorting options including relevance, recency, address label, or other business-defined criteria.
17. Pagination shall be applied when the number of matching addresses exceeds the configured page size.
18. The system shall ensure consistent search results throughout a single request lifecycle.
19. Address Search operations shall not update default address configuration, customer profile information, or historical order data.
20. Search operations shall not trigger delivery partner assignment, order creation, or payment processing.
21. Search requests initiated during checkout may be followed by delivery serviceability, delivery charge, ETA, tax, and restaurant availability calculations after address selection.
22. Customer address information and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be protected throughout the search process.
23. Customer address information shall be processed in accordance with applicable privacy, security, accessibility, audit, geolocation, data protection, and regulatory requirements.
24. Sensitive authentication credentials shall never be stored, logged, cached, or exposed in plain text.
25. Every applicable search request, validation failure, authorization failure, abnormal search activity, security event, and other significant operational event shall be recorded in the audit log where applicable.
26. Security monitoring systems shall receive applicable search-related events according to organizational policy.
27. Failure responses shall not expose authentication credentials, internal implementation details, infrastructure information, search index implementation, or sensitive customer information.
28. Concurrent address search requests from the same or different customers shall be processed independently without affecting search accuracy or platform stability.
29. All Address Search requests shall be processed only through secure HTTPS connections.
30. The Address Search functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, privacy, security, accessibility, audit, cybersecurity, geolocation, data protection, and applicable legal and regulatory requirements.
31. Search results shall reflect the most recent committed customer address information available at the time of request processing.
32. Platform administrators may configure searchable fields, ranking policies, pagination limits, and search behavior without requiring application code changes where supported.
33. The system shall maintain logical consistency between search results and the customer's current saved address records.
34. System-generated search requests shall follow the same validation, authorization, audit, and security controls as customer-initiated requests where applicable.
35. Search processing shall be optimized to provide near real-time response times under normal operating conditions.

#### 3.1.18.7 Validations

| Validation | Rule | Failure Response |
|------------|------|------------------|
| Authentication Validation | The customer shall be successfully authenticated before accessing the Address Search functionality. | "Authentication required." |
| Access Token Validation | The Access Token shall be valid, active, and digitally verifiable. | "Invalid or expired authentication token." |
| Session Validation | An active authenticated session shall exist. | "Session has expired. Please log in again." |
| Session Expiry Validation | The authenticated session shall not have expired. | "Session has expired." |
| Session Revocation Validation | The authenticated session shall not have been revoked or terminated. | "Session is no longer valid." |
| Customer Identification Validation | The authenticated customer shall be uniquely identified from the active session. | "Unable to identify customer." |
| Customer Account Validation | The customer account shall exist. | "Customer account not found." |
| Authorization Validation | The authenticated customer shall be authorized to search delivery addresses associated with their account. | "Access denied." |
| Search Keyword Validation | The search keyword, if provided, shall comply with configured length and format requirements. | "Invalid search keyword." |
| Input Validation | All mandatory request fields shall be provided. | "Required information is missing." |
| Input Sanitization Validation | All submitted input shall be validated and sanitized before processing. | "Invalid search request." |
| Search Filter Validation | Only supported search filters shall be accepted. | "Invalid search filter." |
| Sort Option Validation | The selected sorting option shall be supported by the platform. | "Invalid sorting option." |
| Pagination Validation | Page Number and Page Size shall comply with configured pagination limits. | "Invalid pagination parameters." |
| Customer Ownership Validation | Search results shall contain only addresses belonging to the authenticated customer. | "Access denied." |
| Search Result Visibility Validation | Delivery addresses belonging to other customers shall never appear in search results. | Search results shall exclude unauthorized records. |
| Search Index Validation | Where search indexing is enabled, the search index shall be available and synchronized. | "Search service is temporarily unavailable." |
| HTTPS Validation | Address Search requests shall be accepted only through secure HTTPS connections. | Request shall be rejected. |
| Request Integrity Validation | The request shall not be modified or tampered with during transmission. | "Request validation failed." |
| Service Availability Validation | Required backend services shall be available to process the search request. | "Service temporarily unavailable." |
| Concurrent Request Validation | Concurrent address search requests shall not affect search accuracy or result consistency. | "Unable to process the search request at this time." |
| Audit Validation | Applicable address search events, validation failures, and security events shall be recorded in the audit log where required. | Audit logging failure shall be handled according to operational policy. |
| Security Monitoring Validation | Suspicious or abnormal search activities shall be forwarded to security monitoring systems where applicable. | Security event shall be recorded according to organizational policy. |
| Data Privacy Validation | Customer address information shall be processed in accordance with applicable privacy and data protection requirements. | Request shall be rejected if privacy requirements cannot be satisfied. |

#### 3.1.18.8 Main Flow

1. The customer navigates to the **My Addresses**, **Manage Addresses**, **Checkout**, **Profile**, or another address selection interface within the Food Delivery Platform.
2. The system verifies that the customer is authenticated and has an active session.
3. The system validates the customer's authorization to access saved delivery addresses.
4. The system retrieves the customer's address search interface.
5. The customer enters a search keyword, phrase, or applies one or more supported search filters.
6. The system receives the address search request.
7. The system validates the integrity of the request.
8. The system validates the Access Token and active session.
9. The system identifies the authenticated customer.
10. The system validates the submitted search keyword and optional search filters.
11. The system sanitizes all user-provided input.
12. The system constructs the search query according to the configured search strategy.
13. The system searches only the delivery addresses associated with the authenticated customer.
14. The system applies supported search behaviors such as partial matching, case-insensitive comparison, whitespace normalization, and configured fuzzy matching where applicable.
15. The system excludes all delivery addresses that do not belong to the authenticated customer.
16. The system applies configured business rules and visibility constraints.
17. The system ranks matching search results according to the configured relevance policy.
18. The system applies sorting and pagination where applicable.
19. The system identifies the customer's default delivery address within the search results, if available.
20. The system returns the matching delivery addresses to the user interface.
21. The user interface displays the search results along with relevant address information and indicators such as the default delivery address.
22. The customer may select one of the returned addresses for subsequent platform operations such as checkout or address management.
23. If an address is selected, dependent platform services may retrieve delivery serviceability, restaurant availability, delivery charges, estimated delivery time, taxation, and other location-dependent information where applicable.
24. The system records applicable search events in the audit log.
25. The system records applicable operational and security monitoring events.
26. The system returns a successful response containing the search results.
27. The Address Search operation completes without modifying any customer address records.

#### 3.1.18.9 Alternate Flows

**Alternate Flow 1: Empty Search Keyword**

1. The customer opens the Address Search interface without entering a search keyword.
2. The system validates the request.
3. The system retrieves all saved delivery addresses associated with the authenticated customer according to the configured pagination policy.
4. The system displays the complete address list.

---

**Alternate Flow 2: No Matching Addresses Found**

1. The customer enters a search keyword or filter.
2. The system processes the search request.
3. No matching delivery addresses are found.
4. The system returns an empty result set.
5. The customer is informed that no matching addresses were found.
6. The customer may modify the search criteria or create a new delivery address.

---

**Alternate Flow 3: Search by Address Label**

1. The customer searches using an address label such as **Home**, **Office**, or **Other**.
2. The system searches the authenticated customer's saved address labels.
3. Matching addresses are returned and displayed.

---

**Alternate Flow 4: Search Using Multiple Filters**

1. The customer specifies one or more supported search filters in addition to a search keyword.
2. The system validates all supplied filters.
3. The system applies the configured filtering rules.
4. Only addresses satisfying all applicable search criteria are returned.

---

**Alternate Flow 5: Checkout Address Search**

1. The customer searches for a saved delivery address during checkout.
2. The system retrieves matching addresses.
3. The customer selects one of the returned addresses.
4. Checkout continues using the selected delivery address.
5. Dependent services perform delivery serviceability, ETA, delivery charge, tax, and restaurant availability calculations where applicable.

---

**Alternate Flow 6: Pagination Required**

1. The search returns more results than the configured page size.
2. The system applies pagination.
3. The first page of search results is displayed.
4. The customer may request additional pages.

---

**Alternate Flow 7: Search Index Unavailable**

1. The customer submits a search request.
2. The search indexing service is temporarily unavailable.
3. The platform applies the configured fallback search mechanism where supported.
4. If no fallback mechanism is available, an appropriate failure response is returned.

---

**Alternate Flow 8: Concurrent Search Requests**

1. Multiple address search requests are initiated simultaneously.
2. The system processes each request independently.
3. Search operations do not affect one another.
4. Each request receives its own search results.

---

**Alternate Flow 9: Customer Selects Default Address**

1. The customer searches for a delivery address.
2. The search results include the customer's default delivery address.
3. The customer selects the default address.
4. The platform proceeds with subsequent operations using the selected address.

---

**Alternate Flow 10: Search Request Cancelled**

1. The customer cancels the search before processing completes.
2. The system terminates the active search request.
3. No customer data is modified.
4. The Address Search operation ends without further processing.

#### 3.1.18.10 Postconditions

1. The address search request shall be successfully processed after all applicable validations are completed.
2. The system shall return only delivery addresses that belong to the authenticated customer.
3. No delivery addresses belonging to other customers shall be included in the search results.
4. The search results shall reflect the most recent committed customer address information available at the time of processing.
5. Matching delivery addresses shall be presented according to the configured search ranking, sorting, and pagination policies.
6. The customer's default delivery address, if applicable, shall be clearly identified within the search results.
7. The Address Search operation shall not modify any customer address records.
8. The Address Search operation shall not create, update, delete, or deactivate any delivery address.
9. The customer's default address configuration shall remain unchanged unless explicitly modified through the Default Address Management functionality.
10. Historical, completed, cancelled, and archived orders shall remain unaffected by the Address Search operation.
11. Customer profile information and delivery preferences shall remain unchanged.
12. The integrity and ownership of customer address data shall remain preserved.
13. All applicable authentication, authorization, validation, and business rule checks shall have been successfully completed.
14. Applicable search requests, validation failures, authorization failures, and security events shall be recorded in the audit log in accordance with organizational policies.
15. Applicable operational and security monitoring events shall be generated for observability and incident monitoring.
16. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and customer address information shall remain protected in accordance with applicable privacy, security, accessibility, and data protection requirements.
17. Authentication credentials, session identifiers, access tokens, and other sensitive security information shall never be exposed in responses, logs, or audit records.
18. If the search request fails, no customer data shall be modified and no partial processing state shall remain.
19. The platform shall remain in a consistent state regardless of whether matching search results are found.
20. The Address Search functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, cybersecurity, geolocation, accessibility, data protection, and applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.18.11 Success Response

Upon successful completion of the Address Search operation, the system shall return a success response containing the list of delivery addresses that match the customer's search criteria. The response shall include only delivery addresses owned by the authenticated customer and shall not expose any unauthorized customer information.

**Response Attributes:**

| Field | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Status | Success |
| HTTP Status Code | 200 OK |
| Response Code | ADDRESS_SEARCH_SUCCESS |
| Message | Address search completed successfully. |
| Customer ID | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. |
| Search Keyword | Search keyword submitted by the customer, if applicable. |
| Applied Filters | List of filters applied during the search, if any. |
| Total Results | Total number of matching delivery addresses. |
| Page Number | Current page number of the returned results. |
| Page Size | Number of records returned in the current page. |
| Total Pages | Total number of available result pages. |
| Addresses | Collection of matching delivery addresses. |
| Request ID | Unique identifier of the processed request. |
| Search Timestamp | Date and time when the search was completed. |
| Audit Status | Indicates whether the applicable audit log entry was successfully created. |

**Example Response**

```json
{
  "status": "SUCCESS",
  "statusCode": 200,
  "responseCode": "ADDRESS_SEARCH_SUCCESS",
  "message": "Address search completed successfully.",
  "customerId": "CUS-102458",
  "searchKeyword": "Home",
  "appliedFilters": {
    "city": "Bengaluru"
  },
  "totalResults": 2,
  "pageNumber": 1,
  "pageSize": 10,
  "totalPages": 1,
  "addresses": [
    {
      "addressId": "ADDR-845217",
      "label": "Home",
      "recipientName": "Sathish Kumar",
      "city": "Bengaluru",
      "postalCode": "560094",
      "isDefault": true
    },
    {
      "addressId": "ADDR-845654",
      "label": "Home 2",
      "recipientName": "Sathish Kumar",
      "city": "Bengaluru",
      "postalCode": "560092",
      "isDefault": false
    }
  ],
  "requestId": "REQ-202601180021",
  "searchTimestamp": "2026-01-18T11:42:15Z",
  "auditStatus": "RECORDED"
}
```

**Success Conditions**

- The search request has been successfully authenticated and authorized.
- All applicable validations and business rules have passed.
- Only delivery addresses belonging to the authenticated customer are included in the response.
- Search results have been generated using the configured search strategy.
- Search results have been ranked, filtered, sorted, and paginated according to platform configuration.
- The customer's default delivery address, where applicable, is clearly identified within the returned results.
- The Address Search operation has not modified any customer address records.
- Applicable audit and operational events have been recorded according to platform policy.
- No sensitive customer information, authentication credentials, session identifiers, access tokens, or internal implementation details are exposed in the response.

#### 3.1.18.12 Failure Response

The system shall return an appropriate failure response whenever the Address Search request cannot be completed due to authentication failures, authorization failures, validation errors, invalid search criteria, service unavailability, security violations, or unexpected processing errors.

**Response Attributes:**

| Field | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Status | Failure |
| HTTP Status Code | Appropriate HTTP status code based on the error. |
| Response Code | Unique application-specific error code. |
| Error Message | Human-readable description of the failure. |
| Error Category | Classification of the error (Authentication, Authorization, Validation, Business Rule, Security, System, etc.). |
| Customer ID | Authenticated customer identifier where applicable. |
| Search Keyword | Search keyword submitted in the failed request, where applicable. |
| Request ID | Unique identifier of the failed request. |
| Timestamp | Date and time when the failure occurred. |
| Retry Allowed | Indicates whether the customer may retry the request. |
| Audit Status | Indicates whether the failure event was recorded in the audit log. |

**Common Failure Scenarios**

| Scenario | HTTP Status | Response Code | Message |
|----------|-------------|---------------|---------|
| Customer not authenticated | 401 Unauthorized | AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED | Authentication required. |
| Invalid or expired access token | 401 Unauthorized | INVALID_ACCESS_TOKEN | Invalid or expired authentication token. |
| Session expired | 401 Unauthorized | SESSION_EXPIRED | Your session has expired. Please log in again. |
| Customer account not found | 404 Not Found | CUSTOMER_NOT_FOUND | Customer account not found. |
| Unauthorized access | 403 Forbidden | ACCESS_DENIED | You are not authorized to search delivery addresses. |
| Invalid search keyword | 400 Bad Request | INVALID_SEARCH_KEYWORD | Invalid search keyword. |
| Invalid search filter | 400 Bad Request | INVALID_SEARCH_FILTER | One or more search filters are invalid. |
| Invalid sorting option | 400 Bad Request | INVALID_SORT_OPTION | Unsupported sorting option. |
| Invalid pagination parameters | 400 Bad Request | INVALID_PAGINATION | Invalid pagination parameters. |
| Search service unavailable | 503 Service Unavailable | SEARCH_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE | Address search service is temporarily unavailable. |
| Search index unavailable | 503 Service Unavailable | SEARCH_INDEX_UNAVAILABLE | Search index is temporarily unavailable. |
| Request validation failed | 400 Bad Request | INVALID_REQUEST | Invalid search request. |
| Request integrity validation failed | 400 Bad Request | REQUEST_VALIDATION_FAILED | Request validation failed. |
| Dependent service unavailable | 503 Service Unavailable | DEPENDENT_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE | Required platform service is temporarily unavailable. |
| Rate limit exceeded | 429 Too Many Requests | SEARCH_RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED | Too many search requests. Please try again later. |
| Internal server error | 500 Internal Server Error | INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR | An unexpected error occurred while processing the search request. |

**Example Failure Response**

```json
{
  "status": "FAILURE",
  "statusCode": 400,
  "responseCode": "INVALID_SEARCH_FILTER",
  "errorCategory": "VALIDATION",
  "message": "One or more search filters are invalid.",
  "customerId": "CUS-102458",
  "searchKeyword": "Home",
  "requestId": "REQ-202601180031",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-18T11:58:42Z",
  "retryAllowed": true,
  "auditStatus": "RECORDED"
}
```

**Failure Handling Rules**

- The system shall immediately terminate processing when a critical validation, authentication, or authorization check fails.
- No customer address information belonging to unauthorized customers shall be disclosed under any failure scenario.
- The Address Search operation shall not create, modify, update, or delete any customer address records when processing fails.
- Sensitive information including authentication credentials, access tokens, session identifiers, internal implementation details, database structures, search index implementation details, infrastructure information, and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall never be exposed in failure responses.
- Applicable failure events shall be recorded in audit logs according to organizational security and compliance policies.
- Security-related failures shall be forwarded to security monitoring systems where applicable.
- Failure responses shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, cybersecurity, geolocation, accessibility, data protection, and applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.18.13 Non-Functional Considerations

##### Performance

1. The system shall process address search requests within **2 seconds** under normal operating conditions.
2. The system shall support real-time search with minimal latency while customers enter search keywords.
3. The platform shall efficiently search large address datasets without noticeable degradation in response time.
4. Search queries shall be optimized using appropriate indexing, caching, and query optimization techniques where applicable.
5. The system shall support configurable pagination to efficiently handle large search result sets.
6. The platform shall maintain acceptable response times during peak traffic periods.

##### Availability

7. The Address Search functionality shall maintain a minimum availability of **99.9%**, excluding scheduled maintenance.
8. The platform shall gracefully handle temporary failures of dependent services.
9. Search functionality shall automatically recover after transient infrastructure failures where supported.

##### Scalability

10. The system shall support millions of customer accounts and delivery addresses without requiring architectural redesign.
11. The Address Search service shall scale horizontally to accommodate increasing search traffic.
12. The platform shall support future expansion to multiple regions, cities, states, and countries.

##### Reliability

13. Search results shall accurately reflect the latest committed customer address data available at the time of request processing.
14. Concurrent search requests shall not affect search accuracy or platform stability.
15. Search failures shall not modify customer address records or other business data.
16. Temporary search service failures shall not compromise customer data integrity.

##### Security

17. All Address Search requests shall be transmitted exclusively over secure HTTPS connections.
18. Authentication and authorization shall be enforced before processing every search request.
19. Customer address information and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be encrypted during transmission and protected according to organizational security standards.
20. Sensitive credentials, authentication tokens, and session identifiers shall never be stored or logged in plain text.
21. The system shall protect against unauthorized access, injection attacks, cross-site scripting (XSS), cross-site request forgery (CSRF), parameter tampering, replay attacks, brute-force attacks, excessive search requests, and other common security threats.
22. Security events shall be logged and monitored according to organizational cybersecurity policies.

##### Privacy

23. Customer address information shall be processed in accordance with applicable privacy regulations and organizational data protection policies.
24. Search results shall contain only delivery addresses belonging to the authenticated customer.
25. Data collection, retention, archival, and deletion shall comply with applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

##### Audit & Compliance

26. Applicable successful search requests, validation failures, authorization failures, abnormal search activities, and security events shall be recorded in audit logs.
27. Audit records shall support operational monitoring, security investigations, compliance reporting, and forensic analysis.
28. Audit logs shall be protected against unauthorized modification or deletion.
29. The Address Search functionality shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, cybersecurity, accessibility, geolocation, data protection, and regulatory requirements.

##### Maintainability

30. The functionality shall be implemented using modular, loosely coupled components.
31. Search algorithms, searchable fields, ranking policies, pagination limits, and filtering rules shall be configurable wherever practical.
32. Future enhancements to search capabilities shall be supported without requiring major architectural changes.

##### Monitoring & Observability

33. The platform shall continuously monitor search latency, throughput, request volume, search success rate, error rate, and service availability.
34. Critical failures, abnormal search behavior, excessive request rates, and security incidents shall generate operational alerts.
35. Logs, metrics, and traces shall provide complete end-to-end observability of the Address Search workflow.

##### Backup & Recovery

36. Customer address data and search configuration metadata shall be included in scheduled backup procedures where applicable.
37. Recovery mechanisms shall preserve customer address integrity following infrastructure failures or disaster recovery events.
38. Backup and restoration procedures shall comply with the platform's business continuity and disaster recovery policies.

#### 3.1.18.14 Acceptance Criteria

The Address Search functionality shall be considered successfully implemented when all of the following acceptance criteria are satisfied:

##### Functional Acceptance Criteria

1. The system shall allow authenticated customers to search their saved delivery addresses using supported search criteria.
2. The system shall return only delivery addresses that belong to the authenticated customer.
3. The system shall support partial keyword matching according to the configured search strategy.
4. The system shall support case-insensitive searching.
5. The system shall normalize leading, trailing, and consecutive whitespace before processing search requests.
6. The system shall support configurable search filters such as address label, recipient name, locality, city, state, postal code, and other supported address attributes.
7. The system shall support configurable sorting of search results.
8. The system shall support pagination for large search result sets.
9. The system shall clearly identify the customer's default delivery address within the search results where applicable.
10. The system shall correctly return an empty result set when no matching addresses are found.
11. The system shall not modify customer address records during any search operation.
12. The system shall return standardized success responses for successful searches.
13. The system shall return standardized failure responses for all supported failure scenarios.
14. Search results shall accurately reflect the latest committed customer address information available at the time of processing.
15. The functionality shall support address selection for downstream workflows such as Checkout and Address Management.

##### Business Rule Acceptance Criteria

16. Customers shall only be able to search delivery addresses associated with their own customer accounts.
17. Delivery addresses belonging to other customers shall never appear in search results.
18. Search operations shall remain read-only under all circumstances.
19. Search requests shall comply with all configured business rules before results are returned.
20. Search ranking, filtering, sorting, and pagination shall follow the configured platform policies.

##### Security Acceptance Criteria

21. Only authenticated customers shall access the Address Search functionality.
22. Authorization shall be enforced before every search request.
23. All requests shall be processed exclusively over secure HTTPS connections.
24. Customer address information and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall remain protected throughout the search process.
25. Authentication credentials, session identifiers, access tokens, internal implementation details, and sensitive customer information shall never be exposed in responses, logs, or audit records.
26. Security-related events shall be logged and monitored according to organizational policies.

##### Performance Acceptance Criteria

27. The system shall complete address search requests within **2 seconds** under normal operating conditions.
28. The platform shall maintain acceptable response times under expected production workloads.
29. Concurrent search requests from multiple customers shall not impact search accuracy, response consistency, or platform stability.

##### Reliability Acceptance Criteria

30. Search results shall remain accurate and consistent throughout request processing.
31. Search operations shall never modify customer address data.
32. Temporary failures shall not compromise customer data integrity.
33. The platform shall maintain consistent behavior under concurrent search operations.

##### Audit & Compliance Acceptance Criteria

34. Applicable successful search requests, validation failures, authorization failures, abnormal search activities, and security events shall be recorded in audit logs where required.
35. Audit records shall support operational monitoring, security investigations, compliance reporting, and forensic analysis.
36. The Address Search functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, cybersecurity, accessibility, geolocation, data protection, and applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

##### Overall Acceptance Criteria

37. All functional, business, validation, security, performance, reliability, audit, and compliance requirements defined for the Address Search module shall successfully pass User Acceptance Testing (UAT), System Integration Testing (SIT), Functional Testing, Regression Testing, Security Testing, Performance Testing, and Accessibility Testing before production deployment.

### 3.1.19 Contact Preferences

#### 3.1.19.1 Description

The **Contact Preferences** functionality enables authenticated customers to configure and manage their preferred contact information used by the Food Delivery Platform for account-related communication, order updates, customer support, delivery coordination, marketing, security notifications, and other platform interactions.

The Contact Preferences module shall allow customers to specify and maintain their preferred contact details, including primary mobile number, secondary mobile number (where supported), primary email address, alternate email address (where supported), and other customer contact channels as configured by the platform.

The system shall ensure that all customer contact information is uniquely associated with the authenticated customer account and shall validate ownership, authenticity, and eligibility before accepting any contact preference changes.

The Contact Preferences functionality shall allow customers to designate preferred communication channels for different categories of platform interactions. The platform may prioritize one or more verified contact methods based on business policies and customer preferences.

The system shall ensure that changes to contact preferences affect only future communications and shall not modify historical communication records, completed orders, invoices, audit logs, or other immutable business records.

The Contact Preferences functionality shall integrate with Authentication, Customer Profile Management, Order Management, Delivery Management, Customer Support, Notification Services, Marketing Services, Security Services, Two-Factor Authentication (2FA), Account Recovery, Communication Preferences, Privacy Settings, and other platform components requiring verified customer contact information.

The system shall verify customer ownership of newly added or modified contact information using configurable verification mechanisms such as One-Time Passwords (OTP), verification links, confirmation codes, or other approved verification methods before activating updated contact details.

If verification is not successfully completed within the configured validity period, the new contact information shall remain inactive and the previously verified contact information shall continue to be used according to platform policy.

The Contact Preferences functionality shall protect customer contact information and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) by enforcing authentication, authorization, encryption, privacy controls, audit logging, security monitoring, and applicable cybersecurity policies throughout the contact preference lifecycle.

Every contact preference creation, update, verification attempt, verification failure, removal, security event, and other significant lifecycle activity shall be recorded in the audit log where required by organizational policies and applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

The Contact Preferences functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, cybersecurity, data protection, communication, accessibility, and applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.19.2 Actors

- Customer
- Authentication Service
- Authorization Service
- Customer Profile Service
- Contact Preferences Service
- Mobile Verification Service
- Email Verification Service
- OTP Service
- Notification Service
- Communication Service
- Customer Support Service
- Order Management Service
- Delivery Management Service
- Marketing Service
- Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Service
- Account Recovery Service
- Privacy Settings Service
- Audit Logging Service
- Security Monitoring Service
- Data Privacy & Compliance Service

#### 3.1.19.3 Preconditions

1. The customer account shall exist in the platform.
2. The customer shall be successfully authenticated before accessing the Contact Preferences functionality.
3. The customer shall have an active authenticated session.
4. The customer shall be authorized to manage only the contact preferences associated with their own customer account.
5. The Authentication Service shall be available.
6. The Authorization Service shall be available.
7. The Customer Profile Service shall be available.
8. The Contact Preferences Service shall be available.
9. The Mobile Verification Service shall be available when mobile number verification is required.
10. The Email Verification Service shall be available when email verification is required.
11. The OTP Service shall be available when OTP-based verification is configured.
12. The Notification Service shall be available for sending verification messages and contact-related notifications.
13. The Communication Service shall be available for managing communication channels.
14. The Customer Support Service shall be available where customer assistance is required.
15. The Order Management Service shall be available where verified contact information is used for order-related communications.
16. The Delivery Management Service shall be available where verified contact information is required for delivery coordination.
17. The Marketing Service shall be available where promotional communications depend on verified customer contact information.
18. The Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Service shall be available where verified contact information is used for authentication.
19. The Account Recovery Service shall be available where verified contact information is used for account recovery.
20. The Privacy Settings Service shall be available for enforcing customer privacy preferences.
21. The Audit Logging Service shall be available for recording contact preference lifecycle events.
22. The Security Monitoring Service shall be available for monitoring contact preference-related security activities.
23. The Data Privacy & Compliance Service shall be available for enforcing privacy and regulatory requirements.
24. The customer shall have permission to view and manage contact preferences.
25. The contact preference management request shall be initiated through a secure HTTPS connection.
26. Any new or modified contact information shall comply with the platform's validation rules before verification.
27. The system shall be capable of verifying ownership of newly added or modified contact information using supported verification mechanisms.
28. All backend services participating in the Contact Preferences workflow shall be operational.
29. The platform shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, cybersecurity, communication, accessibility, data protection, and regulatory requirements before processing any contact preference management request.

#### 3.1.19.4 Trigger

The **Contact Preferences** process is initiated when an authenticated customer navigates to the **Profile**, **Account Settings**, **Contact Preferences**, **Communication Settings**, **Privacy Settings**, **Security Settings**, or any other customer account management interface and chooses to view, add, update, verify, or remove contact information. The process may also be initiated automatically by the system when contact verification, account recovery, security validation, or regulatory compliance requires updated customer contact information.

---

#### 3.1.19.5 Input Fields

| Field Name | Data Type | Mandatory | Description | Validation |
|------------|-----------|-----------|-------------|------------|
| Customer ID | UUID | System Generated | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. | Shall belong to the authenticated customer account. |
| Primary Mobile Number | String | Yes | Customer's primary contact mobile number. | Shall comply with supported country format and uniqueness rules. |
| Secondary Mobile Number | String | Optional | Customer's alternate mobile number. | Shall comply with supported country format and shall not duplicate the primary mobile number. |
| Primary Email Address | String | Yes | Customer's primary email address. | Shall comply with RFC-compliant email format and uniqueness rules. |
| Alternate Email Address | String | Optional | Customer's alternate email address. | Shall comply with email format and shall not duplicate the primary email address. |
| Preferred Contact Method | Enum | Yes | Customer's preferred communication channel. | Shall contain a supported value (Mobile, Email, Both, or platform-supported options). |
| Verification Method | Enum | Optional | Verification mechanism for updated contact information. | Shall contain a supported verification method. |
| OTP / Verification Code | String | Conditional | Verification code received through the selected verification method. | Shall be valid and unexpired when verification is required. |
| Access Token | String | Yes | Authentication token used to authorize the request. | Shall be valid, active, and associated with the authenticated customer. |
| Session ID | String | Yes | Active authenticated session identifier. | Shall be valid and not expired. |
| Device Information | Object | Optional | Information about the requesting device. | Shall comply with platform security policies. |
| IP Address | String | System Generated | IP address from which the request originates. | Shall be captured for audit and security monitoring purposes. |
| User Agent | String | System Generated | Browser or application information of the requesting device. | Shall be captured for audit and security monitoring purposes. |

**Notes:**

- **Customer ID** shall be derived from the authenticated session and shall not be editable.
- **Primary Mobile Number** and **Primary Email Address** shall uniquely identify the customer's primary contact channels according to platform policy.
- Any newly added or modified mobile number or email address shall remain in a pending state until successful verification.
- **Preferred Contact Method** shall determine the default communication channel used by supported platform services.
- **OTP / Verification Code** shall be mandatory only when ownership verification is required.
- Duplicate mobile numbers or email addresses shall be validated according to the platform's uniqueness policy.
- The **Access Token** and **Session ID** shall be validated before processing the request.
- **Device Information**, **IP Address**, and **User Agent** may be recorded for fraud detection, security monitoring, operational analytics, and audit purposes.
- Authentication credentials shall never be stored, logged, or transmitted in plain text.
- All requests shall be transmitted only through secure HTTPS connections.
- Personally Identifiable Information (PII), customer contact information, and communication data shall be processed in accordance with the platform's privacy, security, audit, cybersecurity, accessibility, data protection, communication, and applicable regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.19.6 Business Rules

1. Only authenticated customers shall be permitted to access the Contact Preferences functionality.
2. Customers shall be permitted to manage only the contact preferences associated with their own customer account.
3. The system shall validate customer authentication and authorization before processing any contact preference management request.
4. Each customer account shall have one primary mobile number.
5. Each customer account shall have one primary email address.
6. Secondary mobile numbers and alternate email addresses may be supported according to platform configuration.
7. Any newly added or modified mobile number shall remain unverified until ownership verification is successfully completed.
8. Any newly added or modified email address shall remain unverified until ownership verification is successfully completed.
9. The system shall activate newly added or modified contact information only after successful verification.
10. Previously verified contact information shall remain active until replacement contact information is successfully verified.
11. Mobile number verification may be performed using One-Time Password (OTP) or other platform-supported verification mechanisms.
12. Email address verification may be performed using verification links, OTPs, confirmation codes, or other approved verification mechanisms.
13. Verification codes shall remain valid only for the configured validity period.
14. Expired verification codes shall not be accepted.
15. Invalid verification attempts shall be limited according to the platform's security policy.
16. The system may temporarily block further verification attempts after exceeding the configured retry limit.
17. Mobile numbers and email addresses shall comply with configured validation and formatting rules.
18. Duplicate mobile numbers and email addresses shall be validated according to the platform's uniqueness policy.
19. The preferred contact method shall be selected only from platform-supported communication channels.
20. Changes to contact preferences shall affect only future platform communications.
21. Historical communications, completed orders, invoices, audit logs, and immutable business records shall not be modified by contact preference updates.
22. Verified contact information may be used by Authentication, Two-Factor Authentication (2FA), Account Recovery, Order Management, Delivery Management, Customer Support, and other authorized platform services.
23. Customer contact information and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be protected throughout the contact preference lifecycle.
24. Customer contact information shall be processed in accordance with applicable privacy, security, audit, communication, accessibility, cybersecurity, data protection, and regulatory requirements.
25. Sensitive authentication credentials, verification codes, session identifiers, and access tokens shall never be stored, logged, cached, or exposed in plain text.
26. Every contact preference creation, update, verification attempt, verification failure, contact removal, authorization failure, security event, and other significant lifecycle event shall be recorded in the audit log where applicable.
27. Security monitoring systems shall receive applicable contact preference lifecycle events according to organizational policy.
28. Failure responses shall not expose authentication credentials, verification codes, internal implementation details, infrastructure information, or sensitive customer information.
29. Concurrent contact preference modification requests shall be processed according to the platform's concurrency control policy.
30. All Contact Preferences requests shall be processed only through secure HTTPS connections.
31. The Contact Preferences functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, cybersecurity, communication, accessibility, data protection, and applicable legal and regulatory requirements.
32. System-generated contact verification requests shall follow the same validation, authorization, audit, and security controls as customer-initiated requests.
33. Verified contact information shall remain consistently synchronized across all authorized dependent platform services.
34. If any mandatory verification or processing step fails, the contact preference update shall not be activated.
35. The system shall maintain referential integrity between the customer account, contact preferences, authentication services, notification services, and communication services at all times.
36. Contact preference updates shall not interrupt active customer sessions unless required by platform security policies.
37. The platform shall ensure that verified contact information remains available for critical account operations such as account recovery, security notifications, and order communication.

#### 3.1.19.7 Validations

| Validation | Rule | Failure Response |
|------------|------|------------------|
| Authentication Validation | The customer shall be successfully authenticated before accessing the Contact Preferences functionality. | "Authentication required." |
| Access Token Validation | The Access Token shall be valid, active, and digitally verifiable. | "Invalid or expired authentication token." |
| Session Validation | An active authenticated session shall exist. | "Session has expired. Please log in again." |
| Session Expiry Validation | The authenticated session shall not have expired. | "Session has expired." |
| Session Revocation Validation | The authenticated session shall not have been revoked or terminated. | "Session is no longer valid." |
| Customer Identification Validation | The authenticated customer shall be uniquely identified from the active session. | "Unable to identify customer." |
| Customer Account Validation | The customer account shall exist. | "Customer account not found." |
| Authorization Validation | The authenticated customer shall be authorized to manage contact preferences associated with their account. | "Access denied." |
| Primary Mobile Validation | The primary mobile number shall be provided and comply with the configured mobile number format. | "Invalid primary mobile number." |
| Secondary Mobile Validation | The secondary mobile number, if provided, shall comply with the configured mobile number format and shall not duplicate the primary mobile number. | "Invalid secondary mobile number." |
| Primary Email Validation | The primary email address shall be provided and comply with the configured email format. | "Invalid primary email address." |
| Alternate Email Validation | The alternate email address, if provided, shall comply with the configured email format and shall not duplicate the primary email address. | "Invalid alternate email address." |
| Mobile Uniqueness Validation | The mobile number shall satisfy the platform's uniqueness policy. | "Mobile number is already associated with another account." |
| Email Uniqueness Validation | The email address shall satisfy the platform's uniqueness policy. | "Email address is already associated with another account." |
| Preferred Contact Method Validation | The preferred contact method shall contain a supported value. | "Invalid preferred contact method." |
| Verification Method Validation | The selected verification method shall be supported by the platform. | "Unsupported verification method." |
| OTP / Verification Code Validation | The verification code shall be valid, active, and unexpired when verification is required. | "Invalid or expired verification code." |
| Verification Retry Limit Validation | Verification attempts shall not exceed the configured retry limit. | "Verification attempt limit exceeded." |
| Input Validation | All mandatory fields shall be provided. | "Required information is missing." |
| Input Sanitization Validation | All submitted values shall be validated and sanitized before processing. | "Invalid request." |
| HTTPS Validation | Contact Preferences requests shall be accepted only through secure HTTPS connections. | Request shall be rejected. |
| Request Integrity Validation | The request shall not be modified or tampered with during transmission. | "Request validation failed." |
| Service Availability Validation | Required backend services shall be available before processing the request. | "Required service is temporarily unavailable." |
| Concurrent Modification Validation | Concurrent contact preference update requests shall be processed according to the configured concurrency policy. | "Another contact preference update is currently being processed." |
| Audit Validation | Contact preference creation, updates, verification events, failures, and security events shall be recorded in the audit log where applicable. | Audit logging failure shall be handled according to operational policy. |
| Data Privacy Validation | Customer contact information shall be processed in accordance with applicable privacy and data protection requirements. | Request shall be rejected if privacy requirements cannot be satisfied. |

#### 3.1.19.8 Main Flow

1. The customer navigates to the **Profile**, **Account Settings**, **Contact Preferences**, **Communication Settings**, **Privacy Settings**, or another customer account management interface.
2. The system verifies that the customer is authenticated and has an active session.
3. The system validates the customer's authorization to manage contact preferences.
4. The system retrieves the customer's existing contact preferences.
5. The system displays the current verified and pending contact information, if applicable.
6. The customer adds, updates, or removes supported contact information.
7. The customer selects the preferred contact method where applicable.
8. The system receives the contact preference management request.
9. The system validates the integrity of the request.
10. The system validates the Access Token and active session.
11. The system identifies the authenticated customer.
12. The system validates all submitted contact information.
13. The system verifies that mobile numbers and email addresses comply with platform validation rules.
14. The system validates uniqueness according to the configured business policies.
15. The system sanitizes all user-provided input.
16. The system determines whether ownership verification is required.
17. If verification is required, the system generates a verification request using the configured verification mechanism.
18. The system sends an OTP, verification link, confirmation code, or other supported verification request to the new contact information.
19. The customer completes the verification process.
20. The system validates the verification response.
21. Upon successful verification, the system begins a database transaction.
22. The system updates the customer's contact preferences.
23. The system updates the verification status of the modified contact information.
24. The system updates the preferred contact method where applicable.
25. The system synchronizes the verified contact information with authorized dependent platform services.
26. The system commits the transaction after all validations and updates have completed successfully.
27. The system records the contact preference update event in the audit log.
28. The system records applicable operational and security monitoring events.
29. The system returns a successful response indicating that the contact preferences have been successfully updated.
30. The updated contact preferences become immediately available for future platform communications, authentication workflows, account recovery, notifications, delivery coordination, customer support, and other authorized platform services according to the configured business policies.

#### 3.1.19.9 Alternate Flows

**Alternate Flow 1: No Contact Information Updated**

1. The customer opens the Contact Preferences page.
2. The customer reviews the existing contact information.
3. The customer chooses not to make any changes.
4. The system closes the operation without modifying any customer data.

---

**Alternate Flow 2: Mobile Number Verification Required**

1. The customer updates the primary or secondary mobile number.
2. The system validates the submitted mobile number.
3. The system generates an OTP using the configured verification mechanism.
4. The OTP is delivered to the submitted mobile number.
5. The customer successfully enters the OTP.
6. The system verifies ownership of the mobile number.
7. The updated mobile number becomes verified and active.

---

**Alternate Flow 3: Email Verification Required**

1. The customer updates the primary or alternate email address.
2. The system validates the submitted email address.
3. The system sends a verification email, OTP, or confirmation code.
4. The customer successfully completes email verification.
5. The system activates the updated email address.
6. The verified email becomes available for future platform communications.

---

**Alternate Flow 4: Verification Pending**

1. The customer submits updated contact information.
2. The system initiates the verification process.
3. The customer does not complete verification within the configured validity period.
4. The updated contact information remains in a pending verification state.
5. Previously verified contact information continues to be used.

---

**Alternate Flow 5: Verification Retry**

1. The customer enters an incorrect OTP or verification code.
2. The system rejects the verification attempt.
3. The customer requests another verification attempt.
4. The system generates and sends a new verification code according to platform policy.
5. Verification continues until successful completion or retry limits are reached.

---

**Alternate Flow 6: Contact Information Already Exists**

1. The customer submits a mobile number or email address.
2. The system determines that the submitted contact information violates the platform's uniqueness policy.
3. The update request is rejected.
4. The customer is informed to provide different contact information.

---

**Alternate Flow 7: Preferred Contact Method Changed**

1. The customer changes the preferred communication method.
2. The system validates the selected communication channel.
3. The updated preference is saved.
4. Future supported platform communications use the newly selected preferred contact method.

---

**Alternate Flow 8: Concurrent Contact Preference Update**

1. Multiple contact preference update requests are submitted simultaneously.
2. The system applies concurrency control mechanisms.
3. One request is processed successfully.
4. Remaining requests are rejected or retried according to platform policy.

---

**Alternate Flow 9: Verification Service Temporarily Unavailable**

1. The customer submits updated contact information.
2. The verification service is temporarily unavailable.
3. The system does not activate the updated contact information.
4. The customer is informed to retry verification later.

---

**Alternate Flow 10: Transaction Rollback**

1. The system encounters an error while updating the verified contact information.
2. The active database transaction is rolled back.
3. Previously verified contact information remains unchanged.
4. The system returns an appropriate failure response.

#### 3.1.19.10 Postconditions

1. The customer's contact preferences shall be successfully updated after all applicable validations and verification requirements are completed.
2. Newly added or modified contact information shall become active only after successful ownership verification where required.
3. Previously verified contact information shall remain active until replacement contact information is successfully verified.
4. The customer's preferred contact method shall be updated according to the submitted request.
5. Verified contact information shall be permanently stored in the system upon successful transaction completion.
6. The updated contact preferences shall be synchronized with all authorized dependent platform services.
7. Future platform communications shall use the updated contact preferences according to the configured business policies.
8. Authentication, Two-Factor Authentication (2FA), Account Recovery, Notification Services, Customer Support, Order Management, Delivery Management, and other authorized platform services shall utilize the updated verified contact information where applicable.
9. Historical communications, completed orders, invoices, audit records, and other immutable business records shall remain unchanged.
10. Customer profile information unrelated to contact preferences shall remain unchanged.
11. Customer ownership and integrity of contact information shall remain preserved.
12. All applicable authentication, authorization, validation, verification, and business rule checks shall have been successfully completed.
13. Contact preference creation, update, verification, removal, validation failures, authorization failures, and applicable security events shall be recorded in the audit log in accordance with organizational policies.
14. Applicable operational and security monitoring events shall be generated for observability and incident monitoring.
15. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and customer contact information shall remain protected in accordance with applicable privacy, security, communication, accessibility, and data protection requirements.
16. Authentication credentials, verification codes, session identifiers, access tokens, and other sensitive security information shall never be exposed in responses, logs, or audit records.
17. If the transaction fails at any stage, all pending changes shall be rolled back and the previously verified contact information shall remain unchanged.
18. The platform shall remain in a consistent state regardless of the success or failure of the Contact Preferences operation.
19. Pending contact information awaiting verification shall not be used by dependent platform services until successful verification has been completed.
20. The Contact Preferences functionality shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, cybersecurity, communication, accessibility, data protection, and applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.19.11 Success Response

Upon successful completion of the Contact Preferences operation, the system shall return a success response confirming that the customer's contact preferences have been successfully updated and, where applicable, verified. The response shall contain only authorized customer information and shall not expose sensitive security or verification data.

**Response Attributes:**

| Field | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Status | Success |
| HTTP Status Code | 200 OK |
| Response Code | CONTACT_PREFERENCES_UPDATED_SUCCESSFULLY |
| Message | Contact preferences updated successfully. |
| Customer ID | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. |
| Primary Mobile Number | Verified primary mobile number (masked where applicable). |
| Primary Mobile Verification Status | Verification status of the primary mobile number. |
| Secondary Mobile Number | Verified secondary mobile number (masked where applicable), if available. |
| Secondary Mobile Verification Status | Verification status of the secondary mobile number. |
| Primary Email Address | Verified primary email address (masked where applicable). |
| Primary Email Verification Status | Verification status of the primary email address. |
| Alternate Email Address | Verified alternate email address (masked where applicable), if available. |
| Alternate Email Verification Status | Verification status of the alternate email address. |
| Preferred Contact Method | Customer's configured preferred communication channel. |
| Verification Required | Indicates whether additional verification is pending. |
| Request ID | Unique identifier of the processed request. |
| Transaction ID | Unique identifier of the completed transaction. |
| Updated Timestamp | Date and time when the contact preferences were successfully updated. |
| Audit Status | Indicates whether the audit log entry was successfully created. |

**Example Response**

```json
{
  "status": "SUCCESS",
  "statusCode": 200,
  "responseCode": "CONTACT_PREFERENCES_UPDATED_SUCCESSFULLY",
  "message": "Contact preferences updated successfully.",
  "customerId": "CUS-102458",
  "primaryMobileNumber": "+91******4321",
  "primaryMobileVerificationStatus": "VERIFIED",
  "secondaryMobileNumber": "+91******8765",
  "secondaryMobileVerificationStatus": "VERIFIED",
  "primaryEmailAddress": "sa****@gmail.com",
  "primaryEmailVerificationStatus": "VERIFIED",
  "alternateEmailAddress": "sa****@outlook.com",
  "alternateEmailVerificationStatus": "VERIFIED",
  "preferredContactMethod": "BOTH",
  "verificationRequired": false,
  "requestId": "REQ-202601190001",
  "transactionId": "TXN-202601190001",
  "updatedTimestamp": "2026-01-19T10:45:30Z",
  "auditStatus": "RECORDED"
}
```

**Success Conditions**

- The contact preference update request has been successfully authenticated and authorized.
- All mandatory validations and business rules have passed.
- Newly added or modified contact information has been successfully verified where verification is required.
- The updated contact preferences have been successfully persisted.
- The preferred contact method has been updated according to the customer's request.
- The updated contact information has been synchronized with authorized dependent platform services according to platform policy.
- Applicable audit and operational events have been successfully recorded.
- Future platform communications shall use the updated verified contact preferences according to the configured business rules.
- No sensitive customer information, authentication credentials, verification codes, session identifiers, access tokens, or internal implementation details shall be exposed in the response.

#### 3.1.19.12 Failure Response

The system shall return an appropriate failure response whenever the Contact Preferences request cannot be completed due to authentication failures, authorization failures, validation errors, verification failures, business rule violations, service unavailability, security violations, or unexpected processing errors.

**Response Attributes:**

| Field | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Status | Failure |
| HTTP Status Code | Appropriate HTTP status code based on the error. |
| Response Code | Unique application-specific error code. |
| Error Message | Human-readable description of the failure. |
| Error Category | Classification of the error (Authentication, Authorization, Validation, Verification, Business Rule, Security, System, etc.). |
| Customer ID | Authenticated customer identifier where applicable. |
| Request ID | Unique identifier of the failed request. |
| Timestamp | Date and time when the failure occurred. |
| Retry Allowed | Indicates whether the customer may retry the request. |
| Audit Status | Indicates whether the failure event was recorded in the audit log. |

**Common Failure Scenarios**

| Scenario | HTTP Status | Response Code | Message |
|----------|-------------|---------------|---------|
| Customer not authenticated | 401 Unauthorized | AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED | Authentication required. |
| Invalid or expired access token | 401 Unauthorized | INVALID_ACCESS_TOKEN | Invalid or expired authentication token. |
| Session expired | 401 Unauthorized | SESSION_EXPIRED | Your session has expired. Please log in again. |
| Customer account not found | 404 Not Found | CUSTOMER_NOT_FOUND | Customer account not found. |
| Unauthorized access | 403 Forbidden | ACCESS_DENIED | You are not authorized to manage contact preferences. |
| Invalid mobile number | 400 Bad Request | INVALID_MOBILE_NUMBER | Invalid mobile number format. |
| Invalid email address | 400 Bad Request | INVALID_EMAIL_ADDRESS | Invalid email address format. |
| Mobile number already exists | 409 Conflict | MOBILE_ALREADY_EXISTS | Mobile number is already associated with another account. |
| Email address already exists | 409 Conflict | EMAIL_ALREADY_EXISTS | Email address is already associated with another account. |
| Invalid preferred contact method | 400 Bad Request | INVALID_CONTACT_METHOD | Unsupported preferred contact method. |
| Invalid verification code | 400 Bad Request | INVALID_VERIFICATION_CODE | Invalid verification code. |
| Verification code expired | 400 Bad Request | VERIFICATION_CODE_EXPIRED | Verification code has expired. |
| Verification retry limit exceeded | 429 Too Many Requests | VERIFICATION_RETRY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED | Verification attempt limit exceeded. Please try again later. |
| Verification service unavailable | 503 Service Unavailable | VERIFICATION_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE | Verification service is temporarily unavailable. |
| Request validation failed | 400 Bad Request | INVALID_REQUEST | Invalid contact preference request. |
| Concurrent update detected | 409 Conflict | CONCURRENT_UPDATE_DETECTED | Another contact preference update is currently in progress. |
| Database operation failed | 500 Internal Server Error | DATABASE_OPERATION_FAILED | Unable to update contact preferences. |
| Dependent service unavailable | 503 Service Unavailable | DEPENDENT_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE | Required platform service is temporarily unavailable. |
| Internal server error | 500 Internal Server Error | INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR | An unexpected error occurred while processing the request. |

**Example Failure Response**

```json
{
  "status": "FAILURE",
  "statusCode": 400,
  "responseCode": "INVALID_VERIFICATION_CODE",
  "errorCategory": "VERIFICATION",
  "message": "Invalid verification code.",
  "customerId": "CUS-102458",
  "requestId": "REQ-202601190011",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T11:15:42Z",
  "retryAllowed": true,
  "auditStatus": "RECORDED"
}
```

**Failure Handling Rules**

- The system shall immediately terminate processing when a critical authentication, authorization, validation, or verification failure occurs.
- Newly submitted contact information shall not become active unless ownership verification has been successfully completed.
- Previously verified contact information shall remain unchanged whenever the update operation fails.
- No partial updates shall be committed to the database.
- Any active transaction shall be completely rolled back upon failure.
- Sensitive information including authentication credentials, verification codes, OTPs, access tokens, session identifiers, internal implementation details, infrastructure information, database structures, and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall never be exposed in failure responses.
- Applicable failure events shall be recorded in audit logs according to organizational security and compliance policies.
- Security-related failures shall be forwarded to security monitoring systems where applicable.
- Failure responses shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, cybersecurity, communication, accessibility, data protection, and applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.19.13 Non-Functional Considerations

##### Performance

1. The system shall process Contact Preferences requests within **3 seconds** under normal operating conditions, excluding external verification delays.
2. Mobile number and email verification requests shall be initiated within **2 seconds** after successful request validation.
3. The platform shall support concurrent contact preference update requests from multiple customers without performance degradation.
4. Contact information retrieval and updates shall be optimized using efficient database queries and indexing mechanisms.
5. Synchronization of verified contact information across dependent services shall occur with minimal latency.
6. The platform shall maintain acceptable response times during peak business hours.

##### Availability

7. The Contact Preferences functionality shall maintain a minimum availability of **99.9%**, excluding scheduled maintenance.
8. Temporary failures of verification providers shall be handled gracefully according to configured retry and fallback policies.
9. The functionality shall automatically recover after transient infrastructure failures where supported.

##### Scalability

10. The platform shall support millions of customer accounts and verified contact records without requiring architectural redesign.
11. Verification services shall scale horizontally to support increasing verification workloads.
12. The solution shall support multiple countries, mobile numbering plans, email providers, and communication channels.

##### Reliability

13. Contact preference updates shall be executed as fully atomic transactions.
14. Verified contact information shall remain consistent across all authorized dependent platform services.
15. Verification failures shall not modify previously verified customer contact information.
16. Temporary failures shall not compromise customer contact data integrity.
17. The platform shall guarantee consistency between customer profiles and verified contact information.

##### Security

18. All Contact Preferences requests shall be transmitted exclusively over secure HTTPS connections.
19. Authentication and authorization shall be enforced before processing every request.
20. Customer contact information and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be encrypted during transmission and protected according to organizational security standards.
21. Sensitive authentication credentials, verification codes, OTPs, access tokens, and session identifiers shall never be stored or logged in plain text.
22. The system shall protect against unauthorized access, injection attacks, brute-force attacks, replay attacks, cross-site scripting (XSS), cross-site request forgery (CSRF), parameter tampering, OTP abuse, and other common security threats.
23. Verification requests shall be protected using configurable rate-limiting and abuse detection mechanisms.
24. Security events shall be logged and monitored according to organizational cybersecurity policies.

##### Privacy

25. Customer contact information shall be processed in accordance with applicable privacy regulations and organizational data protection policies.
26. Only authorized users and services shall access verified customer contact information.
27. Data collection, retention, archival, masking, and deletion shall comply with applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

##### Audit & Compliance

28. All successful and failed contact preference updates, verification requests, verification attempts, verification failures, authorization failures, and security events shall be recorded in audit logs where applicable.
29. Audit records shall support operational monitoring, compliance reporting, security investigations, and forensic analysis.
30. Audit logs shall be protected against unauthorized modification or deletion.
31. The Contact Preferences functionality shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, security, audit, cybersecurity, communication, accessibility, data protection, and regulatory requirements.

##### Maintainability

32. The functionality shall be implemented using modular, loosely coupled components.
33. Verification providers, communication channels, retry policies, and business rules shall be configurable wherever practical.
34. Future enhancements to supported communication channels and verification mechanisms shall not require major architectural changes.

##### Monitoring & Observability

35. The platform shall continuously monitor request processing time, verification success rate, verification failure rate, service availability, error rates, and abnormal verification activity.
36. Critical failures, excessive verification attempts, suspicious activities, and service outages shall generate operational alerts.
37. Logs, metrics, and traces shall provide complete end-to-end observability of the Contact Preferences workflow.

##### Backup & Recovery

38. Customer contact information, verification metadata, and configuration data shall be included in scheduled backup procedures where applicable.
39. Recovery mechanisms shall preserve customer contact integrity following infrastructure failures or disaster recovery events.
40. Backup and restoration procedures shall comply with the platform's business continuity and disaster recovery policies.

#### 3.1.19.14 Acceptance Criteria

| AC ID | Acceptance Criteria |
|-------|----------------------|
| AC-CP-001 | The system shall allow only authenticated customers to access the Contact Preferences functionality. |
| AC-CP-002 | The system shall authorize only the owner of the customer account to view or modify contact preferences. |
| AC-CP-003 | The system shall display the customer's current verified and pending contact information upon successful access. |
| AC-CP-004 | The system shall allow customers to add, update, or remove supported contact information according to business rules. |
| AC-CP-005 | The system shall validate all submitted mobile numbers, email addresses, and communication preferences before processing the request. |
| AC-CP-006 | The system shall reject invalid, malformed, or unsupported contact information. |
| AC-CP-007 | The system shall enforce uniqueness rules for customer mobile numbers and email addresses wherever applicable. |
| AC-CP-008 | The system shall initiate ownership verification whenever a new mobile number or email address requires verification. |
| AC-CP-009 | The system shall activate newly submitted contact information only after successful verification. |
| AC-CP-010 | Previously verified contact information shall remain active until replacement contact information has been successfully verified. |
| AC-CP-011 | The system shall support OTP-based and other configured verification mechanisms according to platform configuration. |
| AC-CP-012 | The system shall enforce verification expiration periods and retry limits according to business policies. |
| AC-CP-013 | The system shall allow customers to resend verification requests within configured platform limits. |
| AC-CP-014 | The system shall allow customers to select their preferred communication method from supported communication channels. |
| AC-CP-015 | The updated preferred communication method shall be applied only to future eligible platform communications. |
| AC-CP-016 | The system shall synchronize verified contact information with authorized dependent platform services after successful completion. |
| AC-CP-017 | Customer profile data unrelated to contact preferences shall remain unchanged after the operation. |
| AC-CP-018 | The system shall execute all contact preference updates as atomic transactions. |
| AC-CP-019 | Partial updates shall never be committed when transaction failures occur. |
| AC-CP-020 | Failed operations shall automatically roll back all pending database changes. |
| AC-CP-021 | The system shall return appropriate success responses for successfully completed operations. |
| AC-CP-022 | The system shall return standardized failure responses for authentication failures, authorization failures, validation failures, verification failures, business rule violations, and system errors. |
