| AC-CP-023 | Sensitive information including OTPs, verification codes, passwords, access tokens, session identifiers, internal implementation details, and security information shall never be exposed in API responses. |
| AC-CP-024 | All communication between clients and platform services shall occur over secure HTTPS connections. |
| AC-CP-025 | Customer contact information and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be protected according to organizational security and privacy standards. |
| AC-CP-026 | All successful and failed contact preference operations shall be recorded in immutable audit logs. |
| AC-CP-027 | Security-related events including failed verification attempts, excessive retries, unauthorized access attempts, and suspicious activities shall be logged for monitoring purposes. |
| AC-CP-028 | The Contact Preferences functionality shall remain available according to the platform's defined service availability objectives. |
| AC-CP-029 | The functionality shall support concurrent customer requests without compromising data consistency or integrity. |
| AC-CP-030 | Contact preference updates shall remain consistent across all authorized platform services following successful synchronization. |
| AC-CP-031 | The functionality shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, cybersecurity, audit, communication, accessibility, data protection, and legal/regulatory requirements. |
| AC-CP-032 | The Contact Preferences module shall successfully pass all functional, integration, security, performance, regression, and user acceptance testing before production deployment. |

### 3.1.20 Language Preferences

#### 3.1.20.1 Description

The **Language Preferences** module enables authenticated customers to configure and manage their preferred language for interacting with the Food Delivery Platform. This functionality provides a personalized user experience by allowing customers to select the language used across the platform's user interface, notifications, transactional communications, emails, SMS messages, push notifications, help content, invoices, and other customer-facing services.

The platform shall maintain a centralized language preference associated with each customer account, ensuring a consistent multilingual experience across web applications, mobile applications, APIs, customer support interactions, and integrated platform services. Authorized platform components shall retrieve the customer's preferred language dynamically to deliver localized content wherever multilingual support is available.

Language preference management shall support configurable platform languages while maintaining compatibility with existing customer accounts, authentication services, order management, payment services, notification services, customer support systems, marketing communications, and future internationalization (i18n) and localization (l10n) initiatives. Changes to language preferences shall affect only future customer interactions and shall not modify historical business records, completed transactions, invoices, audit logs, legal documents, or previously generated communications.

The platform shall validate every language preference update against the list of supported languages before applying the change. Unsupported, deprecated, disabled, or invalid language selections shall be rejected according to platform business rules. Whenever a preferred language becomes unavailable, the platform shall automatically apply a configurable fallback language while preserving service availability and usability.

All language preference operations shall be protected through authentication and authorization controls. Every successful or failed language preference modification shall be recorded within the platform's audit logging framework to support operational monitoring, security analysis, compliance requirements, and troubleshooting. Sensitive customer information shall remain protected throughout the language preference lifecycle in accordance with organizational privacy, cybersecurity, accessibility, and data protection standards.

The Language Preferences functionality shall be designed to support future expansion into additional countries, regional language variants, locale-specific formatting, multilingual customer support, and enterprise-scale international deployments without requiring significant architectural changes.

#### 3.1.20.2 Actors

The following actors participate in the Language Preferences functionality:

| Actor | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Customer | Authenticated customer who views or updates language preferences. |
| Authentication Service | Authenticates the customer before allowing access to language preference settings. |
| Authorization Service | Ensures only the account owner is authorized to modify language preferences. |
| Customer Profile Service | Retrieves and stores the customer's preferred language. |
| Language Preferences Service | Manages language preference configuration, validation, and updates. |
| Localization (i18n) Service | Provides translated resources, localized labels, messages, and UI content based on the selected language. |
| Locale Management Service | Applies locale-specific configurations such as language, date/time formats, number formats, currency display, and regional settings where applicable. |
| User Interface Service | Displays the platform using the customer's preferred language. |
| Notification Service | Delivers notifications using the configured preferred language whenever multilingual content is available. |
| Email Service | Sends transactional and non-transactional emails in the customer's preferred language where supported. |
| SMS Service | Sends SMS communications using the customer's preferred language where supported. |
| Push Notification Service | Delivers mobile push notifications using localized message templates. |
| Customer Support Service | Retrieves the customer's preferred language to improve support interactions. |
| Marketing Communication Service | Uses the preferred language for promotional campaigns and customer engagement communications where applicable. |
| Order Management Service | Retrieves language preferences for localized customer-facing order communications. |
| Payment Service | Uses the configured language for supported payment-related notifications and customer-facing messages. |
| Invoice & Billing Service | Generates customer-facing billing communications using supported localized templates where applicable. |
| Audit Logging Service | Records all successful and failed language preference operations for compliance and auditing purposes. |
| Security Monitoring Service | Monitors unauthorized access attempts, suspicious activities, and security-related events. |
| Configuration Management Service | Maintains the list of supported languages, default language, fallback language, and localization configurations. |
| Data Privacy & Compliance Service | Ensures language preference management complies with applicable privacy, security, accessibility, and regulatory requirements. |

#### 3.1.20.3 Preconditions

Before a customer can access or modify Language Preferences, the following preconditions shall be satisfied:

1. The customer shall have a registered customer account.
2. The customer account shall be active and not permanently deleted.
3. The customer shall be successfully authenticated.
4. The customer shall possess a valid authenticated session.
5. The customer's access token shall be valid and unexpired.
6. The customer shall be authorized to modify only their own language preferences.
7. The Language Preferences module shall be enabled within the platform configuration.
8. The Customer Profile Service shall be available.
9. The Language Preferences Service shall be operational.
10. The Localization (i18n) Service shall be available.
11. The Locale Management Service shall be operational.
12. The Configuration Management Service shall provide the current list of supported languages.
13. At least one supported language shall be configured within the platform.
14. A default platform language shall be configured.
15. A fallback language shall be configured for unsupported or unavailable languages.
16. The platform shall have successfully loaded all required localization resources for supported languages.
17. The customer profile shall be retrievable from the database.
18. The selected language shall exist within the supported language configuration.
19. The selected language shall not be disabled or deprecated.
20. The database shall be available for reading and updating language preferences.
21. Communication between the client and backend services shall occur over secure HTTPS connections.
22. The Audit Logging Service shall be available to record language preference operations.
23. The Security Monitoring Service shall be available to capture security-related events.
24. Required validation services shall be operational.
25. The Notification Service shall be available to utilize updated language preferences for future notifications where applicable.
26. Dependent platform services shall be capable of retrieving the customer's preferred language after successful updates.
27. The platform shall satisfy applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, security, accessibility, localization, and compliance requirements before processing the request.
28. No conflicting language preference update transaction shall currently be in progress for the same customer account.
29. All required platform services shall be functioning within acceptable operational thresholds.

#### 3.1.20.4 Trigger

The Language Preferences functionality shall be initiated whenever one of the following events occurs:

1. An authenticated customer navigates to **Profile → Settings → Language Preferences**.
2. An authenticated customer selects the option to view their current language preference.
3. An authenticated customer submits a request to change their preferred language.
4. An authenticated customer restores their account settings and reviews language preferences.
5. The customer switches between supported platform languages from the application settings.
6. The platform detects that the customer's previously selected language has become unavailable or deprecated and initiates fallback language assignment.
7. The customer signs in from a new device, and the platform retrieves the stored language preference for localization.
8. A mobile application initializes and requests the customer's preferred language for rendering localized content.
9. A web application session is established and retrieves the customer's preferred language before loading the user interface.
10. The Notification Service retrieves the customer's preferred language before sending future notifications.
11. The Email Service retrieves the preferred language before generating localized email templates.
12. The SMS Service retrieves the preferred language before sending multilingual SMS communications where supported.
13. The Push Notification Service retrieves the preferred language before delivering localized push notifications.
14. The Customer Support Service retrieves the customer's preferred language to facilitate multilingual support interactions.
15. The Marketing Communication Service retrieves the preferred language before sending promotional communications where localization is available.
16. The Order Management Service retrieves the preferred language to generate customer-facing order communications.
17. The Payment Service retrieves the preferred language to generate localized payment-related customer messages where supported.
18. The Invoice & Billing Service retrieves the preferred language to generate supported localized billing communications.
19. An administrator updates the platform's supported language configuration, requiring validation of existing customer language preferences.
20. The platform restores customer profile information following backup or disaster recovery operations.
21. A customer profile synchronization process retrieves or updates the customer's preferred language across authorized platform services.
22. A localization resource update becomes available, enabling additional translations for the customer's selected language.
23. The system validates customer language preferences during profile synchronization or account maintenance activities.
24. Any authorized platform service requires the customer's preferred language to provide a localized customer experience.

#### 3.1.20.5 Input Fields

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Description |
|-------|------|-----------|-------------|
| Customer ID | UUID / String | Yes | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer whose language preference is being managed. |
| Preferred Language Code | String | Yes | Language selected by the customer (e.g., `en`, `en-US`, `ta`, `hi`). |
| Preferred Language Name | String | No | Human-readable name of the selected language. |
| Locale | String | No | Locale associated with the selected language (e.g., `en-IN`, `ta-IN`). |
| Fallback Language | String | No | Platform-configured fallback language if the selected language becomes unavailable. |
| Language Version | Integer | No | Version used for optimistic concurrency control during updates. |
| Access Token | JWT / String | Yes | Valid authentication token identifying the authenticated customer. |
| Session ID | String | Yes | Active authenticated session identifier. |
| Device ID | String | No | Unique identifier of the customer's device. |
| Device Type | Enum | No | Device category such as Web, Android, iOS, or Tablet. |
| Application Version | String | No | Version of the client application initiating the request. |
| Time Zone | String | No | Customer's configured time zone where applicable. |
| Request ID | UUID / String | Yes | Unique identifier for request traceability. |
| Correlation ID | UUID / String | No | Identifier used for distributed request tracing across platform services. |
| IP Address | String | Yes | Source IP address of the customer request. |
| User Agent | String | No | Client browser or application identification information. |

**Field Notes**

1. **Customer ID** shall always reference the authenticated customer's own account.
2. **Preferred Language Code** shall match one of the languages configured as supported by the platform.
3. Unsupported, disabled, deprecated, malformed, or invalid language codes shall be rejected.
4. **Preferred Language Name** is optional and may be derived from the configured language catalog.
5. **Locale** may be automatically associated with the selected language when regional localization is supported.
6. **Fallback Language** shall be maintained by platform configuration and shall not normally be provided by customers.
7. **Language Version** may be used to prevent concurrent update conflicts.
8. **Access Token** shall be validated before processing the request.
9. **Session ID** shall correspond to an active authenticated customer session.
10. **Device ID**, **Device Type**, **Application Version**, **IP Address**, and **User Agent** may be recorded for security monitoring, auditing, analytics, and troubleshooting.
11. **Request ID** shall uniquely identify each Language Preferences operation.
12. **Correlation ID** shall enable end-to-end tracing across distributed platform services.
13. Sensitive authentication information shall never be exposed in logs, API responses, or audit records.
14. All input fields shall undergo validation, sanitization, and authorization checks before processing.
15. All requests shall be transmitted over secure HTTPS connections.

#### 3.1.20.6 Business Rules

1. Only authenticated customers shall be permitted to view or modify their Language Preferences.
2. Customers shall be authorized to manage only their own language preferences.
3. Each customer account shall have only one active preferred language at any given time.
4. The selected language shall exist within the platform's list of supported languages.
5. Unsupported, disabled, deprecated, or invalid language selections shall be rejected.
6. If no language preference has been configured, the platform shall automatically assign the configured default language.
7. If a customer's preferred language becomes unavailable, the platform shall automatically switch to the configured fallback language.
8. Language preference updates shall affect only future customer interactions.
9. Historical orders, invoices, audit records, completed transactions, legal documents, and previously generated communications shall never be modified due to a language preference change.
10. Language preference changes shall immediately apply to newly rendered user interface components after successful update.
11. Future notifications shall use the customer's updated preferred language whenever localized content is available.
12. Future email communications shall use the updated preferred language where multilingual templates are supported.
13. Future SMS communications shall use the updated preferred language where supported.
14. Future push notifications shall use the updated preferred language where localized templates exist.
15. Customer support systems may retrieve the preferred language to improve multilingual customer assistance.
16. Marketing communications shall utilize the preferred language only where localized campaign content is available.
17. Dependent platform services shall retrieve the latest stored language preference from the centralized customer profile.
18. Language preference updates shall not modify customer authentication credentials, contact information, payment information, addresses, privacy settings, or any unrelated profile data.
19. Every successful language preference update shall be recorded in the audit log.
20. Failed update attempts shall also be recorded where required by organizational security policies.
21. Authentication, authorization, validation, and business rule verification shall be completed before updating any language preference.
22. All language preference updates shall be executed as atomic database transactions.
23. Partial updates shall never be committed.
24. If any processing error occurs, the entire transaction shall be rolled back.
25. Concurrent update requests for the same customer shall be handled using optimistic or pessimistic concurrency control mechanisms.
26. The platform shall preserve data consistency across all authorized dependent services after successful synchronization.
27. Language preferences shall be stored using standardized language codes and locale conventions supported by the platform.
28. The platform shall support future addition of new languages without requiring changes to existing customer records.
29. Language preference configuration shall remain independent of customer location, device, browser, operating system, or network.
30. Requests shall be transmitted only through secure HTTPS connections.
31. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall remain protected throughout the language preference lifecycle.
32. Sensitive authentication credentials, access tokens, session identifiers, and internal implementation details shall never be exposed in responses, logs, or audit records.
33. Security-related events including unauthorized access attempts, suspicious modification attempts, and abnormal request patterns shall be monitored according to cybersecurity policies.
34. All language preference operations shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, security, accessibility, localization, audit, data protection, and legal/regulatory requirements.
35. The platform shall support internationalization (i18n) and localization (l10n) standards to ensure consistent multilingual behavior across all customer-facing services.
36. Locale-specific formatting such as dates, time, numbers, currency display, and regional conventions may be applied according to the configured language and locale where supported.
37. Platform administrators may modify the list of supported languages through authorized administrative functions without affecting historical customer records.
38. The system shall maintain complete traceability of all language preference modifications for auditing, compliance, operational monitoring, and troubleshooting purposes.

#### 3.1.20.7 Validations

| Validation | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| Authentication Validation | The customer shall be successfully authenticated before accessing or modifying Language Preferences. |
| Access Token Validation | The access token shall be valid, active, and unexpired. |
| Session Validation | The customer session shall be active and associated with the authenticated customer account. |
| Customer Validation | The customer account shall exist and be active. |
| Authorization Validation | Customers shall be authorized to modify only their own language preferences. |
| Preferred Language Validation | The selected language shall not be null or empty. |
| Supported Language Validation | The selected language shall exist within the platform's configured list of supported languages. |
| Disabled Language Validation | Disabled or deprecated languages shall not be accepted. |
| Language Code Validation | The language code shall conform to the platform's supported language code format (e.g., ISO language codes where applicable). |
| Locale Validation | If a locale is provided, it shall correspond to the selected language and supported regional configuration. |
| Default Language Validation | If no language preference exists, the configured default language shall be assigned automatically. |
| Fallback Language Validation | If the selected language becomes unavailable, the configured fallback language shall be available and valid. |
| Duplicate Update Validation | Updating to the customer's currently configured language shall not result in unnecessary database modifications. |
| Concurrent Update Validation | Simultaneous language preference updates for the same customer shall be detected and handled safely. |
| Input Validation | All request fields shall satisfy mandatory field, datatype, length, and format requirements. |
| Request Integrity Validation | Request payloads shall be validated for completeness and integrity before processing. |
| Sanitization Validation | All input values shall be sanitized to prevent malicious input and injection attacks. |
| HTTPS Validation | Requests shall be accepted only through secure HTTPS connections. |
| Service Availability Validation | Required backend services shall be operational before processing the request. |
| Database Validation | The customer profile and language preference records shall be accessible for reading and updating. |
| Configuration Validation | Supported language configurations shall be successfully loaded from the configuration management service. |
| Localization Resource Validation | Required localization resources shall be available for the selected language before activation. |
| Synchronization Validation | Updated language preferences shall be eligible for synchronization with authorized dependent platform services. |
| Audit Validation | Audit logging shall be available to record successful and failed language preference operations. |
| Security Validation | Security monitoring and policy enforcement shall be active throughout the request lifecycle. |
| Privacy Validation | Customer language preference information shall be processed according to applicable privacy and data protection requirements. |
| Compliance Validation | The operation shall satisfy applicable authentication, authorization, accessibility, localization, audit, security, privacy, and regulatory requirements before completion. |

#### 3.1.20.8 Main Flow

1. The customer navigates to **Profile → Settings → Language Preferences**.
2. The system authenticates the customer and validates the active session.
3. The system authorizes the customer to access only their own Language Preferences.
4. The Language Preferences Service retrieves the customer's currently configured preferred language.
5. The system retrieves the latest list of supported languages from the Configuration Management Service.
6. The Localization (i18n) Service retrieves the language metadata, localized names, and available locale configurations.
7. The current preferred language and available language options are displayed to the customer.
8. The customer selects a new preferred language or retains the existing language.
9. The customer submits the language preference update request.
10. The system validates all mandatory input fields.
11. The system verifies that the selected language exists within the supported language configuration.
12. The system verifies that the selected language is active and not disabled or deprecated.
13. The system validates any associated locale configuration, where applicable.
14. The system verifies that the customer is authorized to modify the requested language preference.
15. The system checks for duplicate updates to determine whether the selected language is already configured.
16. If no changes are detected, the system completes the request without modifying existing records.
17. If a new language has been selected, the system initiates a database transaction.
18. The customer's preferred language is updated within the Customer Profile.
19. Associated locale information is updated where applicable.
20. The updated language preference is synchronized with authorized dependent platform services.
21. The Notification Service retrieves the updated language preference for future notifications.
22. The Email Service retrieves the updated language preference for future email communications where multilingual templates are available.
23. The SMS Service retrieves the updated language preference for future SMS communications where supported.
24. The Push Notification Service retrieves the updated language preference for future push notifications.
25. The Customer Support Service retrieves the updated language preference for multilingual customer assistance.
26. The Marketing Communication Service retrieves the updated language preference for future localized campaigns where applicable.
27. The Order Management Service retrieves the updated language preference for future customer-facing order communications.
28. The Payment Service retrieves the updated language preference for supported payment-related customer communications.
29. The Invoice & Billing Service retrieves the updated language preference for future localized billing communications where supported.
30. The database transaction is successfully committed.
31. The Audit Logging Service records the successful language preference update.
32. The Security Monitoring Service records applicable operational and security events.
33. The system refreshes the customer's active session context to use the updated language where applicable.
34. Newly rendered user interface components begin using the updated preferred language.
35. Future customer-facing communications utilize the updated language preference whenever localized content is available.
36. The system returns a successful response confirming completion of the Language Preferences update.

#### 3.1.20.9 Alternate Flows

**Alternate Flow 1: No Language Preference Updated**

1. The customer opens the Language Preferences page.
2. The current preferred language is displayed.
3. The customer reviews the available language options.
4. The customer exits the page without making any changes.
5. The existing language preference remains unchanged.

---

**Alternate Flow 2: Same Language Selected**

1. The customer selects the language that is already configured as the preferred language.
2. The system detects that no changes are required.
3. No database update is performed.
4. The system returns a successful response indicating that the selected language is already active.

---

**Alternate Flow 3: Unsupported Language Selected**

1. The customer selects a language that is not supported by the platform.
2. The system validates the selected language.
3. The validation fails.
4. The system rejects the request.
5. The customer is prompted to select a supported language.

---

**Alternate Flow 4: Disabled or Deprecated Language**

1. The customer selects a language that has been disabled or deprecated.
2. The system verifies the language configuration.
3. The selected language is determined to be unavailable.
4. The request is rejected.
5. The customer is instructed to select another supported language.

---

**Alternate Flow 5: Fallback Language Applied**

1. The customer's previously configured preferred language becomes unavailable.
2. The platform detects the unavailable language during profile retrieval or application initialization.
3. The configured fallback language is automatically assigned.
4. The customer continues using the platform without service interruption.
5. Future interactions use the fallback language until the customer selects another supported language.

---

**Alternate Flow 6: Concurrent Language Preference Update**

1. Multiple requests attempt to update the customer's language preference simultaneously.
2. The system detects the concurrent modification.
3. Concurrency control mechanisms are applied.
4. One request completes successfully.
5. Remaining conflicting requests are rejected or retried according to platform policy.

---

**Alternate Flow 7: Configuration Service Unavailable**

1. The customer submits a language preference update.
2. The Configuration Management Service is unavailable.
3. The system cannot validate the selected language.
4. The update request is rejected.
5. The customer is informed to retry later.

---

**Alternate Flow 8: Localization Resources Unavailable**

1. The selected language is supported.
2. Required localization resources are temporarily unavailable.
3. The system temporarily applies the configured fallback language where applicable.
4. The customer's stored language preference remains unchanged until localization resources become available, or the request is processed according to platform policy.

---

**Alternate Flow 9: Database Transaction Failure**

1. The language preference update passes all validations.
2. A database error occurs during the update.
3. The active transaction is rolled back.
4. The existing language preference remains unchanged.
5. The system returns an appropriate failure response.

---

**Alternate Flow 10: Dependent Service Synchronization Failure**

1. The language preference is successfully updated.
2. One or more dependent platform services are temporarily unavailable.
3. The primary language preference update is committed successfully.
4. Synchronization with affected dependent services is retried according to platform synchronization policies.
5. The system records the synchronization event for monitoring and operational follow-up.

#### 3.1.20.10 Postconditions

1. The customer's preferred language shall be successfully updated upon completion of all authentication, authorization, validation, and business rule checks.
2. The updated language preference shall be permanently stored within the customer's profile.
3. The customer's previous language preference shall be replaced by the newly selected supported language.
4. Only one preferred language shall remain active for the customer account at any given time.
5. The updated language preference shall be immediately available for newly rendered user interface components where applicable.
6. Future notifications, emails, SMS messages, push notifications, customer support interactions, marketing communications, and other supported customer-facing services shall utilize the updated preferred language whenever localized content is available.
7. Historical orders, invoices, completed transactions, legal documents, audit records, notifications, emails, and previously generated communications shall remain unchanged.
8. The updated language preference shall be synchronized with all authorized dependent platform services.
9. Locale-specific configurations associated with the selected language shall be applied where supported.
10. Customer profile information unrelated to language preferences shall remain unchanged.
11. All authentication, authorization, validation, configuration, localization, and business rule verifications shall have completed successfully.
12. Every successful language preference modification shall be recorded within the Audit Logging Service.
13. Applicable operational events and security events shall be recorded by the Security Monitoring Service.
14. The customer's active session context may be refreshed to apply the updated language preference immediately where supported.
15. Customer language preference data shall remain protected according to applicable privacy, security, accessibility, localization, and data protection policies.
16. Sensitive authentication credentials, access tokens, session identifiers, internal implementation details, and security information shall never be exposed in API responses, logs, or audit records.
17. If any failure occurs before transaction completion, all pending updates shall be rolled back automatically.
18. Previously stored language preferences shall remain unchanged whenever transaction rollback occurs.
19. The platform shall remain in a consistent state regardless of whether the Language Preferences operation succeeds or fails.
20. The Language Preferences functionality shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, cybersecurity, accessibility, localization, audit, data protection, and legal/regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.20.11 Success Response

Upon successful completion of the Language Preferences operation, the system shall return a success response confirming that the customer's preferred language has been successfully updated. The response shall include only authorized customer information and shall not expose any sensitive authentication, security, or internal implementation details.

**Response Attributes:**

| Field | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Status | Success |
| HTTP Status Code | 200 OK |
| Response Code | LANGUAGE_PREFERENCE_UPDATED_SUCCESSFULLY |
| Message | Language preference updated successfully. |
| Customer ID | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. |
| Preferred Language Code | Updated language code selected by the customer. |
| Preferred Language Name | Display name of the selected language. |
| Locale | Locale associated with the selected language, where applicable. |
| Fallback Language | Configured fallback language for the selected language. |
| Effective From | Timestamp from which the updated language preference becomes effective. |
| Request ID | Unique identifier of the processed request. |
| Transaction ID | Unique identifier of the completed transaction. |
| Updated Timestamp | Date and time when the language preference was successfully updated. |
| Audit Status | Indicates whether the audit log entry was successfully created. |

**Example Response**

```json
{
  "status": "SUCCESS",
  "statusCode": 200,
  "responseCode": "LANGUAGE_PREFERENCE_UPDATED_SUCCESSFULLY",
  "message": "Language preference updated successfully.",
  "customerId": "CUS-102458",
  "preferredLanguageCode": "ta-IN",
  "preferredLanguageName": "Tamil",
  "locale": "ta-IN",
  "fallbackLanguage": "en-IN",
  "effectiveFrom": "2026-01-20T09:15:00Z",
  "requestId": "REQ-202601200001",
  "transactionId": "TXN-202601200001",
  "updatedTimestamp": "2026-01-20T09:15:01Z",
  "auditStatus": "RECORDED"
}
```

**Success Conditions**

- The customer has been successfully authenticated and authorized.
- The selected language has passed all validation and business rule checks.
- The selected language exists within the platform's supported language configuration.
- The customer's preferred language has been successfully updated in the customer profile.
- Locale information has been updated where applicable.
- The updated language preference has been synchronized with authorized dependent platform services.
- Future customer-facing interactions shall utilize the updated preferred language wherever multilingual support is available.
- Audit logging and operational monitoring events have been successfully recorded.
- The transaction has been successfully committed without any data integrity violations.
- No sensitive authentication credentials, access tokens, session identifiers, security information, or internal implementation details shall be included in the response.

#### 3.1.20.12 Failure Response

The system shall return an appropriate failure response whenever the Language Preferences request cannot be completed due to authentication failures, authorization failures, validation errors, configuration issues, business rule violations, service unavailability, security violations, or unexpected system failures.

**Response Attributes:**

| Field | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Status | Failure |
| HTTP Status Code | Appropriate HTTP status code based on the error. |
| Response Code | Unique application-specific error code. |
| Error Message | Human-readable description of the failure. |
| Error Category | Classification of the error (Authentication, Authorization, Validation, Configuration, Business Rule, Security, System, etc.). |
| Customer ID | Authenticated customer identifier where applicable. |
| Request ID | Unique identifier of the failed request. |
| Timestamp | Date and time when the failure occurred. |
| Retry Allowed | Indicates whether the customer may retry the request. |
| Audit Status | Indicates whether the failure event was recorded in the audit log. |

**Common Failure Scenarios**

| Scenario | HTTP Status | Response Code | Message |
|----------|-------------|---------------|---------|
| Customer not authenticated | 401 Unauthorized | AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED | Authentication required. |
| Invalid or expired access token | 401 Unauthorized | INVALID_ACCESS_TOKEN | Invalid or expired authentication token. |
| Session expired | 401 Unauthorized | SESSION_EXPIRED | Your session has expired. Please log in again. |
| Customer account not found | 404 Not Found | CUSTOMER_NOT_FOUND | Customer account not found. |
| Unauthorized access | 403 Forbidden | ACCESS_DENIED | You are not authorized to modify language preferences. |
| Preferred language not specified | 400 Bad Request | LANGUAGE_REQUIRED | Preferred language is required. |
| Unsupported language selected | 400 Bad Request | UNSUPPORTED_LANGUAGE | Selected language is not supported. |
| Disabled or deprecated language selected | 400 Bad Request | LANGUAGE_NOT_AVAILABLE | Selected language is currently unavailable. |
| Invalid language code | 400 Bad Request | INVALID_LANGUAGE_CODE | Invalid language code. |
| Invalid locale configuration | 400 Bad Request | INVALID_LOCALE | Invalid locale configuration. |
| Language configuration unavailable | 503 Service Unavailable | LANGUAGE_CONFIGURATION_UNAVAILABLE | Language configuration service is temporarily unavailable. |
| Localization resources unavailable | 503 Service Unavailable | LOCALIZATION_RESOURCES_UNAVAILABLE | Localization resources are temporarily unavailable. |
| Concurrent update detected | 409 Conflict | CONCURRENT_UPDATE_DETECTED | Another language preference update is currently in progress. |
| Request validation failed | 400 Bad Request | INVALID_REQUEST | Invalid language preference request. |
| Database operation failed | 500 Internal Server Error | DATABASE_OPERATION_FAILED | Unable to update language preference. |
| Dependent service unavailable | 503 Service Unavailable | DEPENDENT_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE | Required platform service is temporarily unavailable. |
| Internal server error | 500 Internal Server Error | INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR | An unexpected error occurred while processing the request. |

**Example Failure Response**

```json
{
  "status": "FAILURE",
  "statusCode": 400,
  "responseCode": "UNSUPPORTED_LANGUAGE",
  "errorCategory": "VALIDATION",
  "message": "Selected language is not supported.",
  "customerId": "CUS-102458",
  "requestId": "REQ-202601200012",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-20T10:18:45Z",
  "retryAllowed": true,
  "auditStatus": "RECORDED"
}
```

**Failure Handling Rules**

- The system shall immediately terminate processing when authentication, authorization, validation, configuration, or business rule verification fails.
- Invalid, unsupported, disabled, or deprecated language selections shall never be stored.
- Previously configured language preferences shall remain unchanged whenever the update operation fails.
- No partial updates shall be committed to the database.
- Any active transaction shall be completely rolled back upon failure.
- Sensitive authentication credentials, access tokens, session identifiers, internal implementation details, infrastructure information, database structures, and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall never be exposed in failure responses.
- Applicable failure events shall be recorded within audit logs according to organizational security and compliance policies.
- Security-related failures shall be forwarded to security monitoring systems where applicable.
- Failure responses shall comply with the platform's authentication, authorization, privacy, security, accessibility, localization, audit, cybersecurity, data protection, and applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.20.13 Non-Functional Considerations

##### Performance

1. The system shall process Language Preferences requests within **3 seconds** under normal operating conditions.
2. Retrieval of the customer's preferred language shall occur with minimal latency to ensure a responsive user experience.
3. Language preference updates shall be optimized using efficient database queries and indexing mechanisms.
4. The platform shall support concurrent language preference requests from multiple customers without performance degradation.
5. Authorized dependent services shall retrieve updated language preferences with minimal synchronization delay.
6. The platform shall maintain acceptable response times during peak business hours.

##### Availability

7. The Language Preferences functionality shall maintain a minimum availability of **99.9%**, excluding scheduled maintenance.
8. If localization resources become temporarily unavailable, the platform shall continue operating using the configured fallback language where applicable.
9. Temporary service failures shall not prevent customers from accessing the platform.

##### Scalability

10. The platform shall support millions of customer language preference records without requiring architectural redesign.
11. The Localization (i18n) Service shall support horizontal scaling as multilingual usage increases.
12. The solution shall support future addition of new languages, locales, and regional configurations without affecting existing customer accounts.

##### Reliability

13. Language preference updates shall be executed as fully atomic database transactions.
14. Customer language preferences shall remain consistent across all authorized dependent platform services.
15. Transaction failures shall automatically trigger rollback operations.
16. Temporary service failures shall not compromise customer profile integrity.
17. The platform shall ensure consistency between customer profiles and synchronized language preferences.

##### Security

18. All Language Preferences requests shall be transmitted exclusively over secure HTTPS connections.
19. Authentication and authorization shall be enforced before processing every request.
20. Customer profile information and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall remain protected during all language preference operations.
21. Sensitive authentication credentials, access tokens, session identifiers, and internal security information shall never be stored or logged in plain text.
22. The platform shall protect against unauthorized access, injection attacks, cross-site scripting (XSS), cross-site request forgery (CSRF), parameter tampering, replay attacks, session hijacking, and other common security threats.
23. Security events shall be logged and monitored according to organizational cybersecurity policies.
24. Access to language preference data shall be restricted to authorized users and platform services only.

##### Privacy

25. Customer language preference information shall be processed according to applicable privacy regulations and organizational data protection policies.
26. Only authorized platform services shall access customer language preference information.
27. Data retention, archival, masking, and deletion shall comply with applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

##### Internationalization & Localization

28. The platform shall support internationally recognized language and locale standards where applicable.
29. Locale-specific formatting for dates, times, numbers, currency, and regional conventions shall be applied according to the configured language and locale where supported.
30. The platform shall support future expansion into additional languages without requiring significant architectural modifications.
31. Localization resources shall remain centrally managed to ensure consistency across all customer-facing applications.

##### Audit & Compliance

32. All successful and failed language preference operations shall be recorded within immutable audit logs.
33. Audit records shall support operational monitoring, compliance reporting, security investigations, and forensic analysis.
34. Audit logs shall be protected against unauthorized modification or deletion.
35. The Language Preferences functionality shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, security, accessibility, localization, audit, cybersecurity, data protection, and regulatory requirements.

##### Maintainability

36. The Language Preferences functionality shall be implemented using modular, loosely coupled services.
37. Supported languages, fallback languages, localization resources, and locale configurations shall be configurable without application code changes wherever practical.
38. Future multilingual enhancements shall require minimal impact on existing platform components.

##### Monitoring & Observability

39. The platform shall continuously monitor request processing time, service availability, synchronization status, localization resource availability, error rates, and abnormal activity.
40. Critical failures, localization service outages, configuration inconsistencies, and security events shall generate operational alerts.
41. Logs, metrics, and traces shall provide complete end-to-end observability of the Language Preferences workflow.

##### Backup & Recovery

42. Customer language preference data and localization configuration shall be included in scheduled backup procedures where applicable.
43. Recovery mechanisms shall preserve customer language preference integrity following infrastructure failures or disaster recovery events.
44. Backup and restoration procedures shall comply with the platform's business continuity and disaster recovery policies.

#### 3.1.20.14 Acceptance Criteria

| AC ID | Acceptance Criteria |
|-------|----------------------|
| AC-LP-001 | The system shall allow only authenticated customers to access the Language Preferences functionality. |
| AC-LP-002 | The system shall authorize customers to modify only their own language preferences. |
| AC-LP-003 | The system shall display the customer's currently configured preferred language upon successful access. |
| AC-LP-004 | The system shall display only languages configured as supported by the platform. |
| AC-LP-005 | The system shall allow the customer to select one preferred language at a time. |
| AC-LP-006 | The system shall validate the selected language before processing the update request. |
| AC-LP-007 | The system shall reject unsupported, disabled, deprecated, malformed, or invalid language selections. |
| AC-LP-008 | The system shall automatically assign the configured default language when no language preference exists. |
| AC-LP-009 | The system shall automatically apply the configured fallback language whenever the customer's preferred language becomes unavailable. |
| AC-LP-010 | The system shall update the customer's preferred language only after all authentication, authorization, validation, and business rule checks have passed. |
| AC-LP-011 | The system shall immediately use the updated language for newly rendered user interface components where supported. |
| AC-LP-012 | Future notifications, emails, SMS messages, push notifications, and customer-facing communications shall utilize the updated preferred language whenever localized content is available. |
| AC-LP-013 | Historical orders, invoices, completed transactions, legal documents, notifications, audit records, and previously generated communications shall remain unchanged following a language preference update. |
| AC-LP-014 | The system shall synchronize updated language preferences with authorized dependent platform services after successful completion. |
| AC-LP-015 | Locale-specific configurations shall be applied according to the selected language where supported. |
| AC-LP-016 | Customer profile information unrelated to language preferences shall remain unchanged after the update. |
| AC-LP-017 | Language preference updates shall be executed as atomic database transactions. |
| AC-LP-018 | Partial database updates shall never be committed. |
| AC-LP-019 | Failed transactions shall automatically roll back all pending changes. |
| AC-LP-020 | The system shall detect and safely handle concurrent language preference update requests. |
| AC-LP-021 | The system shall return standardized success responses for successfully completed language preference updates. |
| AC-LP-022 | The system shall return standardized failure responses for authentication failures, authorization failures, validation failures, configuration failures, business rule violations, and system errors. |
| AC-LP-023 | Sensitive authentication credentials, access tokens, session identifiers, security information, and internal implementation details shall never be exposed in API responses. |
| AC-LP-024 | All communication between clients and platform services shall occur over secure HTTPS connections. |
| AC-LP-025 | Customer language preference information and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be protected according to organizational security and privacy policies. |
| AC-LP-026 | Every successful and failed language preference operation shall be recorded in immutable audit logs. |
| AC-LP-027 | Security-related events including unauthorized access attempts, suspicious modification attempts, and abnormal request patterns shall be monitored and logged. |
| AC-LP-028 | The Language Preferences functionality shall maintain the platform's defined service availability objectives. |
| AC-LP-029 | The functionality shall support concurrent customer requests without compromising data consistency or integrity. |
| AC-LP-030 | The platform shall support future addition of languages and locale configurations without requiring major architectural changes. |
| AC-LP-031 | The functionality shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, cybersecurity, accessibility, localization, audit, data protection, and legal/regulatory requirements. |
| AC-LP-032 | The Language Preferences module shall successfully pass all functional, integration, security, performance, regression, localization, accessibility, and user acceptance testing before production deployment. |

### 3.1.21 Notification Preferences

#### 3.1.21.1 Description

The **Notification Preferences** module enables authenticated customers to configure and manage how, when, and through which communication channels they receive platform notifications. This functionality provides customers with granular control over transactional, operational, promotional, and account-related notifications while ensuring that mandatory service communications continue to be delivered in accordance with business, legal, and regulatory requirements.

The platform shall maintain a centralized notification preference profile for every customer account. Customers shall be able to enable or disable supported notification categories, configure preferred notification channels, define notification delivery preferences, and manage opt-in or opt-out settings for eligible communications. Notification preferences shall be consistently applied across web applications, mobile applications, APIs, notification services, customer support systems, marketing platforms, and other authorized platform services.

The Notification Preferences functionality shall support multiple communication channels including push notifications, email, SMS, and future supported notification mechanisms. The platform shall distinguish between mandatory notifications (such as order confirmations, payment receipts, OTPs, security alerts, password reset requests, and account-related communications) and optional notifications (such as promotional offers, recommendations, newsletters, surveys, loyalty campaigns, and marketing communications). Mandatory notifications shall remain unaffected by customer opt-out preferences wherever required by business or regulatory policies.

Notification preference updates shall affect only future notifications and shall never modify previously delivered notifications, completed transactions, invoices, audit records, legal communications, or historical customer activity. Authorized platform services shall retrieve the customer's latest notification preferences before generating eligible communications to ensure consistent behavior across the platform.

All notification preference modifications shall be protected through authentication, authorization, validation, and audit controls. Every successful or failed update shall be recorded within the platform's audit logging framework to support compliance, operational monitoring, troubleshooting, and security investigations. Customer notification preferences and related personal information shall remain protected in accordance with applicable privacy, cybersecurity, accessibility, communication, and data protection standards.

The Notification Preferences module shall be designed to support future notification categories, communication channels, delivery schedules, localization requirements, customer segmentation, intelligent notification policies, and enterprise-scale communication services without requiring significant architectural changes.

#### 3.1.21.2 Actors

The following actors participate in the Notification Preferences functionality:

| Actor | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Customer | Authenticated customer who views or manages notification preferences. |
| Authentication Service | Authenticates the customer before allowing access to Notification Preferences. |
| Authorization Service | Ensures only the account owner is authorized to modify notification preferences. |
| Customer Profile Service | Retrieves and stores the customer's notification preference configuration. |
| Notification Preferences Service | Manages notification preference creation, retrieval, validation, and updates. |
| Notification Service | Applies customer notification preferences before delivering notifications. |
| Push Notification Service | Sends mobile and web push notifications according to configured preferences. |
| Email Service | Sends email notifications based on customer notification preferences. |
| SMS Service | Sends SMS notifications according to the customer's configured preferences. |
| Order Management Service | Generates transactional order notifications while respecting applicable notification preferences. |
| Payment Service | Generates payment-related notifications in accordance with mandatory communication policies. |
| Delivery Management Service | Generates delivery status notifications based on customer preferences and business rules. |
| Customer Support Service | Retrieves notification preferences during customer support interactions where applicable. |
| Marketing Communication Service | Sends promotional campaigns, offers, newsletters, surveys, and marketing communications according to customer opt-in preferences. |
| Loyalty & Rewards Service | Generates loyalty program notifications based on customer notification preferences. |
| Recommendation Service | Sends personalized recommendations where the customer has opted to receive them. |
| Security Service | Generates mandatory security notifications such as OTPs, login alerts, password reset notifications, suspicious activity alerts, and account security communications. |
| Localization (i18n) Service | Provides localized notification content based on the customer's configured language preferences where supported. |
| Audit Logging Service | Records all successful and failed notification preference operations for compliance and auditing purposes. |
| Security Monitoring Service | Monitors unauthorized access attempts, suspicious activities, notification abuse, and security-related events. |
| Configuration Management Service | Maintains notification categories, communication channels, delivery policies, mandatory notification rules, and platform configuration. |
| Data Privacy & Compliance Service | Ensures notification preference management complies with applicable privacy, communication, security, accessibility, and regulatory requirements. |

#### 3.1.21.3 Preconditions

Before a customer can access or modify Notification Preferences, the following preconditions shall be satisfied:

1. The customer shall have a registered customer account.
2. The customer account shall be active and not permanently deleted.
3. The customer shall be successfully authenticated.
4. The customer shall possess a valid authenticated session.
5. The customer's access token shall be valid and unexpired.
6. The customer shall be authorized to modify only their own notification preferences.
7. The Notification Preferences module shall be enabled within the platform configuration.
8. The Customer Profile Service shall be available.
9. The Notification Preferences Service shall be operational.
10. The Notification Service shall be available.
11. The Push Notification Service shall be operational where supported.
12. The Email Service shall be operational.
13. The SMS Service shall be operational where supported.
14. The Configuration Management Service shall provide the current notification categories, communication channels, mandatory notification policies, and platform rules.
15. At least one notification delivery channel shall be configured within the platform.
16. Mandatory notification categories shall be configured and protected from unauthorized modification.
17. The customer profile shall be retrievable from the database.
18. The customer's current notification preferences shall be available for retrieval and update.
19. Supported notification channels shall be configured and enabled within the platform.
20. The database shall be available for reading and updating notification preference records.
21. Communication between the client and backend services shall occur over secure HTTPS connections.
22. The Audit Logging Service shall be available to record notification preference operations.
23. The Security Monitoring Service shall be available to capture security-related events.
24. Required validation services shall be operational.
25. Authorized dependent platform services shall be capable of retrieving updated notification preferences before sending eligible customer communications.
26. Mandatory business and regulatory notification policies shall be available for validation during preference updates.
27. The platform shall satisfy applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, communication, security, accessibility, audit, and compliance requirements before processing the request.
28. No conflicting notification preference update transaction shall currently be in progress for the same customer account.
29. All required platform services shall be functioning within acceptable operational thresholds.

#### 3.1.21.4 Trigger

The Notification Preferences functionality shall be initiated whenever one of the following events occurs:

1. An authenticated customer navigates to **Profile → Settings → Notification Preferences**.
2. An authenticated customer requests to view their current notification preferences.
3. An authenticated customer submits a request to modify one or more notification preferences.
4. The customer enables or disables a supported notification category.
5. The customer changes the preferred notification delivery channel for eligible communications.
6. The customer opts in to receive optional promotional or marketing communications.
7. The customer opts out of eligible promotional or marketing communications.
8. The customer updates notification preferences after modifying contact information or communication settings.
9. The customer restores account settings and reviews notification preferences.
10. A web or mobile application initializes and retrieves the customer's notification preferences for future communications.
11. The Notification Service retrieves customer notification preferences before generating a notification.
12. The Push Notification Service retrieves the customer's notification preferences before sending push notifications.
13. The Email Service retrieves notification preferences before sending customer emails.
14. The SMS Service retrieves notification preferences before sending SMS notifications.
15. The Marketing Communication Service retrieves notification preferences before sending promotional campaigns, newsletters, surveys, loyalty campaigns, or personalized offers.
16. The Recommendation Service retrieves notification preferences before sending personalized recommendations.
17. The Order Management Service retrieves notification preferences before generating customer-facing order notifications.
18. The Delivery Management Service retrieves notification preferences before sending delivery updates.
19. The Payment Service retrieves notification preferences before sending payment confirmations, receipts, refunds, or billing notifications.
20. The Security Service retrieves notification preferences while enforcing mandatory security communications in accordance with business and regulatory requirements.
21. An administrator updates platform notification categories, communication channels, or notification policies requiring validation of existing customer preferences.
22. Customer profile synchronization retrieves or updates notification preferences across authorized platform services.
23. The platform restores customer profile information following backup or disaster recovery operations.
24. Any authorized platform service requires the customer's latest notification preferences before delivering an eligible customer communication.

#### 3.1.21.5 Input Fields

| Field | Type | Mandatory | Description |
|-------|------|-----------|-------------|
| Customer ID | UUID / String | Yes | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer whose notification preferences are being managed. |
| Notification Category | Enum / Array | Yes | Notification category to be configured (e.g., Order Updates, Delivery Updates, Payment Notifications, Promotions, Security Alerts, Loyalty Rewards, Recommendations). |
| Notification Channel | Enum / Array | Yes | Preferred communication channel(s) such as Push Notification, Email, SMS, or future supported channels. |
| Preference Status | Boolean / Enum | Yes | Indicates whether the selected notification category is enabled or disabled. |
| Mandatory Notification Override | Boolean | No | System-controlled indicator specifying whether the notification category is mandatory and cannot be disabled by the customer. |
| Delivery Preference | Enum | No | Preferred notification delivery option where multiple delivery methods are supported. |
| Language Preference | String | No | Language to be used for notifications where multilingual support is available. |
| Access Token | JWT / String | Yes | Valid authentication token identifying the authenticated customer. |
| Session ID | String | Yes | Active authenticated session identifier. |
| Device ID | String | No | Unique identifier of the customer's device. |
| Device Type | Enum | No | Device category such as Web, Android, iOS, or Tablet. |
| Application Version | String | No | Version of the client application initiating the request. |
| Request ID | UUID / String | Yes | Unique identifier for request traceability. |
| Correlation ID | UUID / String | No | Identifier used for distributed tracing across platform services. |
| IP Address | String | Yes | Source IP address of the customer request. |
| User Agent | String | No | Client browser or application identification information. |

**Field Notes**

1. **Customer ID** shall always reference the authenticated customer's own account.
2. **Notification Category** shall contain only platform-supported notification categories.
3. **Notification Channel** shall contain only supported communication channels configured by the platform.
4. **Preference Status** shall specify whether the selected notification category is enabled or disabled.
5. **Mandatory Notification Override** shall be managed exclusively by the platform and shall not be modifiable by customers.
6. Customers shall not be permitted to disable mandatory notifications required for security, authentication, order processing, payment processing, legal compliance, or regulatory obligations.
7. **Delivery Preference** may be specified only when multiple supported delivery options exist.
8. **Language Preference** may reference the customer's configured language preference for localized notifications where supported.
9. **Access Token** shall be validated before processing the request.
10. **Session ID** shall correspond to an active authenticated customer session.
11. **Device ID**, **Device Type**, **Application Version**, **IP Address**, and **User Agent** may be recorded for security monitoring, auditing, analytics, and troubleshooting.
12. **Request ID** shall uniquely identify every Notification Preferences operation.
13. **Correlation ID** shall enable end-to-end tracing across distributed platform services.
14. Sensitive authentication credentials, session identifiers, and internal security information shall never be exposed in API responses, logs, or audit records.
15. All input fields shall undergo authentication, authorization, validation, sanitization, and business rule verification before processing.
16. All requests shall be transmitted exclusively over secure HTTPS connections.

#### 3.1.21.6 Business Rules

1. Only authenticated customers shall be permitted to view or modify their Notification Preferences.
2. Customers shall be authorized to manage only their own notification preferences.
3. The platform shall maintain a centralized notification preference profile for each customer account.
4. Notification preferences shall apply only to future notifications and communications.
5. Previously delivered notifications, completed transactions, invoices, audit records, and historical communications shall never be modified.
6. Customers may enable or disable only optional notification categories supported by the platform.
7. Mandatory notifications required for authentication, security, account protection, payment processing, order processing, legal obligations, regulatory compliance, or other critical platform operations shall not be disabled by customers.
8. The platform shall distinguish between mandatory and optional notification categories.
9. Customers may configure one or more supported notification delivery channels for eligible notification categories.
10. Only communication channels configured and enabled by the platform shall be available for customer selection.
11. Unsupported notification categories or communication channels shall be rejected.
12. Disabled or deprecated notification categories or communication channels shall not be accepted.
13. The Notification Service shall retrieve the customer's latest notification preferences before sending eligible notifications.
14. Order Management, Delivery Management, Payment, Loyalty, Recommendation, Customer Support, and other authorized platform services shall retrieve the customer's latest notification preferences before generating eligible customer communications.
15. Marketing communications, promotional offers, newsletters, surveys, loyalty campaigns, and personalized recommendations shall be delivered only when the customer has explicitly opted in, where required by applicable policies.
16. Customers may opt out of optional marketing communications at any time.
17. Changes to notification preferences shall become effective immediately after successful transaction completion.
18. Notification preference updates shall not modify customer profile information unrelated to notification settings.
19. Every successful notification preference modification shall be recorded within the audit log.
20. Failed notification preference update attempts shall also be recorded where required by organizational security policies.
21. Authentication, authorization, validation, and business rule verification shall be completed before any notification preference is updated.
22. All notification preference updates shall be executed as atomic database transactions.
23. Partial updates shall never be committed.
24. If any processing error occurs, the active transaction shall be rolled back automatically.
25. Concurrent notification preference update requests for the same customer shall be handled using appropriate concurrency control mechanisms.
26. Updated notification preferences shall be synchronized with authorized dependent platform services following successful completion.
27. Notification preferences shall remain consistent across all authorized customer-facing services.
28. Notification delivery shall honor customer preferences only where business rules and regulatory requirements permit.
29. Mandatory security notifications, including OTPs, password reset notifications, suspicious login alerts, account recovery notifications, and similar security communications, shall always be delivered regardless of optional notification preferences.
30. Notification preference configuration shall remain independent of customer device, browser, operating system, network, or geographical location.
31. All requests shall be transmitted exclusively over secure HTTPS connections.
32. Customer notification preferences and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall remain protected throughout the entire notification preference lifecycle.
33. Sensitive authentication credentials, access tokens, session identifiers, internal implementation details, and security information shall never be exposed in responses, logs, or audit records.
34. Security-related events including unauthorized access attempts, abnormal preference modifications, notification abuse, and suspicious activities shall be monitored according to organizational cybersecurity policies.
35. The platform shall support future notification categories, communication channels, customer segmentation, delivery strategies, and intelligent notification capabilities without requiring significant architectural redesign.
36. Notification templates shall utilize the customer's configured language preference where multilingual support is available.
37. Platform administrators may modify notification categories, mandatory notification policies, communication channels, and delivery rules through authorized administrative functions without affecting historical customer communications.
38. All notification preference operations shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, cybersecurity, accessibility, communication, audit, data protection, consumer protection, and legal/regulatory requirements.
39. The platform shall maintain complete traceability of all notification preference modifications for auditing, compliance, operational monitoring, and troubleshooting purposes.
40. Notification preference changes shall not interrupt or delay active order processing, payment processing, delivery operations, or other critical business workflows.

#### 3.1.21.7 Validations

| Validation | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| Authentication Validation | The customer shall be successfully authenticated before accessing or modifying Notification Preferences. |
| Access Token Validation | The access token shall be valid, active, and unexpired. |
| Session Validation | The customer session shall be active and associated with the authenticated customer account. |
| Customer Validation | The customer account shall exist and be active. |
| Authorization Validation | Customers shall be authorized to modify only their own notification preferences. |
| Notification Category Validation | The selected notification category shall exist within the platform's supported notification categories. |
| Mandatory Notification Validation | Mandatory notification categories shall not be disabled by customers. |
| Notification Channel Validation | The selected communication channel shall be supported and enabled by the platform. |
| Preference Status Validation | The preference status shall contain a valid supported value indicating whether the notification category is enabled or disabled. |
| Delivery Preference Validation | If specified, the selected delivery preference shall be supported for the chosen notification category. |
| Language Preference Validation | If a language preference is provided, it shall correspond to a supported platform language. |
| Duplicate Update Validation | Updating notification preferences without any actual changes shall not result in unnecessary database modifications. |
| Configuration Validation | Notification categories, communication channels, mandatory notification policies, and delivery rules shall be successfully loaded from the Configuration Management Service. |
| Business Rule Validation | Customer notification preferences shall comply with mandatory communication policies, business rules, and regulatory requirements. |
| Concurrent Update Validation | Simultaneous notification preference updates for the same customer shall be detected and handled safely. |
| Input Validation | All request fields shall satisfy mandatory field, datatype, length, format, and enumeration requirements. |
| Request Integrity Validation | Request payloads shall be validated for completeness and integrity before processing. |
| Sanitization Validation | All input values shall be sanitized to prevent malicious input, injection attacks, and parameter tampering. |
| HTTPS Validation | Requests shall be accepted only through secure HTTPS connections. |
| Service Availability Validation | Required backend services shall be operational before processing the request. |
| Database Validation | Customer profile and notification preference records shall be available for reading and updating. |
| Synchronization Validation | Updated notification preferences shall be eligible for synchronization with authorized dependent platform services. |
| Audit Validation | Audit logging shall be available to record successful and failed notification preference operations. |
| Security Validation | Security monitoring, rate limiting, abuse detection, and policy enforcement shall be active throughout the request lifecycle. |
| Privacy Validation | Customer notification preferences and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be processed according to applicable privacy and data protection requirements. |
| Compliance Validation | The operation shall satisfy applicable authentication, authorization, communication, accessibility, privacy, cybersecurity, audit, consumer protection, data protection, and regulatory requirements before completion. |

#### 3.1.21.8 Main Flow

1. The customer navigates to **Profile → Settings → Notification Preferences**.
2. The system authenticates the customer and validates the active session.
3. The system authorizes the customer to access only their own Notification Preferences.
4. The Notification Preferences Service retrieves the customer's current notification preference configuration.
5. The Configuration Management Service retrieves the latest supported notification categories, communication channels, mandatory notification policies, and delivery rules.
6. The system displays the customer's existing notification preferences.
7. The customer reviews the available notification categories and communication channels.
8. The customer enables or disables one or more optional notification categories.
9. The customer selects or updates the preferred notification delivery channels where supported.
10. The customer submits the notification preference update request.
11. The system validates all mandatory input fields.
12. The system validates the selected notification categories.
13. The system validates the selected communication channels.
14. The system verifies that mandatory notification categories are not being disabled.
15. The system validates notification delivery preferences according to configured business rules.
16. The system verifies that the customer is authorized to modify the requested notification preferences.
17. The system checks for duplicate updates to determine whether any changes have actually been made.
18. If no changes are detected, the system completes the request without modifying existing records.
19. If changes are detected, the system initiates a database transaction.
20. The customer's notification preferences are updated within the Customer Profile.
21. Updated notification preferences are synchronized with authorized dependent platform services.
22. The Notification Service retrieves the updated notification preferences for future notification delivery.
23. The Push Notification Service retrieves the updated preferences for future push notifications.
24. The Email Service retrieves the updated preferences for future email communications.
25. The SMS Service retrieves the updated preferences for future SMS communications where supported.
26. The Marketing Communication Service retrieves the updated preferences before sending future promotional campaigns, newsletters, surveys, loyalty campaigns, and marketing communications.
27. The Recommendation Service retrieves the updated preferences before sending personalized recommendations where applicable.
28. The Order Management Service retrieves the updated preferences before sending future order-related notifications.
29. The Delivery Management Service retrieves the updated preferences before sending future delivery status notifications.
30. The Payment Service retrieves the updated preferences before sending future payment confirmations, refunds, receipts, and billing notifications where applicable.
31. Mandatory security, authentication, legal, and regulatory notifications remain enabled according to platform policies regardless of optional customer preferences.
32. The database transaction is successfully committed.
33. The Audit Logging Service records the successful notification preference update.
