Certain communications required for authentication, account security, password resets, multi-factor authentication, payment confirmations, order placement, order status updates, delivery tracking, refunds, disputes, fraud prevention, legal obligations, regulatory compliance, emergency notifications, and other mandatory business operations shall always be delivered regardless of the customer's optional communication preferences.

The Communication Preferences functionality shall integrate with Notification Services, Marketing Services, Customer Profile Management, Recommendation Services, Loyalty Services, Customer Support Services, Analytics Services, Campaign Management Services, and other authorized platform components to ensure customer communication preferences are consistently enforced throughout the platform.

All communication preference modifications shall be protected through authentication, authorization, validation, audit logging, and security controls. Every successful and failed communication preference operation shall be recorded within the platform's audit logging framework to support compliance reporting, operational monitoring, troubleshooting, forensic investigations, and regulatory audits. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and customer communication data shall remain protected throughout the communication preference management lifecycle using appropriate security controls, encryption, access control mechanisms, and organizational data protection standards.

The Communication Preferences module shall be designed to support future communication channels, customer segmentation strategies, multilingual communication capabilities, campaign management enhancements, configurable communication policies, evolving regulatory requirements, consent management improvements, and additional communication features without requiring significant architectural modifications.

#### 3.1.23.2 Actors

The following actors participate in the Communication Preferences functionality:

| Actor | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Customer | Authenticated customer who views and manages communication preferences for their account. |
| Authentication Service | Authenticates the customer before allowing access to Communication Preferences. |
| Authorization Service | Ensures only the account owner is authorized to modify communication preferences. |
| Customer Profile Service | Retrieves and stores the customer's communication preferences. |
| Communication Preferences Service | Manages communication preference configuration, validation, business rules, and updates. |
| Notification Service | Delivers customer communications according to configured communication preferences where applicable. |
| Marketing Communication Service | Sends promotional campaigns, newsletters, offers, announcements, and marketing communications while honoring customer communication preferences. |
| Campaign Management Service | Uses customer communication preferences for campaign targeting, scheduling, and delivery. |
| Recommendation Service | Delivers personalized recommendations according to customer communication preferences where applicable. |
| Loyalty Service | Sends loyalty-related communications including reward updates, membership status, and promotional benefits according to supported communication preferences. |
| Customer Support Service | Retrieves communication preferences while assisting customers through authorized support operations. |
| Analytics Service | Uses communication preference data for communication analytics, delivery optimization, and reporting where permitted. |
| Email Service | Sends email communications according to configured customer preferences. |
| SMS Service | Sends SMS communications according to configured customer preferences. |
| Push Notification Service | Delivers push notifications according to customer communication preferences where applicable. |
| In-App Notification Service | Displays in-app communications according to configured customer preferences. |
| Audit Logging Service | Records all successful and failed communication preference operations for audit, compliance, and operational monitoring. |
| Security Monitoring Service | Monitors unauthorized access attempts, suspicious communication preference modifications, and security-related events. |
| Configuration Management Service | Maintains supported communication channels, communication categories, delivery policies, scheduling rules, and configurable platform settings. |
| Compliance & Governance Service | Ensures communication preference management complies with applicable privacy regulations, consumer protection laws, cybersecurity standards, accessibility requirements, communication policies, and organizational governance requirements. |

#### 3.1.23.3 Preconditions

Before a customer can access or modify Communication Preferences, the following preconditions shall be satisfied:

1. The customer shall have a registered customer account.
2. The customer account shall be active and not permanently deleted.
3. The customer shall be successfully authenticated.
4. The customer shall possess a valid authenticated session.
5. The customer's access token shall be valid and unexpired.
6. The customer shall be authorized to modify only the communication preferences associated with their own account.
7. The Communication Preferences module shall be enabled within the platform configuration.
8. The Customer Profile Service shall be available.
9. The Communication Preferences Service shall be operational.
10. The Notification Service shall be operational.
11. The Configuration Management Service shall provide the latest supported communication channels, communication categories, scheduling rules, and delivery policies.
12. The platform shall have default communication preferences configured for every newly registered customer account.
13. The customer's communication preference profile shall be retrievable from the database.
14. Supported communication channels shall be configured and available for customer selection.
15. Supported communication categories and communication frequency options shall be configured within the platform.
16. Mandatory communication categories required for authentication, account security, password recovery, payment processing, order processing, delivery tracking, fraud prevention, legal obligations, emergency notifications, and regulatory compliance shall be configured and protected from customer modification.
17. The database shall be available for reading and updating communication preference records.
18. Communication between client applications and backend services shall occur exclusively over secure HTTPS connections.
19. The Audit Logging Service shall be available to record communication preference operations.
20. The Security Monitoring Service shall be operational to capture security-related events.
21. Required validation services shall be operational.
22. Authorized dependent platform services shall be capable of retrieving updated communication preferences before sending eligible customer communications.
23. Campaign Management, Marketing Communication, Email, SMS, Push Notification, and In-App Notification services shall be available where applicable.
24. Applicable communication policies, privacy regulations, consent policies, consumer protection requirements, and organizational governance rules shall be available for validation.
25. No conflicting communication preference update transaction shall currently be in progress for the same customer account.
26. Supported communication channels shall have valid platform configurations before processing update requests.
27. System time shall be synchronized to accurately record audit timestamps, communication preference updates, and operational events.
28. All required platform services shall be functioning within acceptable operational thresholds.
29. The platform shall satisfy applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, cybersecurity, accessibility, communication, audit, consumer protection, and regulatory requirements before processing the request.

#### 3.1.23.4 Trigger

The Communication Preferences functionality shall be initiated when one of the following events occurs:

1. An authenticated customer selects the **Communication Preferences** option from the Account Settings or Customer Profile section.
2. An authenticated customer navigates to the Communication Preferences page using the mobile application or web portal.
3. An authenticated customer reviews communication preferences immediately after account registration, where required by platform policy.
4. An authenticated customer chooses to modify preferred communication channels for optional customer communications.
5. An authenticated customer updates communication frequency preferences for supported communication categories.
6. An authenticated customer enables or disables promotional communications.
7. An authenticated customer subscribes to or unsubscribes from newsletters, product announcements, educational content, surveys, loyalty communications, or other supported optional communication categories.
8. An authenticated customer updates preferred communication time windows where supported.
9. An authenticated customer modifies the preferred communication language where multilingual communication is supported.
10. The platform requests customers to review communication preferences following updates to communication policies, privacy policies, or applicable regulations.
11. The platform introduces new communication channels or communication categories requiring customer review or consent.
12. The platform detects missing, outdated, or incomplete communication preferences and requests the customer to update them.
13. An authorized customer support workflow directs the customer to review communication preferences while ensuring that only the customer can approve preference changes.
14. An authenticated API request is received from an authorized client application to retrieve or update the customer's communication preferences.
15. The customer restores default communication preferences.
16. The platform initiates a mandatory communication preference review following significant account security events such as password reset, account recovery, or successful two-factor authentication enrollment where applicable.
17. The platform requests communication preference confirmation due to regional regulatory changes, consumer protection requirements, or updated consent policies.
18. A supported platform feature requiring configurable communication preferences is accessed for the first time by the customer.

#### 3.1.23.5 Input Fields

| Field Name | Type | Required | Description |
|------------|------|----------|-------------|
| Customer ID | UUID | Auto | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. Retrieved from the authenticated session and cannot be modified by the customer. |
| Preferred Email Communication | Boolean | No | Indicates whether optional communications may be delivered via email. |
| Preferred SMS Communication | Boolean | No | Indicates whether optional communications may be delivered via SMS. |
| Preferred Push Notification | Boolean | No | Indicates whether optional communications may be delivered through push notifications. |
| Preferred In-App Notification | Boolean | No | Indicates whether optional communications may be delivered through in-app notifications. |
| Promotional Communication | Boolean | No | Indicates whether promotional offers and marketing campaigns may be sent. |
| Newsletter Subscription | Boolean | No | Indicates whether the customer wishes to receive newsletters. |
| Product Announcements | Boolean | No | Indicates whether product updates and feature announcements may be sent. |
| Educational Content Subscription | Boolean | No | Indicates whether educational articles, tips, and platform guides may be delivered. |
| Loyalty Communication | Boolean | No | Indicates whether loyalty program updates, rewards, and membership communications may be delivered. |
| Survey & Feedback Communication | Boolean | No | Indicates whether customer surveys and feedback requests may be sent. |
| Communication Frequency | Enum | No | Preferred communication frequency (e.g., Immediate, Daily, Weekly, Monthly). |
| Preferred Contact Time | Enum | No | Preferred communication time window (e.g., Morning, Afternoon, Evening, Anytime). |
| Preferred Communication Language | String | No | Preferred language for optional communications where multilingual support is available. |
| Do Not Disturb (DND) | Boolean | No | Indicates whether optional communications should be restricted during configured quiet hours where supported. |
| DND Start Time | Time | Conditional | Start time of the Do Not Disturb period when DND is enabled. |
| DND End Time | Time | Conditional | End time of the Do Not Disturb period when DND is enabled. |
| Communication Consent Version | String | Auto | Current version of the communication consent policy applicable to the customer. |
| Communication Consent Timestamp | Timestamp | Auto | Timestamp when the latest communication consent was recorded. |
| Request Source | Enum | Auto | Source of the communication preference update (e.g., Mobile App, Web Portal, API). |
| Device ID | String | Auto | Identifier of the device initiating the request where available. |
| Session ID | String | Auto | Identifier of the authenticated customer session. |
| IP Address | String | Auto | Client IP address captured for audit, security, and compliance purposes. |
| User Agent | String | Auto | Browser or client application information captured for audit and troubleshooting purposes. |
| Request Timestamp | Timestamp | Auto | Timestamp when the communication preference request is received. |
| Remarks | String | No | Optional customer remarks where supported by the platform. |

#### 3.1.23.6 Business Rules

1. Only authenticated customers shall be permitted to access and manage their Communication Preferences.
2. Customers shall be authorized to modify only the communication preferences associated with their own account.
3. The platform shall maintain a default communication preference configuration for every newly registered customer account.
4. Communication preferences shall be configurable only for communication channels, communication categories, and communication frequencies supported by the platform.
5. Mandatory communications required for authentication, account security, password resets, multi-factor authentication, payment confirmations, order placement, order status updates, delivery tracking, refunds, disputes, fraud prevention, emergency notifications, legal obligations, taxation, audit, and regulatory compliance shall always be delivered regardless of optional communication preferences.
6. Optional communication preferences shall apply only to future eligible communications and shall not affect previously delivered communications.
7. Historical notifications, completed promotional campaigns, invoices, payment receipts, audit records, legal notices, completed order communications, fraud investigation records, and other immutable business records shall remain unchanged after communication preference updates.
8. Customers may independently enable or disable supported optional communication channels without affecting mandatory communication delivery.
9. Communication frequency settings shall apply only to eligible optional communication categories.
10. The platform shall honor the customer's preferred communication language where multilingual content is supported.
11. Do Not Disturb (DND) settings shall apply only to optional communications and shall not delay or suppress mandatory business communications.
12. Communication preference updates shall be validated before being committed to the database.
13. Communication preference updates shall be executed as atomic database transactions.
14. Partial updates shall never be committed.
15. Failed transactions shall automatically trigger rollback operations.
16. Updated communication preferences shall become effective immediately for future eligible communications.
17. Authorized platform services shall retrieve the customer's latest communication preferences before sending eligible communications.
18. Communication preferences shall be consistently synchronized across authorized Notification, Marketing, Campaign Management, Loyalty, Recommendation, Email, SMS, Push Notification, and In-App Notification services.
19. Synchronization failures shall not compromise the integrity of the customer's stored communication preferences.
20. Promotional campaigns shall honor the customer's configured promotional communication preferences.
21. Newsletter subscriptions shall follow the customer's configured newsletter preferences.
22. Survey invitations, educational content, product announcements, and loyalty communications shall be delivered only according to the customer's configured preferences where applicable.
23. The platform shall prevent customers from selecting unsupported, deprecated, disabled, or invalid communication channels, communication categories, communication frequencies, or scheduling options.
24. If a communication preference conflicts with mandatory business operations or applicable legal or regulatory requirements, the mandatory business or regulatory requirement shall take precedence.
25. The platform shall record the timestamp, request source, authenticated customer identity, consent version where applicable, and audit information for every communication preference modification.
26. All successful and failed communication preference operations shall be recorded within the platform's audit logging framework.
27. Unauthorized attempts to access or modify communication preferences shall be rejected and recorded as security events.
28. Concurrent communication preference update requests for the same customer account shall be handled using appropriate concurrency control mechanisms.
29. Communication preferences shall remain associated with the customer account across all supported authenticated devices and sessions.
30. Platform administrators shall not modify customer communication preferences unless explicitly authorized by documented administrative procedures and applicable legal or regulatory requirements.
31. Communication preference processing shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, cybersecurity, accessibility, communication, audit, consumer protection, data governance, and regulatory requirements.
32. Future communication channels, communication categories, scheduling policies, multilingual communication capabilities, customer segmentation strategies, campaign management enhancements, and configurable communication rules shall be supported without requiring major architectural redesign.

#### 3.1.23.7 Validations

##### Authentication & Authorization Validations

1. The customer shall be authenticated before accessing the Communication Preferences functionality.
2. The customer's authenticated session shall be active and valid.
3. The access token shall be valid, unexpired, and untampered.
4. The customer shall be authorized to modify only the communication preferences associated with their own account.
5. Unauthorized requests shall be rejected with an appropriate authorization error.
6. Suspended, blocked, disabled, or permanently deleted customer accounts shall not be permitted to modify communication preferences.

##### Request Validations

7. Every request shall contain a valid authenticated customer identifier.
8. The request payload shall conform to the supported API schema.
9. Unsupported request parameters shall be rejected.
10. Mandatory system-generated fields shall not be modifiable by customers.
11. Duplicate request parameters shall be handled according to platform API standards.
12. Malformed, incomplete, or corrupted requests shall be rejected.

##### Communication Preference Validations

13. Only platform-supported communication channels shall be accepted.
14. Deprecated or disabled communication channels shall be rejected.
15. Only supported communication categories shall be accepted.
16. Communication frequency shall contain only supported values.
17. Preferred contact time shall contain only supported values.
18. Preferred communication language shall match a supported language configured by the platform.
19. Boolean communication preference fields shall accept only valid Boolean values.
20. Unsupported combinations of communication preferences shall be rejected.

##### Do Not Disturb (DND) Validations

21. DND Start Time shall be provided when DND is enabled.
22. DND End Time shall be provided when DND is enabled.
23. DND Start Time and End Time shall represent a valid time range according to platform rules.
24. DND settings shall apply only to optional communications.
25. Mandatory operational communications shall bypass DND restrictions where required.

##### Business Rule Validations

26. Customers shall not disable mandatory communications required for authentication, account security, payment processing, order processing, delivery tracking, fraud prevention, emergency notifications, taxation, legal obligations, audit requirements, or regulatory compliance.
27. Communication preference updates shall affect only future eligible communications.
28. Historical notifications, invoices, completed communications, payment confirmations, audit records, legal notices, and completed business transactions shall never be modified.
29. Communication preferences conflicting with mandatory business policies or legal requirements shall be rejected or overridden according to platform policy.
30. Communication frequency restrictions shall apply only to eligible optional communication categories.

##### Data Integrity Validations

31. The customer account shall exist before processing the request.
32. Communication preference records shall be retrievable.
33. Database constraints shall be satisfied before committing updates.
34. Atomic transaction validation shall succeed before database commit.
35. Referential integrity shall be preserved across all related platform data.
36. Communication preference records shall remain internally consistent following validation.

##### Concurrency Validations

37. Concurrent communication preference update requests for the same customer account shall be detected.
38. Conflicting simultaneous updates shall be resolved using platform concurrency control mechanisms.
39. Duplicate update requests resulting from retries shall not create inconsistent communication preference records.

##### Security Validations

40. All requests shall be transmitted exclusively over secure HTTPS connections.
41. Input values shall be validated against injection attacks, cross-site scripting (XSS), cross-site request forgery (CSRF), parameter tampering, replay attacks, session hijacking, privilege escalation, and other common security threats.
42. Sensitive system-managed fields shall not be accepted from customer input.
43. Rate limiting and abuse detection policies shall be enforced where applicable.
44. Unauthorized communication preference modification attempts shall be rejected and recorded.
45. Security-related validation failures shall be logged within security monitoring systems.

##### Compliance Validations

46. Communication preference updates shall comply with applicable privacy regulations, communication policies, consumer protection laws, accessibility standards, and organizational governance requirements.
47. Consent-related communication preferences shall satisfy applicable legal and regulatory requirements where required.
48. Audit logging shall successfully record communication preference operations according to compliance policies.
49. Communication preference processing shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, cybersecurity, privacy, accessibility, audit, consumer protection, and data governance requirements.

##### System Validations

50. Required platform services shall be operational before processing the request.
51. Configuration data for supported communication channels, communication categories, frequencies, scheduling rules, and multilingual communication options shall be available.
52. Synchronization with authorized dependent platform services shall be initiated after successful validation and database commit.
53. If any critical validation fails, no communication preference changes shall be committed to the database.
54. System-generated timestamps, request identifiers, consent metadata, and audit information shall be generated by the platform and shall not be accepted from customer input.

#### 3.1.23.8 Main Flow

1. The authenticated customer navigates to the **Communication Preferences** section from the Account Settings or Customer Profile page.
2. The client application sends a request to retrieve the customer's current communication preferences.
3. The Authentication Service validates the customer's authenticated session and access token.
4. The Authorization Service verifies that the customer is authorized to access only their own communication preferences.
5. The Communication Preferences Service retrieves the customer's existing communication preferences from the Customer Profile Service.
6. The Configuration Management Service retrieves the latest supported communication channels, communication categories, communication frequency options, preferred contact time options, multilingual configurations, and platform communication policies.
7. The Communication Preferences Service merges customer-specific preferences with the latest platform configuration.
8. The system displays the Communication Preferences screen showing all supported configurable communication options and their current values.
9. The customer reviews the existing communication preferences.
10. The customer updates one or more supported communication preferences as required.
11. The customer submits the updated communication preferences.
12. The client application sends the updated communication preference request to the backend.
13. The Authentication Service revalidates the customer's authenticated session if required by platform policy.
14. The Authorization Service confirms that the customer is permitted to update only their own communication preferences.
15. The Communication Preferences Service validates the request payload, supported communication channels, communication categories, communication frequency, preferred contact time, language preferences, and business rules.
16. The platform verifies that mandatory communication categories remain enabled where required.
17. The system validates Do Not Disturb (DND) configuration if DND is enabled.
18. The platform validates all selected communication options against applicable legal, regulatory, privacy, and organizational policies.
19. The platform performs input sanitization, security validation, concurrency validation, and data integrity checks.
20. The Communication Preferences Service begins an atomic database transaction.
21. The updated communication preferences are stored in the database.
22. The platform records the request source, consent version where applicable, timestamps, audit information, and compliance metadata.
23. The Audit Logging Service records the successful communication preference modification.
24. The database transaction is committed successfully.
25. The updated communication preferences are synchronized with authorized dependent platform services.
26. Notification, Marketing, Campaign Management, Loyalty, Recommendation, Email, SMS, Push Notification, In-App Notification, and other authorized services refresh the customer's communication preferences where applicable.
27. Future eligible customer communications begin using the updated communication preferences.
28. Mandatory communications required for authentication, account security, payment processing, order processing, delivery tracking, fraud prevention, emergency notifications, legal obligations, taxation, audit, and regulatory compliance continue to be delivered according to platform policies.
29. Historical communications, invoices, payment receipts, completed campaigns, audit records, legal notices, fraud investigation records, and other immutable business records remain unchanged.
30. The system generates a standardized success response.
31. The updated Communication Preferences screen is displayed to the customer showing the latest saved configuration.
32. The operation is completed successfully.

#### 3.1.23.9 Alternate Flows

##### AF-1: No Communication Preference Changes

1. The customer opens the Communication Preferences page.
2. The system displays the current communication preferences.
3. The customer reviews the preferences without making any modifications.
4. No update request is submitted.
5. No database changes are performed.
6. No communication preference audit record is created.
7. The customer exits the page.

---

##### AF-2: Communication Preferences Updated Successfully

1. The customer modifies one or more supported communication preferences.
2. The system validates the request.
3. All validations and business rules pass successfully.
4. The updated communication preferences are stored.
5. Audit logs are generated.
6. Authorized dependent platform services receive the updated communication preferences.
7. The customer receives a successful confirmation.

---

##### AF-3: Invalid Communication Channel Selected

1. The customer selects an unsupported, disabled, deprecated, or invalid communication channel.
2. Validation detects the invalid selection.
3. The request is rejected.
4. Existing communication preferences remain unchanged.
5. An appropriate validation error is returned.

---

##### AF-4: Invalid Communication Frequency

1. The customer selects an unsupported communication frequency.
2. The platform validates the selected frequency.
3. Validation fails.
4. No communication preferences are updated.
5. The customer receives an appropriate validation error.

---

##### AF-5: Invalid Preferred Contact Time

1. The customer submits an unsupported preferred contact time.
2. The system detects the invalid configuration.
3. The request is rejected.
4. Existing preferences remain unchanged.
5. The customer receives an appropriate validation error.

---

##### AF-6: Invalid Do Not Disturb Configuration

1. The customer enables Do Not Disturb (DND).
2. Required DND time values are missing or invalid.
3. Validation fails.
4. No communication preferences are updated.
5. The customer is prompted to correct the DND configuration.

---

##### AF-7: Mandatory Communication Disable Attempt

1. The customer attempts to disable a mandatory communication category.
2. The platform detects the protected communication type.
3. The request violates business rules.
4. The modification is rejected.
5. Mandatory communication delivery remains enabled.
6. The customer receives an appropriate business rule violation message.

---

##### AF-8: Authentication Failure

1. The customer's authenticated session has expired or becomes invalid.
2. Authentication validation fails.
3. The request is rejected.
4. No communication preferences are retrieved or updated.
5. The customer is required to authenticate again.

---

##### AF-9: Authorization Failure

1. A customer attempts to access or modify another customer's communication preferences.
2. Authorization validation fails.
3. Access is denied.
4. No customer information is disclosed.
5. A security event is recorded.
6. An authorization error response is returned.

---

##### AF-10: Concurrent Update Conflict

1. Multiple communication preference update requests are received simultaneously for the same customer account.
2. The concurrency control mechanism detects the conflict.
3. Only one transaction proceeds successfully.
4. Conflicting requests are rejected or retried according to platform policy.
5. Data consistency is maintained.

---

##### AF-11: Database Transaction Failure

1. A database error occurs while saving communication preferences.
2. The active transaction is rolled back.
3. No partial updates are committed.
4. Existing communication preferences remain unchanged.
5. Audit logs capture the failure.
6. The customer receives a system error response.

---

##### AF-12: Dependent Service Synchronization Failure

1. Communication preferences are successfully stored in the primary database.
2. Synchronization with one or more authorized dependent services temporarily fails.
3. The primary transaction remains committed.
4. Retry or asynchronous synchronization mechanisms are initiated.
5. Operational alerts are generated.
6. Customer communication preference integrity remains unaffected.

---

##### AF-13: Security Validation Failure

1. The platform detects suspicious, malicious, or tampered input.
2. Security validation fails.
3. The request is rejected immediately.
4. No database transaction is initiated.
5. Security monitoring records the event.
6. Appropriate security responses are triggered according to platform policy.

---

##### AF-14: Service Unavailability

1. A required platform service becomes unavailable during request processing.
2. The request cannot be completed.
3. No communication preference updates are committed.
4. The failure is logged.
5. The customer receives a temporary service unavailable response.
6. The customer may retry the operation later.

---

##### AF-15: Audit Logging Failure

1. Communication preferences are successfully validated.
2. The Audit Logging Service becomes temporarily unavailable.
3. The platform follows configured audit reliability policies.
4. Audit retry or fallback mechanisms are initiated.
5. Operations personnel are notified if required.
6. Processing continues only according to organizational compliance requirements.

---

##### AF-16: Platform Configuration Updated During Processing

1. Communication channel or communication policy configuration changes while the customer is updating preferences.
2. The platform detects a configuration version mismatch.
3. The request is revalidated using the latest platform configuration.
4. Unsupported communication selections are rejected.
5. The customer is prompted to review the updated communication options if necessary.

#### 3.1.23.10 Postconditions

Upon completion of the Communication Preferences operation, the following postconditions shall apply:

1. The customer's communication preferences shall be updated successfully if all validations, authorization checks, and business rules are satisfied.
2. The updated communication preferences shall be stored persistently in the platform database.
3. The customer's latest communication preferences shall become effective immediately for all future eligible communications.
4. Mandatory communications required for authentication, account security, password recovery, multi-factor authentication, payment processing, order placement, order status updates, delivery tracking, fraud prevention, emergency notifications, legal obligations, taxation, audit, and regulatory compliance shall continue to be delivered regardless of optional communication preferences.
5. Historical communications, invoices, payment receipts, completed campaigns, order notifications, audit records, legal notices, fraud investigation records, and other immutable business records shall remain unchanged.
6. Updated communication consent information, consent version where applicable, request source, timestamps, and compliance metadata shall be recorded.
7. Customer profile information unrelated to communication preferences shall remain unchanged.
8. Authorized platform services shall retrieve or synchronize the customer's latest communication preferences according to platform synchronization policies.
9. Notification, Marketing, Campaign Management, Recommendation, Loyalty, Email, SMS, Push Notification, In-App Notification, and other authorized platform services shall honor the updated communication preferences for future eligible communications.
10. Cached communication preference data shall be refreshed or invalidated according to platform caching policies.
11. A complete audit record shall be created for every successful communication preference modification.
12. Failed communication preference update attempts shall also be recorded within audit and security logs where applicable.
13. Security monitoring systems shall record unauthorized access attempts, suspicious modifications, abnormal request patterns, and other security-related events.
14. Database consistency and referential integrity shall be preserved following successful completion of the transaction.
15. Communication preference updates shall be committed as a single atomic database transaction.
16. If any failure occurs before transaction commit, all pending database changes shall be rolled back automatically.
17. Customer authentication status, active sessions, account status, and account credentials shall remain unchanged unless separate security policies require additional verification.
18. Communication preference updates shall not automatically modify notification preferences, privacy settings, language preferences, account credentials, payment methods, wallet balances, loyalty points, saved addresses, or other independent customer settings unless explicitly configured by platform policy.
19. Customer communication preference history and consent history shall remain available for audit, compliance, reporting, and legal evidence purposes where applicable.
20. System-generated timestamps, request identifiers, audit identifiers, and compliance metadata shall accurately reflect the completed operation.
21. Platform monitoring systems shall capture operational metrics, processing duration, synchronization status, communication delivery configuration updates, and error information where applicable.
22. All successful operations shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, cybersecurity, accessibility, communication, audit, consumer protection, data governance, and regulatory requirements.
23. The Communication Preferences module shall remain available for future customer updates according to the platform's defined availability objectives.
24. The platform shall maintain consistency between stored communication preferences and future eligible communication delivery across all authorized platform services.

#### 3.1.23.11 Success Response

Upon successful completion of the Communication Preferences operation, the system shall return a standardized success response.

##### Success Response Structure

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| Timestamp | Timestamp | Date and time when the request was successfully processed. |
| HTTP Status Code | Integer | HTTP status code indicating successful request processing (e.g., 200 OK). |
| Status | String | Overall operation status (e.g., `SUCCESS`). |
| Success Code | String | Platform-defined success code identifying the completed operation. |
| Message | String | Human-readable success message. |
| Customer ID | UUID | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. |
| Communication Preferences ID | UUID | Unique identifier of the customer's communication preference record where applicable. |
| Updated Communication Preferences | Object | Latest communication preferences after successful update. |
| Enabled Communication Channels | Array | List of enabled communication channels after the update. |
| Communication Frequency | String | Customer's configured communication frequency. |
| Preferred Contact Time | String | Customer's preferred communication time window where applicable. |
| Preferred Communication Language | String | Language selected for eligible communications where supported. |
| Communication Consent Version | String | Current communication consent version associated with the customer's updated preferences where applicable. |
| Communication Consent Timestamp | Timestamp | Timestamp indicating when the latest communication consent was recorded. |
| Effective From | Timestamp | Timestamp from which the updated communication preferences become effective. |
| Synchronization Status | String | Status indicating synchronization with authorized dependent platform services. |
| Audit Reference ID | UUID | Unique identifier of the generated audit record. |
| Request ID | UUID | Unique identifier associated with the processed request for traceability. |

##### Sample Success Response

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2027-01-18T09:42:11Z",
  "status": "SUCCESS",
  "code": "COMMUNICATION_PREFERENCES_UPDATED",
  "message": "Communication preferences updated successfully.",
  "customerId": "b9b67d43-49d0-4df7-bce6-6b3b1d2d49f8",
  "communicationPreferencesId": "acb1f8b5-3e2d-49db-91a7-2b7dbe8bfa91",
  "updatedCommunicationPreferences": {
    "email": true,
    "sms": false,
    "pushNotification": true,
    "inAppNotification": true,
    "promotionalCommunication": false,
    "newsletterSubscription": true,
    "communicationFrequency": "WEEKLY",
    "preferredContactTime": "EVENING",
    "preferredLanguage": "en"
  },
  "communicationConsentVersion": "v2.1",
  "communicationConsentTimestamp": "2027-01-18T09:42:11Z",
  "effectiveFrom": "2027-01-18T09:42:11Z",
  "synchronizationStatus": "COMPLETED",
  "auditReferenceId": "6dcfa463-b86b-4e6a-bc90-3c145ff57d6f",
  "requestId": "98c4e98b-47ea-44e8-a62c-c618e7f71f65"
}
```

##### Success Response Business Rules

1. The success response shall be returned only after the communication preferences have been successfully validated, committed to the database, and all required post-commit operations have been initiated.
2. The response shall contain the customer's latest communication preferences.
3. Only the authenticated customer's communication preference information shall be included in the response.
4. Sensitive authentication credentials, passwords, access tokens, refresh tokens, session identifiers, encryption keys, internal configuration values, infrastructure details, and security implementation information shall never be included.
5. Mandatory platform-controlled communication configurations shall not expose internal implementation logic.
6. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be returned only when required and authorized.
7. Communication consent information shall be returned only where applicable.
8. Synchronization status shall accurately reflect the processing state of authorized dependent platform services.
9. Audit identifiers shall support operational traceability without exposing confidential audit information.
10. The response format shall remain consistent across all supported client applications and API versions.
11. The response shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, cybersecurity, accessibility, communication, audit, consumer protection, and data protection requirements.
12. Successful responses shall be logged according to the platform's monitoring, audit, and compliance policies.

#### 3.1.23.12 Failure Response

When the Communication Preferences operation cannot be completed, the system shall return a standardized failure response describing the reason for the failure without exposing sensitive implementation details.

##### Failure Response Structure

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| Timestamp | Timestamp | Date and time when the failure occurred. |
| HTTP Status Code | Integer | Appropriate HTTP status code representing the failure. |
| Status | String | Overall request status (e.g., `FAILED`). |
| Error Code | String | Platform-defined error code identifying the failure. |
| Error Category | String | Classification of the failure (Authentication, Authorization, Validation, Business Rule, Security, Compliance, Database, System, etc.). |
| Message | String | Human-readable error message suitable for the client application. |
| Details | Array | Optional list of validation or business rule violations. |
| Request ID | UUID | Unique request identifier for troubleshooting and traceability. |
| Server Timestamp | Timestamp | Server-generated timestamp for audit and operational monitoring. |

##### Sample Failure Response

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2027-01-18T10:05:32Z",
  "status": "FAILED",
  "httpStatus": 400,
  "errorCode": "INVALID_COMMUNICATION_PREFERENCE",
  "errorCategory": "VALIDATION",
  "message": "One or more communication preferences are invalid.",
  "details": [
    {
      "field": "communicationFrequency",
      "reason": "Unsupported communication frequency selected."
    }
  ],
  "requestId": "c91d8352-4f73-4b71-b61e-c15d84b6af9f",
  "serverTimestamp": "2027-01-18T10:05:32Z"
}
```

##### Failure Scenarios

| Scenario | HTTP Status | Error Code | Description |
|----------|------------|------------|-------------|
| Customer not authenticated | 401 | `AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED` | Customer authentication is required. |
| Invalid or expired session | 401 | `SESSION_EXPIRED` | Customer session is invalid or expired. |
| Invalid access token | 401 | `INVALID_ACCESS_TOKEN` | Authentication token is invalid or expired. |
| Unauthorized access | 403 | `ACCESS_DENIED` | Customer is not authorized to modify the requested communication preferences. |
| Customer account not found | 404 | `CUSTOMER_NOT_FOUND` | Customer account does not exist. |
| Communication preferences not found | 404 | `COMMUNICATION_PREFERENCES_NOT_FOUND` | Customer communication preferences could not be located. |
| Invalid request payload | 400 | `INVALID_REQUEST` | Request format is invalid. |
| Unsupported communication preference | 400 | `INVALID_COMMUNICATION_PREFERENCE` | Unsupported communication preference was submitted. |
| Invalid communication channel | 400 | `INVALID_COMMUNICATION_CHANNEL` | Selected communication channel is not supported. |
| Invalid communication frequency | 400 | `INVALID_COMMUNICATION_FREQUENCY` | Selected communication frequency is invalid. |
| Invalid preferred contact time | 400 | `INVALID_CONTACT_TIME` | Selected preferred contact time is invalid. |
| Invalid Do Not Disturb configuration | 400 | `INVALID_DND_CONFIGURATION` | DND configuration is incomplete or invalid. |
| Mandatory communication modification attempt | 400 | `MANDATORY_COMMUNICATION_PROTECTED` | Customer attempted to disable mandatory communications. |
| Business rule violation | 409 | `BUSINESS_RULE_VIOLATION` | Request violates configured business rules. |
| Concurrent update conflict | 409 | `CONCURRENT_UPDATE_DETECTED` | Another update is already in progress. |
| Rate limit exceeded | 429 | `RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` | Too many requests received within the allowed period. |
| Security validation failure | 403 | `SECURITY_VALIDATION_FAILED` | Security validation detected suspicious or malicious input. |
| Database transaction failure | 500 | `DATABASE_TRANSACTION_FAILED` | Database update could not be completed. |
| Communication synchronization failure | 500 | `COMMUNICATION_SYNC_FAILED` | Synchronization with dependent services failed. |
| Audit logging failure | 500 | `AUDIT_LOG_FAILURE` | Audit recording failed according to platform policy. |
| Internal server error | 500 | `INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR` | Unexpected server error occurred. |
| Service temporarily unavailable | 503 | `SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE` | Required platform service is temporarily unavailable. |

##### Failure Response Business Rules

1. Every failed request shall return a standardized failure response structure.
2. Error messages shall remain consistent across all supported client applications.
3. Internal implementation details, stack traces, SQL statements, encryption keys, access tokens, refresh tokens, session identifiers, infrastructure information, and security configurations shall never be exposed.
4. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall not be disclosed within failure responses unless explicitly required and authorized.
5. Validation failures shall clearly identify the affected input fields whenever appropriate.
6. Authentication and authorization failures shall not reveal confidential account information.
7. Security-related failures shall avoid exposing information that could assist malicious actors.
8. All failed communication preference requests shall be recorded within platform audit logs according to organizational policies.
9. Security-related failures shall additionally be recorded within security monitoring systems.
10. Request identifiers shall support troubleshooting without exposing sensitive operational information.
11. Failure responses shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, cybersecurity, accessibility, communication, audit, consumer protection, and data protection requirements.
12. Client applications shall be able to distinguish failure types programmatically using the HTTP status code and platform-defined error code.

#### 3.1.23.13 Non-Functional Considerations

##### Performance

1. The Communication Preferences functionality shall process customer requests within **3 seconds** under normal operating conditions.
2. Retrieval of customer communication preferences shall occur with minimal latency before rendering the Communication Preferences page.
3. Communication preference updates shall utilize optimized database queries and indexing mechanisms.
4. The platform shall support concurrent communication preference requests from multiple customers without significant performance degradation.
5. Updated communication preferences shall be synchronized with authorized dependent platform services with minimal latency.
6. The platform shall maintain acceptable response times during peak traffic periods.

##### Availability

7. The Communication Preferences functionality shall maintain a minimum availability of **99.9%**, excluding scheduled maintenance.
8. Temporary unavailability of non-critical communication services shall not prevent customers from managing their stored communication preferences whenever possible.
9. Critical communication management services shall remain highly available according to platform service level objectives.

##### Scalability

10. The platform shall support millions of customer communication preference records without requiring major architectural redesign.
11. Communication management services shall support horizontal scaling as customer volume and communication traffic increase.
12. The architecture shall support future communication channels, communication categories, multilingual capabilities, customer segmentation strategies, campaign management enhancements, scheduling policies, and configurable communication rules with minimal system modifications.

##### Reliability

13. Communication preference updates shall be executed as fully atomic database transactions.
14. Customer communication preferences shall remain consistent across all authorized platform services.
15. Transaction failures shall automatically trigger rollback operations.
16. Synchronization failures shall not compromise the integrity of stored communication preferences.
17. The platform shall maintain consistency between stored communication preferences and future eligible communication delivery activities.

##### Security

18. All Communication Preferences requests shall be transmitted exclusively over secure HTTPS connections.
19. Authentication and authorization shall be enforced before processing every request.
20. Customer communication preferences and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall remain protected throughout all processing stages.
21. Passwords, access tokens, refresh tokens, encryption keys, session identifiers, and other sensitive security information shall never be stored or logged in plain text.
22. The platform shall protect against unauthorized access, injection attacks, cross-site scripting (XSS), cross-site request forgery (CSRF), replay attacks, parameter tampering, privilege escalation, session hijacking, and other common security threats.
23. Security monitoring, intrusion detection, rate limiting, and abuse prevention mechanisms shall protect communication preference management operations.
24. Security events shall be logged and monitored according to organizational cybersecurity policies.

##### Privacy

25. Customer communication preferences shall be processed in accordance with applicable privacy regulations and organizational data protection policies.
26. Only authorized platform services shall access customer communication preference information.
27. Communication preference data shall be collected, stored, retained, archived, masked, anonymized, and deleted according to applicable legal and regulatory requirements.
28. Customer consent history related to communication preferences shall be securely maintained where required for compliance and audit purposes.

##### Compliance

29. Communication preference management shall comply with applicable privacy regulations, communication regulations, consumer protection laws, cybersecurity standards, accessibility requirements, anti-spam regulations, data governance policies, and organizational compliance requirements.
30. Communication consent management shall support applicable legal and regulatory evidence requirements.
31. Communication policy versioning shall support future regulatory and organizational policy updates.

##### Audit & Logging

32. All successful and failed communication preference operations shall be recorded within immutable audit logs.
33. Audit records shall support compliance reporting, regulatory inspections, forensic investigations, operational monitoring, and troubleshooting.
34. Audit logs shall be protected against unauthorized modification, deletion, or disclosure.

##### Maintainability

35. The Communication Preferences module shall be implemented using modular and loosely coupled platform components.
36. Communication channels, communication categories, scheduling policies, multilingual configurations, campaign rules, consent policies, and business configurations shall be configurable without requiring application code changes wherever practical.
37. Future communication capabilities shall require minimal impact on existing platform components.

##### Monitoring & Observability

38. The platform shall continuously monitor request processing time, communication preference updates, synchronization status, communication delivery configuration, validation failures, service availability, and abnormal activities.
39. Critical failures, synchronization failures, suspicious modification attempts, compliance violations, and security incidents shall generate operational alerts.
40. Logs, metrics, traces, and audit records shall provide complete end-to-end observability of the Communication Preferences workflow.

##### Backup & Recovery

41. Customer communication preferences, communication consent records, and related configuration data shall be included within scheduled backup procedures where applicable.
42. Recovery mechanisms shall preserve communication preferences, consent history, audit records, and data integrity following infrastructure failures or disaster recovery events.
43. Backup and restoration procedures shall comply with the platform's business continuity, disaster recovery, privacy, communication, and regulatory requirements.

#### 3.1.23.14 Acceptance Criteria

| AC ID | Acceptance Criteria |
|-------|----------------------|
| AC-CP-001 | The system shall allow only authenticated customers to access the Communication Preferences functionality. |
| AC-CP-002 | The system shall authorize customers to modify only the communication preferences associated with their own account. |
| AC-CP-003 | The system shall display the customer's current communication preferences after successful authentication. |
| AC-CP-004 | Only platform-supported communication channels, communication categories, communication frequencies, and scheduling options shall be displayed to the customer. |
| AC-CP-005 | Customers shall be able to modify only configurable communication preferences supported by the platform. |
| AC-CP-006 | Mandatory communications required for authentication, account security, password recovery, payment processing, order processing, delivery tracking, fraud prevention, emergency notifications, legal obligations, taxation, audit, and regulatory compliance shall not be disabled by customers. |
| AC-CP-007 | The system shall validate all submitted communication preferences, communication channels, communication frequencies, preferred contact times, language preferences, and business rules before processing the request. |
| AC-CP-008 | Unsupported, deprecated, disabled, malformed, or invalid communication preferences shall be rejected. |
| AC-CP-009 | The system shall validate Do Not Disturb (DND) configuration whenever DND is enabled. |
| AC-CP-010 | Updated communication preferences shall become effective immediately for future eligible customer communications. |
| AC-CP-011 | Historical notifications, completed campaigns, invoices, payment receipts, audit records, legal notices, fraud investigation records, and other immutable business records shall remain unchanged following communication preference updates. |
| AC-CP-012 | Updated communication preferences shall be synchronized with authorized dependent platform services after successful completion. |
| AC-CP-013 | Notification, Marketing, Campaign Management, Recommendation, Loyalty, Email, SMS, Push Notification, In-App Notification, and other authorized services shall honor the customer's updated communication preferences wherever applicable. |
| AC-CP-014 | Customer profile information unrelated to communication preferences shall remain unchanged following the update. |
| AC-CP-015 | Communication preference updates shall be executed as atomic database transactions. |
| AC-CP-016 | Partial database updates shall never be committed. |
| AC-CP-017 | Failed transactions shall automatically roll back all pending changes. |
| AC-CP-018 | The system shall safely detect and handle concurrent communication preference update requests. |
| AC-CP-019 | Successful requests shall return a standardized success response containing only authorized information. |
| AC-CP-020 | Failed requests shall return standardized failure responses without exposing sensitive implementation details. |
| AC-CP-021 | Passwords, access tokens, refresh tokens, session identifiers, encryption keys, internal configuration values, and other confidential security information shall never be exposed in responses. |
| AC-CP-022 | All communication between client applications and backend services shall occur exclusively over secure HTTPS connections. |
| AC-CP-023 | Customer communication preferences and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be protected according to organizational security and privacy policies. |
| AC-CP-024 | Every successful and failed communication preference operation shall be recorded within immutable audit logs. |
| AC-CP-025 | Security-related events including unauthorized access attempts, suspicious modifications, privilege escalation attempts, abnormal communication preference changes, and policy violations shall be monitored and logged. |
| AC-CP-026 | The Communication Preferences functionality shall maintain the platform's defined service availability objectives. |
| AC-CP-027 | The functionality shall support concurrent customer requests without compromising performance, consistency, reliability, or data integrity. |
| AC-CP-028 | The platform shall support future communication channels, communication categories, multilingual capabilities, customer segmentation strategies, campaign management enhancements, configurable communication policies, and regulatory requirements without requiring major architectural redesign. |
| AC-CP-029 | Communication preference management shall comply with applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, cybersecurity, accessibility, communication, audit, consumer protection, data governance, anti-spam regulations, and legal/regulatory requirements. |
| AC-CP-030 | Customer communication preference history and communication consent history shall remain available for audit, compliance, reporting, and legal evidence purposes where applicable. |
| AC-CP-031 | Mandatory operational communications shall always be delivered according to applicable business policies and regulatory requirements regardless of customer-configured optional communication preferences. |
| AC-CP-032 | The Communication Preferences module shall successfully pass all functional, integration, security, performance, regression, accessibility, communication, privacy, compliance, and user acceptance testing before production deployment. |

### 3.1.24 Customer Wallet

#### 3.1.24.1 Description

The **Customer Wallet** module enables authenticated customers to securely store and manage wallet balances within the Food Delivery Platform. The wallet provides a centralized mechanism for maintaining refundable balances, promotional credits, cashback rewards, loyalty-based monetary benefits, compensation credits, gift credits, and other platform-supported wallet transactions. Customers may utilize their available wallet balance as a payment source for eligible purchases according to configurable business policies.

The platform shall maintain a unique wallet account for every eligible customer. Each wallet shall maintain the current available balance, transaction history, credit entries, debit entries, expiration details where applicable, promotional credits, cashback records, compensation credits, refund transactions, and other wallet-related financial activities. Every wallet transaction shall be uniquely identifiable, immutable after successful completion, and fully auditable.

Customers shall be able to view their current wallet balance, wallet transaction history, transaction details, credit sources, debit purposes, expiration dates for eligible promotional credits, and other wallet information supported by the platform. Wallet funds may originate from approved order refunds, promotional campaigns, referral rewards, loyalty programs, compensation policies, gift credits, manual administrative adjustments, or other authorized platform operations.

During checkout, customers may choose to utilize available wallet balance for eligible orders. The platform shall validate available balance, transaction eligibility, applicable business rules, promotional restrictions, expiration rules, maximum utilization limits, and payment priority before applying wallet funds. Partial wallet payments may be supported according to configurable platform policies.

Wallet operations shall not modify completed financial transactions retrospectively. Historical wallet transactions, completed orders, invoices, payment records, audit logs, tax records, legal evidence, fraud investigation records, and regulatory records shall remain immutable after successful completion.

The Customer Wallet functionality shall integrate with Payment Services, Refund Services, Order Management, Loyalty Services, Referral Services, Promotional Campaign Services, Customer Profile Management, Accounting Services, Fraud Detection Services, Notification Services, Audit Logging Services, and other authorized platform components to ensure accurate wallet balance management and financial consistency across the platform.

All wallet operations shall be protected through authentication, authorization, validation, fraud detection, audit logging, financial reconciliation, transaction management, and security controls. Every successful and failed wallet operation shall be recorded within the platform's audit logging framework to support financial reconciliation, compliance reporting, dispute resolution, operational monitoring, forensic investigations, and regulatory audits. Personally Identifiable Information (PII), wallet balances, financial information, and transaction records shall remain protected throughout the wallet lifecycle using appropriate encryption, access controls, secure transaction processing, and organizational financial security standards.

The Customer Wallet module shall be designed to support future wallet features including multiple wallet types, digital vouchers, gift cards, multi-currency wallets, installment credits, subscription credits, regional financial regulations, configurable promotional campaigns, advanced reconciliation capabilities, and additional payment innovations without requiring significant architectural modifications.

#### 3.1.24.2 Actors

The following actors participate in the Customer Wallet functionality:

| Actor | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Customer | Authenticated customer who views wallet information, utilizes wallet balance, and reviews wallet transactions. |
| Authentication Service | Authenticates the customer before allowing access to Customer Wallet. |
| Authorization Service | Ensures only the wallet owner is authorized to access wallet information and perform eligible wallet operations. |
| Customer Wallet Service | Manages wallet creation, wallet balance calculation, wallet transactions, validations, and wallet operations. |
| Customer Profile Service | Retrieves customer information associated with the wallet account. |
| Payment Service | Applies wallet balance during eligible payment transactions and coordinates payment processing. |
| Refund Service | Credits approved refund amounts to the customer's wallet according to platform policies. |
| Order Management Service | Validates wallet utilization during order placement and maintains order-payment consistency. |
| Loyalty Service | Credits loyalty rewards, cashback, and eligible incentive amounts into the customer's wallet. |
| Referral Service | Credits eligible referral rewards into the customer's wallet according to referral program policies. |
| Promotional Campaign Service | Issues promotional wallet credits, compensation credits, coupons, and campaign-based wallet incentives. |
| Accounting Service | Maintains financial reconciliation, accounting records, settlement information, and wallet transaction consistency. |
| Fraud Detection Service | Monitors wallet activities for suspicious transactions, fraudulent behavior, abnormal usage patterns, and financial risks. |
| Notification Service | Sends wallet-related notifications including credits, debits, refunds, expirations, promotional credits, and transaction confirmations. |
| Audit Logging Service | Records all successful and failed wallet operations for financial auditing, compliance, dispute resolution, and operational monitoring. |
| Security Monitoring Service | Monitors unauthorized wallet access attempts, suspicious wallet activities, and security-related events. |
| Configuration Management Service | Maintains configurable wallet policies, transaction limits, expiration rules, promotional configurations, and wallet business rules. |
| Financial Reconciliation Service | Reconciles wallet balances, transaction records, payment adjustments, refunds, and financial settlements across platform services. |
| Compliance & Governance Service | Ensures wallet operations comply with applicable financial regulations, taxation requirements, privacy regulations, consumer protection laws, cybersecurity standards, accessibility requirements, accounting policies, and organizational governance requirements. |

#### 3.1.24.3 Preconditions

Before a customer can access or use the Customer Wallet functionality, the following preconditions shall be satisfied:

1. The customer shall have a registered customer account.
2. The customer account shall be active and not permanently deleted.
3. The customer shall be successfully authenticated.
4. The customer shall possess a valid authenticated session.
5. The customer's access token shall be valid and unexpired.
6. The customer shall be authorized to access only their own wallet account.
7. The Customer Wallet module shall be enabled within the platform configuration.
8. A unique wallet account shall exist for the customer or shall be automatically created according to platform policy.
9. The Customer Wallet Service shall be operational.
10. The Customer Profile Service shall be available.
11. The Payment Service shall be operational for wallet-based payment processing.
12. The Order Management Service shall be available for validating wallet usage during eligible order transactions.
13. The Refund Service shall be operational for processing wallet refunds where applicable.
14. The Configuration Management Service shall provide the latest wallet configuration, transaction policies, wallet limits, promotional rules, expiration rules, and utilization policies.
15. The customer's wallet information shall be retrievable from the database.
16. The customer's current wallet balance shall be available and accurately calculated.
17. The customer's wallet transaction history shall be available for retrieval.
18. The database shall be available for reading and updating wallet records.
19. Communication between client applications and backend services shall occur exclusively over secure HTTPS connections.
20. The Audit Logging Service shall be available to record wallet operations.
21. The Financial Reconciliation Service shall be operational to maintain transaction consistency.
22. The Fraud Detection Service shall be operational to evaluate wallet transactions where applicable.
23. Required validation services shall be operational.
24. Authorized dependent platform services shall be capable of retrieving updated wallet information during eligible financial operations.
25. Wallet transaction identifiers shall be generated uniquely for every financial transaction.
26. Applicable financial regulations, taxation rules, promotional policies, refund policies, wallet utilization rules, and organizational governance requirements shall be available for validation.
27. No conflicting wallet transaction shall currently be in progress for the same wallet account.
28. System time shall be synchronized to accurately record wallet transaction timestamps, audit events, reconciliation records, and financial evidence.
29. All required platform services shall be functioning within acceptable operational thresholds.
30. The platform shall satisfy applicable authentication, authorization, financial security, privacy, cybersecurity, accessibility, accounting, audit, consumer protection, taxation, payment processing, and regulatory requirements before processing wallet operations.

#### 3.1.24.4 Trigger

The Customer Wallet functionality shall be initiated when one of the following events occurs:

1. An authenticated customer selects the **Customer Wallet** option from the Account, Profile, or Payment section.
2. An authenticated customer navigates to the Wallet page using the mobile application or web portal.
3. An authenticated customer requests to view the current wallet balance.
4. An authenticated customer requests to view wallet transaction history.
5. An authenticated customer selects a wallet transaction to view detailed transaction information.
6. An authenticated customer chooses to use the available wallet balance during checkout for an eligible order.
7. The Checkout Service requests the current wallet balance to determine eligible wallet utilization.
8. An approved order refund is processed for wallet credit according to the platform's refund policy.
9. A cashback reward is successfully earned through an eligible order or promotional campaign.
10. A loyalty reward is credited to the customer's wallet after successful eligibility verification.
11. A referral reward is approved and credited to the customer's wallet.
12. A promotional campaign credits eligible wallet incentives, compensation credits, gift credits, or bonus credits.
13. An authorized administrative wallet adjustment is performed according to documented operational procedures and applicable financial controls.
14. The platform processes the expiration of eligible promotional wallet credits according to configured expiration policies.
15. A wallet debit is initiated for an eligible purchase or approved financial adjustment.
16. A wallet transaction reconciliation process verifies wallet balances and transaction consistency.
17. An authenticated API request is received from an authorized client application to retrieve wallet information or initiate an eligible wallet operation.
18. The platform initiates fraud detection or financial verification for a wallet transaction based on configured risk assessment policies.
19. The Notification Service generates wallet-related notifications following successful wallet credit, debit, refund, expiration, adjustment, or reconciliation events.
20. A customer support workflow retrieves wallet information while ensuring that only authorized personnel access wallet details according to applicable security, privacy, and financial governance policies.

#### 3.1.24.5 Input Fields

| Field Name | Description | Data Type | Mandatory | Validation |
|------------|-------------|-----------|-----------|------------|
| Customer ID | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer associated with the wallet. | UUID | Yes | Must belong to the authenticated customer and exist in the system. |
| Wallet ID | Unique identifier of the customer's wallet account. | UUID | Yes | Must exist, be active, and belong to the authenticated customer. |
| Wallet Transaction ID | Unique identifier for a wallet transaction. | UUID | Conditional | Must exist for transaction detail retrieval and reference a valid wallet transaction. |
| Wallet Operation Type | Type of wallet operation being requested. Examples include View Balance, View Transactions, Wallet Payment, Credit, Debit, Refund, Cashback, Loyalty Credit, Referral Credit, Promotional Credit, Compensation Credit, Expiration, or Administrative Adjustment. | Enum | Yes | Must be one of the supported wallet operation types. |
| Order ID | Unique identifier of the associated customer order. | UUID | Conditional | Required for wallet payments, refunds, cashback, compensation, and order-related wallet transactions. Must reference an existing eligible order. |
| Payment Transaction ID | Identifier of the associated payment transaction. | UUID | Conditional | Required whenever wallet operations are linked to payment processing. Must reference a valid payment transaction. |
| Wallet Amount | Monetary amount involved in the wallet operation. | Decimal(12,2) | Conditional | Must be greater than zero and comply with configured transaction limits. |
| Currency Code | Currency associated with the wallet transaction. | String (ISO 4217) | Yes | Must be a platform-supported currency. |
| Wallet Balance Before Transaction | Wallet balance before processing the transaction. | Decimal(12,2) | System Generated | Calculated automatically by the system. |
| Wallet Balance After Transaction | Wallet balance after successful transaction processing. | Decimal(12,2) | System Generated | Calculated automatically after successful validation and transaction completion. |
| Credit Source | Source from which wallet funds originate (Refund, Cashback, Referral, Loyalty, Promotion, Gift Credit, Compensation, Administrative Credit, etc.). | Enum | Conditional | Must be one of the supported credit sources. |
| Debit Purpose | Business purpose for deducting wallet funds. | Enum | Conditional | Must be one of the supported debit purposes. |
| Promotional Campaign ID | Identifier of the promotional campaign responsible for issuing wallet credits. | UUID | Conditional | Must reference an active and eligible campaign. |
| Referral ID | Identifier of the referral program associated with referral rewards. | UUID | Conditional | Must reference a valid approved referral record. |
| Loyalty Transaction ID | Identifier of the loyalty transaction generating wallet credit. | UUID | Conditional | Must reference an eligible loyalty reward transaction. |
| Refund ID | Identifier of the refund associated with wallet credit. | UUID | Conditional | Must reference an approved refund transaction. |
| Compensation ID | Identifier of the compensation record associated with wallet credit. | UUID | Conditional | Must reference an approved compensation request. |
| Expiration Date | Expiration date applicable to promotional wallet credits where supported. | DateTime | Conditional | Must be generated according to platform policies. |
| Wallet Payment Amount | Amount of wallet balance to be applied during checkout. | Decimal(12,2) | Conditional | Shall not exceed available wallet balance or configured utilization limits. |
| Remaining Payable Amount | Outstanding amount after wallet utilization. | Decimal(12,2) | System Generated | Automatically calculated by the platform. |
| Transaction Remarks | Optional remarks describing the wallet transaction. | String (500) | No | Must not exceed the configured maximum length and shall not contain malicious content. |
| Customer Device ID | Identifier of the customer device initiating the wallet request. | UUID/String | Conditional | Must reference a registered or authorized device where applicable. |
| Session ID | Authenticated customer session identifier. | UUID/String | Yes | Must represent an active authenticated session. |
| Request Timestamp | Timestamp indicating when the wallet request was initiated. | DateTime | System Generated | Automatically generated using synchronized system time. |
| IP Address | IP address from which the wallet request originated. | IPv4 / IPv6 | System Generated | Captured automatically for security monitoring and auditing. |
| User Agent | Device, browser, or application information used to initiate the request. | String | System Generated | Automatically captured for audit and security purposes. |
| Authorization Token | Authentication token used to authorize the wallet operation. | JWT / Access Token | Yes | Must be valid, active, and unexpired. |
| Correlation ID | Unique identifier used for distributed request tracing across platform services. | UUID | System Generated | Automatically generated for observability, diagnostics, and audit purposes. |

#### 3.1.24.6 Business Rules

1. Every eligible customer shall have only one active Customer Wallet associated with their customer account.
2. Wallet ownership shall be permanently associated with the customer account and shall not be transferable to another customer.
3. Only authenticated and authorized customers shall be permitted to access their own wallet information.
4. Customers shall never be permitted to access, modify, or utilize another customer's wallet.
5. Every wallet transaction shall have a globally unique transaction identifier.
6. Every wallet transaction shall be recorded as an immutable financial record after successful completion.
7. Wallet balances shall always be calculated using completed and successfully committed wallet transactions only.
8. Wallet balances shall never become negative unless explicitly supported by configurable organizational policies.
9. Wallet credits shall originate only from authorized platform operations.
10. Supported wallet credit sources may include approved refunds, cashback, loyalty rewards, referral rewards, promotional campaigns, compensation credits, gift credits, subscription benefits, and authorized administrative adjustments.
11. Wallet debits shall occur only through approved customer purchases or authorized financial adjustments.
