12. Wallet balance shall be applied only when the associated order satisfies all wallet eligibility criteria.
13. Wallet utilization shall comply with configurable minimum order value requirements where applicable.
14. Wallet utilization limits per order shall be configurable by platform administrators.
15. Partial wallet payments may be permitted according to configurable platform policies.
16. Remaining payable amounts after wallet utilization shall be processed using other supported payment methods.
17. Wallet funds shall not be redeemable for cash unless explicitly permitted by applicable regulations and organizational policies.
18. Promotional wallet credits may be restricted to eligible products, restaurants, regions, campaigns, customer segments, or order categories.
19. Promotional wallet credits may include configurable expiration dates.
20. Expired promotional wallet credits shall automatically become unavailable for future utilization.
21. Expired wallet credits shall remain visible within historical transaction records.
22. Wallet credits without expiration shall remain available until utilized or otherwise adjusted through authorized operations.
23. Cashback credits shall be issued only after successful completion of qualifying business events.
24. Referral rewards shall be credited only after referral eligibility conditions are fully satisfied.
25. Loyalty rewards shall be credited only after successful validation of loyalty program rules.
26. Refund credits shall be issued only after refund approval according to refund policies.
27. Compensation credits shall be issued only after approval under applicable compensation policies.
28. Duplicate wallet credits for the same business event shall never be permitted.
29. Duplicate wallet debit transactions shall never be permitted.
30. Every wallet transaction shall be processed as an atomic financial transaction.
31. Partial wallet transaction commits shall never occur.
32. Failed wallet transactions shall automatically roll back all associated financial updates.
33. Concurrent wallet transactions for the same wallet shall be managed using appropriate concurrency control mechanisms.
34. Wallet balance calculations shall always maintain financial consistency across all platform services.
35. Every wallet operation shall generate corresponding audit records.
36. Every financial adjustment shall be traceable to its originating business event.
37. Administrative wallet adjustments shall be permitted only for authorized personnel possessing appropriate financial privileges.
38. Administrative wallet adjustments shall include mandatory business justification.
39. Administrative wallet adjustments shall always be audit logged.
40. Wallet transaction history shall remain permanently available according to applicable financial retention policies.
41. Historical wallet records shall never be modified after successful completion except through authorized financial correction procedures.
42. Personally Identifiable Information (PII), wallet balances, transaction details, and financial records shall be protected using organizational security standards.
43. Wallet operations shall be performed only through secure authenticated sessions.
44. Fraud detection rules shall evaluate eligible wallet transactions according to configurable risk assessment policies.
45. Suspicious wallet activities may be delayed, restricted, or blocked pending fraud investigation.
46. Wallet services shall synchronize financial information with Payment, Refund, Accounting, Order Management, Loyalty, Referral, Promotion, Notification, and Audit services.
47. Wallet reconciliation shall ensure financial consistency across all integrated services.
48. Every successful wallet transaction shall update the customer's available wallet balance immediately after successful transaction completion.
49. Wallet operations shall comply with applicable financial regulations, taxation requirements, accounting standards, consumer protection laws, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, accessibility standards, organizational governance policies, and audit requirements.
50. The Customer Wallet module shall support future wallet types, configurable financial products, digital vouchers, gift cards, subscription credits, multi-currency wallets, promotional campaigns, regional financial requirements, and additional payment innovations without requiring significant architectural redesign.

#### 3.1.24.7 Validations

1. The customer shall be successfully authenticated before accessing the Customer Wallet functionality.
2. The authenticated customer shall be authorized to access only their own wallet account.
3. The customer account shall exist within the platform.
4. The customer account shall be active and not permanently deleted.
5. The wallet account shall exist for the authenticated customer.
6. The wallet account shall be active and eligible for wallet operations.
7. The submitted Wallet ID shall belong to the authenticated customer.
8. The submitted Wallet Transaction ID, when provided, shall reference an existing wallet transaction.
9. The Wallet Operation Type shall be one of the supported operation types.
10. The submitted Order ID, when required, shall reference an existing eligible order.
11. The associated order shall belong to the authenticated customer whenever applicable.
12. The associated order shall satisfy all wallet utilization eligibility rules.
13. The submitted Payment Transaction ID, when provided, shall reference a valid payment transaction.
14. The submitted Refund ID, when provided, shall reference an approved refund transaction.
15. The submitted Referral ID, when provided, shall reference an eligible referral transaction.
16. The submitted Loyalty Transaction ID, when provided, shall reference an approved loyalty reward transaction.
17. The submitted Promotional Campaign ID, when provided, shall reference an active and eligible campaign.
18. The submitted Compensation ID, when provided, shall reference an approved compensation record.
19. The wallet amount shall be greater than zero.
20. The wallet amount shall not exceed configured transaction limits.
21. The wallet payment amount shall not exceed the customer's available wallet balance.
22. The wallet payment amount shall not exceed the eligible payable amount for the associated order.
23. Wallet utilization shall satisfy configured minimum order value requirements where applicable.
24. Wallet utilization shall satisfy configured maximum utilization limits where applicable.
25. Wallet balance shall be sufficient before processing wallet debit operations.
26. Wallet balance calculations shall be performed using the latest committed wallet transactions.
27. Duplicate wallet credit transactions for the same business event shall not be permitted.
28. Duplicate wallet debit transactions shall not be permitted.
29. Duplicate refund credits shall be rejected.
30. Duplicate cashback credits shall be rejected.
31. Duplicate referral rewards shall be rejected.
32. Duplicate loyalty rewards shall be rejected.
33. Duplicate promotional credits shall be rejected.
34. Expired promotional wallet credits shall not be utilized.
35. Wallet credits restricted to specific campaigns, restaurants, products, customer segments, or regions shall satisfy all configured eligibility criteria.
36. Wallet transactions shall comply with applicable expiration policies.
37. Currency code shall be supported by the platform.
38. Wallet currency shall be compatible with the associated payment transaction according to platform policies.
39. Administrative wallet adjustments shall be permitted only for authorized administrative users.
40. Administrative adjustments shall include mandatory business justification.
41. Every wallet transaction shall receive a unique transaction identifier.
42. Transaction timestamps shall be generated using synchronized system time.
43. The customer session shall remain active throughout wallet processing.
44. The access token shall remain valid during request processing.
45. Wallet requests originating from blocked, suspended, or unauthorized devices may be rejected according to security policies.
46. Fraud detection validation shall be executed for eligible wallet transactions according to configured risk assessment rules.
47. High-risk wallet transactions may require additional verification before completion.
48. Wallet operations shall not violate financial reconciliation constraints.
49. All mandatory request fields shall be present before processing.
50. Optional fields, when provided, shall satisfy their respective validation rules.
51. Input values shall conform to their defined data types, formats, length restrictions, and precision requirements.
52. Malformed, unsupported, or tampered requests shall be rejected.
53. SQL injection, Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), command injection, parameter manipulation, and other malicious inputs shall be detected and rejected.
54. Personally Identifiable Information (PII), wallet balances, transaction details, and financial information shall never be exposed to unauthorized users.
55. Every successful and failed validation event shall be recorded in the audit logging system where applicable.
56. Wallet operations shall comply with applicable financial regulations, accounting standards, taxation requirements, consumer protection laws, privacy regulations, cybersecurity policies, accessibility standards, organizational governance requirements, and audit policies before successful execution.

#### 3.1.24.8 Main Flow

1. The customer successfully authenticates into the Food Delivery Platform.
2. The customer navigates to **Account → Customer Wallet**.
3. The system verifies the customer's authenticated session.
4. The system validates that the customer is authorized to access the requested wallet.
5. The system retrieves the customer's wallet account.
6. The system retrieves the latest committed wallet balance.
7. The system retrieves wallet summary information, including available balance, pending adjustments (if applicable), promotional credits, cashback balance, loyalty credits, referral rewards, compensation credits, and other supported wallet components.
8. The system calculates the customer's effective available wallet balance according to configured business policies.
9. The system retrieves the customer's wallet transaction history.
10. The system displays the wallet dashboard, including current balance, wallet summary, recent transactions, transaction categories, and supported wallet actions.
11. The customer reviews wallet information.
12. If the customer selects a wallet transaction, the system retrieves the complete transaction details.
13. The system displays the selected wallet transaction information, including transaction amount, transaction type, transaction source, transaction timestamp, reference identifiers, remarks, and applicable expiration information.
14. If the customer initiates a wallet payment during checkout, the Checkout Service requests the customer's latest available wallet balance.
15. The system validates wallet eligibility for the associated order.
16. The system validates minimum order value requirements where applicable.
17. The system validates maximum wallet utilization limits according to configured business rules.
18. The system validates that sufficient wallet balance is available.
19. The system calculates the maximum wallet amount eligible for utilization.
20. The customer confirms the wallet payment.
21. The system reserves the required wallet amount during payment processing where applicable.
22. The Payment Service processes the remaining payable amount using the selected payment method, if required.
23. After successful payment authorization, the system debits the utilized wallet amount.
24. The system generates a unique wallet transaction identifier.
25. The system records the wallet debit transaction.
26. The system recalculates the customer's available wallet balance.
27. The Accounting Service records the corresponding financial transaction.
28. The Audit Logging Service records the completed wallet operation.
29. The Notification Service sends a wallet debit confirmation to the customer.
30. If an eligible refund is approved, the Refund Service initiates a wallet credit request.
31. The system validates refund eligibility according to configured refund policies.
32. The system creates a wallet credit transaction for the approved refund amount.
33. The system updates the customer's wallet balance.
34. The Accounting Service records the refund credit.
35. The Audit Logging Service records the refund wallet transaction.
36. The Notification Service informs the customer about the wallet refund credit.
37. If a cashback reward becomes eligible, the Cashback Service initiates a wallet credit transaction.
38. The system validates cashback eligibility.
39. The system credits the eligible cashback amount to the wallet.
40. The system updates the wallet balance.
41. The wallet transaction is recorded as an immutable financial record.
42. The Notification Service sends cashback confirmation to the customer.
43. If referral, loyalty, promotional, compensation, gift, or other approved wallet credits become eligible, the respective platform service submits an authorized wallet credit request.
44. The system validates eligibility according to the corresponding business rules.
45. The system credits the approved amount to the customer's wallet.
46. The system updates the available wallet balance.
47. The wallet transaction is committed as an atomic financial transaction.
48. The Financial Reconciliation Service synchronizes wallet balances across dependent platform services.
49. The Audit Logging Service records all successful wallet operations.
50. The system displays the updated wallet balance, latest transaction history, and successful wallet operation confirmation to the customer.

#### 3.1.24.9 Alternate Flows

##### Alternate Flow 1: Customer Has No Wallet Balance

1. The customer opens the Customer Wallet module.
2. The system retrieves the customer's wallet information.
3. The system determines that the available wallet balance is zero.
4. The system displays the wallet dashboard with a balance of **0.00**.
5. The system informs the customer that no wallet funds are currently available.
6. The customer may continue viewing wallet history or exit the module.
7. The use case ends successfully.

---

##### Alternate Flow 2: Wallet Does Not Exist

1. The customer requests wallet information.
2. The system searches for the customer's wallet account.
3. The system determines that no wallet exists.
4. If automatic wallet creation is enabled, the system creates a new wallet account.
5. The system initializes the wallet with a zero balance.
6. The system records wallet creation in the audit logs.
7. The system displays the newly created wallet.
8. The use case ends successfully.

If automatic wallet creation is disabled:

1. The system returns an appropriate error indicating that the wallet is unavailable.
2. The request ends.

---

##### Alternate Flow 3: Wallet Payment Covers Entire Order

1. During checkout, the customer chooses to pay using the wallet.
2. The system validates wallet eligibility.
3. The system verifies that the available wallet balance is greater than or equal to the total payable amount.
4. The system debits the required wallet amount.
5. The remaining payable amount becomes zero.
6. No additional payment method is required.
7. The order payment is completed successfully.
8. The wallet balance is updated.
9. Audit logs are recorded.
10. The customer receives payment confirmation.

---

##### Alternate Flow 4: Wallet Payment Covers Partial Order Amount

1. During checkout, the customer selects wallet payment.
2. The system validates wallet eligibility.
3. The system determines that the wallet balance is less than the total payable amount.
4. The system calculates the remaining payable amount.
5. The customer completes payment using another supported payment method.
6. After successful payment authorization, the wallet amount is debited.
7. The remaining amount is collected through the selected payment method.
8. The order payment is completed successfully.
9. Wallet records are updated.
10. Audit logs are generated.

---

##### Alternate Flow 5: Refund Credited to Wallet

1. An order refund is approved.
2. The Refund Service initiates a wallet credit request.
3. The system validates refund eligibility.
4. The approved refund amount is credited to the customer's wallet.
5. The wallet balance is recalculated.
6. The transaction is recorded.
7. Financial reconciliation is updated.
8. The customer receives a refund notification.
9. The use case ends successfully.

---

##### Alternate Flow 6: Cashback Reward Credited

1. The customer completes a cashback-eligible order.
2. Cashback eligibility is verified.
3. The eligible cashback amount is calculated.
4. The cashback amount is credited to the wallet.
5. The wallet balance is updated.
6. The transaction is permanently recorded.
7. The customer receives a cashback notification.
8. The use case ends successfully.

---

##### Alternate Flow 7: Promotional Credit Issued

1. The customer becomes eligible for a promotional campaign.
2. The Promotional Campaign Service submits a wallet credit request.
3. The system validates campaign eligibility.
4. Promotional credit is added to the wallet.
5. Expiration information is assigned where applicable.
6. The wallet balance is updated.
7. Audit records are generated.
8. The customer receives a promotional credit notification.

---

##### Alternate Flow 8: Loyalty or Referral Reward Credited

1. A loyalty milestone or referral requirement is successfully completed.
2. The respective platform service requests wallet credit.
3. Eligibility validation is completed.
4. The approved reward amount is credited.
5. Wallet balance is updated.
6. Transaction history is updated.
7. Audit logs are generated.
8. Notification is delivered to the customer.

---

##### Alternate Flow 9: Promotional Credit Expires

1. The scheduled expiration process identifies expired wallet credits.
2. The system validates expiration eligibility.
3. Expired promotional credits are marked as unavailable.
4. Available wallet balance is recalculated.
5. Historical transaction records remain unchanged.
6. Expiration activity is recorded.
7. The customer may receive an expiration notification according to configured policies.

---

##### Alternate Flow 10: Fraud Review Required

1. A wallet transaction triggers fraud detection rules.
2. The Fraud Detection Service classifies the transaction as high risk.
3. Wallet processing is temporarily suspended.
4. Additional verification or manual review is initiated.
5. The customer is informed that the transaction is under review if permitted by security policies.
6. Upon approval, wallet processing resumes.
7. Upon rejection, the wallet transaction is cancelled.
8. Audit logs capture all fraud review activities.

---

##### Alternate Flow 11: Concurrent Wallet Transaction Detected

1. Multiple wallet transactions are initiated simultaneously.
2. The system detects concurrent modification attempts.
3. Appropriate concurrency controls are applied.
4. One transaction proceeds successfully.
5. Remaining conflicting requests are retried, queued, or rejected according to configured concurrency policies.
6. Wallet balance consistency is maintained.
7. Audit logs capture the concurrency event.

---

##### Alternate Flow 12: Administrative Wallet Adjustment

1. An authorized administrator initiates a wallet adjustment.
2. The system verifies administrative privileges.
3. Mandatory business justification is validated.
4. The adjustment is processed.
5. Wallet balance is recalculated.
6. Financial reconciliation is updated.
7. Administrative audit logs are recorded.
8. The customer may receive a wallet adjustment notification according to platform policy.

---

##### Alternate Flow 13: Wallet Service Temporarily Unavailable

1. The customer requests wallet information.
2. The Customer Wallet Service is unavailable.
3. The system records the failure.
4. No wallet changes are performed.
5. An appropriate service unavailability response is returned.
6. The customer is requested to retry later.
7. The failure is logged for operational monitoring.

#### 3.1.24.10 Postconditions

Upon successful completion of the Customer Wallet functionality, the following postconditions shall be satisfied:

1. The requested wallet operation shall be completed successfully.
2. The customer's wallet account shall remain active unless modified through authorized administrative procedures.
3. The wallet balance shall accurately reflect all successfully committed wallet transactions.
4. The available wallet balance shall be recalculated immediately after every successful wallet credit or debit operation.
5. Every successful wallet transaction shall be permanently recorded within the wallet transaction history.
6. Every wallet transaction shall possess a unique transaction identifier.
7. Wallet transaction records shall become immutable after successful completion.
8. Historical wallet transactions shall remain available for customer viewing, auditing, reconciliation, dispute resolution, and regulatory reporting.
9. If wallet funds were utilized during checkout, the corresponding order payment records shall accurately reflect the wallet contribution.
10. If partial wallet payment was used, the remaining payable amount shall be settled using the selected payment method.
11. If a refund was processed to the wallet, the customer's wallet balance shall increase by the approved refund amount.
12. If cashback was awarded, the eligible cashback amount shall be credited to the wallet.
13. If referral rewards were approved, the eligible referral credits shall be added to the wallet.
14. If loyalty rewards were approved, the eligible loyalty credits shall be reflected in the wallet balance.
15. If promotional or compensation credits were issued, the eligible wallet balance shall be updated accordingly.
16. Expiration details for eligible promotional credits shall be stored according to configured business policies.
17. Expired promotional credits shall no longer contribute to the available wallet balance.
18. Financial consistency shall be maintained across Wallet, Payment, Refund, Accounting, Order Management, Loyalty, Referral, Promotional Campaign, and other integrated platform services.
19. Financial reconciliation records shall be updated for successfully completed wallet transactions.
20. Every successful wallet operation shall be recorded within the Audit Logging Service.
21. Security monitoring systems shall record applicable wallet-related security events.
22. Notification Services shall generate wallet notifications for eligible wallet events according to configured notification preferences.
23. Wallet transaction timestamps shall accurately represent the committed transaction time using synchronized system time.
24. Customer Profile information unrelated to wallet operations shall remain unchanged.
25. Personally Identifiable Information (PII), wallet balances, transaction details, and financial records shall remain protected according to organizational security policies.
26. All completed wallet operations shall comply with applicable financial regulations, accounting standards, taxation requirements, consumer protection laws, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, accessibility standards, audit policies, and organizational governance requirements.
27. All dependent platform services shall receive the latest committed wallet information where synchronization is required.
28. Failed wallet operations shall not modify wallet balances, financial records, historical transactions, accounting records, or audit integrity.
29. Database consistency shall be maintained across all affected services following successful transaction completion.
30. The Customer Wallet module shall remain available for subsequent authorized wallet operations.

#### 3.1.24.11 Success Response

Upon successful completion of a Customer Wallet operation, the system shall return a standardized success response containing only authorized, non-sensitive information.

**Response Structure**

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Timestamp | Date and time when the response was generated. |
| HTTP Status Code | Standard HTTP success status code (200 OK, 201 Created, or other applicable success code). |
| Status | Indicates successful execution of the wallet operation. |
| Message | Human-readable success message. |
| Wallet ID | Unique identifier of the customer's wallet. |
| Customer ID | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. |
| Wallet Operation Type | Type of wallet operation successfully completed. |
| Wallet Transaction ID | Unique identifier of the completed wallet transaction, when applicable. |
| Current Wallet Balance | Latest available wallet balance after successful processing. |
| Transaction Amount | Amount credited or debited during the wallet transaction, when applicable. |
| Transaction Type | Credit, Debit, Refund, Cashback, Loyalty Credit, Referral Credit, Promotional Credit, Compensation Credit, Wallet Payment, Administrative Adjustment, or other supported transaction type. |
| Balance Before Transaction | Wallet balance immediately before transaction execution, when applicable. |
| Balance After Transaction | Wallet balance immediately after successful transaction completion, when applicable. |
| Currency | Currency associated with the wallet balance and transaction. |
| Order ID | Associated order identifier, when applicable. |
| Payment Transaction ID | Associated payment transaction identifier, when applicable. |
| Refund ID | Associated refund identifier, when applicable. |
| Promotional Campaign ID | Associated promotional campaign identifier, when applicable. |
| Loyalty Transaction ID | Associated loyalty transaction identifier, when applicable. |
| Referral ID | Associated referral program identifier, when applicable. |
| Compensation ID | Associated compensation identifier, when applicable. |
| Credit Source | Source of wallet credit where applicable. |
| Debit Purpose | Business purpose of wallet debit where applicable. |
| Remaining Payable Amount | Outstanding payment amount after wallet utilization, when applicable. |
| Expiration Date | Expiration date of promotional wallet credits where applicable. |
| Transaction Timestamp | Timestamp when the wallet transaction was successfully committed. |
| Wallet Summary | Updated wallet summary including available balance and supported wallet categories. |
| Transaction History | Updated wallet transaction history when applicable. |
| Notification Status | Indicates whether wallet notification delivery has been initiated. |
| Audit Reference ID | Identifier associated with the audit log entry for traceability. |
| Correlation ID | Unique request correlation identifier for distributed tracing. |

**Example Success Messages**

- Customer Wallet retrieved successfully.
- Wallet balance retrieved successfully.
- Wallet transaction history retrieved successfully.
- Wallet transaction details retrieved successfully.
- Wallet payment completed successfully.
- Wallet balance updated successfully.
- Refund credited to wallet successfully.
- Cashback credited successfully.
- Loyalty reward credited successfully.
- Referral reward credited successfully.
- Promotional wallet credit issued successfully.
- Compensation credit added successfully.
- Wallet transaction completed successfully.
- Wallet information synchronized successfully.

**Response Characteristics**

1. The response shall contain only information that the authenticated customer is authorized to access.
2. The response shall accurately reflect the latest committed wallet balance.
3. All monetary values shall be presented using the configured currency and decimal precision.
4. Transaction identifiers shall uniquely identify completed wallet transactions.
5. Historical transaction information shall remain unchanged following successful operations.
6. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be included only when required and authorized.
7. Passwords, authentication credentials, access tokens, refresh tokens, encryption keys, internal financial reconciliation details, fraud detection rules, internal configuration values, database identifiers, server information, and other confidential system information shall never be included in the success response.
8. The response shall remain consistent across all supported client applications and APIs.
9. The response shall comply with applicable financial security, privacy, accessibility, accounting, audit, consumer protection, and regulatory requirements.
10. The response shall be securely transmitted over HTTPS.

#### 3.1.24.12 Failure Response

When the Customer Wallet operation cannot be completed successfully, the system shall return a standardized failure response containing only authorized, non-sensitive error information.

**Response Structure**

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Timestamp | Date and time when the error response was generated. |
| HTTP Status Code | Appropriate HTTP error status code (400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 422, 429, 500, 503, or other applicable code). |
| Status | Indicates unsuccessful execution of the wallet operation. |
| Error Code | Unique application-specific error code representing the failure condition. |
| Error Category | Classification of the error (Validation, Authentication, Authorization, Business Rule, Financial, Concurrency, Fraud Detection, System, Network, etc.). |
| Error Message | Human-readable description of the failure. |
| Wallet ID | Wallet identifier when available and authorized. |
| Wallet Transaction ID | Transaction identifier when generated before failure. |
| Wallet Operation Type | Wallet operation that failed. |
| Field Name | Input field associated with the validation failure, when applicable. |
| Rejected Value | Invalid value submitted by the client, when appropriate and safe to expose. |
| Correlation ID | Unique request identifier for distributed tracing and diagnostics. |
| Request ID | Unique identifier associated with the client request. |
| Retry Allowed | Indicates whether the failed operation may be retried safely. |
| Suggested Action | Recommended action for resolving the error when appropriate. |
| Support Reference ID | Reference identifier for customer support and operational investigation. |

**Example Failure Messages**

- Authentication required.
- Customer session has expired.
- Access to the requested wallet is denied.
- Customer account does not exist.
- Customer account is inactive.
- Wallet account not found.
- Wallet account is inactive.
- Invalid wallet operation type.
- Wallet balance is insufficient.
- Wallet utilization exceeds the permitted limit.
- Wallet transaction already processed.
- Duplicate wallet transaction detected.
- Promotional wallet credit has expired.
- Refund is not eligible for wallet credit.
- Cashback eligibility validation failed.
- Referral reward is not eligible.
- Loyalty reward is not eligible.
- Promotional campaign is inactive.
- Wallet transaction is currently under fraud review.
- Concurrent wallet transaction detected.
- Financial reconciliation validation failed.
- Mandatory request fields are missing.
- Invalid request format.
- Unsupported currency.
- Wallet service is temporarily unavailable.
- Internal server error occurred while processing the wallet request.

**Response Characteristics**

1. Failure responses shall use standardized error structures across all platform APIs.
2. Error messages shall clearly describe the failure without exposing confidential implementation details.
3. Validation failures shall identify the relevant input field whenever applicable.
4. Authentication failures shall never disclose whether a customer account exists.
5. Authorization failures shall never reveal information about resources owned by other customers.
6. Financial processing failures shall preserve transaction consistency by ensuring no partial wallet updates are committed.
7. Fraud detection failures shall not disclose internal fraud detection logic, scoring models, security thresholds, or investigation criteria.
8. Database schema information, internal service architecture, SQL statements, stack traces, source code, encryption keys, authentication credentials, access tokens, refresh tokens, server configuration, infrastructure details, internal financial reconciliation information, and other confidential system information shall never be included in failure responses.
9. Every failed wallet operation shall generate corresponding audit log entries.
10. Failure responses shall comply with applicable financial security, privacy, accessibility, accounting, audit, consumer protection, and regulatory requirements.
11. Failure responses shall be transmitted only through secure HTTPS connections.
12. Failed wallet operations shall not modify wallet balances, financial records, accounting records, transaction history, audit integrity, or dependent platform services.

#### 3.1.24.13 Non-Functional Considerations

1. **Performance**
   - The system shall retrieve wallet information within the platform-defined response time under normal operating conditions.
   - Wallet balance retrieval shall remain optimized regardless of wallet transaction history size.
   - Wallet payment processing shall not introduce unacceptable delays during checkout.
   - Wallet transaction processing shall support high transaction throughput during peak business hours.
   - Database queries for wallet operations shall be optimized using appropriate indexing and query optimization techniques.

2. **Availability**
   - The Customer Wallet module shall satisfy the platform's defined service availability objectives.
   - Planned maintenance activities shall minimize customer impact.
   - High availability mechanisms shall minimize downtime for financial operations.
   - Service interruptions shall not compromise financial consistency.

3. **Scalability**
   - The system shall support millions of customer wallet accounts.
   - The platform shall scale horizontally to accommodate increasing wallet transaction volumes.
   - Wallet services shall support seasonal traffic spikes without significant performance degradation.
   - Storage architecture shall support long-term retention of wallet transaction history.

4. **Reliability**
   - Wallet balances shall remain financially accurate under all operating conditions.
   - Every committed wallet transaction shall be durable.
   - System failures shall never result in duplicate wallet transactions.
   - Failed wallet operations shall automatically roll back incomplete financial changes.
   - Recovery mechanisms shall preserve wallet integrity after unexpected failures.

5. **Security**
   - All wallet operations shall require authenticated customer sessions.
   - Authorization shall ensure customers access only their own wallets.
   - Wallet balances and financial information shall be protected using encryption during transmission and storage where applicable.
   - Sensitive financial information shall never be exposed to unauthorized users.
   - Secure coding practices shall protect against SQL Injection, Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF), command injection, insecure direct object references, parameter tampering, session hijacking, replay attacks, and other common security threats.
   - Fraud detection mechanisms shall continuously evaluate eligible wallet transactions.
   - Administrative wallet operations shall require elevated authorization and complete audit trails.

6. **Data Integrity**
   - Every wallet transaction shall maintain ACID transaction properties.
   - Wallet balances shall always equal the cumulative result of committed wallet transactions.
   - Duplicate financial transactions shall be prevented.
   - Referential integrity shall be maintained across Wallet, Orders, Payments, Refunds, Loyalty, Referral, Promotions, Accounting, and Audit modules.
   - Financial reconciliation shall maintain consistency across all integrated services.

7. **Auditability**
   - Every successful and failed wallet operation shall be recorded within immutable audit logs.
   - Audit records shall include transaction identifiers, timestamps, initiating actor, operation type, affected wallet, and outcome.
   - Audit records shall remain available according to applicable financial retention requirements.
   - Audit information shall support compliance reporting, dispute resolution, financial reconciliation, forensic investigation, and operational monitoring.

8. **Usability**
   - Wallet information shall be presented using clear and consistent terminology.
   - Monetary values shall be displayed using standardized currency formatting.
   - Transaction history shall support easy customer review.
   - Wallet balances shall update immediately after successful transaction completion.
   - User interfaces shall remain consistent across supported platforms.

9. **Accessibility**
   - The Customer Wallet functionality shall comply with applicable accessibility standards.
   - Wallet interfaces shall support keyboard navigation where applicable.
   - Screen readers shall correctly interpret wallet information.
   - Color shall not be the sole method of conveying financial information.
   - Appropriate accessibility labels shall be provided for wallet controls.

10. **Maintainability**
    - Wallet business rules shall be configurable without requiring significant source code modifications.
    - Platform configuration shall support future wallet enhancements.
    - Source code shall follow organizational coding standards.
    - Financial calculations shall be modular, reusable, and independently testable.
    - Operational monitoring shall simplify troubleshooting and maintenance.

11. **Interoperability**
    - The Customer Wallet module shall integrate seamlessly with Payment, Refund, Order Management, Loyalty, Referral, Promotion, Notification, Accounting, Fraud Detection, Customer Profile, and Audit services.
    - APIs shall follow organizational API standards.
    - Data exchange between services shall remain secure and reliable.
    - Distributed transaction consistency shall be maintained across integrated services.

12. **Monitoring & Observability**
    - Wallet operations shall generate operational metrics.
    - Financial transaction failures shall trigger operational alerts.
    - Performance metrics shall be continuously monitored.
    - Distributed tracing shall support end-to-end wallet transaction diagnostics.
    - Log aggregation shall support centralized operational monitoring.

13. **Compliance**
    - Customer Wallet functionality shall comply with applicable financial regulations, accounting standards, taxation requirements, consumer protection laws, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, accessibility standards, audit requirements, data retention policies, and organizational governance requirements.
    - Financial records shall be retained according to applicable legal and regulatory requirements.
    - Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be processed in accordance with organizational privacy policies and applicable data protection regulations.

14. **Extensibility**
    - The architecture shall support future wallet capabilities including multiple wallet types, digital vouchers, gift cards, prepaid credits, subscription credits, multi-currency wallets, installment credits, configurable promotional campaigns, regional financial regulations, and future payment innovations without requiring major architectural redesign.

#### 3.1.24.14 Acceptance Criteria

| AC ID | Acceptance Criteria |
|-------|----------------------|
| AC-CW-001 | The system shall allow only authenticated customers to access the Customer Wallet functionality. |
| AC-CW-002 | The system shall authorize customers to access only the wallet associated with their own customer account. |
| AC-CW-003 | The system shall display the customer's current wallet balance after successful authentication and authorization. |
| AC-CW-004 | The system shall display wallet transaction history in chronological order according to configured business rules. |
| AC-CW-005 | Every wallet transaction shall have a unique transaction identifier. |
| AC-CW-006 | Wallet balances shall always equal the cumulative result of successfully committed wallet transactions. |
| AC-CW-007 | The system shall accurately calculate wallet balances immediately after every successful wallet credit or debit transaction. |
| AC-CW-008 | The system shall validate all wallet operations before execution. |
| AC-CW-009 | Wallet debit operations shall not be permitted when the available wallet balance is insufficient unless explicitly supported by configurable business policies. |
| AC-CW-010 | Wallet utilization during checkout shall comply with configured minimum order value requirements where applicable. |
| AC-CW-011 | Wallet utilization shall comply with configured maximum utilization limits where applicable. |
| AC-CW-012 | Partial wallet payments shall be supported only when enabled through platform configuration. |
| AC-CW-013 | Approved refunds shall be credited to the customer's wallet according to configured refund policies. |
| AC-CW-014 | Cashback rewards shall be credited only after successful eligibility validation. |
| AC-CW-015 | Referral rewards shall be credited only after referral eligibility conditions are satisfied. |
| AC-CW-016 | Loyalty rewards shall be credited only after successful validation of loyalty program rules. |
| AC-CW-017 | Promotional wallet credits shall be issued only for eligible customers and eligible campaigns. |
| AC-CW-018 | Promotional wallet credits shall expire according to configured expiration policies where applicable. |
| AC-CW-019 | Expired promotional credits shall not be available for future wallet utilization. |
| AC-CW-020 | Historical wallet transactions shall remain immutable after successful completion. |
| AC-CW-021 | Duplicate wallet credit transactions for the same business event shall not be permitted. |
| AC-CW-022 | Duplicate wallet debit transactions shall not be permitted. |
| AC-CW-023 | Every wallet transaction shall be processed as an atomic financial transaction. |
| AC-CW-024 | Partial financial updates shall never be committed. |
| AC-CW-025 | Failed wallet transactions shall automatically roll back all associated financial updates. |
| AC-CW-026 | Concurrent wallet transactions shall maintain financial consistency using appropriate concurrency control mechanisms. |
| AC-CW-027 | Wallet balances shall remain synchronized across Wallet, Payment, Refund, Accounting, Order Management, Loyalty, Referral, Promotional Campaign, Notification, and other authorized platform services. |
| AC-CW-028 | Every successful wallet operation shall generate immutable audit records. |
| AC-CW-029 | Every failed wallet operation shall generate corresponding audit records where applicable. |
| AC-CW-030 | Security monitoring shall record suspicious wallet activities, unauthorized access attempts, fraud events, and abnormal financial behavior. |
| AC-CW-031 | Administrative wallet adjustments shall be permitted only for authorized personnel with appropriate financial privileges. |
| AC-CW-032 | Administrative wallet adjustments shall require mandatory business justification. |
| AC-CW-033 | Personally Identifiable Information (PII), wallet balances, transaction details, and financial records shall be accessible only to authorized users. |
| AC-CW-034 | Passwords, authentication credentials, access tokens, refresh tokens, encryption keys, fraud detection rules, internal financial reconciliation data, database information, server details, and other confidential information shall never be exposed in responses. |
| AC-CW-035 | All wallet communications shall occur exclusively over secure HTTPS connections. |
| AC-CW-036 | Wallet transaction records shall be retained according to applicable financial retention policies. |
| AC-CW-037 | Financial reconciliation shall maintain consistency across all integrated platform services. |
| AC-CW-038 | The Customer Wallet module shall maintain the platform's defined service availability objectives. |
| AC-CW-039 | The module shall support concurrent wallet operations without compromising performance, reliability, availability, consistency, or financial integrity. |
| AC-CW-040 | The architecture shall support future wallet capabilities including multiple wallet types, digital vouchers, gift cards, prepaid credits, subscription credits, multi-currency wallets, configurable promotional campaigns, regional financial requirements, and future payment innovations without requiring significant architectural redesign. |
| AC-CW-041 | Customer Wallet functionality shall comply with applicable financial regulations, accounting standards, taxation requirements, consumer protection laws, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, accessibility standards, audit requirements, organizational governance policies, and applicable legal obligations. |
| AC-CW-042 | The Customer Wallet module shall successfully pass functional, integration, financial reconciliation, security, performance, regression, accessibility, compliance, disaster recovery, and user acceptance testing before production deployment. |

### 3.1.25 Loyalty Points

#### 3.1.25.1 Description

The **Loyalty Points** module enables the Food Delivery Platform to reward customers with points for eligible activities, encouraging customer retention, repeat purchases, and long-term engagement. The module maintains a centralized loyalty account for each eligible customer, records all point-earning and point-redemption activities, tracks point balances, and enforces configurable loyalty program policies.

Each eligible customer shall have a unique loyalty account associated with their customer profile. The loyalty account shall maintain the customer's available points, earned points, redeemed points, expired points, pending points where applicable, transaction history, loyalty tier information, earning sources, redemption history, and other platform-supported loyalty information.

Customers may earn loyalty points through eligible platform activities including successful order completion, promotional campaigns, referral programs, milestone achievements, seasonal events, subscription benefits, partner programs, customer engagement initiatives, or other authorized reward mechanisms supported by the platform. Point earning rules shall be fully configurable by authorized administrators.

Customers may redeem available loyalty points for eligible benefits including order discounts, wallet credits, promotional rewards, coupons, exclusive offers, membership benefits, gift vouchers, premium services, or other platform-supported redemption options according to configurable business policies. Redemption eligibility shall be validated before successful redemption.

The platform shall support configurable loyalty point expiration policies. Points that reach their configured expiration date shall automatically expire and shall no longer be available for redemption. Historical loyalty transactions, including earned, redeemed, expired, adjusted, or reversed points, shall remain permanently recorded and available for audit, reporting, compliance, financial reconciliation where applicable, customer support, and dispute resolution.

The Loyalty Points module shall integrate with Order Management, Customer Profile, Customer Wallet, Promotional Campaign Management, Referral Program, Payment Services, Notification Services, Customer Dashboard, Analytics Services, Audit Logging Services, Fraud Detection Services, and other authorized platform services to ensure accurate reward calculation, customer engagement tracking, and business consistency.

All loyalty point operations shall be protected using authentication, authorization, business rule validation, fraud detection, transaction management, audit logging, and security controls. Every successful and failed loyalty point operation shall be recorded within the platform's audit logging framework to support compliance reporting, operational monitoring, dispute resolution, forensic investigations, customer support, and regulatory audits where applicable.

Personally Identifiable Information (PII), loyalty balances, reward history, customer engagement information, redemption history, and other sensitive customer data shall remain protected throughout the loyalty lifecycle using organizational security standards, encryption, secure APIs, access controls, and privacy policies.

The Loyalty Points module shall be designed to support future enhancements including multiple loyalty programs, tier-based memberships, partner loyalty ecosystems, gamification features, regional reward programs, coalition loyalty networks, AI-driven reward recommendations, configurable earning strategies, personalized rewards, and additional customer engagement capabilities without requiring significant architectural redesign.

#### 3.1.25.2 Actors

The following actors participate in the Loyalty Points functionality:

| Actor | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Customer | Authenticated customer who earns, views, and redeems loyalty points through eligible platform activities. |
| Authentication Service | Authenticates the customer before allowing access to Loyalty Points functionality. |
| Authorization Service | Ensures only the loyalty account owner is authorized to access and redeem loyalty points. |
| Loyalty Points Service | Manages loyalty accounts, point calculations, earning rules, redemption rules, expiration policies, tier management, and loyalty transactions. |
| Customer Profile Service | Retrieves customer profile information associated with the loyalty account. |
| Order Management Service | Triggers loyalty point calculation after successful completion of eligible orders. |
| Payment Service | Confirms successful payment completion before eligible loyalty points are awarded. |
| Customer Wallet Service | Credits redeemed loyalty rewards into the customer's wallet where applicable. |
| Promotional Campaign Service | Awards bonus loyalty points through promotional campaigns, seasonal offers, and marketing initiatives. |
| Referral Program Service | Awards loyalty points for successful referral activities according to referral program rules. |
| Membership & Subscription Service | Determines additional loyalty benefits for eligible subscription or membership plans. |
| Tier Management Service | Calculates customer loyalty tiers and determines applicable tier-based benefits. |
| Rewards Catalog Service | Maintains redeemable rewards, redemption values, reward inventory, and eligibility criteria. |
| Notification Service | Sends notifications related to earned points, redeemed rewards, expiring points, loyalty tier changes, and promotional loyalty campaigns. |
| Fraud Detection Service | Monitors suspicious loyalty activities, abnormal earning patterns, fraudulent redemptions, and reward abuse. |
| Analytics Service | Generates loyalty analytics, customer engagement metrics, reward utilization statistics, and business intelligence reports. |
| Audit Logging Service | Records all successful and failed loyalty operations for compliance, customer support, dispute resolution, auditing, and operational monitoring. |
| Security Monitoring Service | Detects unauthorized access attempts, suspicious redemption activities, privilege escalation attempts, and other loyalty-related security events. |
| Configuration Management Service | Maintains configurable earning rules, redemption policies, point expiration rules, tier configurations, campaign rules, and loyalty business policies. |
| Compliance & Governance Service | Ensures loyalty operations comply with applicable consumer protection laws, privacy regulations, taxation requirements, accessibility standards, cybersecurity policies, organizational governance requirements, and applicable legal regulations. |

#### 3.1.25.3 Preconditions

Before a customer can access or use the Loyalty Points functionality, the following preconditions shall be satisfied:

1. The customer shall have a registered customer account.
2. The customer account shall be active and not permanently deleted.
3. The customer shall be successfully authenticated.
4. The customer shall possess a valid authenticated session.
5. The customer's access token shall be valid and unexpired.
6. The customer shall be authorized to access only their own loyalty account.
7. The Loyalty Points module shall be enabled within the platform configuration.
8. A unique loyalty account shall exist for the customer or shall be automatically created according to platform policy.
9. The Loyalty Points Service shall be operational.
10. The Customer Profile Service shall be available.
11. The Order Management Service shall be operational for calculating eligible loyalty rewards.
12. The Configuration Management Service shall provide the latest loyalty earning rules, redemption policies, expiration rules, tier configurations, campaign rules, and business policies.
13. The customer's loyalty account information shall be retrievable from the database.
14. The customer's available loyalty points shall be accurately calculated and available.
15. The customer's loyalty transaction history shall be available for retrieval.
16. The Rewards Catalog Service shall be available for validating reward redemption eligibility where applicable.
17. The Customer Wallet Service shall be operational if redeemed loyalty rewards are converted into wallet credits.
18. The Promotional Campaign Service shall be operational for awarding campaign-based loyalty points where applicable.
19. The Referral Program Service shall be operational for referral-based loyalty rewards where applicable.
20. The Membership & Subscription Service shall be available for validating subscription-based loyalty benefits where applicable.
21. The Tier Management Service shall be operational for calculating loyalty tiers and applicable benefits.
22. The database shall be available for reading and updating loyalty records.
23. Communication between client applications and backend services shall occur exclusively over secure HTTPS connections.
24. The Audit Logging Service shall be available to record loyalty operations.
25. The Fraud Detection Service shall be operational to evaluate loyalty earning and redemption requests where applicable.
26. Required validation services shall be operational.
27. Authorized dependent platform services shall be capable of synchronizing updated loyalty information.
28. Loyalty transaction identifiers shall be generated uniquely for every loyalty transaction.
29. Applicable loyalty policies, reward rules, expiration rules, promotional rules, organizational governance requirements, and regulatory requirements shall be available for validation.
30. No conflicting loyalty transaction shall currently be in progress for the same loyalty account.
31. System time shall be synchronized to accurately record loyalty transaction timestamps, audit events, expiration schedules, and operational evidence.
32. All required platform services shall be functioning within acceptable operational thresholds.
33. The platform shall satisfy applicable authentication, authorization, privacy, cybersecurity, accessibility, consumer protection, financial reporting (where applicable), audit, taxation, and regulatory requirements before processing loyalty operations.

#### 3.1.25.4 Trigger

The Loyalty Points functionality shall be initiated when one of the following events occurs:

1. An authenticated customer selects the **Loyalty Points** option from the Account, Profile, Rewards, or Customer Dashboard section.
2. An authenticated customer navigates to the Loyalty Points page using the mobile application or web portal.
3. An authenticated customer requests to view the current loyalty points balance.
4. An authenticated customer requests to view loyalty transaction history.
5. An authenticated customer selects a loyalty transaction to view detailed transaction information.
6. An authenticated customer requests to redeem available loyalty points.
7. The customer selects an eligible reward from the Rewards Catalog.
8. The system validates the customer's eligibility for the selected reward redemption.
9. A customer successfully completes an eligible order that qualifies for loyalty point earning.
10. The Order Management Service notifies the Loyalty Points Service about successful order completion.
11. The Payment Service confirms successful payment completion for an eligible order.
12. A promotional campaign awards bonus loyalty points to eligible customers.
13. A referral program successfully qualifies for loyalty point rewards.
14. A customer achieves a milestone, loyalty tier upgrade, anniversary reward, subscription benefit, seasonal reward, or other eligible engagement event.
15. The Tier Management Service recalculates the customer's loyalty tier based on updated eligibility criteria.
16. A scheduled expiration process identifies loyalty points that have reached their configured expiration date.
17. An authorized administrator performs an approved loyalty point adjustment according to documented operational procedures.
18. An authenticated API request is received from an authorized client application to retrieve loyalty information or initiate an eligible loyalty operation.
19. The Fraud Detection Service initiates additional verification for a loyalty earning or redemption request according to configured risk assessment policies.
20. The Notification Service generates loyalty-related notifications following successful point earning, redemption, expiration, adjustment, or tier updates.
21. Customer Support retrieves loyalty account information while ensuring only authorized personnel access customer loyalty information according to applicable security, privacy, and organizational governance policies.

#### 3.1.25.5 Input Fields

| Field Name | Description | Data Type | Mandatory | Validation |
|------------|-------------|-----------|-----------|------------|
| Customer ID | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer associated with the loyalty account. | UUID | Yes | Must belong to the authenticated customer and exist in the system. |
| Loyalty Account ID | Unique identifier of the customer's loyalty account. | UUID | Yes | Must exist, be active, and belong to the authenticated customer. |
| Loyalty Transaction ID | Unique identifier of a loyalty transaction. | UUID | Conditional | Required for viewing transaction details. Must reference an existing loyalty transaction. |
| Loyalty Operation Type | Type of loyalty operation being requested (View Balance, View History, Earn Points, Redeem Points, Expire Points, Adjustment, Tier Upgrade, Reward Redemption, etc.). | Enum | Yes | Must be one of the platform-supported loyalty operation types. |
| Order ID | Unique identifier of the associated customer order. | UUID | Conditional | Required for order-based point earning or redemption. Must reference an eligible order. |
| Payment Transaction ID | Identifier of the associated payment transaction. | UUID | Conditional | Required when loyalty earning depends on successful payment completion. Must reference a valid payment transaction. |
| Reward ID | Identifier of the selected reward from the Rewards Catalog. | UUID | Conditional | Required for reward redemption. Must reference an active and eligible reward. |
| Redemption Type | Type of reward redemption (Discount, Wallet Credit, Coupon, Voucher, Gift, Membership Benefit, etc.). | Enum | Conditional | Must be one of the supported redemption types. |
| Loyalty Points | Number of loyalty points involved in the operation. | Integer | Conditional | Must be greater than zero and comply with configured earning or redemption limits. |
| Available Loyalty Points | Customer's available redeemable loyalty points before processing. | Integer | System Generated | Automatically calculated by the system. |
| Earned Points | Number of loyalty points awarded for the eligible activity. | Integer | Conditional | Calculated according to configured earning rules. |
| Redeemed Points | Number of loyalty points redeemed by the customer. | Integer | Conditional | Shall not exceed the available loyalty points. |
| Remaining Loyalty Points | Customer's remaining loyalty points after successful transaction completion. | Integer | System Generated | Automatically calculated after successful processing. |
| Loyalty Tier | Current loyalty tier associated with the customer account. | Enum | System Generated | Determined according to configured tier policies. |
| Tier Upgrade Eligibility | Indicates whether the customer qualifies for a loyalty tier upgrade. | Boolean | System Generated | Automatically evaluated using configured business rules. |
| Promotional Campaign ID | Identifier of the promotional campaign awarding bonus loyalty points. | UUID | Conditional | Must reference an active and eligible campaign. |
| Referral ID | Identifier of the referral activity generating loyalty rewards. | UUID | Conditional | Must reference a valid approved referral transaction. |
| Subscription ID | Identifier of the eligible membership or subscription plan. | UUID | Conditional | Required when loyalty benefits originate from subscription services. |
| Expiration Date | Expiration date applicable to earned loyalty points where supported. | DateTime | Conditional | Must comply with configured point expiration policies. |
| Adjustment Reason | Business justification for administrative loyalty point adjustments. | String (500) | Conditional | Mandatory for authorized administrative adjustments. |
| Transaction Remarks | Optional remarks associated with the loyalty transaction. | String (500) | No | Must not exceed the configured maximum length and shall not contain malicious content. |
| Customer Device ID | Identifier of the device initiating the loyalty request. | UUID/String | Conditional | Must reference an authorized or registered customer device where applicable. |
| Session ID | Authenticated customer session identifier. | UUID/String | Yes | Must represent an active authenticated session. |
| Request Timestamp | Timestamp indicating when the loyalty request was initiated. | DateTime | System Generated | Automatically generated using synchronized system time. |
| IP Address | IP address from which the loyalty request originated. | IPv4 / IPv6 | System Generated | Automatically captured for security monitoring and auditing. |
| User Agent | Browser, mobile application, or device information associated with the request. | String | System Generated | Automatically captured for audit and security purposes. |
| Authorization Token | Authentication token used to authorize the loyalty operation. | JWT / Access Token | Yes | Must be valid, active, and unexpired. |
| Correlation ID | Unique identifier used for distributed request tracing across platform services. | UUID | System Generated | Automatically generated for observability, diagnostics, and audit purposes. |

#### 3.1.25.6 Business Rules

1. Every eligible customer shall have only one active Loyalty Account associated with their customer account.
2. Loyalty account ownership shall be permanently associated with the customer account and shall not be transferred to another customer.
3. Only authenticated and authorized customers shall be permitted to access their own loyalty account.
4. Customers shall never be permitted to access, modify, redeem, or manage another customer's loyalty points.
5. Every loyalty transaction shall have a globally unique transaction identifier.
6. Every loyalty transaction shall be permanently recorded as an immutable business record after successful completion.
7. Available loyalty points shall always be calculated using successfully committed loyalty transactions only.
8. Loyalty point balances shall never become negative.
9. Loyalty points shall be earned only through authorized platform activities.
10. Eligible earning activities may include successful order completion, promotional campaigns, referral rewards, milestone achievements, subscription benefits, seasonal events, customer engagement programs, partner programs, and other platform-supported reward mechanisms.
11. Loyalty point earning rules shall be fully configurable by authorized administrators.
12. Loyalty points shall be awarded only after successful validation of all applicable eligibility conditions.
13. Cancelled, refunded, fraudulent, failed, or ineligible transactions shall not earn loyalty points unless explicitly permitted by configurable business policies.
14. Loyalty points may remain in a pending state until the associated business event becomes eligible for final confirmation.
15. Pending loyalty points shall not be redeemable.
16. Customers shall redeem only available and unexpired loyalty points.
17. Redemption shall be permitted only for platform-supported redemption options.
18. Supported redemption options may include order discounts, wallet credits, coupons, vouchers, promotional rewards, premium services, membership benefits, gift rewards, exclusive offers, and other configurable redemption mechanisms.
19. Redemption eligibility shall be validated before successful redemption.
20. Redemption rules including minimum redeemable points, maximum redemption limits, redemption ratios, and reward eligibility shall be configurable.
21. Loyalty points shall expire according to configurable expiration policies where applicable.
22. Expired loyalty points shall automatically become unavailable for redemption.
23. Expired loyalty points shall remain visible within historical transaction records.
24. Loyalty point expiration shall not modify historical loyalty transactions.
25. Duplicate loyalty point earning for the same business event shall never be permitted.
26. Duplicate loyalty point redemption transactions shall never be permitted.
27. Loyalty transactions shall always be processed as atomic business transactions.
28. Partial loyalty transaction commits shall never occur.
29. Failed loyalty operations shall automatically roll back all associated updates.
30. Concurrent loyalty transactions for the same loyalty account shall be managed using appropriate concurrency control mechanisms.
31. Loyalty balances shall remain consistent across all integrated platform services.
32. Loyalty tier calculations shall follow configurable tier qualification rules.
33. Tier upgrades and downgrades shall occur only according to approved business policies.
34. Administrative loyalty point adjustments shall be permitted only for authorized personnel possessing appropriate privileges.
35. Administrative adjustments shall require mandatory business justification.
36. Administrative loyalty adjustments shall always be recorded within audit logs.
37. Historical loyalty records shall remain permanently available according to organizational retention policies.
38. Historical loyalty transactions shall never be modified after successful completion except through authorized correction procedures.
39. Personally Identifiable Information (PII), loyalty balances, transaction history, redemption records, and customer engagement information shall be protected according to organizational security standards.
40. Loyalty operations shall be performed only through secure authenticated sessions.
41. Fraud detection rules shall evaluate eligible loyalty earning and redemption requests according to configurable risk assessment policies.
42. Suspicious loyalty activities may be delayed, restricted, suspended, or blocked pending fraud investigation.
43. Loyalty information shall remain synchronized with Customer Profile, Customer Wallet, Order Management, Payment, Promotional Campaign, Referral, Rewards Catalog, Notification, Analytics, and Audit services.
44. Every successful loyalty operation shall generate corresponding audit records.
45. Every loyalty transaction shall be traceable to its originating business event.
46. Notification Services shall generate loyalty-related notifications according to customer notification preferences and applicable business policies.
47. Loyalty balances and transaction history shall be updated immediately after successful transaction completion.
48. Loyalty operations shall comply with applicable consumer protection laws, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, accessibility standards, taxation requirements where applicable, organizational governance policies, audit requirements, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.
49. Loyalty analytics and reporting shall be generated using successfully committed loyalty transactions only.
50. The Loyalty Points module shall support future capabilities including multiple loyalty programs, tier-based memberships, coalition loyalty ecosystems, partner rewards, gamification, AI-driven recommendations, personalized rewards, regional loyalty programs, configurable earning strategies, and additional customer engagement features without requiring significant architectural redesign.

#### 3.1.25.7 Validations

1. The customer shall be successfully authenticated before accessing the Loyalty Points functionality.
2. The authenticated customer shall be authorized to access only their own loyalty account.
3. The customer account shall exist within the platform.
4. The customer account shall be active and not permanently deleted.
5. The loyalty account shall exist for the authenticated customer.
6. The loyalty account shall be active and eligible for loyalty operations.
7. The submitted Loyalty Account ID shall belong to the authenticated customer.
8. The submitted Loyalty Transaction ID, when provided, shall reference an existing loyalty transaction.
9. The Loyalty Operation Type shall be one of the supported operation types.
10. The submitted Order ID, when provided, shall reference an existing eligible order.
11. The associated order shall belong to the authenticated customer whenever applicable.
12. The associated order shall satisfy all configured loyalty point earning eligibility rules.
13. The submitted Payment Transaction ID, when provided, shall reference a valid payment transaction.
14. The associated payment transaction shall be successfully completed before awarding loyalty points where applicable.
15. The submitted Reward ID, when provided, shall reference an active and eligible reward.
16. The selected reward shall be available for redemption.
17. The customer shall satisfy all reward redemption eligibility criteria.
18. The submitted Redemption Type shall be one of the supported redemption types.
19. The submitted Promotional Campaign ID, when provided, shall reference an active and eligible campaign.
20. The submitted Referral ID, when provided, shall reference a valid approved referral transaction.
21. The submitted Subscription ID, when provided, shall reference an active and eligible membership or subscription plan.
22. Loyalty points involved in earning or redemption shall be greater than zero.
23. Loyalty point values shall comply with configured earning and redemption limits.
24. The customer shall possess sufficient available loyalty points before redemption.
25. Pending loyalty points shall not be eligible for redemption.
26. Expired loyalty points shall not be eligible for redemption.
27. Redemption shall satisfy configured minimum redeemable point requirements.
28. Redemption shall comply with configured maximum redemption limits where applicable.
29. Reward redemption ratios shall comply with configured business policies.
30. Duplicate loyalty point earning for the same business event shall be rejected.
31. Duplicate loyalty point redemption transactions shall be rejected.
32. Duplicate promotional loyalty rewards shall be rejected.
33. Duplicate referral loyalty rewards shall be rejected.
34. Duplicate order-based loyalty rewards shall be rejected.
35. Loyalty tier calculations shall follow configured qualification rules.
36. Administrative loyalty point adjustments shall be permitted only for authorized administrative users.
37. Administrative adjustments shall include mandatory business justification.
38. Every loyalty transaction shall receive a unique transaction identifier.
39. Transaction timestamps shall be generated using synchronized system time.
40. The customer session shall remain active throughout request processing.
41. The access token shall remain valid during request processing.
42. Requests originating from blocked, suspended, compromised, or unauthorized devices may be rejected according to security policies.
43. Fraud detection validation shall be executed for eligible loyalty earning and redemption requests.
44. High-risk loyalty transactions may require additional verification before completion.
45. Loyalty operations shall not violate configured business rules, reward policies, campaign rules, expiration policies, or tier qualification rules.
46. All mandatory request fields shall be present before processing.
47. Optional fields, when provided, shall satisfy their respective validation rules.
48. Input values shall conform to their defined data types, formats, length restrictions, and precision requirements.
49. Malformed, unsupported, tampered, or inconsistent requests shall be rejected.
50. SQL Injection, Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF), command injection, parameter manipulation, replay attacks, and other malicious inputs shall be detected and rejected.
51. Personally Identifiable Information (PII), loyalty balances, transaction history, redemption records, and customer engagement information shall never be exposed to unauthorized users.
52. Every successful and failed validation event shall be recorded within the audit logging system where applicable.
53. Loyalty operations shall comply with applicable consumer protection laws, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, accessibility standards, taxation requirements where applicable, organizational governance policies, audit requirements, and applicable legal and regulatory obligations before successful execution.

#### 3.1.25.8 Main Flow

1. The customer successfully authenticates into the Food Delivery Platform.
2. The customer navigates to **Account → Loyalty Points**.
3. The system verifies the customer's authenticated session.
4. The system validates that the customer is authorized to access the requested loyalty account.
5. The system retrieves the customer's loyalty account.
6. The system retrieves the latest available loyalty point balance.
7. The system retrieves loyalty summary information, including available points, pending points, redeemed points, expired points, loyalty tier, upcoming rewards, and other supported loyalty information.
8. The system retrieves the customer's loyalty transaction history.
9. The system displays the Loyalty Dashboard containing the customer's current loyalty balance, tier information, recent transactions, available rewards, and redemption options.
10. The customer reviews the loyalty information.
11. If the customer selects a loyalty transaction, the system retrieves the complete transaction details.
12. The system displays detailed loyalty transaction information, including transaction type, earned or redeemed points, source, timestamp, reference identifiers, expiration details, and applicable remarks.
13. If the customer completes an eligible order, the Order Management Service notifies the Loyalty Points Service after successful order completion.
14. The Loyalty Points Service validates the order's eligibility for loyalty point earning.
15. The system verifies successful payment completion where required.
16. The system evaluates all applicable loyalty earning rules.
17. The system calculates the loyalty points to be awarded.
18. The system generates a unique loyalty transaction identifier.
19. The system creates a loyalty point earning transaction.
20. The system updates the customer's available loyalty point balance.
21. The system recalculates the customer's loyalty tier where applicable.
22. The system records the earning transaction as an immutable loyalty record.
23. The Audit Logging Service records the completed loyalty earning operation.
24. The Notification Service informs the customer about the newly earned loyalty points.
25. If the customer initiates loyalty point redemption, the system retrieves the selected reward information.
26. The system validates reward eligibility.
27. The system validates that sufficient redeemable loyalty points are available.
28. The system validates redemption limits, redemption ratios, and other applicable business rules.
29. The customer confirms the redemption request.
30. The system deducts the redeemed loyalty points.
31. The system creates a loyalty redemption transaction.
32. The system updates the customer's available loyalty point balance.
33. If the selected redemption type is Wallet Credit, the Customer Wallet Service credits the corresponding wallet amount.
34. If the selected redemption type is Order Discount, the discount is applied to the eligible order.
35. If the selected redemption type is Coupon, Voucher, Membership Benefit, Premium Service, or another supported reward, the corresponding platform service activates the approved benefit.
36. The system updates the customer's loyalty tier if tier qualification changes.
37. The loyalty redemption transaction is committed as an atomic business transaction.
38. The Audit Logging Service records the completed redemption operation.
39. The Notification Service sends redemption confirmation to the customer.
40. If scheduled expiration processing identifies expired loyalty points, the system validates expiration eligibility.
41. The system marks eligible points as expired.
42. The available loyalty point balance is recalculated.
43. Historical loyalty transactions remain unchanged.
44. The expiration transaction is permanently recorded.
45. The Notification Service informs the customer about point expiration where configured.
46. The Analytics Service updates loyalty analytics and customer engagement metrics.
47. The Fraud Detection Service evaluates eligible loyalty operations according to configured security policies.
48. All dependent platform services synchronize the latest committed loyalty information where required.
49. The system records all successful loyalty operations within immutable audit logs.
50. The system displays the updated loyalty balance, latest loyalty transactions, current loyalty tier, and successful operation confirmation to the customer.

#### 3.1.25.9 Alternate Flows

##### Alternate Flow 1: Customer Has No Loyalty Points

1. The customer accesses the Loyalty Points module.
2. The system retrieves the customer's loyalty account.
3. The system determines that the available loyalty point balance is zero.
4. The system displays the loyalty dashboard with zero available points.
5. The system informs the customer that no redeemable loyalty points are currently available.
6. The customer may review loyalty program information or exit the module.
7. The use case ends successfully.

---

##### Alternate Flow 2: Loyalty Account Does Not Exist
