31. Promotional Campaign Service shall maintain consistent campaign reward information.
32. Customer Profile Service shall remain synchronized with referral relationships.
33. Order Management Service shall maintain referral eligibility references where applicable.
34. Payment Service shall maintain successful payment references associated with referral eligibility where applicable.
35. Analytics Service shall update referral conversion metrics, campaign performance statistics, customer acquisition metrics, and referral performance indicators.
36. Audit Logging Service shall permanently record every successful referral operation.
37. Failed referral operations shall generate corresponding audit records where applicable.
38. Every referral lifecycle event shall remain historically traceable.
39. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall remain protected throughout referral processing.
40. Confidential system information shall never be exposed as part of referral processing.
41. Database consistency shall be maintained across all integrated services.
42. Distributed transaction consistency shall be preserved where applicable.
43. Failed referral transactions shall not partially update referral records or reward balances.
44. Temporary processing resources shall be released after completion.
45. System logs shall contain sufficient operational information for monitoring and troubleshooting.
46. Security monitoring systems shall record referral-related security events where applicable.
47. All referral operations shall comply with applicable consumer protection laws, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, accessibility standards, taxation requirements where applicable, organizational governance policies, audit requirements, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.
48. The Referral Program module shall remain available for subsequent customer referral operations.

#### 3.1.26.11 Success Response

Upon successful completion of a Referral Program operation, the system shall return a standardized success response containing only authorized, non-sensitive information.

**Response Structure**

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Timestamp | Date and time when the response was generated. |
| HTTP Status Code | Standard HTTP success status code (200 OK, 201 Created, or other applicable success code). |
| Status | Indicates successful execution of the referral operation. |
| Message | Human-readable success message. |
| Referral Request ID | Unique identifier of the referral request. |
| Referral ID | Unique identifier of the completed referral record. |
| Referral Profile ID | Unique identifier of the referrer's referral profile. |
| Referrer Customer ID | Unique identifier of the customer who initiated the referral. |
| Referred Customer ID | Unique identifier of the referred customer, when applicable. |
| Referral Code | Referral code associated with the successful referral. |
| Referral Link | Platform-generated referral link, when applicable. |
| Referral Campaign ID | Identifier of the associated referral campaign, when applicable. |
| Referral Status | Current status of the referral lifecycle. |
| Referral Reward Status | Indicates whether referral rewards have been issued, are pending, or are not applicable. |
| Wallet Reward Amount | Wallet reward credited to the eligible customer, when applicable. |
| Loyalty Reward Points | Loyalty points awarded through the referral, when applicable. |
| Coupon Reward ID | Identifier of the issued coupon or voucher, when applicable. |
| Cashback Amount | Cashback awarded through the referral, when applicable. |
| Discount Reward | Discount reward issued through the referral, when applicable. |
| Free Delivery Benefit | Indicates whether a free delivery benefit has been granted. |
| Membership Benefit | Membership or subscription benefit awarded, when applicable. |
| Qualifying Order ID | Identifier of the qualifying order associated with the referral reward, when applicable. |
| Payment Transaction ID | Identifier of the qualifying payment transaction, when applicable. |
| Reward Issuance Timestamp | Timestamp when referral rewards were successfully issued. |
| Referral Completion Timestamp | Timestamp when the referral lifecycle reached successful completion. |
| Referral Statistics | Updated referral statistics including successful referrals, pending referrals, rejected referrals, and total earned rewards. |
| Notification Status | Indicates whether referral notifications have been successfully initiated. |
| Audit Reference ID | Identifier associated with the audit log entry. |
| Correlation ID | Unique request correlation identifier for distributed tracing. |

**Example Success Messages**

- Referral profile retrieved successfully.
- Referral code generated successfully.
- Referral invitation shared successfully.
- Referral invitation delivered successfully.
- Referral registration completed successfully.
- Referral validated successfully.
- Referral eligibility confirmed successfully.
- Referral reward issued successfully.
- Wallet reward credited successfully.
- Loyalty points awarded successfully.
- Referral coupon issued successfully.
- Referral campaign applied successfully.
- Referral statistics updated successfully.
- Referral information retrieved successfully.

**Response Characteristics**

1. The response shall contain only information that the authenticated customer is authorized to access.
2. The response shall accurately reflect the latest committed referral status.
3. Referral reward information shall accurately represent all successfully issued incentives.
4. Referral identifiers shall uniquely identify completed referral records.
5. Historical referral records shall remain unchanged following successful operations.
6. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be included only when required and authorized.
7. Passwords, authentication credentials, access tokens, refresh tokens, encryption keys, fraud detection rules, internal configuration values, internal analytics, database identifiers, server information, and other confidential system information shall never be included in the success response.
8. The response shall remain consistent across all supported client applications and APIs.
9. The response shall comply with applicable consumer protection laws, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, accessibility standards, taxation requirements where applicable, audit policies, organizational governance requirements, and applicable legal and regulatory obligations.
10. The response shall be securely transmitted over HTTPS.

#### 3.1.26.12 Failure Response

When a Referral Program operation cannot be completed successfully, the system shall return a standardized failure response containing only authorized, non-sensitive error information.

**Response Structure**

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Timestamp | Date and time when the error response was generated. |
| HTTP Status Code | Appropriate HTTP error status code (400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 422, 429, 500, 503, or other applicable code). |
| Status | Indicates unsuccessful execution of the referral operation. |
| Error Code | Unique application-specific error code representing the failure condition. |
| Error Category | Classification of the error (Validation, Authentication, Authorization, Business Rule, Referral Eligibility, Campaign, Fraud Detection, Concurrency, System, Network, etc.). |
| Error Message | Human-readable description of the failure. |
| Referral Request ID | Referral request identifier when available. |
| Referral ID | Referral identifier when available. |
| Referral Profile ID | Referral profile identifier when available and authorized. |
| Referral Code | Referral code associated with the failed request when applicable. |
| Referral Campaign ID | Referral campaign identifier when applicable. |
| Referral Status | Referral status at the time of failure when available. |
| Field Name | Input field associated with the validation failure, when applicable. |
| Rejected Value | Invalid value submitted by the client, when appropriate and safe to expose. |
| Correlation ID | Unique request identifier for distributed tracing and diagnostics. |
| Request ID | Unique identifier associated with the client request. |
| Retry Allowed | Indicates whether the failed operation may be safely retried. |
| Suggested Action | Recommended action for resolving the error when appropriate. |
| Support Reference ID | Reference identifier for customer support and operational investigation. |

**Example Failure Messages**

- Authentication required.
- Customer session has expired.
- Access to the requested referral information is denied.
- Customer account does not exist.
- Customer account is inactive.
- Referral profile not found.
- Referral Program is currently unavailable.
- Invalid referral code.
- Referral code has expired.
- Referral link is invalid.
- Referral link has expired.
- Referral campaign not found.
- Referral campaign is inactive.
- Referral campaign has expired.
- Referral eligibility validation failed.
- Referral reward is not eligible.
- Referral reward has already been issued.
- Duplicate referral detected.
- Duplicate reward detected.
- Self-referral is not permitted.
- Circular referral detected.
- Referral invitation limit exceeded.
- Referral reward limit exceeded.
- Qualifying order not found.
- Minimum order value requirement not satisfied.
- Payment verification failed.
- Referral is under fraud review.
- Suspicious referral activity detected.
- Device validation failed.
- Geographic eligibility validation failed.
- Mandatory request fields are missing.
- Invalid request format.
- Referral service is temporarily unavailable.
- Internal server error occurred while processing the referral request.

**Response Characteristics**

1. Failure responses shall use standardized error structures across all platform APIs.
2. Error messages shall clearly describe the failure without exposing confidential implementation details.
3. Validation failures shall identify the relevant input field whenever applicable.
4. Authentication failures shall never disclose whether a customer account exists.
5. Authorization failures shall never reveal information about referral records owned by other customers.
6. Business rule failures shall clearly indicate that the request violates configured referral policies without exposing internal rule implementation.
7. Fraud detection failures shall not disclose internal fraud detection logic, scoring models, security thresholds, investigation criteria, or risk assessment algorithms.
8. Database schema information, internal service architecture, SQL statements, stack traces, source code, encryption keys, authentication credentials, access tokens, refresh tokens, internal configuration values, analytics algorithms, server configuration, infrastructure details, and other confidential system information shall never be included in failure responses.
9. Every failed referral operation shall generate corresponding audit log entries.
10. Failure responses shall comply with applicable consumer protection laws, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, accessibility standards, taxation requirements where applicable, audit policies, organizational governance requirements, and applicable legal and regulatory obligations.
11. Failure responses shall be transmitted only through secure HTTPS connections.
12. Failed referral operations shall not modify referral relationships, referral rewards, customer wallet balances, loyalty point balances, campaign statistics, analytics data, audit integrity, or dependent platform services.

#### 3.1.26.13 Non-Functional Considerations

1. **Performance**
   - The Referral Program module shall retrieve referral information within the platform-defined response time under normal operating conditions.
   - Referral code validation shall complete efficiently regardless of the total number of registered customers.
   - Referral reward calculation shall be optimized for high-volume referral campaigns.
   - Referral invitation generation and sharing shall not introduce noticeable delays to customer workflows.
   - Database queries associated with referral processing shall be optimized using appropriate indexing, caching, and query optimization techniques.

2. **Availability**
   - The Referral Program module shall satisfy the platform's defined service availability objectives.
   - Planned maintenance activities shall minimize disruption to referral services.
   - High availability mechanisms shall ensure uninterrupted referral processing whenever possible.
   - Service interruptions shall not compromise referral records or reward consistency.

3. **Scalability**
   - The platform shall support millions of referral profiles and referral transactions.
   - The Referral Program module shall scale horizontally to accommodate increasing customer acquisition campaigns.
   - Referral services shall support peak promotional events without significant degradation in performance.
   - The storage architecture shall support long-term retention of referral history, campaign records, and referral analytics.

4. **Reliability**
   - Referral processing shall maintain consistent referral relationships under all operating conditions.
   - Successfully completed referral transactions shall be durable.
   - Duplicate referral rewards shall never be generated due to retries or concurrent requests.
   - Failed referral operations shall automatically roll back incomplete updates.
   - Recovery mechanisms shall preserve referral integrity following unexpected failures.

5. **Security**
   - All referral operations shall require authenticated customer sessions.
   - Authorization mechanisms shall ensure customers access only their own referral information.
   - Referral codes, referral rewards, referral history, and customer information shall be protected using encryption during transmission and storage where applicable.
   - Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall never be exposed to unauthorized users.
   - Secure coding practices shall protect against SQL Injection, Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF), command injection, insecure direct object references, parameter tampering, replay attacks, session hijacking, and other common security threats.
   - Fraud detection mechanisms shall continuously monitor referral activities for abuse.
   - Administrative referral operations shall require elevated authorization and complete audit trails.

6. **Data Integrity**
   - Every referral transaction shall satisfy ACID transaction properties where applicable.
   - Referral relationships shall remain consistent throughout the referral lifecycle.
   - Duplicate referral registrations and duplicate reward issuance shall be prevented.
   - Referential integrity shall be maintained across Customer Profile, Authentication, Customer Wallet, Loyalty Points, Order Management, Payments, Promotional Campaigns, Notifications, Analytics, Fraud Detection, and Audit modules.
   - Distributed transactions shall maintain data consistency across integrated platform services.

7. **Auditability**
   - Every successful and failed referral operation shall be recorded within immutable audit logs.
   - Audit records shall include timestamps, initiating actor, operation type, referral identifiers, affected customer accounts, and execution outcome.
   - Audit records shall remain available according to organizational retention policies.
   - Audit information shall support compliance reporting, customer support, dispute resolution, fraud investigation, operational monitoring, and business analytics.

8. **Usability**
   - Referral information shall be presented using clear, consistent, and user-friendly terminology.
   - Customers shall easily access their referral code, referral link, referral history, and earned rewards.
   - Referral progress shall be clearly displayed throughout the referral lifecycle.
   - User interfaces shall remain consistent across supported web and mobile platforms.

9. **Accessibility**
   - The Referral Program module shall comply with applicable accessibility standards.
   - Referral interfaces shall support keyboard navigation where applicable.
   - Screen readers shall correctly interpret referral information, referral statistics, and reward details.
   - Color shall not be the sole method of conveying referral status or reward eligibility.
   - Appropriate accessibility labels shall be provided for all referral-related controls.

10. **Maintainability**
    - Referral campaigns, reward strategies, fraud detection rules, referral limits, eligibility policies, and campaign configurations shall be configurable without requiring significant source code modifications.
    - Source code shall follow organizational coding standards and architecture guidelines.
    - Referral processing logic shall be modular, reusable, independently testable, and easily maintainable.
    - Operational monitoring shall simplify troubleshooting and maintenance activities.

11. **Interoperability**
    - The Referral Program module shall integrate seamlessly with Customer Profile, Authentication, Customer Wallet, Loyalty Points, Order Management, Payments, Promotional Campaigns, Notifications, Analytics, Fraud Detection, Customer Dashboard, and Audit services.
    - APIs shall comply with organizational API standards.
    - Data exchange between integrated services shall remain secure, reliable, and consistent.
    - Distributed transaction consistency shall be maintained wherever applicable.

12. **Monitoring & Observability**
    - Referral operations shall generate operational metrics for monitoring.
    - Failed referral processing shall trigger operational alerts.
    - Referral campaign performance shall be continuously monitored.
    - Distributed tracing shall support end-to-end diagnostics across referral workflows.
    - Centralized logging shall support monitoring, troubleshooting, and operational analysis.

13. **Compliance**
    - The Referral Program module shall comply with applicable consumer protection laws, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, accessibility standards, taxation requirements where applicable, audit policies, organizational governance requirements, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.
    - Referral records shall be retained according to organizational retention and regulatory requirements.
    - Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be processed according to organizational privacy policies and applicable data protection regulations.

14. **Extensibility**
    - The architecture shall support future enhancements including multi-level referral programs, affiliate marketing integrations, influencer referral campaigns, partner referral ecosystems, AI-driven referral recommendations, configurable campaign strategies, regional referral programs, enterprise referral models, gamification features, and additional customer acquisition capabilities without requiring significant architectural redesign.

#### 3.1.26.14 Acceptance Criteria

| AC ID | Acceptance Criteria |
|-------|----------------------|
| AC-RP-001 | The system shall allow only authenticated customers to access the Referral Program functionality. |
| AC-RP-002 | The system shall authorize customers to access only their own referral information and referral activities. |
| AC-RP-003 | Every eligible customer shall have a unique referral profile. |
| AC-RP-004 | Every eligible customer shall receive a unique referral code generated by the system. |
| AC-RP-005 | Every referral code shall uniquely identify a single referrer. |
| AC-RP-006 | The system shall generate secure referral links associated with the corresponding referral profile. |
| AC-RP-007 | Customers shall be able to share referral invitations only through supported communication channels. |
| AC-RP-008 | The system shall validate every referral code before accepting a referral request. |
| AC-RP-009 | Expired or invalid referral codes shall not be accepted. |
| AC-RP-010 | Expired or invalid referral links shall not be accepted. |
| AC-RP-011 | The system shall allow only eligible new customers to participate in referral campaigns according to configured business rules. |
| AC-RP-012 | The referred customer shall satisfy all configured referral eligibility requirements before referral rewards are issued. |
| AC-RP-013 | Referral rewards shall be issued only after successful completion of all configured referral milestones. |
| AC-RP-014 | Referral rewards shall accurately reflect configured campaign rules. |
| AC-RP-015 | Wallet rewards shall be credited only after successful referral validation where applicable. |
| AC-RP-016 | Loyalty points shall be awarded only after successful referral validation where applicable. |
| AC-RP-017 | Coupons, vouchers, discounts, cashback, memberships, free delivery benefits, and other incentives shall be issued only after successful eligibility verification where applicable. |
| AC-RP-018 | Duplicate referral registrations shall not generate duplicate referral rewards. |
| AC-RP-019 | Duplicate referral rewards shall never be generated. |
| AC-RP-020 | Self-referrals shall be rejected unless explicitly permitted by configurable business policies. |
| AC-RP-021 | Circular referral relationships shall not be permitted. |
| AC-RP-022 | Fraudulent, fake, automated, or bot-generated referrals shall be detected and rejected according to configured fraud prevention policies. |
| AC-RP-023 | Referral invitation limits and reward limits shall comply with configured organizational policies. |
| AC-RP-024 | Referral campaign eligibility shall comply with configured campaign rules. |
| AC-RP-025 | Referral campaigns shall honor configured activation dates, expiration dates, budgets, and reward limits. |
| AC-RP-026 | Every referral transaction shall be processed as an atomic business transaction. |
| AC-RP-027 | Partial referral updates shall never be committed. |
| AC-RP-028 | Failed referral transactions shall automatically roll back all associated updates. |
| AC-RP-029 | Concurrent referral processing shall maintain transaction consistency using appropriate concurrency control mechanisms. |
| AC-RP-030 | Referral information shall remain synchronized across Customer Profile, Customer Wallet, Loyalty Points, Order Management, Payment, Promotional Campaign, Notification, Analytics, Fraud Detection, Customer Dashboard, and other authorized platform services. |
| AC-RP-031 | Every successful referral operation shall generate immutable audit records. |
| AC-RP-032 | Every failed referral operation shall generate corresponding audit records where applicable. |
| AC-RP-033 | Administrative referral adjustments shall be permitted only for authorized personnel possessing appropriate privileges. |
| AC-RP-034 | Administrative referral adjustments shall require mandatory business justification. |
| AC-RP-035 | Personally Identifiable Information (PII), referral history, referral rewards, customer information, and referral analytics shall be accessible only to authorized users. |
| AC-RP-036 | Passwords, authentication credentials, access tokens, refresh tokens, encryption keys, fraud detection rules, analytics algorithms, internal configuration values, database information, server details, and other confidential system information shall never be exposed in responses. |
| AC-RP-037 | All referral-related communications shall occur exclusively over secure HTTPS connections. |
| AC-RP-038 | Referral records shall be retained according to applicable organizational retention policies and regulatory requirements. |
| AC-RP-039 | The Referral Program module shall maintain the platform's defined service availability objectives. |
| AC-RP-040 | The module shall support concurrent referral operations without compromising performance, reliability, availability, scalability, consistency, or data integrity. |
| AC-RP-041 | The architecture shall support future enhancements including multi-level referral programs, affiliate marketing integrations, influencer referral campaigns, partner referral ecosystems, AI-driven referral recommendations, configurable campaign strategies, regional referral programs, enterprise referral models, gamification capabilities, and additional customer acquisition features without requiring significant architectural redesign. |
| AC-RP-042 | The Referral Program module shall comply with applicable consumer protection laws, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, accessibility standards, taxation requirements where applicable, organizational governance policies, audit requirements, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations. |
| AC-RP-043 | The Referral Program module shall successfully pass functional, integration, security, performance, regression, accessibility, compliance, disaster recovery, and user acceptance testing before production deployment. |

### 3.1.27 Saved Payment Methods

#### 3.1.27.1 Description

The **Saved Payment Methods** module enables authenticated customers to securely save, manage, update, and remove payment methods for faster and more convenient checkout experiences. The module supports multiple payment instruments including credit cards, debit cards, UPI accounts, bank accounts (where supported), digital wallets, Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) options, and other platform-supported payment methods. Sensitive payment information shall never be stored directly by the Food Delivery Platform unless explicitly permitted under applicable security standards and regulatory requirements.

The platform shall integrate with authorized Payment Gateway Providers and Payment Tokenization Services to securely store payment credentials using tokenization. Instead of storing actual card numbers or other sensitive payment information, the system shall store payment tokens, masked payment details, issuer information, payment network information, expiration details, payment method status, and other non-sensitive metadata required for payment processing.

Customers shall be able to add new payment methods, view saved payment methods, update payment preferences, set a default payment method, remove existing payment methods, and manage supported payment instruments through the Customer Dashboard. Customers shall only be able to access and manage payment methods associated with their own customer account.

When adding a payment method, the system shall validate payment details through the integrated Payment Gateway Provider. Where applicable, card verification, OTP verification, 3-D Secure (3DS) authentication, token generation, UPI verification, bank verification, wallet verification, or other supported verification mechanisms shall be completed before the payment method becomes available for future transactions.

The platform shall maintain payment method metadata including payment type, payment network, issuing bank, card brand, masked account number, expiration date, payment token reference, default payment status, verification status, creation timestamp, last updated timestamp, last usage timestamp, payment method lifecycle status, and other platform-supported attributes. Sensitive authentication data including CVV, PIN, passwords, OTP values, authentication credentials, cryptographic keys, full Primary Account Numbers (PAN), and other regulated payment information shall never be stored after authorization.

Customers may designate one saved payment method as the default payment method. The default payment method shall automatically appear as the preferred payment option during checkout unless the customer explicitly selects another supported payment method.

Customers may remove previously saved payment methods at any time unless the payment method is currently associated with an active subscription, recurring payment authorization, unresolved payment dispute, pending payment transaction, or other configurable business dependency. When a payment method is removed, the associated payment token shall be invalidated or deleted according to payment provider requirements and applicable regulatory standards.

The Saved Payment Methods module shall integrate with Customer Profile Management, Authentication Services, Authorization Services, Payment Gateway Services, Payment Tokenization Services, Checkout Services, Order Management, Subscription Management, Customer Wallet, Fraud Detection Services, Notification Services, Audit Logging Services, Analytics Services, Customer Dashboard, and other authorized platform services to ensure secure payment processing and consistent customer experience.

All payment method operations shall be protected using authentication, authorization, encryption, tokenization, fraud detection, secure communication protocols, audit logging, rate limiting, and organizational security controls. Every successful and failed payment method operation shall be recorded within the platform's audit logging framework to support compliance reporting, operational monitoring, customer support, dispute resolution, forensic investigation, and regulatory audits.

Personally Identifiable Information (PII), payment metadata, payment tokens, customer preferences, payment history references, and other sensitive customer information shall remain protected throughout the payment method lifecycle using industry-standard encryption, tokenization, secure APIs, access controls, and applicable privacy regulations. The module shall comply with PCI DSS, applicable payment industry standards, cybersecurity requirements, consumer protection regulations, organizational security policies, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

The Saved Payment Methods module shall be designed to support future enhancements including network tokenization, card lifecycle management, digital identity verification, biometric payment authentication, cross-platform payment synchronization, multiple default payment profiles, AI-assisted payment recommendations, regional payment providers, enterprise payment accounts, and additional payment technologies without requiring significant architectural redesign.

#### 3.1.27.2 Actors

| Actor | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Customer | Authenticated customer who adds, views, updates, sets a default, or removes saved payment methods associated with their account. |
| Authentication Service | Authenticates the customer's identity before allowing payment method operations. |
| Authorization Service | Ensures customers can access and manage only their own saved payment methods. |
| Customer Profile Service | Retrieves and maintains customer profile information associated with saved payment methods. |
| Saved Payment Methods Service | Manages payment method creation, retrieval, update, deletion, default payment selection, and payment method lifecycle management. |
| Payment Gateway Provider | Validates payment instruments, performs payment verification, generates payment tokens where applicable, and supports secure payment processing. |
| Payment Tokenization Service | Generates, stores, manages, refreshes, and invalidates secure payment tokens according to payment industry standards. |
| Payment Verification Service | Performs card verification, UPI verification, bank verification, wallet verification, 3-D Secure (3DS) authentication, OTP verification, and other supported payment validations. |
| Checkout Service | Retrieves eligible saved payment methods during the checkout process. |
| Order Management Service | Uses saved payment methods for authorized order payment processing where applicable. |
| Subscription Management Service | Verifies whether a payment method is associated with active subscriptions or recurring payment authorizations. |
| Customer Wallet Service | Integrates wallet-based payment methods where supported. |
| Fraud Detection Service | Detects suspicious payment method activities, unauthorized payment attempts, duplicate payment methods, account takeover attempts, and other payment-related fraud. |
| Notification Service | Sends payment method addition, update, removal, verification, default payment change, and security notifications to customers. |
| Audit Logging Service | Records all successful and failed payment method operations for compliance, security, operational monitoring, and forensic investigation. |
| Analytics Service | Collects payment method usage statistics, customer payment preferences, payment adoption trends, and operational analytics. |
| Customer Dashboard | Displays saved payment methods and allows customers to manage supported payment instruments. |
| Customer Support Representative | Assists customers with payment method-related inquiries, verification issues, disputes, and troubleshooting according to authorized permissions. |
| System Administrator | Configures supported payment methods, payment provider integrations, tokenization settings, security policies, and payment-related system configurations. |
| Compliance Officer | Reviews payment method operations for compliance with PCI DSS, organizational security policies, audit requirements, and applicable regulatory obligations where authorized. |
| External Payment Networks | Process payment verification requests and support payment instrument validation where applicable. |
| Issuing Bank | Verifies customer payment instruments and participates in payment authentication processes where applicable. |
| Digital Wallet Provider | Supports validation and management of supported wallet-based payment methods where applicable. |

#### 3.1.27.3 Preconditions

1. The customer shall possess a successfully registered and active customer account.
2. The customer shall be authenticated before accessing the Saved Payment Methods functionality.
3. The customer session shall be valid and not expired.
4. The customer account shall not be suspended, blocked, locked, disabled, or permanently deleted.
5. The customer shall be authorized to access only their own saved payment methods.
6. The Saved Payment Methods feature shall be enabled by system configuration.
7. The Payment Gateway Provider shall be operational.
8. The Payment Tokenization Service shall be operational.
9. The Payment Verification Service shall be operational.
10. The Authentication Service shall be available.
11. The Authorization Service shall be operational.
12. The Customer Profile Service shall be operational.
13. The Saved Payment Methods Service shall be operational.
14. The Customer Dashboard shall be available.
15. The Fraud Detection Service shall be operational.
16. The Notification Service shall be operational.
17. The Audit Logging Service shall be operational.
18. The Analytics Service shall be operational.
19. The Checkout Service shall be available where saved payment methods are supported during checkout.
20. The Order Management Service shall be operational where payment methods are associated with order processing.
21. The Subscription Management Service shall be operational where recurring payments or subscriptions are supported.
22. The Customer Wallet Service shall be operational where wallet-based payment methods are supported.
23. Secure communication channels between the platform and external payment providers shall be available.
24. Tokenization capabilities shall be enabled for supported payment methods.
25. Supported payment method configurations shall exist within the platform.
26. Payment provider integrations shall be correctly configured.
27. Customer consent for securely storing payment method references shall be obtained where applicable.
28. Payment industry compliance requirements including PCI DSS controls shall be enforced before storing payment method references.
29. Full payment credentials including CVV, PIN, OTP values, passwords, and full Primary Account Numbers (PAN) shall never be stored by the platform after successful authorization.
30. Supported encryption mechanisms shall be enabled for payment-related communications and sensitive data.
31. Fraud detection policies shall be enabled before payment method registration.
32. Payment rate limiting policies shall be active where applicable.
33. System date and time shall be synchronized for accurate audit logging, payment lifecycle management, and payment token validity.
34. Required database connectivity shall be available.
35. All payment method operations shall be executed over secure HTTPS connections.
36. Organizational security policies, privacy policies, payment processing policies, tokenization standards, fraud prevention policies, audit policies, PCI DSS requirements, and all applicable legal and regulatory requirements shall be enforced before processing payment method requests.

#### 3.1.27.4 Trigger

The Saved Payment Methods process shall be initiated whenever one of the following events occurs:

1. An authenticated customer accesses the **Saved Payment Methods** section from the Customer Dashboard.
2. An authenticated customer requests to view all saved payment methods.
3. An authenticated customer requests to add a new payment method.
4. An authenticated customer submits payment details for secure verification.
5. The Payment Gateway Provider receives a request to verify the submitted payment method.
6. The Payment Verification Service initiates payment instrument validation.
7. The Payment Tokenization Service generates a secure payment token.
8. The Payment Gateway Provider successfully verifies the payment instrument.
9. The Payment Verification Service successfully completes OTP verification where applicable.
10. The Payment Verification Service successfully completes 3-D Secure (3DS) authentication where applicable.
11. The Payment Verification Service successfully verifies a UPI account where applicable.
12. The Payment Verification Service successfully verifies a digital wallet where applicable.
13. The Payment Verification Service successfully verifies a supported bank account where applicable.
14. The customer requests to designate a saved payment method as the default payment method.
15. The customer requests to update payment method preferences.
16. The customer requests to rename a saved payment method where supported.
17. The customer requests to remove a saved payment method.
18. The Subscription Management Service validates whether the payment method is associated with active subscriptions or recurring payment authorizations.
19. The Checkout Service retrieves saved payment methods during checkout.
20. The Order Management Service requests the customer's default payment method during payment processing.
21. The Customer Wallet Service requests eligible wallet-based payment methods where applicable.
22. The Fraud Detection Service initiates payment method fraud validation.
23. The Fraud Detection Service identifies suspicious payment method activity.
24. The Payment Tokenization Service refreshes or rotates payment tokens according to payment provider requirements.
25. The Payment Gateway Provider reports payment method expiration.
26. The system detects an expired payment instrument.
27. The system detects an invalid payment token.
28. The system detects a duplicate payment method registration attempt.
29. The system detects unauthorized access to saved payment methods.
30. The customer requests payment method verification status.
31. The customer requests payment method details.
32. The Notification Service sends payment method verification notifications.
33. The Notification Service sends payment method addition confirmations.
34. The Notification Service sends payment method removal confirmations.
35. The Notification Service sends default payment method change notifications.
36. An administrator performs an authorized payment configuration update.
37. A scheduled background process validates payment token integrity.
38. A scheduled background process synchronizes payment metadata with external payment providers.
39. A scheduled background process identifies expired payment methods.
40. A scheduled background process invalidates expired payment tokens where applicable.
41. An authorized customer support representative reviews a payment method-related customer request.
42. Any other platform-supported payment method event requiring verification, tokenization, fraud detection, audit logging, notification generation, synchronization, compliance verification, or administrative processing occurs.

#### 3.1.27.5 Input Fields

| Field | Description | Mandatory | Editable | Validation |
|-------|-------------|-----------|----------|------------|
| Payment Method ID | Unique identifier of the saved payment method. | No (System Generated) | No | Shall be unique across the platform. |
| Customer ID | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. | Yes | No | Must reference an active authenticated customer account. |
| Payment Method Type | Type of payment method (Credit Card, Debit Card, UPI, Wallet, Bank Account, BNPL, etc.). | Yes | Yes | Must be one of the supported payment method types. |
| Payment Provider ID | Identifier of the integrated payment gateway or payment provider. | Yes | No | Must reference a supported payment provider. |
| Payment Token | Secure token generated by the Payment Tokenization Service. | No (System Generated) | No | Must be unique, valid, and generated by the authorized tokenization service. |
| Token Reference ID | Identifier associated with the payment token. | No (System Generated) | No | Must reference a valid payment token. |
| Card Holder Name | Name printed on the payment card where applicable. | Conditional | Yes | Must satisfy configured length and character validation rules. |
| Card Number | Primary Account Number (PAN) submitted during payment verification. | Conditional | Yes | Must satisfy payment network validation rules. Full PAN shall never be stored after successful tokenization. |
| Masked Card Number | Masked representation of the stored payment card. | No (System Generated) | No | Shall display only the permitted masked digits. |
| Card Brand | Brand of the payment card (Visa, Mastercard, RuPay, American Express, etc.). | No | No | Determined during payment verification. |
| Card Network | Payment network associated with the card. | No | No | Determined by the Payment Gateway Provider. |
| Issuing Bank | Financial institution that issued the payment instrument. | No | No | Determined during payment verification where applicable. |
| Expiration Month | Card expiration month. | Conditional | Yes | Must be a valid calendar month. |
| Expiration Year | Card expiration year. | Conditional | Yes | Must not represent an expired payment instrument. |
| CVV | Card Verification Value used during payment verification. | Conditional | Yes | Used only for verification. Shall never be stored after authorization. |
| UPI ID | Unified Payments Interface identifier where applicable. | Conditional | Yes | Must satisfy UPI format validation rules. |
| Bank Account Number | Bank account number where supported. | Conditional | Yes | Used only during verification. Shall not be stored unless permitted by applicable standards. |
| IFSC Code | Bank IFSC code where applicable. | Conditional | Yes | Must satisfy IFSC format validation rules. |
| Wallet Provider | Digital wallet provider where applicable. | Conditional | Yes | Must reference a supported wallet provider. |
| BNPL Provider | Buy Now Pay Later provider where applicable. | Conditional | Yes | Must reference a supported BNPL provider. |
| Payment Nickname | Customer-defined name for the payment method. | No | Yes | Must satisfy configured length and character validation rules. |
| Default Payment Method | Indicates whether this payment method is the customer's default payment option. | Yes | Yes | Only one payment method may be designated as default at any given time. |
| Verification Status | Indicates whether the payment method has been successfully verified. | No | No | Maintained by the system. |
| Payment Method Status | Current lifecycle status of the payment method. | No | No | Maintained by the system. |
| Last Used Timestamp | Date and time when the payment method was last used. | No | No | Automatically updated by the system. |
| Created Timestamp | Date and time when the payment method was created. | No | No | Automatically generated by the system. |
| Updated Timestamp | Date and time when the payment method was last modified. | No | No | Automatically updated by the system. |
| Device Identifier | Identifier of the device initiating the payment method operation. | No | No | Captured automatically for security validation. |
| IP Address | IP address from which the payment method request originated. | No | No | Captured automatically for security monitoring. |
| Device Fingerprint | Device fingerprint associated with the payment method operation. | No | No | Used for fraud detection where applicable. |
| Authentication Result | Result of payment authentication or verification. | No | No | Maintained by the system. |
| Fraud Verification Status | Indicates fraud validation outcome. | No | No | Generated by the Fraud Detection Service. |
| Customer Consent Status | Indicates whether customer consent for storing payment references has been obtained. | Conditional | Yes | Must satisfy applicable privacy and payment regulations. |
| Terms and Conditions Acceptance | Indicates acceptance of payment provider terms where applicable. | Conditional | Yes | Must be accepted before payment method registration when required. |
| Audit Reference ID | Identifier of the audit record associated with the payment operation. | No | No | Generated automatically by the Audit Logging Service. |
| Correlation ID | Unique identifier for distributed request tracing. | No | No | Generated automatically by the platform. |

#### 3.1.27.6 Business Rules

1. Only authenticated customers shall be permitted to manage saved payment methods.
2. Customers shall be authorized to access and manage only the payment methods associated with their own customer account.
3. Every saved payment method shall have a unique system-generated identifier.
4. The platform shall support only payment methods approved by organizational configuration.
5. Payment methods shall be verified through authorized Payment Gateway Providers before being saved.
6. Payment credentials shall be tokenized using an approved Payment Tokenization Service before storage.
7. The platform shall never permanently store sensitive authentication data including CVV, PIN, OTP values, passwords, full Primary Account Numbers (PAN), cryptographic keys, or similar regulated payment information.
8. Only payment tokens and approved non-sensitive payment metadata shall be stored after successful verification.
9. Payment tokens shall remain unique and securely associated with a single customer account.
10. Invalid or expired payment tokens shall not be used for payment processing.
11. Customers may save multiple payment methods up to the configured platform limit.
12. The maximum number of saved payment methods per customer shall be configurable.
13. Only one payment method may be designated as the default payment method at any given time.
14. When a new payment method is designated as default, the previously designated default payment method shall automatically lose its default status.
15. Customers may update payment preferences only for supported editable attributes.
16. System-generated payment metadata shall not be editable by customers.
17. Payment method verification shall successfully complete before the payment method becomes available for checkout.
18. Payment verification may include OTP validation, 3-D Secure (3DS) authentication, UPI verification, bank verification, wallet verification, or other supported verification mechanisms.
19. Failed payment verification shall prevent payment method registration.
20. Duplicate payment methods shall not be registered unless explicitly permitted by organizational policy.
21. Expired payment methods shall not be available for future payment transactions until successfully updated or revalidated.
22. Customers may remove saved payment methods unless restricted by active subscriptions, recurring payment authorizations, pending payment transactions, unresolved payment disputes, chargebacks, or other configurable business dependencies.
23. Removal of a saved payment method shall invalidate or delete the associated payment token according to payment provider requirements.
24. Removal of a payment method shall not modify historical payment transaction records.
25. Payment method deletion shall not affect completed orders or successfully processed payments.
26. Payment method status shall accurately reflect the current lifecycle state.
27. Fraud detection mechanisms shall continuously evaluate payment method operations for suspicious activity.
28. Suspicious payment method activities may require additional verification before completion.
29. Administrative payment method operations shall require elevated authorization.
30. Administrative overrides shall require mandatory business justification.
31. Every successful payment method operation shall generate immutable audit records.
32. Every failed payment method operation shall generate corresponding audit records where applicable.
33. Payment method lifecycle events shall remain historically traceable.
34. Customers shall receive notifications for payment method addition, removal, verification, default payment changes, and other security-sensitive events.
35. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be processed only according to applicable privacy regulations and organizational security policies.
36. Payment-related metadata shall remain encrypted during transmission and protected during storage where applicable.
37. Secure HTTPS communication shall be mandatory for all payment method operations.
38. Payment method information shall remain synchronized across Customer Profile, Checkout, Payment Gateway, Order Management, Subscription Management, Customer Wallet, Fraud Detection, Notification, Analytics, and Audit services.
39. Payment provider synchronization shall maintain token validity and payment method consistency.
40. Payment tokens shall be refreshed, rotated, or invalidated according to payment provider requirements where applicable.
41. Payment rate limiting and abuse prevention mechanisms shall protect payment method operations.
42. All payment method operations shall comply with PCI DSS, applicable payment industry standards, consumer protection laws, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, organizational governance policies, audit requirements, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

#### 3.1.27.7 Validations

1. The customer shall be authenticated before performing any Saved Payment Methods operation.
2. The customer session shall be active and not expired.
3. The customer account shall exist.
4. The customer account shall be active.
5. The customer account shall not be suspended, blocked, locked, disabled, or permanently deleted.
6. The authenticated customer shall be authorized to access only their own saved payment methods.
7. The Saved Payment Methods feature shall be enabled.
8. The selected payment method type shall be supported by the platform.
9. The configured Payment Gateway Provider shall be available.
10. The Payment Tokenization Service shall be operational.
11. The Payment Verification Service shall be operational.
12. The payment provider configuration shall be valid and active.
13. Mandatory request fields shall be present.
14. Input values shall satisfy configured length, format, range, and character validation rules.
15. Card holder name shall satisfy configured validation rules where applicable.
16. Card number shall satisfy payment network validation rules including checksum validation where applicable.
17. Card expiration month shall represent a valid calendar month.
18. Card expiration year shall not represent an expired payment instrument.
19. The payment card shall not be expired.
20. CVV shall satisfy payment network validation rules during verification.
21. CVV shall never be stored after successful authorization.
22. UPI ID shall satisfy supported UPI format validation rules where applicable.
23. Bank account information shall satisfy applicable validation rules where supported.
24. IFSC code shall satisfy banking validation rules where applicable.
25. Wallet provider shall reference a supported digital wallet provider where applicable.
26. BNPL provider shall reference a supported Buy Now Pay Later provider where applicable.
27. Payment verification shall successfully complete before the payment method is saved.
28. OTP verification shall successfully complete where required.
29. 3-D Secure (3DS) authentication shall successfully complete where required.
30. Payment token generation shall successfully complete before storing payment method references.
31. Generated payment tokens shall be unique.
32. Payment tokens shall be valid and active.
33. Duplicate payment methods shall be rejected unless explicitly permitted by configurable business policies.
34. The maximum number of saved payment methods configured for the customer shall not be exceeded.
35. Only one payment method shall be designated as the default payment method at any given time.
36. Payment method lifecycle status shall permit the requested operation.
37. Payment methods associated with active subscriptions, recurring payment authorizations, pending payment transactions, unresolved disputes, or chargebacks shall not be removable unless organizational policies explicitly permit such removal.
38. Fraud detection validation shall successfully complete before payment method registration or modification.
39. Device fingerprint validation shall successfully complete where enabled.
40. IP address validation shall successfully complete according to configured fraud prevention policies.
41. Suspicious payment method activity shall be rejected or subjected to additional verification according to organizational policies.
42. Customer consent for storing payment references shall be obtained where required.
43. Required payment provider terms and conditions shall be accepted where applicable.
44. Encryption mechanisms shall be successfully applied to protected payment-related information where applicable.
45. Secure HTTPS communication shall be used for all payment method operations.
46. Audit logging shall successfully record every payment method operation.
47. All integrated platform services required for payment method processing shall be operational.
48. System-generated identifiers shall be unique.
49. The request shall comply with PCI DSS requirements and applicable payment industry standards.
50. Every payment method operation shall comply with applicable consumer protection laws, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, accessibility standards, taxation requirements where applicable, organizational governance policies, audit requirements, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations before successful completion.

#### 3.1.27.8 Main Flow

1. The authenticated customer navigates to the **Saved Payment Methods** section from the Customer Dashboard.
2. The system validates the customer's authentication and authorization.
3. The system retrieves the customer's existing saved payment methods.
4. The system displays the list of supported payment methods and available management options.
5. The customer selects **Add Payment Method**.
6. The system displays the supported payment method types.
7. The customer selects a payment method type.
8. The customer enters the required payment details.
9. The system validates the submitted input fields.
10. The system establishes a secure connection with the Payment Gateway Provider.
11. The Payment Verification Service validates the submitted payment instrument.
12. Where applicable, the customer successfully completes OTP verification.
13. Where applicable, the customer successfully completes 3-D Secure (3DS) authentication.
14. Where applicable, the system verifies the UPI account, digital wallet, bank account, or other supported payment instrument.
15. The Payment Gateway Provider confirms successful payment instrument verification.
16. The Fraud Detection Service performs payment method risk assessment.
17. The Fraud Detection Service confirms that no suspicious activity is detected.
18. The Payment Tokenization Service generates a secure payment token.
19. The system securely stores the payment token and approved payment metadata.
20. The system ensures that sensitive authentication data is not stored.
21. The system creates a new saved payment method record.
22. If the customer selects the payment method as the default payment method, the system removes the default designation from any previously designated payment method.
23. The system assigns the new payment method as the customer's default payment method where applicable.
24. The system updates the customer's saved payment methods.
25. The system records the payment method creation timestamp.
26. The system updates payment method lifecycle information.
27. The Audit Logging Service records the successful payment method registration.
28. The Analytics Service updates payment method usage statistics.
29. The Notification Service sends a payment method addition confirmation.
30. The Customer Dashboard displays the updated list of saved payment methods.
31. The customer optionally selects an existing payment method.
32. The customer updates supported editable payment preferences where applicable.
33. The system validates the requested modifications.
34. The system updates the payment method information.
35. The Audit Logging Service records the payment method update.
36. The Notification Service sends a payment method update notification where applicable.
37. The customer optionally selects **Set as Default** for another saved payment method.
38. The system validates the selected payment method.
39. The system removes the previous default designation.
40. The system assigns the selected payment method as the new default payment method.
41. The system updates the payment preferences.
42. The Audit Logging Service records the default payment method change.
43. The Notification Service sends a default payment method change notification.
44. The customer optionally selects **Remove Payment Method**.
45. The system validates whether the payment method can be removed.
46. The system verifies that no active subscription, recurring payment authorization, pending payment transaction, unresolved dispute, or other configured dependency prevents deletion.
47. The Payment Tokenization Service invalidates or deletes the associated payment token according to payment provider requirements.
48. The system removes the saved payment method.
49. The system updates the customer's saved payment method records.
50. The Audit Logging Service records the payment method removal.
51. The Notification Service sends a payment method removal confirmation.
52. The Customer Dashboard displays the updated saved payment methods.
53. The system returns a successful response indicating completion of the requested payment method operation.

#### 3.1.27.9 Alternate Flows

##### AF-1: First Saved Payment Method

1. The authenticated customer accesses the Saved Payment Methods module for the first time.
2. The system determines that no saved payment methods exist.
3. The system prompts the customer to add a payment method.
4. The customer successfully registers a payment method.
5. The newly added payment method is automatically designated as the default payment method.
6. The operation is recorded in the audit log.

---

##### AF-2: Payment Verification Requires OTP

1. The customer submits payment details.
2. The Payment Verification Service determines that OTP verification is required.
3. The customer successfully completes OTP verification.
4. Payment verification continues.
5. The payment method is processed normally.

---

##### AF-3: 3-D Secure (3DS) Authentication Required

1. The Payment Gateway Provider requires 3-D Secure authentication.
2. The customer completes the authentication process.
3. Authentication succeeds.
4. Payment verification continues.
5. The payment method is saved.

---

##### AF-4: UPI Verification

1. The customer selects UPI as the payment method.
2. The customer enters a valid UPI ID.
3. The Payment Verification Service validates the UPI account.
4. Verification succeeds.
5. The payment method is saved.

---

##### AF-5: Digital Wallet Registration

1. The customer selects a supported digital wallet.
2. The Payment Provider validates the wallet account.
3. Verification succeeds.
4. The wallet payment method is saved.
5. The wallet becomes available during checkout.

---

##### AF-6: Bank Account Verification

1. The customer selects a supported bank account payment method.
2. The customer provides the required banking information.
3. The Payment Verification Service validates the account.
4. Verification succeeds.
5. The payment method is saved.

---

##### AF-7: Default Payment Method Updated

1. The customer selects an existing payment method.
2. The customer chooses **Set as Default**.
3. The system removes the default designation from the previously selected payment method.
4. The selected payment method becomes the new default.
5. The system records the update.

---

##### AF-8: Payment Method Removed Successfully

1. The customer selects **Remove Payment Method**.
2. The system validates that no business dependency prevents deletion.
3. The associated payment token is invalidated according to payment provider requirements.
4. The payment method is removed.
5. The customer receives a confirmation notification.

---

##### AF-9: Payment Method Associated with Active Subscription

1. The customer requests removal of a payment method.
2. The Subscription Management Service determines that the payment method is associated with an active subscription or recurring payment authorization.
3. The removal request is rejected.
4. The customer is informed of the dependency.
5. The payment method remains active.

---

##### AF-10: Duplicate Payment Method Detected

1. The customer attempts to register an existing payment method.
2. The system detects a duplicate payment method.
3. Duplicate registration is prevented.
4. The duplicate attempt is recorded.
5. Appropriate validation feedback is returned.

---

##### AF-11: Expired Payment Method Detected

1. The customer attempts to save or use an expired payment method.
2. The system validates the expiration information.
3. The payment instrument is identified as expired.
4. Registration or usage is rejected.
5. The customer is prompted to update the payment information.

---

##### AF-12: Payment Token Rotation

1. The Payment Tokenization Service identifies that a payment token requires rotation.
2. A new payment token is securely generated.
3. The old token is invalidated.
4. The payment method record is updated.
5. Payment processing continues without customer interruption.

---

##### AF-13: Fraud Review Required

1. The Fraud Detection Service identifies suspicious payment activity.
2. The payment method operation is temporarily suspended.
3. Additional verification or manual review is initiated.
4. The operation resumes only after successful validation.
5. Audit records capture the fraud review process.

---

##### AF-14: Payment Provider Synchronization

1. A scheduled synchronization process communicates with the external Payment Gateway Provider.
2. Updated payment method metadata is retrieved.
3. The system synchronizes payment status and token information.
4. Inconsistent records are corrected where applicable.
5. Synchronization results are recorded.

---

##### AF-15: Payment Method Automatically Disabled

1. The Payment Gateway Provider reports that a payment method has become invalid.
2. The system updates the payment method status.
3. The payment method becomes unavailable for checkout.
4. The customer receives a notification.
5. Historical payment records remain unchanged.

---

##### AF-16: Payment Method Used During Checkout

1. The customer proceeds to checkout.
2. The Checkout Service retrieves eligible saved payment methods.
3. The default payment method is automatically pre-selected.
4. The customer may continue with the default payment method or choose another supported option.
5. Checkout processing continues normally.

---

##### AF-17: Administrative Payment Method Review

1. An authorized administrator reviews a payment method operation.
2. The administrator validates the operational details.
3. Administrative actions are performed according to organizational policies.
4. Every action is recorded within immutable audit logs.
5. The customer is notified where applicable.

---

##### AF-18: Scheduled Expired Payment Cleanup

1. A scheduled background process identifies expired payment methods or invalid payment tokens.
2. The system updates their lifecycle status according to organizational policies.
3. Expired payment methods become unavailable for future transactions.
4. Audit logs capture the scheduled maintenance activity.
5. Customers receive notifications where applicable.

#### 3.1.27.10 Postconditions

1. The requested Saved Payment Method operation shall be successfully completed.
2. The customer's saved payment methods shall accurately reflect the completed operation.
3. Newly added payment methods shall be securely registered in the customer account.
4. Successfully verified payment methods shall become available for future checkout transactions.
5. Payment methods shall be associated with the authenticated customer account only.
6. A unique Payment Method ID shall exist for every saved payment method.
7. Secure payment tokens shall be generated and stored for supported payment methods.
8. Sensitive authentication data including CVV, PIN, OTP values, full card numbers, and similar regulated payment information shall not be stored.
9. Payment-related metadata shall remain securely protected according to applicable security standards.
10. If a payment method is designated as the default payment method, all previously designated default payment methods shall automatically lose their default status.
11. At any given time, only one payment method shall remain designated as the default payment method.
12. Updated payment preferences shall be reflected immediately across all platform services.
13. Removed payment methods shall no longer be available for checkout or future payment transactions.
14. Deleted payment tokens shall be invalidated according to Payment Gateway Provider requirements.
15. Historical payment transactions shall remain unchanged regardless of payment method updates or deletion.
16. Payment method lifecycle status shall accurately represent the current operational state.
17. Payment verification status shall accurately reflect the latest verification outcome.
18. Payment provider synchronization shall remain consistent after successful operations.
19. Fraud detection results shall be recorded where applicable.
20. Device information and security metadata shall be updated where applicable.
21. Customer profile information shall remain synchronized with the latest payment method data.
22. Checkout Service shall retrieve the updated payment methods during future checkout sessions.
23. Customer Wallet, Subscription Management, Order Management, Payment Gateway, Notification Service, Fraud Detection Service, Analytics Service, and Audit Logging Service shall remain synchronized.
24. Customer notifications shall be generated for applicable payment method operations.
25. Analytics data shall be updated for reporting purposes.
26. Immutable audit logs shall capture the completed operation.
27. Administrative activity logs shall be updated where applicable.
28. Security monitoring systems shall receive the required operational events.
29. Operational metrics shall be updated for monitoring and observability.
30. All completed operations shall comply with PCI DSS requirements, applicable payment industry standards, organizational security policies, privacy regulations, consumer protection laws, audit requirements, cybersecurity standards, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

#### 3.1.27.11 Success Response

| Response Element | Description |
|------------------|-------------|
| Response Status | Indicates that the Saved Payment Method operation completed successfully. |
| HTTP Status Code | **200 OK**, **201 Created**, or other appropriate success status based on the operation performed. |
| Status | SUCCESS |
| Response Code | PAYMENT_METHOD_OPERATION_SUCCESS |
| Message | Saved payment method operation completed successfully. |
| Customer ID | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. |
| Payment Method ID | Unique identifier of the saved payment method. |
| Payment Method Type | Type of the saved payment method. |
| Payment Provider | Payment Gateway Provider associated with the payment method. |
| Payment Token Reference | Secure token reference generated by the Payment Tokenization Service. |
| Masked Payment Details | Masked payment information displayed to the customer (for example, masked card number or masked account identifier). |
| Card Brand | Card brand where applicable. |
| Wallet Provider | Digital wallet provider where applicable. |
| UPI ID (Masked) | Masked UPI identifier where applicable. |
| Payment Nickname | Customer-defined payment method nickname where applicable. |
| Verification Status | Indicates that payment verification completed successfully. |
| Default Payment Method | Indicates whether the payment method is currently the default payment method. |
| Payment Method Status | Current lifecycle status of the payment method. |
| Created Timestamp | Date and time when the payment method was successfully registered. |
| Updated Timestamp | Date and time of the latest successful modification where applicable. |
| Last Used Timestamp | Most recent usage timestamp where applicable. |
| Notification Status | Indicates whether the customer notification was successfully generated. |
| Audit Reference ID | Identifier of the generated audit record. |
| Correlation ID | Unique request identifier for distributed tracing. |
| Additional Information | Optional informational messages related to the completed operation. |

#### 3.1.27.12 Failure Response

| Response Element | Description |
|------------------|-------------|
| Response Status | Indicates that the Saved Payment Method operation failed. |
| HTTP Status Code | Appropriate error status such as **400 Bad Request**, **401 Unauthorized**, **403 Forbidden**, **404 Not Found**, **409 Conflict**, **422 Unprocessable Entity**, **429 Too Many Requests**, **500 Internal Server Error**, **502 Bad Gateway**, or **503 Service Unavailable**. |
| Status | FAILED |
| Response Code | System-defined error code representing the failure reason. |
| Error Message | Human-readable description of the failure. |
| Failure Reason | Detailed explanation of why the operation could not be completed. |
| Customer ID | Authenticated customer identifier where available. |
| Payment Method ID | Payment method identifier where applicable. |
| Payment Method Type | Payment method type associated with the failed operation where applicable. |
| Payment Provider | Payment Gateway Provider associated with the request where applicable. |
| Validation Errors | List of field-level validation errors when input validation fails. |
| Authentication Status | Indicates authentication failure where applicable. |
| Authorization Status | Indicates authorization failure where applicable. |
| Verification Status | Indicates payment verification failure where applicable. |
| Fraud Check Status | Indicates fraud detection outcome where applicable. |
| Tokenization Status | Indicates payment token generation or validation failure where applicable. |
| Dependency Status | Indicates failure caused by active subscriptions, recurring payments, pending transactions, disputes, or other business dependencies where applicable. |
| Retry Allowed | Indicates whether the customer may safely retry the operation. |
| Retry After | Suggested retry duration when applicable. |
| Timestamp | Date and time when the failure occurred. |
| Audit Reference ID | Identifier of the generated audit record where available. |
| Correlation ID | Unique request identifier for troubleshooting and distributed tracing. |
| Support Reference | Reference identifier to assist customer support investigations where applicable. |
| Additional Information | Optional diagnostic information that does not expose sensitive system or payment details. |

#### 3.1.27.13 Non-Functional Considerations

1. The Saved Payment Methods module shall be available **99.9% or higher** excluding planned maintenance windows.
2. The system shall support horizontal scaling without service interruption.
3. The module shall support concurrent payment method operations for millions of customers.
4. The system shall maintain high availability using redundant application and database infrastructure.
5. All communication involving payment method operations shall occur exclusively over secure HTTPS/TLS connections.
6. Sensitive payment information shall never be stored in plaintext.
7. Full Primary Account Numbers (PAN), CVV, PIN, OTP values, cryptographic keys, and other sensitive authentication data shall never be permanently stored.
8. Payment references shall be securely tokenized using PCI DSS-compliant tokenization services.
9. Payment-related metadata stored by the platform shall be encrypted using approved encryption standards.
10. Encryption keys shall be securely managed using centralized Key Management Services (KMS) or equivalent infrastructure.
11. Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) shall restrict administrative access to authorized personnel only.
12. Every payment method operation shall be authenticated and authorized before execution.
13. Fraud detection mechanisms shall continuously monitor payment method activities.
14. Suspicious activities shall generate security alerts for monitoring systems.
15. Every successful and failed payment method operation shall generate immutable audit logs.
16. Audit logs shall remain tamper-resistant and retained according to organizational retention policies.
17. Distributed tracing shall be supported using Correlation IDs for every request.
18. Operational metrics shall be collected for monitoring, observability, and capacity planning.
19. The module shall integrate reliably with Payment Gateway Providers, Payment Tokenization Services, Fraud Detection Services, Notification Services, Customer Profile Services, Checkout Services, Subscription Management Services, Customer Wallet Services, Analytics Services, and Audit Logging Services.
20. Temporary external dependency failures shall be handled using configurable retry mechanisms, timeout policies, circuit breakers, and graceful degradation strategies.
21. The system shall prevent duplicate payment method creation caused by repeated client requests through idempotent request handling where applicable.
22. Database operations shall maintain transactional consistency throughout payment method lifecycle operations.
23. Data synchronization across integrated services shall remain eventually or strongly consistent according to platform architecture.
24. Payment method operations shall be resilient against partial failures.
25. The module shall support configurable business rules without requiring application redeployment wherever possible.
26. The system shall support localization and internationalization for customer-facing messages.
27. Accessibility requirements shall comply with applicable accessibility standards (such as WCAG) where required.
28. The platform shall maintain comprehensive disaster recovery and backup mechanisms for payment-related metadata.
29. Recovery procedures shall support restoration within defined Recovery Time Objectives (RTO) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPO).
30. Performance shall remain within acceptable service-level objectives under expected production workloads.
31. The module shall support configurable rate limiting, abuse prevention, and bot protection mechanisms.
32. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be processed according to applicable privacy regulations.
33. Customer data shall be retained and deleted according to configured data retention policies and applicable legal requirements.
34. Administrative activities shall remain fully traceable for audit and compliance purposes.
35. The module shall comply with PCI DSS, applicable payment industry standards, ISO 27001 security controls where adopted, consumer protection regulations, privacy laws, cybersecurity requirements, organizational governance policies, audit requirements, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

#### 3.1.27.14 Acceptance Criteria

1. Authenticated customers shall be able to view all payment methods associated with their own customer account.
2. Unauthenticated users shall not be able to access the Saved Payment Methods module.
3. Unauthorized users shall not be able to access or modify another customer's saved payment methods.
4. Customers shall be able to add supported payment methods after successful verification.
5. Payment methods shall not be saved unless verification successfully completes.
6. Secure payment tokens shall be generated before storing payment method references.
7. The platform shall never permanently store CVV, PIN, OTP values, full Primary Account Numbers (PAN), or other sensitive authentication data.
8. Saved payment methods shall display only masked payment information.
9. Every saved payment method shall have a unique Payment Method ID.
10. Duplicate payment methods shall be prevented according to configured business policies.
11. Customers shall be able to save multiple payment methods within the configured platform limit.
12. The configured maximum number of saved payment methods shall not be exceeded.
13. Customers shall be able to designate one payment method as the default payment method.
14. Only one payment method shall remain designated as the default payment method at any given time.
15. Assigning a new default payment method shall automatically remove the previous default designation.
16. Customers shall be able to update only supported editable payment method attributes.
17. System-generated payment metadata shall remain read-only.
18. Customers shall be able to remove eligible saved payment methods.
19. Payment methods associated with active subscriptions, recurring payment authorizations, pending payment transactions, unresolved disputes, chargebacks, or other configured business dependencies shall not be removable unless organizational policies explicitly permit removal.
20. Deleted payment methods shall no longer be available during checkout.
21. Historical payment transactions shall remain unchanged after payment method updates or deletion.
22. Payment tokens shall be invalidated or removed according to Payment Gateway Provider requirements after payment method deletion.
23. Expired or inactive payment methods shall not be available for future payment transactions.
24. Fraud detection mechanisms shall evaluate payment method operations before completion.
