4. Compliance records are updated.
5. The deletion lifecycle is fully traceable for future investigations.

#### 3.1.29.10 Postconditions

1. The requested Delete Saved Device operation shall be successfully completed.
2. The selected device registration shall be permanently removed from the customer's account.
3. The deleted device shall no longer appear in the customer's registered device list.
4. The deleted device shall no longer be recognized as a trusted device.
5. All trusted-device privileges associated with the deleted device shall be revoked.
6. All active authentication sessions associated with the deleted device shall be terminated.
7. All authentication tokens associated with the deleted device shall become invalid immediately.
8. The deleted device shall no longer be permitted to access protected platform resources until successfully authenticated and registered again.
9. Historical login activity records associated with the deleted device shall remain preserved.
10. Historical audit records associated with the deleted device shall remain immutable.
11. Historical security events related to the deleted device shall remain available for investigation and compliance purposes.
12. Device lifecycle status shall accurately reflect permanent deletion.
13. Customer Profile Service shall reflect the updated list of registered devices.
14. Authentication Service shall no longer recognize the deleted device as an authorized registered device.
15. Authorization Service shall remove all device-related access associated with the deleted device.
16. Session Management Service shall remove all active sessions associated with the deleted device.
17. Fraud Detection Service shall update device security information where applicable.
18. Security Monitoring Service shall record the completed deletion event.
19. Notification Service shall successfully deliver applicable customer notifications.
20. Analytics Service shall update operational metrics related to device deletion.
21. Immutable audit records shall capture the complete device deletion lifecycle.
22. Administrative activity logs shall be updated where applicable.
23. Device information shall remain synchronized across Authentication Service, Authorization Service, Device Management Service, Session Management Service, Login Activity History Service, Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Service, Fraud Detection Service, Security Monitoring Service, Customer Profile Service, Notification Service, Analytics Service, and Audit Logging Service.
24. Operational metrics shall be updated for monitoring, observability, reporting, and capacity planning.
25. The Delete Saved Device module shall remain in a consistent, secure, and recoverable state after successful completion of the requested operation.
26. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall continue to be processed according to applicable privacy regulations and organizational security policies.
27. The completed operation shall satisfy organizational security policies, authentication standards, cybersecurity requirements, audit requirements, consumer protection regulations where applicable, accessibility standards where applicable, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.
28. The customer account shall remain active and unaffected except for the removal of the selected registered device.
29. Other registered devices associated with the customer account shall remain unchanged and fully operational.
30. The deleted device may be registered again only after successful future authentication and device registration according to current organizational security policies.

#### 3.1.29.11 Success Response

| Response Element | Description |
|------------------|-------------|
| Response Status | Indicates that the Delete Saved Device operation completed successfully. |
| HTTP Status Code | **200 OK** or other appropriate success status based on the completed operation. |
| Status | SUCCESS |
| Response Code | DEVICE_DELETION_SUCCESS |
| Message | The selected device has been permanently removed from the customer's account successfully. |
| Customer ID | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. |
| Device ID | Unique identifier of the deleted device. |
| Device Name | Customer-defined or system-generated name of the deleted device. |
| Device Type | Type of the deleted device. |
| Platform | Operating platform associated with the deleted device. |
| Operating System | Operating system of the deleted device where available. |
| Browser/Application | Browser or mobile application information associated with the deleted device where applicable. |
| Trusted Device Status | Indicates that trusted-device privileges have been revoked where applicable. |
| Session Termination Status | Indicates that all active sessions associated with the deleted device have been terminated. |
| Authentication Token Status | Indicates that all authentication tokens associated with the deleted device have been invalidated. |
| Device Lifecycle Status | Indicates that the device has been permanently removed from the customer's registered devices. |
| Remaining Registered Devices | Number of registered devices remaining in the customer's account after successful deletion. |
| Notification Status | Indicates whether the customer notification was successfully generated and delivered. |
| Audit Reference ID | Identifier of the generated audit record for the completed deletion operation. |
| Correlation ID | Unique request identifier for distributed tracing and troubleshooting. |
| Deleted Timestamp | Date and time when the device deletion was successfully completed. |
| Additional Information | Optional informational messages related to the completed device deletion operation. |

#### 3.1.29.12 Failure Response

| Response Element | Description |
|------------------|-------------|
| Response Status | Indicates that the Delete Saved Device operation failed. |
| HTTP Status Code | Appropriate error status such as **400 Bad Request**, **401 Unauthorized**, **403 Forbidden**, **404 Not Found**, **409 Conflict**, **422 Unprocessable Entity**, **429 Too Many Requests**, **500 Internal Server Error**, **502 Bad Gateway**, or **503 Service Unavailable**. |
| Status | FAILED |
| Response Code | System-defined error code representing the reason for failure. |
| Error Message | Human-readable description of the failure. |
| Failure Reason | Detailed explanation of why the device deletion request could not be completed. |
| Customer ID | Authenticated customer identifier where available. |
| Device ID | Identifier of the device associated with the failed deletion request where applicable. |
| Device Name | Name of the selected device where applicable. |
| Device Type | Type of the selected device where applicable. |
| Device Status | Current lifecycle status of the selected device where applicable. |
| Trusted Device Status | Current trusted-device status where applicable. |
| Session Status | Indicates whether active sessions remain associated with the device where applicable. |
| Authentication Status | Indicates authentication failure where applicable. |
| Authorization Status | Indicates authorization failure where applicable. |
| Two-Factor Authentication Status | Indicates failure of required 2FA verification where applicable. |
| Ownership Verification Status | Indicates whether device ownership verification failed where applicable. |
| Fraud Check Status | Indicates the result of fraud detection validation where applicable. |
| Security Validation Status | Indicates the outcome of security policy validation where applicable. |
| Validation Errors | List of field-level validation errors when request validation fails. |
| Dependency Status | Indicates failure caused by unavailable dependent platform services where applicable. |
| Retry Allowed | Indicates whether the deletion request may be safely retried. |
| Retry After | Suggested retry duration where applicable. |
| Timestamp | Date and time when the failure occurred. |
| Audit Reference ID | Identifier of the generated audit record where available. |
| Correlation ID | Unique request identifier for distributed tracing and troubleshooting. |
| Support Reference | Reference identifier to assist customer support investigations where applicable. |
| Additional Information | Optional diagnostic information that does not expose sensitive security information, authentication credentials, internal system details, or confidential implementation data. |

#### 3.1.29.13 Non-Functional Considerations

1. The Delete Saved Device module shall maintain **99.9% or higher availability** excluding planned maintenance windows.
2. The module shall support horizontal and vertical scalability without service interruption.
3. The system shall support concurrent device deletion requests from millions of customer accounts.
4. Device deletion operations shall be processed with high availability using redundant infrastructure.
5. All Delete Saved Device communications shall occur exclusively over secure HTTPS/TLS connections.
6. Device-related metadata shall be encrypted during transmission and protected during storage where applicable.
7. Authentication tokens, session identifiers, cryptographic secrets, OTP values, passwords, and other sensitive authentication information shall never be exposed through customer-facing interfaces.
8. Authentication tokens associated with deleted devices shall be securely invalidated immediately after successful deletion.
9. Encryption keys shall be securely managed using centralized Key Management Services (KMS) or equivalent secure infrastructure.
10. Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) shall restrict administrative device deletion operations to authorized personnel only.
11. Every device deletion request shall be authenticated and authorized before execution.
12. Security-sensitive deletion operations shall support configurable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) requirements.
13. Fraud Detection Services shall continuously evaluate device deletion requests for suspicious activities.
14. Security Monitoring Services shall generate alerts for abnormal or high-risk deletion attempts.
15. Every successful and failed device deletion operation shall generate immutable audit records.
16. Audit records shall remain tamper-resistant and retained according to configured organizational retention policies.
17. Every request shall include a Correlation ID to support distributed tracing, troubleshooting, and observability.
18. Operational metrics shall be collected for monitoring, reporting, analytics, and capacity planning.
19. The module shall integrate reliably with Device Management, Authentication, Authorization, Session Management, Login Activity History, Two-Factor Authentication (2FA), Fraud Detection, Security Monitoring, Notification, Customer Profile, Analytics, and Audit Logging services.
20. Temporary failures of dependent services shall be handled using configurable retry mechanisms, timeout policies, circuit breakers, fallback strategies, and graceful degradation.
21. Device deletion requests shall support idempotent processing wherever applicable to prevent duplicate deletion operations.
22. Database operations shall maintain transactional consistency throughout the complete device deletion lifecycle.
23. Device information shall remain synchronized across integrated platform services according to the configured consistency model.
24. Device deletion operations shall remain resilient against partial failures and unexpected infrastructure interruptions.
25. Business rules governing device deletion shall be configurable without requiring application redeployment wherever possible.
26. Customer-facing interfaces shall support localization and internationalization.
27. Accessibility requirements shall comply with applicable accessibility standards such as WCAG where required.
28. Backup and disaster recovery mechanisms shall protect device-related metadata and historical security records.
29. Recovery procedures shall satisfy configured Recovery Time Objectives (RTO) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPO).
30. Device deletion operations shall maintain acceptable response times under expected production workloads and peak traffic conditions.
31. Configurable rate limiting, abuse prevention, anomaly detection, and bot protection mechanisms shall protect Delete Saved Device APIs.
32. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be processed according to applicable privacy regulations.
33. Historical login records, audit logs, and security events shall be retained according to configured data retention and regulatory policies.
34. Administrative device deletion activities shall remain fully traceable for audit, forensic investigation, and compliance purposes.
35. The Delete Saved Device module shall comply with organizational security policies, authentication standards, ISO 27001 security controls where adopted, OWASP security recommendations, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, consumer protection laws where applicable, accessibility standards where applicable, audit requirements, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

#### 3.1.29.14 Acceptance Criteria

1. Only authenticated customers shall be able to access the Delete Saved Device module.
2. Customers shall be able to delete only devices associated with their own account.
3. Unauthorized users shall not be able to delete devices belonging to another customer.
4. The selected device shall exist before the deletion process begins.
5. The selected device shall have a valid lifecycle status.
6. Device ownership shall be successfully verified before deletion.
7. Devices that have already been deleted shall not be eligible for deletion again.
8. Customers shall be required to explicitly confirm the deletion request before processing.
9. Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) shall be successfully completed where required by organizational security policies.
10. High-risk deletion requests shall require additional identity verification or be rejected according to configured security policies.
11. Fraud Detection Service shall successfully evaluate applicable deletion requests before completion.
12. Devices involved in active fraud investigations, legal holds, or administrative restrictions shall not be deleted unless explicitly authorized.
13. The currently authenticated device shall not be deleted where prohibited by organizational security policies.
14. Successful device deletion shall permanently remove the device registration from the customer's account.
15. Successful device deletion shall revoke all trusted-device privileges associated with the deleted device.
16. All active authentication sessions associated with the deleted device shall be terminated immediately.
17. All authentication tokens associated with the deleted device shall be invalidated immediately after deletion.
18. Deleted devices shall no longer be able to access protected platform resources until successfully authenticated and registered again.
19. Historical login activity records shall remain preserved after device deletion.
20. Historical audit records shall remain immutable after device deletion.
21. Historical security events shall remain available for compliance, forensic investigation, and reporting purposes.
22. Customer Profile Service shall accurately reflect the updated registered device information.
23. Authentication, Authorization, Device Management, Session Management, Login Activity History, Two-Factor Authentication (2FA), Fraud Detection, Security Monitoring, Notification, Analytics, Customer Profile, and Audit Logging services shall remain synchronized following successful deletion.
24. Customers shall receive security notifications after successful device deletion.
25. Every successful device deletion shall generate immutable audit records.
26. Every failed deletion request shall generate appropriate audit records where applicable.
27. Device deletion operations shall remain available under expected production workloads without unacceptable performance degradation.
28. All Delete Saved Device communications shall use secure HTTPS/TLS encryption.
29. Device-related information shall remain protected according to organizational security policies.
30. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be processed according to applicable privacy regulations.
31. Administrative device deletion operations shall require appropriate authorization and complete audit traceability.
32. The Delete Saved Device module shall comply with organizational security policies, authentication standards, cybersecurity requirements, privacy regulations, consumer protection laws where applicable, accessibility standards where applicable, audit requirements, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

### 3.1.30 Login Activity History

#### 3.1.30.1 Description

The Login Activity History module enables authenticated customers to securely view and monitor the complete history of authentication activities associated with their customer account. The module provides visibility into successful logins, failed login attempts, logout events, session expirations, password-related authentication events, Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) events where applicable, trusted device activities, and other security-relevant account access events.

The primary objective of this module is to improve account transparency, strengthen customer security awareness, support fraud detection, and enable customers to quickly identify unauthorized or suspicious access attempts. Login activity records provide sufficient contextual information—including login timestamp, device details, operating system, browser or application, IP address where permitted by organizational privacy policies, approximate geographic location where available, authentication method, login status, session outcome, and security event type—to help customers recognize legitimate account usage.

The Login Activity History module shall integrate with Authentication Service, Authorization Service, Session Management Service, Device Management Service, Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Service, Security Monitoring Service, Fraud Detection Service, Customer Profile Service, Notification Service, Analytics Service, and Audit Logging Service to ensure that authentication events are consistently recorded, securely stored, and accurately presented.

The module shall support configurable retention policies for login history records according to organizational security requirements, operational policies, and applicable legal or regulatory obligations. Historical records shall remain immutable after creation and shall not be modifiable by customers. Administrative access to authentication history shall be governed by Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and organizational security policies.

Customers shall be able to review recent account activity to identify unfamiliar devices, unexpected login locations, unusual authentication behavior, repeated failed login attempts, or other indicators of potential account compromise. Where supported by organizational security policies, customers may initiate appropriate follow-up actions such as terminating active sessions, removing trusted devices, changing passwords, enabling Two-Factor Authentication (2FA), or contacting customer support.

The Login Activity History module shall maintain complete auditability, integrity, confidentiality, and availability of authentication history while ensuring compliance with organizational security standards, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, consumer protection laws where applicable, accessibility standards where applicable, audit requirements, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

#### 3.1.30.2 Actors

| Actor | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Customer | Authenticated customer who views and monitors the login activity history associated with their own account. |
| Authentication Service | Records successful and failed authentication events generated during customer login processes. |
| Authorization Service | Verifies that only authorized customers can access their own login activity history. |
| Session Management Service | Records session creation, expiration, timeout, logout, and session termination events. |
| Device Management Service | Provides registered device information associated with authentication events where applicable. |
| Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Service | Records successful and failed multi-factor authentication events where enabled. |
| Security Monitoring Service | Detects, records, and monitors authentication-related security events and suspicious account activities. |
| Fraud Detection Service | Evaluates authentication events for anomalous behavior, suspicious login attempts, impossible travel scenarios, and fraud indicators. |
| Customer Profile Service | Retrieves authenticated customer account information associated with login history records. |
| Notification Service | Generates security notifications for significant authentication events according to configured notification preferences. |
| Analytics Service | Collects aggregated authentication metrics for operational reporting, monitoring, and security analytics. |
| Audit Logging Service | Maintains immutable audit records for authentication activities and access to login history information. |
| Customer Support Representative | May access login history information only when explicitly authorized according to organizational policies and Role-Based Access Control (RBAC). |
| Security Administrator | Reviews authentication history for security investigations, fraud analysis, compliance verification, and incident response according to assigned administrative privileges. |

#### 3.1.30.3 Preconditions

1. The customer shall have successfully authenticated using a valid account.
2. The customer account shall exist and remain active.
3. The customer account shall not be permanently deleted.
4. The customer account shall not be suspended, locked, disabled, or restricted from accessing account security features unless permitted by organizational policies.
5. The customer's authentication session shall be valid and unexpired.
6. Authorization Service shall verify that the customer is requesting only their own login activity history.
7. The Login Activity History module shall be available.
8. Authentication Service shall be operational.
9. Authorization Service shall be operational.
10. Session Management Service shall be operational.
11. Device Management Service shall be available where device information is displayed.
12. Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Service shall be available where additional authentication is required.
13. Customer Profile Service shall be available.
14. Security Monitoring Service shall be operational.
15. Fraud Detection Service shall be operational where authentication risk evaluation is applicable.
16. Audit Logging Service shall be operational.
17. Notification Service shall be available where security notifications are generated.
18. Login activity records shall exist for the authenticated customer account or the system shall support displaying an empty history.
19. Historical authentication records shall remain intact and unmodified.
20. Customer privacy preferences shall permit display of login activity information according to organizational policies.
21. Applicable authentication history retention policies shall be enforced.
22. Required customer permissions shall be successfully validated before login history is displayed.
23. All requests shall be transmitted through secure HTTPS/TLS communication channels.
24. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) contained within login history shall be processed according to applicable privacy regulations and organizational security policies.
25. System clocks across integrated services shall remain synchronized to ensure accurate authentication timestamps.
26. Integrated services shall maintain consistent authentication history information.
27. Organizational security policies governing login history access shall be successfully validated before processing the request.
28. Required audit logging infrastructure shall be available to record access to login activity history where applicable.
29. The platform shall comply with organizational security standards, authentication policies, cybersecurity requirements, privacy regulations, audit requirements, accessibility standards where applicable, consumer protection laws where applicable, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations before processing the request.

#### 3.1.30.4 Trigger

The Login Activity History process shall be initiated when one of the following events occurs:

1. The authenticated customer selects **Login Activity History** from the Account Security section of the customer profile.
2. The authenticated customer selects **Recent Login Activity** from the account dashboard.
3. The authenticated customer navigates to the Security Settings page and requests to view login history.
4. The authenticated customer selects a notification related to account login activity and requests additional details.
5. The authenticated customer reviews recent account access after receiving a security alert.
6. The authenticated customer investigates suspicious account activity.
7. The authenticated customer verifies recent login attempts after changing their password.
8. The authenticated customer reviews account activity after enabling or disabling Two-Factor Authentication (2FA).
9. The authenticated customer verifies login events after registering or deleting a trusted device.
10. The authenticated customer reviews authentication history before terminating active sessions.
11. The authenticated customer accesses login history during routine account security verification.
12. Customer Support requests the customer to review recent login history during an account security investigation.
13. A Security Administrator accesses login activity history according to assigned administrative privileges and organizational security policies.
14. An automated security workflow requests authentication history for fraud analysis or incident investigation where permitted.
15. The platform redirects the authenticated customer to Login Activity History after detecting suspicious authentication events where supported by organizational security policies.
16. The platform requests login history as part of account recovery verification where applicable.
17. The customer refreshes or reloads the Login Activity History page.
18. The customer applies search, filtering, sorting, or pagination to existing login history records.
19. The customer requests additional details for a specific login activity record.
20. An authenticated API request is received from an authorized client application requesting the customer's login activity history.

#### 3.1.30.5 Input Fields

| Field Name | Description | Mandatory | Validation |
|------------|-------------|-----------|------------|
| Customer ID | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. Retrieved from the authenticated session. | Yes | Must correspond to a valid authenticated customer account and cannot be modified by the customer. |
| Session ID | Unique identifier of the active authenticated session. | Yes | Must represent a valid, active, and authorized session. |
| Authentication Token | Secure authentication token associated with the active customer session. | Yes | Must be valid, unexpired, and successfully verified before processing the request. |
| Search Keyword | Keyword used to search login activity records. | No | Supports partial matching within permitted searchable fields. |
| Login Status | Filters records by login outcome (Successful, Failed, Blocked, Expired, Logged Out, etc.). | No | Must contain only supported login status values. |
| Event Type | Filters authentication events such as Login, Logout, Session Expired, Password Changed, Password Reset, 2FA Verification, Trusted Device Registration, Device Removal, etc. | No | Must contain only supported event types. |
| Authentication Method | Filters records based on authentication mechanism such as Password, OTP, Social Login, Biometric, Passkey, or Two-Factor Authentication (2FA). | No | Must contain supported authentication methods only. |
| Device Type | Filters login history by device category such as Mobile, Tablet, Desktop, Laptop, or Other. | No | Must contain supported device categories only. |
| Operating System | Filters records by operating system. | No | Must contain supported operating system values where applicable. |
| Browser/Application | Filters records by browser or mobile application used during authentication. | No | Must contain supported browser or application values where applicable. |
| Device Name | Filters login history by registered device name where available. | No | Must reference a valid device associated with the customer account where applicable. |
| Trusted Device Status | Filters records based on trusted device usage. | No | Accepts supported trusted device status values only. |
| Date From | Start date for the login history search period. | No | Must not exceed the current date and shall not violate configured retention policies. |
| Date To | End date for the login history search period. | No | Must be greater than or equal to Date From and must not exceed the current date. |
| Time Zone | Customer-selected time zone used to display login timestamps. | No | Must be a supported system time zone. |
| Location Filter | Filters login history by approximate geographic location where available. | No | Supports only available and permitted location information according to privacy policies. |
| IP Address Filter | Filters records using IP address where permitted. | No | Must follow supported IPv4 or IPv6 formats and comply with organizational privacy policies. |
| Risk Level | Filters authentication events by calculated security risk level (Low, Medium, High, Critical). | No | Must contain supported risk classification values only. |
| Sort By | Specifies the attribute used for sorting login activity records. | No | Supported values include Login Time, Event Type, Device Name, Risk Level, or Login Status. |
| Sort Order | Specifies ascending or descending sorting order. | No | Allowed values: Ascending or Descending. |
| Page Number | Specifies the page to retrieve for paginated login history results. | No | Must be a positive integer greater than zero. |
| Page Size | Specifies the number of records returned per page. | No | Must remain within configured minimum and maximum pagination limits. |
| Include Device Details | Indicates whether additional registered device information should be returned where available. | No | Boolean value only. |
| Include Location Details | Indicates whether approximate login location information should be displayed where permitted. | No | Boolean value only and subject to organizational privacy policies. |
| Include Failed Attempts | Indicates whether failed authentication attempts should be included in the results. | No | Boolean value only. |
| Correlation ID | Unique request identifier for distributed tracing and diagnostics. | Yes | Must be unique for each request and generated by the platform or client according to system standards. |

#### 3.1.30.6 Business Rules

1. Only authenticated customers shall be permitted to view the login activity history associated with their own customer account.
2. Customers shall not be permitted to access login activity belonging to another customer account.
3. Every request to access login activity history shall be authenticated and authorized before processing.
4. Login activity records shall be displayed in reverse chronological order by default, with the most recent activity appearing first.
5. The system shall record every successful authentication event.
6. The system shall record every failed authentication attempt.
7. The system shall record logout events initiated by customers.
8. The system shall record automatic session expiration and session timeout events.
9. The system shall record password change events.
10. The system shall record password reset events where applicable.
11. The system shall record successful and failed Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) verification events where enabled.
12. The system shall record trusted device registration and trusted device removal events where applicable.
13. Login activity records shall include the authentication timestamp.
14. Login activity records shall include the authentication outcome.
15. Login activity records shall include the authentication method used.
16. Login activity records shall include device information where available.
17. Login activity records shall include browser or mobile application information where available.
18. Login activity records shall include operating system information where available.
19. Login activity records shall include IP address information only where permitted by applicable privacy regulations and organizational security policies.
20. Login activity records shall include approximate geographic location information only where available and permitted by organizational privacy policies.
21. The platform shall clearly distinguish successful authentication events from failed authentication attempts.
22. Security-sensitive events shall be visually distinguishable from normal authentication events where applicable.
23. Customers shall be able to search login activity history using supported search criteria.
24. Customers shall be able to filter login activity history using supported filter options.
25. Customers shall be able to sort login activity history using supported sorting options.
26. The system shall support configurable pagination for large login history datasets.
27. Historical login activity records shall remain immutable after creation.
28. Customers shall not be permitted to modify, delete, or alter historical login activity records.
29. Login history shall be retained according to configured organizational retention policies.
30. Expired login history records shall be archived or removed according to applicable retention policies.
31. Every access to login activity history shall generate audit records where required by organizational security policies.
32. Administrative access to login history shall require Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).
33. Administrative access shall be fully auditable.
34. Fraud Detection Service shall evaluate authentication events for suspicious account activity where applicable.
35. Security Monitoring Service shall continuously monitor authentication events for abnormal behavior.
36. High-risk authentication events may trigger additional security notifications according to organizational security policies.
37. Customers may initiate security actions such as session termination, password change, trusted device removal, or enabling Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) from supported login activity records where permitted.
38. Login timestamps shall be displayed using the customer's preferred time zone where configured; otherwise, the system default time zone shall be used.
39. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be displayed only to the extent permitted by applicable privacy regulations and organizational policies.
40. Login activity information shall remain synchronized across Authentication Service, Authorization Service, Session Management Service, Device Management Service, Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Service, Security Monitoring Service, Fraud Detection Service, Customer Profile Service, Notification Service, Analytics Service, and Audit Logging Service.
41. All login activity data shall be transmitted using secure HTTPS/TLS communication.
42. Authentication history shall be protected against unauthorized disclosure, tampering, or unauthorized modification.
43. Operational metrics related to login activity history shall be collected for monitoring, analytics, reporting, auditing, capacity planning, and security analysis.
44. The Login Activity History module shall comply with organizational security policies, authentication standards, cybersecurity requirements, privacy regulations, audit requirements, consumer protection laws where applicable, accessibility standards where applicable, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

#### 3.1.30.7 Validations

1. The customer shall be successfully authenticated before login activity history is displayed.
2. The customer account shall exist and remain active.
3. The authenticated session shall be valid and unexpired.
4. The authentication token shall be valid and successfully verified.
5. Authorization Service shall verify that the customer is requesting only their own login activity history.
6. Unauthorized attempts to access another customer's login activity shall be rejected.
7. Customer permissions shall be validated before processing the request.
8. The Login Activity History module shall be available.
9. Authentication Service shall be operational.
10. Authorization Service shall be operational.
11. Session Management Service shall be operational.
12. Customer Profile Service shall be available.
13. Audit Logging Service shall be operational.
14. Search keywords shall not exceed the configured maximum length.
15. Search keywords shall be sanitized to prevent SQL Injection, NoSQL Injection, Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), command injection, and other injection attacks.
16. Login Status filters shall contain only supported values.
17. Event Type filters shall contain only supported authentication event types.
18. Authentication Method filters shall contain only supported authentication mechanisms.
19. Device Type filters shall contain only supported device categories.
20. Operating System filters shall contain supported operating system values where applicable.
21. Browser/Application filters shall contain supported browser or application values where applicable.
22. Device Name filters shall reference only devices associated with the authenticated customer where applicable.
23. Trusted Device Status filters shall contain supported trusted device status values.
24. Date From shall not exceed the current system date.
25. Date To shall not exceed the current system date.
26. Date To shall be greater than or equal to Date From.
27. The requested date range shall comply with configured login history retention policies.
28. Time Zone values shall correspond to supported system time zones.
29. IP Address filters shall comply with supported IPv4 or IPv6 formats where applicable.
30. Location filters shall contain supported location values where available.
31. Risk Level filters shall contain supported risk classification values.
32. Sort By values shall contain only supported sorting attributes.
33. Sort Order shall be limited to supported ascending or descending values.
34. Page Number shall be a positive integer greater than zero.
35. Page Size shall remain within configured minimum and maximum pagination limits.
36. Boolean input fields shall contain only valid Boolean values.
37. Correlation ID shall be present and unique for request tracing.
38. All requests shall be transmitted through secure HTTPS/TLS communication channels.
39. Rate limiting policies shall be enforced to prevent excessive or abusive requests.
40. Fraud Detection Service may perform additional validation for suspicious request patterns.
41. Security Monitoring Service may evaluate requests for abnormal access behavior.
42. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be displayed only in accordance with applicable privacy regulations and organizational security policies.
43. Every successful and failed request shall generate audit records where required by organizational security policies.
44. The request shall comply with organizational authentication policies, cybersecurity standards, privacy regulations, audit requirements, accessibility standards where applicable, consumer protection laws where applicable, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations before login activity history is displayed.

#### 3.1.30.8 Main Flow

1. The authenticated customer navigates to the **Login Activity History** section from the Account Security settings or another supported entry point.
2. The system verifies that the customer's authentication session is valid and active.
3. The Authorization Service verifies that the customer is authorized to access only their own login activity history.
4. The system validates the incoming request and all provided input parameters.
5. The system validates search, filtering, sorting, pagination, and date range parameters where applicable.
6. The system validates that the requested date range complies with configured login history retention policies.
7. The system generates a unique Correlation ID for end-to-end request tracing if one is not already provided.
8. The Audit Logging Service records the login history access request where required by organizational security policies.
9. The system retrieves login activity records associated with the authenticated customer account.
10. The system retrieves authentication events from the Authentication Service.
11. The system retrieves session-related events from the Session Management Service.
12. The system retrieves trusted device information from the Device Management Service where applicable.
13. The system retrieves Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) events where applicable.
14. The system retrieves security-related authentication events from the Security Monitoring Service where applicable.
15. The system retrieves fraud-related authentication risk information from the Fraud Detection Service where available.
16. The system aggregates authentication events into a unified login activity history.
17. The system removes duplicate or inconsistent records if detected according to organizational data consistency policies.
18. The system enriches each login activity record with available metadata such as device name, device type, operating system, browser/application, authentication method, login status, approximate location where permitted, trusted device status, session outcome, and security risk level where available.
19. The system applies the requested search criteria where applicable.
20. The system applies the requested filters where applicable.
21. The system applies the requested sorting preferences.
22. The system applies pagination according to the requested page number and page size.
23. The system converts timestamps to the customer's preferred time zone where configured.
24. The system masks or omits sensitive information according to organizational privacy policies and applicable regulations.
25. The system verifies that only records belonging to the authenticated customer are included in the response.
26. The system prepares the login activity history response.
27. The system updates operational metrics for monitoring, reporting, analytics, and observability.
28. The Audit Logging Service records successful completion of the login history retrieval process where required.
29. The system securely returns the login activity history to the authenticated customer.
30. The customer reviews recent authentication events and, where supported, may initiate additional security actions such as terminating active sessions, deleting trusted devices, changing the account password, enabling Two-Factor Authentication (2FA), or contacting customer support for suspicious account activity.

#### 3.1.30.9 Alternate Flows

##### A. No Login Activity Available

1. The customer accesses the Login Activity History module for the first time or no authentication records exist within the configured retention period.
2. The system validates the customer's authentication and authorization.
3. The system determines that no login activity records are available.
4. The system displays an informative message indicating that no login activity is currently available.
5. The system continues to display available search and filtering options where applicable.
6. The request shall complete successfully without generating an application error.

##### B. Search Returns No Matching Results

1. The customer enters search keywords or applies one or more filters.
2. The system validates all search parameters.
3. The system executes the search operation.
4. No matching login activity records satisfy the specified criteria.
5. The system displays a "No matching login activity found" message.
6. The customer may modify or clear the search criteria and submit a new request.

##### C. Invalid Search or Filter Criteria

1. The customer submits unsupported or invalid search parameters.
2. The system validates all input fields.
3. Validation failures are identified.
4. The system rejects the invalid request.
5. Appropriate validation messages are displayed.
6. Previously displayed login history shall remain unchanged until a valid request is submitted.

##### D. Requested Date Range Exceeds Retention Policy

1. The customer requests login history outside the configured retention period.
2. The system validates the requested date range.
3. The system determines that the request exceeds the permitted retention policy.
4. The system rejects the invalid date range.
5. The customer is informed of the supported historical period.
6. The customer may submit another request using a valid date range.

##### E. Authentication Session Expired

1. The customer requests login history after the authenticated session has expired.
2. Session Management Service detects the expired session.
3. The request is rejected.
4. No login activity information is disclosed.
5. The customer is redirected to the authentication page.
6. The customer must successfully authenticate before retrying the request.

##### F. Unauthorized Access Attempt

1. A request attempts to retrieve login activity belonging to another customer.
2. Authorization Service validates ownership.
3. Ownership verification fails.
4. The request is immediately rejected.
5. No login activity information is returned.
6. The event is recorded by the Audit Logging Service and Security Monitoring Service.
7. Additional security controls may be triggered according to organizational security policies.

##### G. Dependent Service Temporarily Unavailable

1. One or more integrated services become temporarily unavailable during processing.
2. The system performs configured retry operations.
3. Circuit breaker, timeout, or fallback mechanisms are applied where supported.
4. If recovery succeeds, processing continues normally.
5. If recovery fails, an appropriate service unavailable response is returned.
6. Diagnostic information is logged for operational monitoring.

##### H. Fraud Detection Identifies Suspicious Activity

1. Fraud Detection Service identifies abnormal authentication behavior.
2. The request is evaluated according to organizational risk policies.
3. Additional verification may be required before displaying sensitive authentication information.
4. Security Monitoring Service records the event.
5. Customer notifications may be generated where applicable.
6. Administrative review may be initiated for high-risk scenarios.

##### I. Partial Metadata Unavailable

1. Login activity records are successfully retrieved.
2. Device information, approximate location, browser details, or other optional metadata cannot be retrieved.
3. Core authentication history remains available.
4. Missing optional fields are displayed as unavailable.
5. The login activity history is returned without failing the entire request.
6. Operational logs record the missing metadata for investigation where necessary.

##### J. Internal System Failure

1. An unexpected internal error occurs during request processing.
2. The system safely terminates the current operation.
3. No incomplete or inconsistent login activity information is returned.
4. A generic error response is presented without exposing internal implementation details.
5. Diagnostic information is recorded in operational and audit logs.
6. The customer may retry the request after the issue has been resolved.

#### 3.1.30.10 Postconditions

1. The requested Login Activity History operation shall be successfully completed.
2. The authenticated customer shall be presented with only the login activity records associated with their own customer account.
3. No login activity belonging to another customer shall be disclosed.
4. Login activity records shall remain immutable after retrieval.
5. Historical authentication records shall not be modified during the retrieval process.
6. Customer authentication history shall remain synchronized with Authentication Service.
7. Session-related events shall remain synchronized with Session Management Service.
8. Trusted device information shall remain synchronized with Device Management Service where applicable.
9. Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) events shall remain synchronized with the Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Service where applicable.
10. Security-related authentication events shall remain synchronized with the Security Monitoring Service.
11. Fraud-related authentication information shall remain synchronized with the Fraud Detection Service where applicable.
12. Customer profile information used during the request shall remain synchronized with the Customer Profile Service.
13. Retrieved login activity records shall accurately reflect the current retained authentication history according to configured retention policies.
14. Search, filtering, sorting, and pagination preferences shall be successfully applied where requested.
15. Login timestamps shall be presented using the customer's preferred time zone where configured.
16. Sensitive information shall remain masked or omitted according to organizational privacy policies and applicable regulations.
17. Every successful access to Login Activity History shall generate audit records where required by organizational security policies.
18. Administrative access to login history shall remain fully traceable through immutable audit logs.
19. Operational metrics related to login history retrieval shall be updated for monitoring, analytics, reporting, capacity planning, and observability.
20. Security Monitoring Service shall record security-relevant access events where applicable.
21. Fraud Detection Service shall update risk evaluation metrics where applicable.
22. Notification preferences shall remain unaffected unless a separate customer action modifies them.
23. The customer's authentication session shall remain active unless terminated by independent security policies or session management rules.
24. No customer account settings shall be modified as part of viewing login activity history.
25. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall continue to be processed in accordance with applicable privacy regulations and organizational security policies.
26. Immutable audit records shall preserve the complete lifecycle of the login history access request where required.
27. Integrated services shall remain synchronized across Authentication Service, Authorization Service, Session Management Service, Device Management Service, Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Service, Security Monitoring Service, Fraud Detection Service, Customer Profile Service, Notification Service, Analytics Service, and Audit Logging Service.
28. The Login Activity History module shall remain in a secure, consistent, available, and recoverable state after successful completion of the request.
29. The completed operation shall comply with organizational security policies, authentication standards, cybersecurity requirements, privacy regulations, audit requirements, consumer protection laws where applicable, accessibility standards where applicable, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.
30. The customer may continue performing additional permitted security operations from the Login Activity History module where supported by the platform.

#### 3.1.30.11 Success Response

| Response Element | Description |
|------------------|-------------|
| Response Status | Indicates that the Login Activity History request completed successfully. |
| HTTP Status Code | **200 OK** or other appropriate success status based on the completed operation. |
| Status | SUCCESS |
| Response Code | LOGIN_ACTIVITY_HISTORY_RETRIEVED_SUCCESSFULLY |
| Message | Login activity history has been retrieved successfully. |
| Customer ID | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. |
| Login Activity Count | Total number of login activity records returned in the current response. |
| Total Records | Total number of login activity records available for the specified search criteria. |
| Current Page | Current page number returned by the pagination mechanism. |
| Page Size | Number of login activity records included in the current response. |
| Total Pages | Total number of available pages based on the current search criteria. |
| Applied Filters | List of search filters successfully applied to the request where applicable. |
| Sort Configuration | Sorting attribute and sorting order applied to the returned records. |
| Date Range | Date range used for retrieving login activity history where specified. |
| Time Zone | Time zone used for displaying authentication timestamps. |
| Login Activity Records | Collection of login activity entries containing supported authentication event information. |
| Event Timestamp | Date and time when each authentication event occurred. |
| Event Type | Authentication event type such as Login, Logout, Failed Login, Password Change, Password Reset, Session Expired, Trusted Device Registration, Device Removal, or Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) event. |
| Login Status | Status of the authentication attempt associated with each record. |
| Authentication Method | Authentication mechanism used for each authentication event. |
| Device Information | Device name, device type, operating system, browser/application, and trusted device status where available. |
| Approximate Location | Approximate login location where available and permitted by organizational privacy policies. |
| IP Address | Masked or permitted IP address information according to applicable privacy regulations and organizational policies. |
| Risk Level | Security risk classification associated with the authentication event where applicable. |
| Session Information | Session identifier or session outcome where applicable. |
| Audit Reference ID | Identifier of the generated audit record associated with the completed request where applicable. |
| Correlation ID | Unique request identifier for distributed tracing and troubleshooting. |
| Generated Timestamp | Date and time when the response was generated. |
| Additional Information | Optional informational messages related to the successful retrieval of login activity history. |

#### 3.1.30.12 Failure Response

| Response Element | Description |
|------------------|-------------|
| Response Status | Indicates that the Login Activity History request failed. |
| HTTP Status Code | Appropriate error status such as **400 Bad Request**, **401 Unauthorized**, **403 Forbidden**, **404 Not Found**, **408 Request Timeout**, **409 Conflict**, **422 Unprocessable Entity**, **429 Too Many Requests**, **500 Internal Server Error**, **502 Bad Gateway**, or **503 Service Unavailable**. |
| Status | FAILED |
| Response Code | System-defined error code representing the reason for failure. |
| Error Message | Human-readable description of the failure. |
| Failure Reason | Detailed explanation of why the login activity history request could not be processed. |
| Customer ID | Authenticated customer identifier where available. |
| Authentication Status | Indicates whether authentication validation failed. |
| Authorization Status | Indicates whether authorization validation failed. |
| Session Status | Indicates whether the authenticated session is expired, invalid, or inactive. |
| Search Validation Status | Indicates whether submitted search parameters failed validation. |
| Filter Validation Status | Indicates whether one or more filtering criteria are invalid. |
| Date Range Validation Status | Indicates whether the requested date range violates configured validation or retention policies. |
| Pagination Validation Status | Indicates whether pagination parameters are invalid. |
| Dependency Service Status | Indicates whether one or more integrated platform services are unavailable or failed during processing. |
| Fraud Detection Status | Indicates whether fraud detection validation prevented request completion where applicable. |
| Security Validation Status | Indicates whether security policy validation failed. |
| Privacy Validation Status | Indicates whether privacy policy restrictions prevented disclosure of requested information. |
| Validation Errors | Collection of field-level validation errors where applicable. |
| Retry Allowed | Indicates whether the request may be safely retried. |
| Retry After | Suggested retry duration where applicable. |
| Timestamp | Date and time when the failure occurred. |
| Audit Reference ID | Identifier of the generated audit record where applicable. |
| Correlation ID | Unique request identifier for distributed tracing and troubleshooting. |
| Support Reference | Reference identifier for customer support and operational investigations where applicable. |
| Additional Information | Optional diagnostic information that does not expose sensitive authentication details, internal implementation information, confidential security controls, or Personally Identifiable Information (PII). |

#### 3.1.30.13 Non-Functional Considerations

1. The Login Activity History module shall maintain **99.9% or higher availability**, excluding planned maintenance windows.
2. The module shall support horizontal and vertical scalability without service interruption.
3. The system shall support concurrent login history requests from millions of authenticated customers.
4. Login activity retrieval shall be optimized for low-latency response under normal and peak production workloads.
5. The module shall support configurable pagination to efficiently process large authentication history datasets.
6. Historical login records shall be indexed to support efficient searching, filtering, and sorting.
7. The module shall remain operational even when authentication history contains large volumes of records.
8. All communications between clients and platform services shall use secure HTTPS/TLS encryption.
9. Login activity records shall be protected against unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, or deletion.
10. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) displayed within login history shall be processed according to applicable privacy regulations and organizational security policies.
11. Sensitive authentication information including passwords, OTP values, authentication tokens, cryptographic secrets, session secrets, and security credentials shall never be displayed in login history.
12. IP address information shall be masked or partially displayed where required by applicable privacy regulations.
13. Authentication timestamps shall remain accurate through synchronized system clocks across distributed services.
14. Historical login records shall remain immutable after creation.
15. Audit logs associated with login history access shall be tamper-resistant and retained according to configured organizational retention policies.
16. Every successful and failed login history request shall generate audit records where required by organizational security policies.
17. Every request shall include a Correlation ID to support distributed tracing, diagnostics, and troubleshooting.
18. Authentication history shall remain synchronized across Authentication Service, Authorization Service, Session Management Service, Device Management Service, Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Service, Security Monitoring Service, Fraud Detection Service, Customer Profile Service, Notification Service, Analytics Service, and Audit Logging Service.
19. Temporary failures of dependent services shall be handled using configurable retry mechanisms, timeout policies, circuit breakers, fallback strategies, and graceful degradation wherever applicable.
20. Login history retrieval operations shall remain resilient against infrastructure failures and partial service outages.
21. Database operations supporting authentication history retrieval shall maintain transactional consistency.
22. Search, filtering, sorting, and pagination operations shall remain performant under expected production workloads.
23. Business rules governing login history visibility, retention, and privacy shall be configurable without requiring application redeployment wherever possible.
24. Operational metrics shall be collected for monitoring, reporting, analytics, capacity planning, and observability.
25. Security Monitoring Service shall continuously monitor abnormal access patterns to login history.
26. Fraud Detection Service shall evaluate suspicious requests according to configured organizational risk policies.
27. Configurable rate limiting, abuse prevention, anomaly detection, and bot protection mechanisms shall protect Login Activity History APIs.
28. Customer-facing interfaces shall support localization and internationalization.
29. Accessibility requirements shall comply with applicable accessibility standards such as WCAG where required.
30. Backup and disaster recovery mechanisms shall protect authentication history records and audit information.
31. Recovery procedures shall satisfy configured Recovery Time Objectives (RTO) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPO).
32. Historical authentication records shall be retained according to configured retention policies and applicable legal or regulatory requirements.
33. Administrative access to login history shall be governed by Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and remain fully traceable.
34. The Login Activity History module shall comply with organizational security policies, authentication standards, ISO 27001 security controls where adopted, OWASP security recommendations, privacy regulations, cybersecurity requirements, consumer protection laws where applicable, accessibility standards where applicable, audit requirements, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.
35. The module shall maintain confidentiality, integrity, availability, auditability, and consistency of authentication history throughout its operational lifecycle.

#### 3.1.30.14 Acceptance Criteria

1. Only authenticated customers shall be able to access the Login Activity History module.
2. Customers shall be able to view only the login activity associated with their own customer account.
3. Unauthorized users shall not be able to access another customer's login history.
4. Every login history request shall be authenticated and authorized before processing.
5. Login activity records shall be displayed in reverse chronological order by default.
6. The system shall accurately display successful login events.
7. The system shall accurately display failed authentication attempts.
8. The system shall accurately display logout events.
9. The system shall accurately display session timeout and session expiration events where applicable.
10. The system shall accurately display password change and password reset events where applicable.
11. The system shall accurately display Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) events where enabled.
12. Login activity records shall display authentication timestamps.
13. Login activity records shall display authentication methods where available.
14. Login activity records shall display login status for each authentication event.
15. Login activity records shall display device information where available.
16. Login activity records shall display browser or application information where available.
17. Login activity records shall display operating system information where available.
18. Login activity records shall display approximate location information only where permitted by organizational privacy policies.
19. IP address information shall be masked or displayed according to applicable privacy regulations and organizational security policies.
20. Customers shall be able to search login history using supported search criteria.
21. Customers shall be able to filter login history using supported filter options.
22. Customers shall be able to sort login history using supported sorting options.
23. The system shall support configurable pagination for authentication history records.
24. Historical login activity records shall remain immutable after creation.
25. Customers shall not be permitted to modify or delete historical login activity records.
26. Login history shall be retained according to configured organizational retention policies.
27. Every successful login history request shall generate audit records where required by organizational security policies.
28. Administrative access to login history shall require Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).
29. Administrative access shall remain fully auditable.
30. Authentication history shall remain synchronized across Authentication Service, Authorization Service, Session Management Service, Device Management Service, Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Service, Security Monitoring Service, Fraud Detection Service, Customer Profile Service, Notification Service, Analytics Service, and Audit Logging Service.
31. Search, filtering, sorting, and pagination shall return accurate and consistent results.
32. All communications related to login history shall use secure HTTPS/TLS encryption.
33. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be processed according to applicable privacy regulations and organizational security policies.
34. Sensitive authentication information including passwords, OTP values, authentication tokens, session secrets, and cryptographic credentials shall never be displayed in login activity history.
35. Operational metrics shall be updated for monitoring, reporting, analytics, observability, and capacity planning.
36. The Login Activity History module shall remain available under expected production workloads without unacceptable performance degradation.
37. The module shall comply with organizational security policies, authentication standards, cybersecurity requirements, privacy regulations, audit requirements, consumer protection laws where applicable, accessibility standards where applicable, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

### 3.1.31 Session Management

#### 3.1.31.1 Description

The Session Management module enables authenticated customers to securely manage all active and historical authenticated sessions associated with their customer account. The module provides complete visibility into currently active sessions across supported devices, browsers, operating systems, and applications while allowing customers to terminate individual sessions or all sessions except the current one, thereby improving account security and reducing the risk of unauthorized access.

The primary objective of the Session Management module is to provide customers with transparency and control over authenticated sessions while ensuring secure session lifecycle management throughout session creation, validation, renewal, expiration, timeout, revocation, and termination. The module helps customers quickly identify unfamiliar or suspicious sessions and take immediate corrective actions to protect their accounts.

The Session Management module shall integrate with Authentication Service, Authorization Service, Device Management Service, Login Activity History Service, Two-FFactor Authentication (2FA) Service, Security Monitoring Service, Fraud Detection Service, Customer Profile Service, Notification Service, Analytics Service, and Audit Logging Service to maintain accurate, synchronized, and secure session information across the platform.

The module shall display relevant session information including session identifier where permitted, device name, device type, operating system, browser or application, login timestamp, last activity timestamp, authentication method, session status, approximate location where permitted by organizational privacy policies, IP address where permitted, trusted device status, and session expiration details where applicable.

Customers shall be able to terminate individual active sessions, terminate all active sessions except the currently authenticated session where supported, review session details, and verify recent account activity. Session termination shall immediately invalidate associated authentication tokens and revoke access to protected platform resources.

Historical session records shall remain immutable after creation and shall be retained according to configured organizational retention policies. Active session information shall remain synchronized across integrated platform services to ensure consistency, security, auditability, and operational reliability.

The Session Management module shall maintain confidentiality, integrity, availability, traceability, and recoverability of session information while complying with organizational security policies, authentication standards, cybersecurity requirements, privacy regulations, consumer protection laws where applicable, accessibility standards where applicable, audit requirements, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

#### 3.1.31.2 Actors

| Actor | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Customer | Authenticated customer who views, monitors, and manages active sessions associated with their own account. |
| Authentication Service | Creates, validates, renews, revokes, and invalidates authentication sessions and tokens. |
| Authorization Service | Verifies that only authorized customers can access and manage their own sessions. |
| Session Management Service | Maintains the complete lifecycle of customer sessions including creation, validation, timeout, expiration, renewal, and termination. |
| Device Management Service | Provides registered device information associated with active customer sessions. |
| Login Activity History Service | Records session-related authentication events including login, logout, timeout, and session termination activities. |
| Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Service | Performs additional identity verification for security-sensitive session management operations where required. |
| Security Monitoring Service | Continuously monitors active sessions for suspicious behavior, abnormal access patterns, and security incidents. |
| Fraud Detection Service | Evaluates active sessions for fraud indicators, impossible travel scenarios, device anomalies, and account takeover attempts. |
| Customer Profile Service | Retrieves authenticated customer profile information required during session management operations. |
| Notification Service | Sends security notifications related to new sessions, terminated sessions, suspicious activities, and other session-related security events according to configured notification preferences. |
| Analytics Service | Collects aggregated session management metrics for monitoring, reporting, operational analytics, and capacity planning. |
| Audit Logging Service | Maintains immutable audit records for all session creation, validation, renewal, expiration, revocation, termination, and management activities. |
| Customer Support Representative | May review session information only when explicitly authorized according to organizational policies and Role-Based Access Control (RBAC). |
| Security Administrator | Reviews session information during fraud investigations, security incidents, compliance verification, forensic analysis, and administrative security operations according to assigned privileges. |

#### 3.1.31.3 Preconditions

1. The customer shall have successfully authenticated using valid account credentials.
2. The customer account shall exist and remain active.
3. The customer account shall not be permanently deleted.
4. The customer account shall not be suspended, locked, disabled, or restricted from accessing session management features unless permitted by organizational security policies.
5. The customer's authenticated session shall be valid and unexpired.
6. Authorization Service shall verify that the customer is authorized to manage only their own sessions.
7. The Session Management module shall be available.
8. Authentication Service shall be operational.
9. Authorization Service shall be operational.
10. Session Management Service shall be operational.
11. Device Management Service shall be available.
12. Login Activity History Service shall be available where historical session information is displayed.
13. Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) Service shall be operational where additional identity verification is required.
14. Security Monitoring Service shall be operational.
15. Fraud Detection Service shall be operational where risk evaluation is applicable.
16. Customer Profile Service shall be available.
17. Notification Service shall be operational where session-related notifications are generated.
18. Audit Logging Service shall be operational.
19. Active session information shall be available for retrieval or the system shall support displaying an empty session list.
20. Historical session records shall remain intact and unmodified.
21. Customer permissions shall be successfully validated before any session information is displayed or modified.
22. Organizational security policies governing session management shall be successfully validated before processing the request.
23. Session timeout policies shall be available and enforced according to organizational configuration.
24. Authentication token validation mechanisms shall be operational.
25. All requests shall be transmitted through secure HTTPS/TLS communication channels.
26. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) associated with session information shall be processed according to applicable privacy regulations and organizational security policies.
27. System clocks across integrated services shall remain synchronized to ensure accurate session timestamps.
28. Integrated platform services shall maintain consistent session information across Authentication Service, Session Management Service, Device Management Service, Login Activity History Service, and other dependent services.
29. Required audit logging infrastructure shall be available to record session management activities where applicable.
30. The platform shall comply with organizational security policies, authentication standards, cybersecurity requirements, privacy regulations, audit requirements, accessibility standards where applicable, consumer protection laws where applicable, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations before processing session management requests.

#### 3.1.31.4 Trigger

The Session Management process shall be initiated when one of the following events occurs:

1. The authenticated customer navigates to the **Session Management** section from the Account Security settings.
2. The authenticated customer selects **Manage Active Sessions** from the customer dashboard.
3. The authenticated customer reviews active sessions after receiving a security notification.
4. The authenticated customer identifies suspicious account activity and requests to review active sessions.
5. The authenticated customer chooses to terminate a specific active session.
6. The authenticated customer chooses to terminate all active sessions except the currently authenticated session.
7. The authenticated customer changes their account password and elects to revoke other active sessions where supported.
8. The authenticated customer enables or disables Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) and reviews existing sessions.
9. The authenticated customer registers or removes a trusted device and reviews associated sessions.
10. The authenticated customer performs a routine account security review.
11. Customer Support instructs the authenticated customer to review active sessions during an account security investigation.
12. A Security Administrator accesses session information according to assigned administrative privileges and organizational security policies.
13. The platform detects suspicious authentication behavior and redirects the authenticated customer to Session Management for security review where supported.
14. Fraud Detection Service identifies abnormal session behavior requiring customer verification where applicable.
15. Security Monitoring Service detects concurrent or suspicious active sessions requiring customer attention according to organizational security policies.
16. The customer refreshes or reloads the Session Management page.
17. The customer requests detailed information for a specific active session.
18. The customer applies search, filtering, sorting, or pagination to the active session list where supported.
19. The platform automatically requests session information during account recovery or security verification processes where applicable.
20. An authenticated API request is received from an authorized client application requesting session management information for the authenticated customer.

#### 3.1.31.5 Input Fields

| Field Name | Description | Mandatory | Validation |
|------------|-------------|-----------|------------|
| Customer ID | Unique identifier of the authenticated customer. Retrieved from the authenticated session. | Yes | Must correspond to a valid authenticated customer account and cannot be modified by the customer. |
| Session ID | Unique identifier of the currently authenticated session. | Yes | Must represent a valid, active, and authorized session. |
| Authentication Token | Secure authentication token associated with the active customer session. | Yes | Must be valid, unexpired, and successfully verified before processing the request. |
| Action Type | Session management action requested by the customer (View Sessions, View Session Details, Terminate Session, Terminate All Other Sessions, Refresh Session List, etc.). | Yes | Must contain only supported action values. |
| Target Session ID | Unique identifier of the session selected for management where applicable. | No | Must reference an existing active session belonging to the authenticated customer. |
| Search Keyword | Keyword used to search session records. | No | Supports partial matching within permitted searchable fields. |
| Session Status | Filters sessions based on status such as Active, Expired, Revoked, Logged Out, Timed Out, or Terminated. | No | Must contain only supported session status values. |
| Device Type | Filters sessions by device category such as Mobile, Tablet, Desktop, Laptop, or Other. | No | Must contain supported device category values only. |
| Device Name | Filters sessions by registered device name where available. | No | Must reference a valid customer-associated device where applicable. |
| Operating System | Filters sessions by operating system. | No | Must contain supported operating system values where applicable. |
| Browser/Application | Filters sessions by browser or mobile application. | No | Must contain supported browser or application values where applicable. |
| Authentication Method | Filters sessions by authentication mechanism such as Password, OTP, Social Login, Biometric, Passkey, or Two-Factor Authentication (2FA). | No | Must contain supported authentication methods only. |
