22. Security Monitoring Service shall continuously monitor authentication behavior.
23. High-risk authentication attempts may require Two-Factor Authentication.
24. Trusted devices may bypass additional verification according to configured security policies.
25. Login Activity History shall record every successful and failed Social Login.
26. Every Social Login event shall generate immutable audit records.
27. Notification Service shall generate login notifications according to configured preferences.
28. Customer Profile Service shall synchronize profile information received from trusted Identity Providers according to configured synchronization policies.
29. Profile synchronization shall never overwrite customer-managed fields without authorization where applicable.
30. Identity Provider profile pictures may be imported according to customer preferences.
31. Authentication sessions shall be created only after successful identity verification.
32. Failed authentication shall never create authenticated sessions.
33. Authentication tokens shall be generated only after successful authorization.
34. Administrative overrides shall require Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).
35. All administrative activities shall remain fully auditable.
36. Customer consent shall be obtained before requesting optional profile information beyond required authentication scopes.
37. Requested OAuth scopes shall follow the principle of least privilege.
38. Access tokens received from Identity Providers shall be securely stored only when operationally required.
39. Refresh tokens shall be encrypted when retained.
40. Identity Provider access tokens shall never be exposed through customer-facing interfaces.
41. Revoked or expired identity tokens shall immediately become invalid.
42. Temporary authentication artifacts shall be securely destroyed after authentication completes.
43. Authentication decisions shall comply with organizational cybersecurity policies.
44. The architecture shall support future integration with additional Identity Providers without major architectural changes.

---

#### 3.1.33.7 Validations

1. Selected Identity Provider shall be supported.
2. Identity Provider shall be active.
3. Identity Provider integration shall be operational.
4. OAuth client credentials shall be valid.
5. Redirect URI shall exactly match registered values.
6. State parameter shall be present.
7. State parameter shall match the generated request.
8. PKCE validation shall succeed where applicable.
9. Authorization code shall be present.
10. Authorization code shall not be expired.
11. Authorization code shall not have been previously consumed.
12. Authorization code shall belong to the current authentication request.
13. ID Token shall be present where required.
14. ID Token signature shall be valid.
15. ID Token issuer shall match trusted configuration.
16. ID Token audience shall match platform client configuration.
17. ID Token expiration time shall be valid.
18. ID Token nonce shall match the original authentication request where applicable.
19. Access Token validation shall succeed where required.
20. Email address shall be verified before account linking where required.
21. Identity Provider User ID shall exist.
22. Identity claims shall be received from trusted providers only.
23. Customer account shall not be suspended.
24. Customer account shall not be permanently blocked.
25. Duplicate account creation shall be prevented.
26. Linked accounts shall belong to the same verified customer identity.
27. Device identifier shall be valid.
28. Browser information shall be captured.
29. IP address shall pass security validation.
30. Fraud Detection Service shall evaluate authentication risk.
31. Security Monitoring Service shall evaluate abnormal login behavior.
32. High-risk authentication shall trigger additional verification where required.
33. Correlation ID shall be generated.
34. Audit logging shall be available before authentication completes.
35. Authentication responses shall never expose OAuth client secrets, Identity Provider credentials, cryptographic keys, or internal implementation details.
36. Sensitive authentication data shall never appear in customer-facing error messages.

---

#### 3.1.33.8 Main Flow

1. Customer selects a supported Social Login provider.
2. Platform creates a secure authentication request.
3. State parameter is generated.
4. PKCE values are generated where applicable.
5. Customer is redirected to the selected Identity Provider.
6. Customer authenticates with the Identity Provider.
7. Identity Provider requests customer consent where required.
8. Customer grants requested permissions.
9. Identity Provider validates customer credentials.
10. Identity Provider generates an authorization code.
11. Customer is redirected back to the configured callback URL.
12. Platform validates the returned state parameter.
13. Platform validates PKCE verification where applicable.
14. Authorization code is exchanged for authentication tokens.
15. Platform validates ID Token cryptographically.
16. Platform validates issuer, audience, expiration, nonce, and signature.
17. Platform retrieves authenticated customer identity claims.
18. Fraud Detection Service evaluates authentication risk.
19. Security Monitoring Service evaluates behavioral indicators.
20. Platform determines whether an existing customer account already exists.
21. Existing customer account is identified using verified identity claims where applicable.
22. Customer account is securely linked where organizational policies permit.
23. New customer account is created when no matching account exists.
24. Customer Profile Service initializes customer profile information.
25. Authentication Service authenticates the customer.
26. Authorization Service assigns customer permissions.
27. Session Management Service creates an authenticated session.
28. Device Management Service registers or updates the customer device.
29. Login Activity History records the authentication event.
30. Notification Service generates login notifications according to configured preferences.
31. Analytics Service records authentication metrics.
32. Audit Logging Service creates immutable audit records.
33. Customer is redirected to the Customer Dashboard or originally requested protected resource.
34. Authentication process completes successfully.


#### 3.1.33.9 Alternate Flows

##### AF-1: Existing Customer Account Found

1. The Identity Provider successfully authenticates the customer.
2. The platform identifies an existing customer account using verified identity claims.
3. The existing customer account is validated.
4. Organizational account-linking policies are evaluated.
5. The social account is securely linked to the existing customer account where permitted.
6. Customer profile synchronization is performed according to configured synchronization policies.
7. Authentication succeeds.
8. Login Activity History is updated.
9. Audit logs are generated.

---

##### AF-2: New Customer Registration

1. No existing customer account is found.
2. The platform validates the identity claims received from the Identity Provider.
3. A new customer account is created.
4. Customer Profile Service initializes the customer profile.
5. Default customer preferences are created.
6. Default privacy settings are initialized.
7. Notification preferences are initialized.
8. Session Management Service creates an authenticated session.
9. Customer is redirected to profile completion or dashboard.

---

##### AF-3: Customer Denies Consent

1. The Identity Provider requests customer consent.
2. Customer declines the requested permissions.
3. The Identity Provider returns an authorization denial.
4. Authentication process is cancelled.
5. No customer session is created.
6. Customer is redirected to the login page.
7. Audit logs are generated.

---

##### AF-4: Invalid Authorization Code

1. Identity Provider returns an invalid authorization code.
2. Platform attempts token exchange.
3. Token exchange fails.
4. Authentication request is rejected.
5. No authenticated session is created.
6. Technical logs are generated.
7. Customer receives an appropriate authentication failure message.

---

##### AF-5: Expired Authorization Code

1. Customer delays completion of the authentication process.
2. Authorization code expires.
3. Token exchange request is rejected by the Identity Provider.
4. Authentication process fails.
5. Customer is requested to initiate authentication again.
6. Audit logs are recorded.

---

##### AF-6: Invalid ID Token

1. ID Token validation begins.
2. Signature verification fails or validation rules are not satisfied.
3. Authentication request is rejected.
4. No authenticated session is created.
5. Fraud Detection Service is notified where applicable.
6. Audit logs are generated.

---

##### AF-7: Identity Provider Unavailable

1. Customer selects a Social Login provider.
2. Platform attempts to redirect the authentication request.
3. Identity Provider is unavailable.
4. Platform retries according to retry policy.
5. Customer is informed that the authentication service is temporarily unavailable.
6. Customer may select another authentication method.
7. Audit records are generated.

---

##### AF-8: Account Linking Required

1. Platform identifies an existing customer account using the same verified email address.
2. Organizational policies require explicit account linking.
3. Customer is requested to verify account ownership.
4. Additional authentication may be performed.
5. Accounts are securely linked.
6. Authentication completes successfully.

---

##### AF-9: Duplicate Account Detected

1. Identity claims match multiple customer records.
2. Authentication process is suspended.
3. Fraud Detection Service evaluates the inconsistency.
4. Customer identity verification is initiated.
5. Duplicate account resolution workflow begins.
6. Authentication completes only after successful verification.

---

##### AF-10: Two-Factor Authentication Required

1. Social Login authentication succeeds.
2. Risk assessment identifies additional verification requirements.
3. Platform initiates Two-Factor Authentication.
4. Customer completes secondary verification.
5. Authenticated session is created.
6. Customer gains access to protected resources.

---

##### AF-11: High-Risk Authentication

1. Fraud Detection Service assigns a high-risk score.
2. Trusted-device bypass is ignored.
3. Additional verification is initiated.
4. Security Monitoring Service begins enhanced monitoring.
5. Authentication proceeds only after successful verification.
6. Security events are recorded.

---

##### AF-12: Customer Cancels Authentication

1. Customer closes the Identity Provider authentication window.
2. OAuth authentication flow is interrupted.
3. Authorization request expires.
4. Platform cancels the authentication session.
5. Customer returns to the login page.
6. Audit logs are generated.

---

##### AF-13: Redirect URI Validation Failure

1. Identity Provider redirects the customer.
2. Redirect URI validation fails.
3. Authentication process immediately stops.
4. Security event is logged.
5. Customer receives a generic authentication error.
6. No authenticated session is created.

---

##### AF-14: State Parameter Validation Failure

1. Authentication response is received.
2. State parameter does not match the original request.
3. Possible CSRF attack is suspected.
4. Authentication request is rejected.
5. Security Monitoring Service is notified.
6. Audit records are generated.

---

##### AF-15: PKCE Validation Failure

1. Token exchange begins.
2. PKCE verification fails.
3. OAuth authentication is rejected.
4. Authentication session is cancelled.
5. Customer receives an authentication failure response.
6. Security logs are generated.

---

##### AF-16: Customer Account Suspended

1. Identity Provider successfully authenticates the customer.
2. Platform identifies the associated customer account.
3. Customer account is suspended.
4. Platform denies authentication.
5. No authenticated session is created.
6. Customer receives an appropriate account status notification.

---

##### AF-17: Internal Authentication Failure

1. Unexpected internal application failure occurs.
2. Authentication workflow terminates safely.
3. Temporary authentication artifacts are securely removed.
4. No authenticated session is created.
5. Technical logs are generated.
6. Customer receives a generic system error message.

---

#### 3.1.33.10 Postconditions

##### Successful Completion

1. Customer identity is successfully verified by the selected Identity Provider.
2. Customer account is successfully authenticated.
3. Existing account is linked where applicable.
4. New customer account is created where applicable.
5. Authenticated customer session is created.
6. Authentication tokens are securely generated.
7. Authorization permissions are assigned.
8. Customer Profile Service is updated.
9. Device Management Service records the authenticated device.
10. Login Activity History records the successful authentication.
11. Fraud Detection Service updates customer risk history.
12. Security Monitoring Service updates behavioral analytics.
13. Notification Service sends login notifications according to configured preferences.
14. Analytics Service records authentication metrics.
15. Immutable audit records are generated.
16. Customer gains access to authorized platform resources.

##### Failed Completion

1. Customer remains unauthenticated.
2. No authenticated session is created.
3. Authentication tokens are not generated.
4. Authorization to protected resources is denied.
5. Login failure is recorded.
6. Fraud Detection Service records authentication outcome where applicable.
7. Security Monitoring Service records abnormal authentication behavior where applicable.
8. Login Activity History records failed authentication.
9. Immutable audit logs are generated.
10. Customer receives an appropriate failure response.
11. Temporary OAuth artifacts are securely destroyed.
12. Platform remains in a secure state.

---

#### 3.1.33.11 Success Response

Upon successful completion of Social Login, the platform shall:

1. Display a successful authentication confirmation.
2. Create an authenticated customer session.
3. Generate secure authentication tokens.
4. Generate refresh tokens where applicable.
5. Redirect the customer to the originally requested protected resource or Customer Dashboard.
6. Update Login Activity History.
7. Update trusted device information where applicable.
8. Synchronize customer profile information according to configured synchronization policies.
9. Generate immutable audit records.
10. Update authentication analytics.
11. Generate configured login notifications.
12. Synchronize authentication status across Authentication Service, Authorization Service, Session Management Service, Customer Profile Service, Device Management Service, Login Activity History Service, Fraud Detection Service, Security Monitoring Service, Analytics Service, Notification Service, and Audit Logging Service.


#### 3.1.33.12 Failure Response

If Social Login cannot be completed successfully, the platform shall:

1. Reject the authentication request.
2. Prevent creation of an authenticated customer session.
3. Prevent generation of authentication tokens.
4. Prevent access to protected platform resources.
5. Display an appropriate customer-friendly error message without exposing internal security implementation details.
6. Record the authentication failure for auditing purposes.
7. Record the failed authentication in Login Activity History.
8. Notify Fraud Detection Service where suspicious behavior is detected.
9. Notify Security Monitoring Service where abnormal authentication behavior is detected.
10. Generate immutable audit records.
11. Trigger security notifications according to configured organizational policies.
12. Continue enforcing rate-limiting and abuse prevention mechanisms.
13. Invalidate temporary OAuth authorization artifacts.
14. Securely discard temporary authentication tokens received during incomplete authentication.
15. Require a new authentication request if the current OAuth session expires or becomes invalid.

---

#### 3.1.33.13 Non-Functional Considerations

1. The Social Login module shall maintain **99.9% or higher availability**, excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
2. The module shall support millions of concurrent authentication requests without unacceptable performance degradation.
3. Authentication workflows shall support horizontal and vertical scalability.
4. OAuth authentication requests shall complete within acceptable production response times under normal operating conditions.
5. All communications between the platform and Identity Providers shall use HTTPS/TLS encryption.
6. OAuth access tokens, refresh tokens, ID tokens, authorization codes, and cryptographic secrets shall never be stored in plaintext.
7. OAuth client secrets shall remain encrypted at rest.
8. Temporary authentication artifacts shall be securely destroyed immediately after authentication completes.
9. Authentication APIs shall implement configurable rate limiting.
10. The module shall protect against CSRF attacks, replay attacks, token substitution attacks, authorization code interception, open redirect vulnerabilities, session hijacking, credential stuffing, phishing attacks, and man-in-the-middle attacks.
11. PKCE shall be supported for public clients wherever applicable.
12. OAuth state validation shall protect against Cross-Site Request Forgery attacks.
13. Only trusted Identity Providers shall be permitted.
14. Identity Provider metadata shall be validated before usage.
15. Authentication secrets shall never appear in logs, API responses, notifications, analytics events, or customer-facing interfaces.
16. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be processed according to applicable privacy regulations.
17. Authentication events shall generate immutable audit records.
18. Audit records shall remain tamper-resistant.
19. Authentication records shall support compliance reporting, investigations, and forensic analysis.
20. Fraud Detection Service shall continuously evaluate authentication risks.
21. Security Monitoring Service shall continuously monitor abnormal authentication behavior.
22. Temporary failures of dependent services shall be handled using retry mechanisms, circuit breakers, timeout policies, fallback strategies, and graceful degradation.
23. Authentication operations shall support distributed tracing using Correlation IDs.
24. Authentication configuration including supported Identity Providers, OAuth scopes, synchronization policies, trusted-device rules, and account-linking behavior shall be configurable without application redeployment wherever possible.
25. Operational metrics shall be collected for monitoring, reporting, observability, analytics, capacity planning, and performance optimization.
26. Backup and disaster recovery mechanisms shall protect authentication configuration, audit records, and customer account-linking information.
27. Administrative access shall be governed by Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).
28. Customer-facing interfaces shall support localization and internationalization.
29. Customer-facing interfaces shall comply with applicable accessibility standards such as WCAG where required.
30. The module shall comply with OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect (OIDC), organizational security policies, ISO 27001 controls where adopted, OWASP Authentication recommendations, privacy regulations, cybersecurity standards, audit requirements, consumer protection laws where applicable, accessibility standards where applicable, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

---

#### 3.1.33.14 Acceptance Criteria

1. Customers shall be able to authenticate using supported Social Login providers.
2. Only supported and trusted Identity Providers shall be accepted.
3. Social Login shall use OAuth 2.0 and/or OpenID Connect (OIDC).
4. Authentication shall occur only at the selected Identity Provider.
5. The Food Delivery Platform shall never request or store customer passwords belonging to third-party Identity Providers.
6. Authorization codes shall be single-use only.
7. Authorization codes shall expire according to configured security policies.
8. OAuth state parameters shall be validated for every authentication request.
9. PKCE validation shall succeed where applicable.
10. Redirect URIs shall exactly match configured values.
11. ID Tokens shall undergo cryptographic signature verification.
12. ID Tokens shall be validated for issuer, audience, expiration, nonce, and signature.
13. Authentication shall fail if any required token validation fails.
14. Verified customer identities shall be securely mapped to platform customer accounts.
15. Existing customer accounts shall be securely linked where permitted by organizational policies.
16. Duplicate customer accounts shall be prevented wherever possible.
17. New customer accounts shall be created only after successful identity verification.
18. Customer profile synchronization shall follow configured synchronization policies.
19. Social Login shall integrate with Two-Factor Authentication where required by organizational security policies.
20. High-risk authentication requests shall trigger additional verification.
21. Fraud Detection Service shall evaluate every authentication request.
22. Security Monitoring Service shall monitor authentication behavior.
23. Login Activity History shall record every successful and failed Social Login.
24. Every authentication event shall generate immutable audit records.
25. Session Management Service shall create authenticated sessions only after successful authentication.
26. Authentication tokens shall only be generated after successful authentication.
27. Notification Service shall generate authentication notifications according to configured preferences.
28. Administrative operations shall require Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).
29. Administrative activities shall remain fully auditable.
30. Sensitive authentication information shall never be exposed through APIs, logs, notifications, analytics events, or customer-facing interfaces.
31. OAuth client secrets shall never be exposed.
32. Operational metrics shall be updated for monitoring, analytics, reporting, observability, performance optimization, and capacity planning.
33. Authentication data shall remain synchronized across Authentication Service, Authorization Service, Customer Profile Service, Session Management Service, Device Management Service, Login Activity History Service, Fraud Detection Service, Security Monitoring Service, Notification Service, Analytics Service, and Audit Logging Service.
34. The Social Login module shall remain available under expected production workloads without unacceptable performance degradation.
35. The Social Login module shall comply with OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect (OIDC), organizational security policies, authentication standards, cybersecurity standards, privacy regulations, audit requirements, consumer protection laws where applicable, accessibility standards where applicable, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

---

### 3.1.34 Customer Account Recovery

#### 3.1.34.1 Description

The Customer Account Recovery module enables customers to securely regain access to their Food Delivery Platform account when they are unable to authenticate using normal login mechanisms. The module provides controlled identity verification workflows that prevent unauthorized account takeover while minimizing customer inconvenience.

The module shall support multiple secure recovery mechanisms based on organizational security policies, customer account configuration, and risk assessment results.

Supported recovery methods may include:

- Password Reset via Email OTP
- Password Reset via SMS OTP
- Two-Factor Authentication Recovery
- Backup Recovery Codes
- Social Login Recovery
- Identity Verification Workflow
- Customer Support Assisted Recovery
- Device-Based Recovery
- Trusted Device Verification (where applicable)

The Customer Account Recovery module shall support recovery scenarios including:

- Forgotten password
- Lost mobile device
- Lost authenticator application
- Forgotten Two-Factor Authentication credentials
- Locked customer account
- Expired authentication credentials
- Compromised customer account
- Suspicious login activity
- Device migration
- Recovery after prolonged inactivity

The module shall integrate with:

- Authentication Service
- Authorization Service
- Identity Management Service
- Customer Profile Service
- Session Management Service
- Device Management Service
- Login Activity History Service
- Two-Factor Authentication Service
- Notification Service
- Fraud Detection Service
- Security Monitoring Service
- Customer Support System
- Analytics Service
- Audit Logging Service

Every recovery request shall undergo identity verification, fraud analysis, security monitoring, audit logging, and compliance validation before recovery is completed.


#### 3.1.34.2 Actors

**Primary Actors**

- Customer
- Registered Customer

**Secondary Actors**

- Authentication Service
- Authorization Service
- Identity Management Service
- Customer Profile Service
- Session Management Service
- Device Management Service
- Login Activity History Service
- Two-Factor Authentication Service
- Notification Service
- SMS Gateway
- Email Service
- Fraud Detection Service
- Security Monitoring Service
- Customer Support Executive
- Customer Support Manager
- Analytics Service
- Audit Logging Service
- System Administrator

---

#### 3.1.34.3 Preconditions

1. The customer account shall exist in the platform.
2. The Authentication Service shall be operational.
3. The Authorization Service shall be operational.
4. The Identity Management Service shall be operational.
5. The Notification Service shall be operational.
6. The Email Service shall be available when email recovery is selected.
7. The SMS Gateway shall be available when SMS recovery is selected.
8. The Two-Factor Authentication Service shall be operational where applicable.
9. The Device Management Service shall be operational.
10. The Fraud Detection Service shall be operational.
11. The Security Monitoring Service shall be operational.
12. The Customer Profile Service shall be operational.
13. The Audit Logging Service shall be operational.
14. The Login Activity History Service shall be operational.
15. The customer shall provide sufficient identity information to initiate recovery.
16. Recovery shall comply with organizational security policies.
17. Recovery shall be permitted for the current customer account status.
18. Customer Support personnel shall possess appropriate permissions for assisted recovery workflows.
19. System clocks shall remain synchronized for OTP verification.
20. Recovery configuration shall be enabled by the platform administrator.

---

#### 3.1.34.4 Trigger

The Customer Account Recovery process may be initiated when one or more of the following events occur:

1. Customer selects **Forgot Password**.
2. Customer forgets login password.
3. Customer loses registered mobile device.
4. Customer loses access to registered email.
5. Customer cannot access the Authenticator Application.
6. Customer loses Two-Factor Authentication device.
7. Customer exhausts all backup recovery codes.
8. Customer account becomes temporarily locked.
9. Customer detects suspicious login activity.
10. Customer believes the account has been compromised.
11. Fraud Detection Service recommends credential reset.
12. Security Monitoring Service identifies possible account takeover.
13. Customer migrates to a new device.
14. Customer requests assistance from Customer Support.
15. Administrator initiates recovery under approved security procedures.

---

#### 3.1.34.5 Input Fields

| Field | Description | Mandatory |
|--------|-------------|-----------|
| Username / Email / Mobile Number | Customer account identifier | Yes |
| Recovery Method | Selected recovery option | Yes |
| Email OTP | OTP received via email | Conditional |
| SMS OTP | OTP received via SMS | Conditional |
| Authenticator Code | TOTP generated by authenticator application | Conditional |
| Backup Recovery Code | Emergency recovery code | Conditional |
| New Password | New customer password | Conditional |
| Confirm New Password | Password confirmation | Conditional |
| Identity Verification Information | Additional verification details | Conditional |
| Device Identifier | Current device identifier | Yes |
| Browser Information | Browser metadata | Yes |
| Operating System | Client operating system | Yes |
| IP Address | Client IP address | System Generated |
| Location Information | Estimated customer location | System Generated |
| Correlation ID | Distributed request identifier | System Generated |
| Recovery Session ID | Recovery workflow session identifier | System Generated |
| Recovery Timestamp | Recovery initiation timestamp | System Generated |

---

#### 3.1.34.6 Business Rules

1. Every recovery request shall begin with customer identity verification.
2. Account recovery shall not bypass organizational security policies.
3. Password reset shall require successful identity verification.
4. Recovery methods shall be configurable by administrators.
5. Only enabled recovery methods shall be available to customers.
6. Recovery OTPs shall be cryptographically secure.
7. Recovery OTPs shall have configurable expiration periods.
8. Expired OTPs shall become permanently invalid.
9. Used OTPs shall never be reusable.
10. Maximum OTP verification attempts shall be configurable.
11. Maximum OTP resend attempts shall be configurable.
12. Exceeding configured verification limits may temporarily suspend recovery.
13. Fraud Detection Service shall evaluate every recovery request.
14. Security Monitoring Service shall continuously evaluate recovery behavior.
15. High-risk recovery requests may require additional identity verification.
16. Trusted devices may simplify recovery according to configured organizational policies.
17. Password reset links shall be single-use only.
18. Password reset links shall expire according to configured policies.
19. Backup recovery codes shall be single-use only.
20. Regenerating recovery codes shall invalidate all previous recovery codes.
21. Customer Support assisted recovery shall require identity verification and elevated authorization.
22. Administrative recovery shall require Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).
23. Every successful recovery shall invalidate all active authentication sessions unless organizational policy specifies otherwise.
24. Every successful password reset shall invalidate existing authentication tokens.
25. New authentication tokens shall only be issued after successful authentication.
26. Customers shall be notified whenever recovery activities occur.
27. Login Activity History shall record every recovery attempt.
28. Every recovery event shall generate immutable audit records.
29. Customer Profile Service shall update recovery timestamps.
30. Analytics Service shall record recovery metrics.
31. Recovery information shall never expose confidential customer data.
32. Temporary recovery artifacts shall be securely removed after completion.
33. Authentication secrets shall never be stored in plaintext.
34. Customer passwords shall always be stored using approved password hashing algorithms.
35. Recovery decisions shall comply with organizational cybersecurity policies.
36. The architecture shall support future recovery mechanisms without major architectural changes.

---

#### 3.1.34.7 Validations

1. Customer account shall exist.
2. Customer account shall be active or eligible for recovery.
3. Recovery request shall belong to the identified customer.
4. Selected recovery method shall be enabled.
5. Recovery session shall exist.
6. Recovery session shall not be expired.
7. Recovery session shall not already be completed.
8. Recovery session shall not be cancelled.
9. OTP shall not be null.
10. OTP shall not be empty.
11. OTP shall match the generated recovery challenge.
12. OTP shall not be expired.
13. OTP shall not have been previously used.
14. Password reset token shall be valid.
15. Password reset token shall not be expired.
16. Password reset token shall be single-use only.
17. New password shall satisfy configured password policies.
18. New password and confirmation password shall match.
19. New password shall not match recent password history according to configured security policies.
20. Backup recovery code shall be valid.
21. Backup recovery code shall not have been previously consumed.
22. Verification attempts shall remain within configured limits.
23. Recovery requests shall comply with API rate-limiting policies.
24. Fraud Detection Service shall evaluate recovery risk.
25. Security Monitoring Service shall evaluate abnormal recovery behavior.
26. Device information shall be validated.
27. IP address shall pass security validation.
28. Correlation ID shall be generated.
29. Audit logging shall be available.
30. Sensitive recovery information shall never appear in customer-facing error messages.

---

#### 3.1.34.8 Main Flow

1. Customer selects **Forgot Password** or another supported recovery option.
2. Platform requests customer account identification.
3. Customer provides username, email address, or registered mobile number.
4. Platform validates the customer account.
5. Fraud Detection Service performs initial risk assessment.
6. Security Monitoring Service evaluates recovery behavior.
7. Platform displays supported recovery methods.
8. Customer selects the preferred recovery method.
9. Platform creates a secure recovery session.
10. Correlation ID is generated.
11. Recovery challenge is generated.
12. Recovery challenge is securely delivered using the selected communication channel.
13. Customer receives the recovery challenge.
14. Customer submits the required verification information.
15. Platform validates the submitted verification data.
16. Identity verification succeeds.
17. Platform requests the customer to create a new password where applicable.
18. Customer enters a new password.
19. Password policy validation succeeds.
20. Customer password is securely updated.
21. Existing authentication tokens are invalidated.
22. Existing authenticated sessions are terminated according to configured organizational policies.
23. Login Activity History records the successful recovery.
24. Customer Profile Service updates recovery information.
25. Notification Service sends recovery confirmation.
26. Analytics Service records recovery metrics.
27. Audit Logging Service creates immutable audit records.
28. Customer is redirected to the login page.
29. Customer authenticates using the newly established credentials.
30. Account recovery process completes successfully.


#### 3.1.34.9 Alternate Flows

##### AF-1: Invalid Recovery OTP

1. Customer enters an incorrect OTP.
2. Platform validates the submitted OTP.
3. OTP verification fails.
4. Remaining verification attempts are updated.
5. Customer is informed that the verification code is invalid.
6. Customer may retry while verification attempts remain.
7. Recovery session remains active.

---

##### AF-2: Recovery OTP Expired

1. Customer submits an expired OTP.
2. Platform detects OTP expiration.
3. Verification request is rejected.
4. Customer is informed that the OTP has expired.
5. Customer requests a new OTP.
6. Previous OTP becomes permanently invalid.
7. Recovery process continues using the new OTP.

---

##### AF-3: Maximum Verification Attempts Exceeded

1. Customer repeatedly enters incorrect verification codes.
2. Verification attempt counter reaches the configured limit.
3. Recovery session is temporarily blocked.
4. Fraud Detection Service is notified.
5. Security Monitoring Service records the suspicious activity.
6. Customer receives an account protection notification.
7. Recovery must be restarted after the configured restriction period.

---

##### AF-4: Recovery OTP Resend

1. Customer selects **Resend OTP**.
2. Platform validates resend eligibility.
3. Rate-limiting policies are evaluated.
4. Previous OTP is invalidated according to configured policy.
5. A new OTP is generated.
6. OTP validity duration is reset.
7. Notification Service delivers the new OTP.
8. Customer continues the recovery process.

---

##### AF-5: Password Policy Validation Failure

1. Customer enters a new password.
2. Platform validates password complexity.
3. Password fails one or more policy requirements.
4. Password update is rejected.
5. Customer receives validation guidance.
6. Customer submits another password.
7. Recovery process continues.

---

##### AF-6: Password Confirmation Mismatch

1. Customer enters the new password.
2. Customer enters the confirmation password.
3. The entered passwords do not match.
4. Platform rejects the password update.
5. Customer is informed of the mismatch.
6. Customer re-enters both password fields.

---

##### AF-7: Password Reuse Detected

1. Customer enters a previously used password.
2. Password history validation begins.
3. Platform identifies password reuse.
4. Password update is rejected.
5. Customer is requested to choose a different password.
6. Recovery process remains active.

---

##### AF-8: Backup Recovery Code Used

1. Customer selects Backup Recovery Code.
2. Platform requests the recovery code.
3. Customer enters the code.
4. Platform validates the recovery code.
5. Recovery code is marked as consumed.
6. Identity verification succeeds.
7. Customer continues password reset.

---

##### AF-9: Invalid Backup Recovery Code

1. Customer enters an invalid recovery code.
2. Platform rejects the submitted code.
3. Remaining verification attempts are updated.
4. Customer is informed that the recovery code is invalid.
5. Customer may retry if attempts remain.
6. Audit logs are generated.

---

##### AF-10: Customer Support Assisted Recovery

1. Customer cannot complete self-service recovery.
2. Customer contacts Customer Support.
3. Support Executive verifies customer identity.
4. Additional verification procedures are completed.
5. Support Executive receives managerial approval where required.
6. Recovery workflow is initiated.
7. Customer completes password reset.
8. Audit logs capture every administrative activity.

---

##### AF-11: Social Login Recovery

1. Customer selects a linked Social Login provider.
2. Identity Provider authenticates the customer.
3. Platform validates the returned identity claims.
4. Customer identity is confirmed.
5. Recovery workflow proceeds.
6. Customer regains account access.

---

##### AF-12: Two-Factor Authentication Recovery

1. Customer chooses Two-Factor Authentication recovery.
2. Platform initiates secondary verification.
3. Customer successfully completes verification.
4. Identity is confirmed.
5. Password reset workflow continues.
6. Recovery completes successfully.

---

##### AF-13: High-Risk Recovery Request

1. Fraud Detection Service assigns a high-risk score.
2. Additional identity verification is required.
3. Security Monitoring Service begins enhanced monitoring.
4. Customer completes enhanced verification.
5. Recovery proceeds only after successful verification.
6. Security events are logged.

---

##### AF-14: Suspicious Account Takeover Attempt

1. Security Monitoring Service detects abnormal recovery behavior.
2. Recovery request is immediately suspended.
3. Customer account is temporarily protected.
4. Customer receives a security notification.
5. Security team may be alerted.
6. Recovery is denied until manual verification is completed.

---

##### AF-15: Recovery Session Expired

1. Customer delays completing recovery.
2. Recovery session expires.
3. Submitted verification data is rejected.
4. Customer is informed that the session has expired.
5. Customer initiates a new recovery request.
6. Expired session is archived for audit purposes.

---

##### AF-16: Notification Service Failure

1. Recovery OTP generation succeeds.
2. Notification Service fails to deliver the OTP.
3. Platform retries delivery according to retry policies.
4. Customer may select another recovery method.
5. Recovery remains pending.
6. Audit logs are generated.

---

##### AF-17: Email Service Failure

1. Platform attempts email delivery.
2. Email delivery fails.
3. Platform retries according to configured retry policy.
4. Customer may choose SMS recovery if available.
5. Recovery continues using the selected method.
6. Failure is recorded.

---

##### AF-18: SMS Gateway Failure

1. Platform attempts SMS OTP delivery.
2. SMS Gateway is unavailable.
3. Retry attempts are performed.
4. Customer may switch to email recovery.
5. Recovery process continues.
6. Operational logs are generated.

---

##### AF-19: Customer Cancels Recovery

1. Customer cancels the recovery process.
2. Recovery session is terminated.
3. Temporary recovery artifacts are securely removed.
4. Generated OTPs become invalid.
5. Audit logs are created.
6. Customer returns to the login page.

---

##### AF-20: Internal System Failure

1. Unexpected application failure occurs during recovery.
2. Recovery workflow is safely terminated.
3. No password changes are applied.
4. Temporary recovery information is securely removed.
5. Technical logs are generated.
6. Customer receives a generic system error message.

---

#### 3.1.34.10 Postconditions

##### Successful Completion

1. Customer identity is successfully verified.
2. Customer password is securely updated where applicable.
3. Existing authentication tokens are invalidated.
4. Active customer sessions are terminated according to organizational policies.
5. Recovery session is completed.
6. Customer Profile Service updates recovery information.
7. Login Activity History records the successful recovery.
8. Fraud Detection Service updates recovery history.
9. Security Monitoring Service updates behavioral analytics.
10. Notification Service sends recovery confirmation.
11. Analytics Service records recovery metrics.
12. Immutable audit logs are generated.
13. Customer regains access to the account after successful authentication.

##### Failed Completion

1. Customer identity remains unverified.
2. Password remains unchanged.
3. Existing authentication tokens remain governed by current security policies.
4. No authenticated session is created.
5. Recovery session is terminated or expires.
6. Failed recovery attempt is recorded.
7. Fraud Detection Service updates customer risk indicators.
8. Security Monitoring Service records suspicious behavior where applicable.
9. Login Activity History records the failed recovery attempt.
10. Immutable audit logs are generated.
11. Customer receives an appropriate recovery failure response.
12. Platform remains in a secure state.

---

#### 3.1.34.11 Success Response

Upon successful completion of Customer Account Recovery, the platform shall:

1. Display a confirmation indicating successful account recovery.
2. Confirm that the customer password has been successfully updated where applicable.
3. Confirm that previous authentication sessions have been invalidated according to configured organizational policies.
4. Redirect the customer to the login page or authentication workflow.
5. Generate recovery confirmation notifications according to configured notification preferences.
6. Record the successful recovery in Login Activity History.
7. Update Customer Profile recovery information.
8. Generate immutable audit records.
9. Update operational analytics.
10. Synchronize recovery status across Authentication Service, Authorization Service, Customer Profile Service, Session Management Service, Device Management Service, Login Activity History Service, Two-Factor Authentication Service, Fraud Detection Service, Security Monitoring Service, Notification Service, Analytics Service, and Audit Logging Service.


#### 3.1.34.12 Failure Response

If Customer Account Recovery cannot be completed successfully, the platform shall:

1. Reject the recovery request.
2. Prevent unauthorized password reset.
3. Prevent creation of an authenticated customer session.
4. Preserve existing account security controls.
5. Display an appropriate customer-friendly error message without exposing internal security implementation details.
6. Record the recovery failure in Login Activity History.
7. Generate immutable audit records.
8. Notify Fraud Detection Service where suspicious recovery behavior is detected.
9. Notify Security Monitoring Service where abnormal recovery behavior is identified.
10. Trigger configured security notifications according to organizational security policies.
11. Continue enforcing rate-limiting and abuse prevention controls.
12. Invalidate expired recovery sessions and temporary recovery artifacts.
13. Require the customer to initiate a new recovery request when necessary.
14. Preserve platform security posture throughout the failed recovery process.

---

#### 3.1.34.13 Non-Functional Considerations

1. The Customer Account Recovery module shall maintain **99.9% or higher availability**, excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
2. The module shall support millions of concurrent recovery requests without unacceptable performance degradation.
3. Recovery workflows shall support horizontal and vertical scalability.
4. Recovery requests shall complete within acceptable production response times under expected workloads.
5. All recovery communications shall use HTTPS/TLS encryption.
6. Recovery OTPs shall be generated using cryptographically secure random number generators.
7. Password reset tokens shall be cryptographically secure and single-use.
8. Password reset URLs shall expire according to configured organizational policies.
9. Customer passwords shall always be stored using approved password hashing algorithms with unique salts.
10. Authentication secrets, recovery tokens, OTPs, backup recovery codes, cryptographic keys, and session secrets shall never be stored in plaintext.
11. Temporary recovery artifacts shall be securely destroyed immediately after recovery completion.
12. Recovery APIs shall enforce configurable rate limiting.
13. The module shall protect against brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, replay attacks, phishing attacks, OTP guessing, account takeover attacks, session hijacking, password spraying, and denial-of-service attacks.
14. Fraud Detection Service shall continuously evaluate recovery requests.
15. Security Monitoring Service shall continuously monitor abnormal recovery behavior.
16. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be processed according to applicable privacy regulations.
17. Every recovery request shall generate immutable audit records.
18. Audit records shall remain tamper-resistant and support forensic investigations.
19. Recovery operations shall support distributed tracing using Correlation IDs.
20. Recovery configuration including OTP validity, retry limits, password policies, recovery methods, and risk assessment rules shall be configurable without application redeployment wherever possible.
21. Operational metrics shall be collected for monitoring, reporting, observability, analytics, performance optimization, and capacity planning.
22. Temporary failures of dependent services shall be handled using retries, timeout policies, circuit breakers, fallback mechanisms, and graceful degradation.
23. Backup and disaster recovery mechanisms shall protect recovery configuration, audit records, customer recovery history, and security metadata.
24. Administrative recovery operations shall be governed by Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).
25. Administrative activities shall remain fully auditable.
26. Customer-facing interfaces shall support localization and internationalization.
27. Customer-facing interfaces shall comply with applicable accessibility standards such as WCAG where required.
28. The module shall comply with organizational security policies, password management standards, cybersecurity standards, privacy regulations, ISO 27001 controls where adopted, OWASP Authentication recommendations, audit requirements, consumer protection laws where applicable, accessibility standards where applicable, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

---

#### 3.1.34.14 Acceptance Criteria

1. Customers shall be able to initiate account recovery using supported recovery methods.
2. Only existing customer accounts shall be eligible for recovery.
3. Customer identity shall be verified before recovery is completed.
4. Recovery shall never bypass organizational security policies.
5. Recovery OTPs shall be cryptographically secure.
6. Recovery OTPs shall expire according to configured organizational policies.
7. Expired OTPs shall be rejected.
8. Used OTPs shall never be accepted again.
9. Password reset tokens shall be single-use only.
10. Expired password reset links shall be rejected.
11. Password reset shall require successful identity verification.
12. New passwords shall satisfy configured password policies.
13. New passwords shall not match restricted password history according to configured organizational policies.
14. Password confirmation shall match the entered password.
15. Existing authentication tokens shall be invalidated after successful password reset.
16. Existing authenticated sessions shall be terminated according to configured organizational policies.
17. Login Activity History shall record every successful recovery.
18. Login Activity History shall record every failed recovery.
19. Fraud Detection Service shall evaluate every recovery request.
20. Security Monitoring Service shall monitor recovery behavior.
21. Notification Service shall generate recovery notifications according to configured customer preferences.
22. Backup recovery codes shall be single-use only.
23. Regeneration of backup recovery codes shall invalidate all previously generated recovery codes.
24. Administrative recovery shall require Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).
25. Administrative activities shall remain fully auditable.
26. Every recovery event shall generate immutable audit records.
27. Sensitive recovery information shall never be exposed through APIs, logs, notifications, analytics events, or customer-facing interfaces.
28. Operational metrics shall be updated for monitoring, reporting, observability, analytics, performance optimization, and capacity planning.
29. Recovery information shall remain synchronized across Authentication Service, Authorization Service, Customer Profile Service, Session Management Service, Device Management Service, Login Activity History Service, Two-Factor Authentication Service, Fraud Detection Service, Security Monitoring Service, Notification Service, Analytics Service, Customer Support System, and Audit Logging Service.
30. The Customer Account Recovery module shall remain available under expected production workloads without unacceptable performance degradation.
31. The Customer Account Recovery module shall comply with organizational security policies, password management standards, cybersecurity standards, privacy regulations, audit requirements, consumer protection laws where applicable, accessibility standards where applicable, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

---

### 3.1.35 Customer Dashboard

#### 3.1.35.1 Description

The Customer Dashboard serves as the centralized landing page for authenticated customers after successful login. It provides a unified view of customer information, account status, ongoing orders, recent activities, personalized recommendations, loyalty benefits, wallet balance, notifications, saved addresses, favorite restaurants, offers, and quick access to frequently used platform features.

The Customer Dashboard is designed to enhance customer engagement by providing real-time information, personalized experiences, operational transparency, and convenient navigation across all platform services.

The dashboard shall dynamically display information based on:

- Customer profile
- Active orders
- Previous orders
- Delivery status
- Saved addresses
- Favorite restaurants
- Recently viewed restaurants
- Wallet balance
- Loyalty points
- Coupons
- Promotional campaigns
- Personalized recommendations
- Notification preferences
- Privacy settings
- Language preferences
- Customer location
- Device capabilities
- Organizational business rules

The dashboard shall integrate with:

- Customer Profile Service
- Authentication Service
- Authorization Service
- Order Management Service
- Restaurant Service
- Menu Service
- Delivery Service
- Wallet Service
- Loyalty Points Service
- Coupon Service
- Recommendation Service
- Notification Service
- Customer Preferences Service
- Address Management Service
- Favorites Service
- Analytics Service
- Fraud Detection Service
- Audit Logging Service

The dashboard shall provide customers with quick access to:

- Place New Order
- Search Restaurants
- Browse Menus
- Track Active Orders
- View Order History
- Manage Wallet
- Redeem Loyalty Points
- View Coupons
- Manage Saved Addresses
- Manage Saved Payment Methods
- Update Customer Profile
- Configure Account Settings
- Contact Customer Support
- View Notifications
- Access Privacy Settings
- View Rewards
- Logout

The dashboard shall continuously refresh dynamic information such as order status, delivery progress, wallet balance, loyalty points, promotional offers, and notifications according to configured synchronization policies while maintaining optimal performance and minimizing unnecessary network usage.


#### 3.1.35.2 Actors

**Primary Actors**

- Customer
- Registered Customer

**Secondary Actors**

- Authentication Service
- Authorization Service
- Customer Profile Service
- Order Management Service
- Restaurant Service
- Menu Service
- Delivery Service
- Wallet Service
- Loyalty Points Service
- Coupon Service
- Recommendation Service
- Notification Service
- Customer Preferences Service
- Address Management Service
- Favorites Service
- Analytics Service
- Fraud Detection Service
- Audit Logging Service
- System Administrator

---

#### 3.1.35.3 Preconditions

1. Customer shall possess a registered account.
2. Customer shall be successfully authenticated.
3. Customer session shall be active.
4. Customer account shall be active.
5. Authentication Service shall be operational.
6. Authorization Service shall successfully authorize dashboard access.
7. Customer Profile Service shall be operational.
8. Order Management Service shall be operational.
9. Restaurant Service shall be operational.
10. Delivery Service shall be operational.
11. Wallet Service shall be operational.
12. Loyalty Points Service shall be operational.
13. Coupon Service shall be operational.
14. Recommendation Service shall be operational.
15. Notification Service shall be operational.
16. Customer Preferences Service shall be operational.
17. Address Management Service shall be operational.
18. Favorites Service shall be operational.
19. Analytics Service shall be operational.
20. Audit Logging Service shall be operational.
21. Fraud Detection Service shall be operational.
22. Customer shall possess permission to access dashboard resources.
23. Required backend services shall be available according to service dependency policies.
24. Dashboard configuration shall be enabled.
25. Customer language preferences shall be available where applicable.

---

#### 3.1.35.4 Trigger

The Customer Dashboard shall be displayed when one or more of the following events occur:

1. Customer successfully logs into the platform.
2. Customer selects **Home**.
3. Customer opens the mobile application.
4. Customer accesses the web application.
5. Customer refreshes the dashboard.
6. Customer returns from another platform module.
7. Customer completes an order.
8. Customer completes payment.
9. Customer redeems loyalty points.
10. Customer receives new notifications.
11. Customer updates profile information.
12. Customer updates saved addresses.
13. Customer updates payment methods.
14. Customer returns after session restoration.
15. Customer selects Dashboard from the navigation menu.

---

#### 3.1.35.5 Input Fields

| Field | Description | Mandatory |
|--------|-------------|-----------|
| Customer ID | Authenticated customer identifier | System Generated |
| Session ID | Active authenticated session | System Generated |
| Dashboard Refresh Request | Indicates manual refresh | No |
| Device Identifier | Customer device identifier | Yes |
| Browser Information | Browser metadata | Yes |
| Operating System | Client operating system | Yes |
| Preferred Language | Customer language preference | System Generated |
| Preferred Currency | Customer currency preference | System Generated |
| Customer Location | Current customer location | Conditional |
| GPS Coordinates | Current location coordinates | Conditional |
| Notification Status | Notification synchronization status | System Generated |
| Correlation ID | Distributed request identifier | System Generated |
| Dashboard Timestamp | Dashboard generation timestamp | System Generated |

---

#### 3.1.35.6 Business Rules

1. Dashboard access shall require successful authentication.
2. Dashboard information shall be personalized for the authenticated customer.
3. Customers shall only view their own information.
4. Dashboard shall display real-time data where applicable.
5. Dashboard shall display only authorized information.
6. Customer Profile information shall be retrieved from Customer Profile Service.
7. Active orders shall be retrieved from Order Management Service.
8. Delivery status shall be retrieved from Delivery Service.
9. Wallet balance shall be retrieved from Wallet Service.
10. Loyalty points shall be retrieved from Loyalty Points Service.
11. Available coupons shall be retrieved from Coupon Service.
12. Personalized recommendations shall be generated by Recommendation Service.
13. Favorite restaurants shall be retrieved from Favorites Service.
14. Saved addresses shall be retrieved from Address Management Service.
15. Notifications shall be retrieved from Notification Service.
16. Dashboard content shall respect customer privacy settings.
17. Dashboard content shall respect language preferences.
18. Dashboard content shall respect notification preferences.
19. Dashboard layout may be personalized according to customer preferences.
20. Promotional banners shall be displayed according to active campaigns.
21. Promotional campaigns shall comply with business eligibility rules.
22. Personalized recommendations shall consider customer order history where permitted.
23. Recommendation algorithms shall respect customer privacy preferences.
24. Dashboard widgets shall support configurable visibility.
25. Administrators may enable or disable dashboard widgets.
26. Dashboard shall continue operating even if non-critical services become temporarily unavailable.
27. Partial dashboard rendering shall be supported during dependent service failures.
28. Cached dashboard information may be displayed where organizational policies permit.
29. Dashboard refresh intervals shall be configurable.
30. Dashboard shall synchronize dynamic information without requiring full page reload wherever possible.
31. Every dashboard access shall generate audit records where required.
32. Dashboard analytics shall be collected for usage reporting.
33. Fraud Detection Service shall evaluate abnormal dashboard access behavior.
34. Dashboard shall support localization and internationalization.
35. Dashboard shall support future dashboard widgets without major architectural changes.

---

#### 3.1.35.7 Validations

1. Customer session shall exist.
2. Customer session shall be active.
3. Customer session shall not be expired.
4. Customer account shall exist.
5. Customer account shall be active.
6. Customer account shall not be suspended.
7. Customer shall possess authorization to access the dashboard.
8. Customer identifier shall match the authenticated session.
9. Dashboard request shall belong to the authenticated customer.
10. Requested dashboard widgets shall be enabled.
11. Customer preferences shall be validated before personalization.
12. Language preference shall be supported.
13. Currency preference shall be supported.
14. Location information shall be validated where location-based services are enabled.
15. Dashboard requests shall comply with API rate-limiting policies.
16. Correlation ID shall be generated.
17. Required backend services shall be available according to dependency policies.
18. Fraud Detection Service shall evaluate suspicious dashboard access.
19. Security Monitoring mechanisms shall validate abnormal access behavior where applicable.
20. Sensitive customer information shall never be exposed through unauthorized dashboard widgets.
21. Dashboard responses shall exclude confidential internal implementation details.
22. Customer data displayed on the dashboard shall comply with configured privacy policies.

---

#### 3.1.35.8 Main Flow

1. Customer successfully authenticates.
2. Authentication Service validates the active session.
3. Authorization Service authorizes dashboard access.
4. Dashboard request is initiated.
5. Correlation ID is generated.
6. Customer Profile Service retrieves customer profile information.
7. Order Management Service retrieves active orders.
8. Order History information is retrieved.
9. Delivery Service retrieves current delivery status.
10. Wallet Service retrieves wallet balance.
11. Loyalty Points Service retrieves current loyalty point balance.
12. Coupon Service retrieves available coupons.
13. Favorites Service retrieves favorite restaurants.
14. Address Management Service retrieves saved delivery addresses.
15. Recommendation Service generates personalized recommendations.
16. Notification Service retrieves unread notifications.
17. Customer Preferences Service retrieves dashboard preferences.
18. Language preferences are applied.
19. Privacy settings are applied.
20. Notification preferences are applied.
21. Dashboard widgets are assembled.
22. Promotional banners are evaluated.
23. Business rules determine widget visibility.
24. Dashboard layout is generated.
25. Analytics Service records dashboard access metrics.
26. Audit Logging Service generates audit records where applicable.
27. Fraud Detection Service evaluates access behavior.
28. Dashboard response is returned.
29. Customer views the personalized dashboard.
30. Dashboard remains synchronized according to configured refresh policies.


#### 3.1.35.9 Alternate Flows

##### AF-1: No Active Orders

1. Customer accesses the Customer Dashboard.
2. Order Management Service determines that no active orders exist.
3. Dashboard displays a **No Active Orders** widget.
4. Recently completed orders are displayed where available.
5. Personalized restaurant recommendations are presented.
6. Customer is encouraged to place a new order.

---

##### AF-2: No Previous Orders

1. Customer is a newly registered customer.
2. Order History is empty.
3. Dashboard displays a welcome message.
4. Personalized onboarding tips are displayed.
5. Popular restaurants are recommended.
6. Introductory promotional offers are displayed.

---

##### AF-3: Wallet Service Unavailable

1. Dashboard requests wallet information.
2. Wallet Service is temporarily unavailable.
3. Dashboard displays cached wallet information where permitted.
4. If cached data is unavailable, the wallet widget displays a temporary service message.
5. Remaining dashboard widgets continue loading.
6. Operational logs are generated.

---

##### AF-4: Loyalty Points Service Failure

1. Dashboard requests loyalty point information.
2. Loyalty Points Service becomes unavailable.
3. Dashboard displays cached loyalty points where permitted.
4. If cached information is unavailable, the loyalty widget displays a temporary service notification.
5. Remaining dashboard components continue functioning.
6. Audit and operational logs are updated.

---

##### AF-5: Recommendation Service Failure

1. Dashboard requests personalized recommendations.
2. Recommendation Service is unavailable.
3. Platform displays popular restaurants instead.
4. Trending dishes may be displayed.
5. Customer experience continues without interruption.
6. Analytics records the fallback usage.

---

##### AF-6: Notification Service Failure

1. Dashboard requests customer notifications.
2. Notification Service becomes temporarily unavailable.
3. Notification widget displays an unavailable status.
4. Remaining dashboard information loads successfully.
5. Customer continues using the dashboard.
6. Technical logs are recorded.

---

##### AF-7: Customer Refreshes Dashboard

1. Customer selects **Refresh Dashboard**.
2. Platform validates the active session.
3. Dashboard widgets request the latest information.
4. Updated information replaces cached data.
5. Dashboard refresh completes successfully.
6. Analytics records the refresh event.

---

##### AF-8: Customer Location Changed

1. Platform detects customer location change.
2. Restaurant availability is recalculated.
3. Nearby restaurants are refreshed.
4. Delivery availability is updated.
5. Promotional campaigns are recalculated.
6. Dashboard reflects location-specific information.

---

##### AF-9: Session Expired

1. Customer attempts to access the dashboard.
2. Session Management Service determines that the session has expired.
3. Dashboard access is denied.
4. Customer receives a session expiration notification.
5. Customer is redirected to the login page.
6. Login Activity History is updated.

---

##### AF-10: Unauthorized Dashboard Request

1. An unauthorized request attempts to access the dashboard.
2. Authorization Service rejects the request.
3. Dashboard is not generated.
4. Security Monitoring records the event.
5. Audit logs are created.
6. Access is denied.

---

##### AF-11: Privacy Settings Restrict Dashboard Data

1. Customer has configured restrictive privacy settings.
2. Dashboard generation begins.
3. Privacy policies are evaluated.
4. Restricted customer information is hidden.
5. Remaining dashboard widgets continue rendering.
6. Dashboard respects customer privacy preferences.

---

##### AF-12: Dashboard Personalization Disabled

1. Customer disables personalization.
2. Dashboard generation begins.
3. Personalized recommendations are skipped.
4. Generic recommendations are displayed.
5. Dashboard continues normally.
6. Customer preferences are respected.

---

##### AF-13: Partial Backend Service Failure

1. Dashboard requests information from multiple services.
2. One or more non-critical backend services become unavailable.
3. Remaining services continue responding.
4. Dashboard renders available information.
5. Failed widgets display temporary service messages.
6. Customer continues using available dashboard features.

---

##### AF-14: Fraud Detection Flags Dashboard Access

1. Fraud Detection Service identifies suspicious dashboard access.
2. Risk score exceeds configured threshold.
3. Additional verification may be initiated.
4. Dashboard access is temporarily restricted where required.
5. Security Monitoring Service records the event.
6. Audit logs are generated.

---

##### AF-15: Internal System Failure

1. Unexpected internal application failure occurs.
2. Dashboard generation is safely terminated.
3. Partial responses are discarded.
4. Customer receives a generic error message.
5. Technical logs are generated.
6. Platform remains secure.

---

#### 3.1.35.10 Postconditions

##### Successful Completion

1. Customer Dashboard is successfully generated.
2. Customer receives personalized dashboard information.
3. Active session remains valid.
4. Customer Profile information is displayed.
5. Active order information is updated.
6. Delivery status is synchronized.
7. Wallet balance is displayed.
8. Loyalty points are displayed.
9. Available coupons are displayed.
10. Notifications are synchronized.
11. Personalized recommendations are displayed where applicable.
12. Dashboard analytics are updated.
13. Fraud Detection Service records dashboard access.
14. Audit logs are generated where required.

##### Failed Completion

1. Dashboard is not fully generated.
2. Customer session remains governed by existing authentication policies.
3. Partial dashboard information may be displayed where permitted.
4. Failed service requests are logged.
5. Customer receives appropriate service notifications.
6. Fraud Detection Service records abnormal behavior where applicable.
7. Analytics records dashboard failure metrics.
8. Audit records are generated.
9. Platform remains operational and secure.

---

#### 3.1.35.11 Success Response

Upon successful dashboard generation, the platform shall:

1. Display the personalized Customer Dashboard.
2. Display customer profile summary.
3. Display active order information where applicable.
4. Display delivery tracking information where applicable.
5. Display wallet balance.
6. Display loyalty points.
7. Display available coupons and promotional offers.
8. Display favorite restaurants.
9. Display personalized restaurant recommendations.
10. Display unread notifications.
11. Display saved delivery addresses where applicable.
12. Display quick navigation shortcuts.
13. Update dashboard analytics.
14. Generate audit records where required.
15. Synchronize dashboard information across Customer Profile Service, Order Management Service, Delivery Service, Wallet Service, Loyalty Points Service, Coupon Service, Recommendation Service, Notification Service, Address Management Service, Favorites Service, Analytics Service, Fraud Detection Service, and Audit Logging Service.

---

#### 3.1.35.12 Failure Response

If the Customer Dashboard cannot be generated successfully, the platform shall:

1. Display an appropriate customer-friendly error message without exposing internal implementation details.
2. Prevent unauthorized dashboard access.
3. Continue displaying available dashboard information where partial rendering is supported.
4. Record dashboard generation failures.
5. Generate immutable audit records where required.
6. Notify operational monitoring systems.
7. Trigger fallback mechanisms for unavailable backend services where applicable.
8. Preserve customer session integrity.
9. Continue enforcing authorization and security policies.
10. Maintain overall platform security throughout the failure.


#### 3.1.35.13 Non-Functional Considerations

1. The Customer Dashboard module shall maintain **99.9% or higher availability**, excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
2. The dashboard shall support millions of concurrent authenticated customers without unacceptable performance degradation.
3. Dashboard generation shall support horizontal and vertical scalability.
4. Initial dashboard loading shall complete within acceptable production response times under expected workloads.
5. Individual dashboard widgets shall support asynchronous loading to improve perceived responsiveness.
6. Dashboard services shall support lazy loading for non-critical widgets.
7. Frequently accessed dashboard data may be cached according to configured cache policies.
8. Cache invalidation shall occur immediately after relevant customer data changes where required.
9. Dashboard refresh operations shall minimize unnecessary network traffic.
10. Dashboard APIs shall support pagination for large datasets such as notifications, recent orders, coupons, and activity history.
11. Dashboard APIs shall support configurable rate limiting.
12. Dashboard services shall continue operating using graceful degradation when non-critical backend services become unavailable.
13. Partial dashboard rendering shall be supported during dependent service failures.
14. All dashboard communications shall use HTTPS/TLS encryption.
15. Customer data shall only be displayed after successful authentication and authorization.
16. Personally Identifiable Information (PII) shall be processed according to applicable privacy regulations.
17. Sensitive customer information shall never be exposed through unauthorized dashboard widgets.
18. Dashboard responses shall never expose confidential internal implementation details.
19. Dashboard shall prevent unauthorized access through secure session validation.
20. Dashboard shall support protection against Cross-SSite Scripting (XSS), Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF), session hijacking, clickjacking, injection attacks, insecure direct object references (IDOR), and other common web security vulnerabilities.
21. Dashboard APIs shall validate every incoming request.
22. Dashboard operations shall support distributed tracing using Correlation IDs.
23. Every dashboard access shall generate immutable audit records where required by organizational policies.
24. Audit records shall remain tamper-resistant.
25. Operational metrics shall be collected for monitoring, reporting, observability, analytics, capacity planning, and performance optimization.
26. Dashboard configuration, widget visibility, personalization rules, refresh intervals, recommendation policies, and promotional campaign settings shall be configurable without application redeployment wherever possible.
27. Fraud Detection Service shall continuously evaluate dashboard access behavior.
28. Administrative dashboard configuration shall be governed by Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).
29. Administrative activities shall remain fully auditable.
30. Dashboard shall support localization, internationalization, multiple currencies, multiple time zones, and accessibility requirements.
31. Customer-facing interfaces shall comply with applicable accessibility standards such as WCAG where required.
32. Backup and disaster recovery mechanisms shall protect dashboard configuration, personalization settings, analytics, and audit records.
33. Dashboard shall support responsive layouts across desktop, tablet, mobile web, Android, and iOS platforms.
34. Dashboard shall remain compatible with supported browser versions and mobile application releases.
35. Dashboard architecture shall support future widgets, integrations, and personalization capabilities without significant architectural modifications.
36. The module shall comply with organizational security policies, privacy regulations, cybersecurity standards, ISO 27001 controls where adopted, OWASP recommendations, audit requirements, consumer protection laws where applicable, accessibility standards where applicable, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

---

#### 3.1.35.14 Acceptance Criteria

1. Only authenticated customers shall be able to access the Customer Dashboard.
2. Customers shall only view information associated with their own account.
3. Dashboard information shall be personalized for the authenticated customer.
4. Dashboard shall display customer profile information.
5. Dashboard shall display active orders where available.
6. Dashboard shall display recent order history according to configured business rules.
7. Dashboard shall display current delivery status for active deliveries.
8. Dashboard shall display customer wallet balance.
9. Dashboard shall display available loyalty points.
10. Dashboard shall display eligible coupons and promotional offers.
11. Dashboard shall display unread notifications.
12. Dashboard shall display favorite restaurants where available.
13. Dashboard shall display personalized restaurant recommendations where personalization is enabled.
14. Dashboard shall display saved delivery addresses where applicable.
15. Dashboard shall provide quick access to major platform features.
16. Dashboard shall respect customer privacy settings.
17. Dashboard shall respect notification preferences.
18. Dashboard shall respect language preferences.
19. Dashboard shall respect accessibility settings where applicable.
20. Dashboard widgets shall support configurable visibility according to organizational policies.
21. Dashboard shall support partial rendering when non-critical backend services become unavailable.
22. Dashboard shall display appropriate fallback messages for unavailable services.
23. Dashboard shall synchronize real-time information according to configured refresh policies.
24. Dashboard refresh requests shall update the latest customer information.
25. Fraud Detection Service shall evaluate dashboard access behavior.
26. Dashboard access shall comply with configured authorization policies.
27. Every dashboard access shall generate audit records where required.
28. Dashboard analytics shall be updated after successful dashboard generation.
29. Administrative dashboard configuration shall require Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).
30. Administrative activities shall remain fully auditable.
31. Sensitive customer information shall never be exposed through unauthorized widgets, APIs, logs, analytics events, or customer-facing interfaces.
32. Dashboard APIs shall enforce rate limiting and abuse prevention controls.
33. Operational metrics shall be updated for monitoring, reporting, observability, analytics, performance optimization, and capacity planning.
34. Dashboard information shall remain synchronized across Customer Profile Service, Order Management Service, Restaurant Service, Delivery Service, Wallet Service, Loyalty Points Service, Coupon Service, Recommendation Service, Notification Service, Customer Preferences Service, Address Management Service, Favorites Service, Analytics Service, Fraud Detection Service, and Audit Logging Service.
35. The Customer Dashboard shall remain available under expected production workloads without unacceptable performance degradation.
36. The Customer Dashboard module shall comply with organizational security policies, privacy regulations, cybersecurity standards, audit requirements, consumer protection laws where applicable, accessibility standards where applicable, and all applicable legal and regulatory obligations.

---

## End of Functional Requirements – Customer Module

The functional requirements defined in this section comprehensively describe the customer-facing capabilities of the Food Delivery Platform, including customer registration, authentication, profile management, account security, preferences, wallet management, loyalty program, referrals, payment methods, device management, session management, Two-Factor Authentication (2FA), social authentication, account recovery, and the customer dashboard.

Each functional requirement specifies:

- Functional behavior
- Business rules
- Input validations
- Processing workflows
- Alternate scenarios
- Success and failure responses
- Security controls
- Audit requirements
- Compliance requirements
- Non-functional expectations
- Acceptance criteria

These requirements provide the baseline specification for solution architecture, API design, database modeling, UI/UX implementation, security engineering, quality assurance, user acceptance testing (UAT), production deployment, operational monitoring, and future enhancements for the Customer Module.

The Customer Module shall integrate seamlessly with all dependent platform services while maintaining confidentiality, integrity, availability, scalability, observability, auditability, and compliance throughout the customer lifecycle.

---
